### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T17:53:12+0300

today's report is the same old same old.

Went to see The Avengers** yesterday. Hubby ordered a bag of popcorn. I had a couple nibbles out of it. Notice my sense of smell is sharpening up. I could smell the corn! That delicate odor it has with a slight hint of mustiness. WOW. Guess the Mg is finally working. I did eat a couple of handfuls of the stuff but at least I didn't order a bag for myself. I would have stayed out of it but I got hungry.

Lunch yesterday was a chicken leg and part of the thigh, those ubiquitous Terra veggie chips and olives. I couldn't finish it all but I had been a tad hungry so that's why I fixed it.

Dinner was pretty much nothing. Movie wasn't done until 7 so I just grabbed the last of the package of braunswieger and called it good.

Slept like a rock until 8 this morning. Still trying to wake up so better get off the computer and get to breakfast.

Weight still up from the jerky the other day. nuts.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-15T22:46:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-24353>

Um, normally I don't snack. I've been sticking to 3 meals and nothing else. But I'm not perfect (not matter HOW I come across in my posts, lol) and I sometimes slip. Yesterday was slight slippage. IN the past I would have had a whole bag of popcorn to myself. But yesterday just a couple handfuls was enough and honestly I probably could have skipped those. Old habits die hard. Movies = movie popcorn.

And yeah, brausnwieger does have a few carbs. I DON'T CARE. I don't eat it often because I have a severe MSG sensitivity and most prepared meats are loaded with it. This particular brand seems to be ok as long as I only eat a couple of pieces every other day so so until it's gone (small package, less temptation).

As for sleep ... what has finally fixed mine is:

BHRT a la Wiley Protocol

paleo diet

BLU BLOCKER wraparound sunglasses worn every single night starting at sundown. MAN what differences those babies have made.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-16T17:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-24594>

Too much of a good thing?

Last night, about 9:30 I started to stand up and my legs felt really really weak. I got to the kitchen and almost fainted. I felt light-headed for a bit then got nauseated and then all of a sudden it passed.

I suspect (although I will be checking with my doc) that I may have finally gone over the limit with the mag. I'm feeling a bit funky again this morning but not too bad. Just having a bit of trouble with those damnable allergies. Yet another reason to call the doc.

Nothing else to report. Boring as usual. Yesterday's lunch was a bit of the rest of the leftover chicken. Dinner was a salmon burger and 1/2 a sweet potato with lots of butter.

I drank a lot of water last night when I though my blood pressure might have dropped. I was thinking maybe I was dehydrated again. Must have woke up at least 4 or 5 times during the night so had a sip of water each time.

haven't forgotten my pledge to do a cold bath this week. Yesterday I used my new toy (IR thermometer) to measure the water temp of my shower. I'm not sure how accurate it was as I measured it both in the tub and coming out of the shower. So It was between 70-78Â°. I think what I'm going to do is take my usual shower and when I'm all clean and rinsed off I'll stop the drain and put the overflow cover into place and sit down. Since I'm already used to THAT temp it shouldn't be an issue. Then I can slowly cool the water down until the tub is full. We'll see.

Speaking of cool/cold showers. Here's a tip I figured out early on. Don't get you head wet right away. I used to wash my hair first now I do it last. My reason for doing it that way is that when that cool water hits my head I instantly get cold.

Scale still isn't moving. But my energy level seems to be (finally!) starting to come up. Small steps. Small steps.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-16T21:49:56+0300

If it's not uncommon and will pass before I gain TOO much, that's one thing. Did you gain on it? Do you think I should ask Dr. K? Somebody said if you're gaining on it, your hormones are wacky, but I don't know if my body's just getting used to it since I just started? ### DarleenMB — 2012-05-17T02:41:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-24742>

Hey Joan. Yes I did indeed gain last fall. I started the reset in October and in 2 weeks dropped 10 pounds. Because the breakfast was so much food I was sick and not hungry the rest of the day. It came back and brought some friends. And no, that's not why I went on the BHRT.

I found the Wiley Protocol early last summer referenced in one of Suzanne somer's books. I got the book from the library, devoured it, ordered my own copy and found a WP certified doc to prescribe the hormones for me.

If you've read any of the Dr. Eades's books, Mary Dann Eades says that it wasn't until she got her hormones balanced that she finally started losing which is partly why I went on them myself. I know age isn't a deterrent to losing because I dropped 58 pounds 5 years ago on the jenny craig diet. It took 18 months, I was still a long ways from goal and the last 6 months I only dropped 8 pounds so that tells you how bad THAT diet is.

ANYway. After the first of the year I got back on the reset and MADE myself get at least 60g of protein for breakfast. I did it mostly by using Bioplex whey protein and making shakes for breakfast. Once I felt I was adjusted to that much protein for breakfast and switched over to real food and kept it up for several months. I'm now eating regular meals because I just can't take that much food all in one sitting.

I don't know what to tell you except to trust that this WILL work. If I didn't believe that I'd have quit ages ago but I'm at the end of my rope. NOTHING works. Even starving at 500-1200 calories just doesn't do it. Either this works or I spend the rest of my life being obese.

Thanks for the info Darlene! I did read it on your blog too. Wanted you to know that after all my whiney posts today, suddenly on my journal Dr. Kruse chimed in and said "If your weighing yourself your not doing the reset correctly........I said to throw the scale out and you are just raising your cortisol looking at it." And I sat there like "ok, yes sir, Dr. Kruse". You gotta love him!! So by the time the day ended I had called our local compounding pharmacy and asked who works with BHRT here and there's ONE doctor. I called to make an appointment, and the receptionist said "actually she just told me she'd be working a couple of days next week that she originally wasn't going to, so do you want to come in then? Otherwise, it'll be the end of June." I thought, this is supposed to be happening just the way it is. Everything's falling into place. I trust Jack and feel like I found him by accident for a reason. Nothing else with mainstream medicine has offered me any help, but to tell me if you don't lose exercising one hour, then exercise two hours! So I will trust Jack and give this my best shot. I can't promise what I'll do if I gain TOO much weight cause I just got it all off with hcg from being hit by a car in 2003 and gaining 50 pounds. ### DarleenMB — 2012-05-18T18:13:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-25204>

I was taking 1200 mg of Mg oxide at night and just added in the neuro mag (144mg) in the morning about a week ago. I have been mag depleted evidently for decades. My sense of smell left me ages ago, my sense of taste had deteriorated to about salt/sweet/sour and that's it and my hair ... sigh. Half my hair fell out when I was 17 (stress like you wouldn't believe from my living situation) and over the years it has thinned to where I now have to wear it cut super short because otherwise it looks like crap. I have NEVER had decent fingernails. They are thin and either brittle or peeling. And don't get me started on the thyroid thing.

It was taking that 1200 mg to get me moving on a regular basis. I tried cutting back to 800 a couple of months ago and stopped right up so went back to the 1200. It has only been in the past few weeks I noticed my sense of smell returning.

I mean honestly! When we were at the movies the other day I actually could smell the corn ... I mean really smell it. I remembered that scent from my childhood. Corn has a delicate scent with just a hint of mustiness. I was amazed. So I really needed that much mag but now I'm going to cut back to just the NM in the morning and maybe ONE 400mg mag oxide at night or maybe every other night and see how I do.

It's all good ... the lightheadedness was caused by fluid loss from the diarrhea which I had thought was the mag but it turns out there's a bug trolling around town. Which explains the nausea I had and the lack of appetite.

I'm a little better today (obviously since I am running off at the mouth as usual) and was a bit hungry for breakfast but did have trouble finishing it. Bloated up almost immediately so am not sure what THAT'S about unless it's the last vestiges of the bug.

Gotta run. Hair cut in a bit ... am looking positively shaggy. And a bit like a little Dutch boy. I HATE it when my hair does that "bob" thing. I hated it when I was in the 5th grade and I hate it now.

>

> for the doc ... you may want to get, and read, TS Wiley's ["Sex, Lies and menopause"](http://http://amzn.to/JoayDb) before you go. I found it really helped me when I talked to my doctor.

Current recommendation on his site is the NeuroMag stuff. Mag Oxide is one of the least bio-available forms of magnesium, as I understand it. This is why it takes so much higher of a dose to do any good to the body - because so much of it is getting bound in the intestinal tract. Bound minerals are not absorbed by the body. Again, that's all the layman science I've turned up on it. I could easily be completely wrong, as much as it pains me to admit it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-19T22:07:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-25558>

Check-in time. First off I now will only wear my sunglasses at night. Hmm. Isn't that a song? At the end of today's webinar Jack made a comment (NOT related to factor X and please don't ask) about why we shouldn't wear sunglasses in the daytime. Seems that sunlight entering through the eye promotes (I think that's right) the body's synthesis of vitamin D.

I just made my first foray out into the bright sunshine (with attendant GLARE which is why I wear the things to begin with) and I ... SURVIVED! Okeydokey.

I'm still feeling peckish. Not much appetite and still have diarrhea. I'm now wondering if it's because I ran out of bile acid factors about a week ago and have been taking digestive enzymes instead. The timing is about right. Not having a gallbladder is the pits when you're trying to eat lots of fats. I've put in a recurring order for them at Amazon so I won't run out again. And no, the local health food store hadn't even heard of them, let alone carried them.

The downside of this is that I woke up at least 4 times last night with really bad cramps in my feet and toes. At about 6 I woke up with a major cramp in my left calf which mirrored the one I had in my RIGHT calf 2 days ago. Do you think that I could remember (in the middle of the night) WHERE I had hidden (i.e. "put away") my bottle of potassium? HELL no. Found it when I woke up. Took one, had breakfast and about 4 hours later waved g'bye to it. I haven't suffered the runs like this since I was in college and had diarrhea for a solid week. In the front door, out the back without even stopping to say hello/goodbye. I guess I"m going to have to cut waaay back on the fats for a few days until the BAF gets here.

bother.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-20T22:29:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-25810>

Joan you might have the low=carb flu aka detox. As I understand it (which doesn't mean I know what I'm talking about) you can go through a kind of withdrawal when you eliminate carbs (and esp wheat) and/or as you start burning body fat for fuel stored toxins are released and can make you feel ill.

Don't know if that helps. As to the rest of it it is ALWAYS ALWAYS YMMV (your mileage may vary). Everyone is different, no 2 people are going to have the same experience, therefore, it's kind of problematic to say when, or even if, you might experience what anyone else has gone through.

whew.

So far so good today. No diarrhea since yesterday morning. Of course, today I skipped ALL my supplements, on purpose, just in case something new I have recently added may have been a contributing factor. I'm about 99% sure it has been a bug. Went to bed last night, read for about 10 minutes, turned off the light, rolled over and my damned foot cramped up on me AGAIN. So I got up, took a potassium tab and went back to bed. left calf was complaining just a wee bit this morning but that's been it. I may stay on the potassium for a few more days just to be sure. Drinking water with lemon in it to help with electrolytes and drinking a LOT of water. Will be getting a good probiotic tomorrow as the light dawned this morning that (as DH puts it) my entire bug colony has more than likely left the building along with everything else over the past week.

Was looking out at the creek this morning and thinking "I bet I could have a great CT right about now" since the run-off (as we are laughingly referring to it this year) has finally begun so those waters have gotta be ICY. Last year's run-off was spectacular ... high water for over a month! Most water we've seen in that creek since we moved out here 16 years ago. It churned up a lot of beautiful rocks from the bottom of the stream bed and moved the course of the creek just enough that we now have a lovely, albeit small, pool about mid creek. I should go snap a pic. Anyhoo. DH says it's nice and deep, the current is quick but not worrisome. It IS after all, just a creek. I've seen irrigation ditches that were bigger and carried more water.

OK, was bad yesterday and just now. Bought a small bag of trail mix (all dried fruit and nuts, no sugar or any junky stuff) the other day and just polished it off. sigh. I know I need to go eat something but ... I don't wanna. I'm a little hungry but just not motivated to go eat. I did manage most of my breakfast before it became a chore but I did choke it all down. Maybe I'll go fix another mug of BPC.

Maybe I should go light my torch and melt some glass. I keep threatening to do that but but but

sigh

OHOHOH .... have you seen the may issue of ALLURE magazine? OMFG there is a small spread (no pun intended) of b&w photos of several female celebrities au natural. The photo of Leslie Bibb (who is currently on GCB) just knocked me flat. I try not to look at it too often because I would cause some serious hurt to someone if I could just look like THAT. Morena Baccarin who is, next to Angelina Jolie, my idea of one of the most beautiful women in the world (but only with LONG hair why in hell did she cut it waif short?!) is also featured and I sure wouldn't mind looking like THAT either.

So that's my new goal. Then I looked at my butt in the bathroom mirror. HFMOP (that's holy fracking mother of pearl for those who don't do acronyms well) WHERE DID ALL THAT COTTAGE CHEESE COME FROM? {insert sound of my head banging off the keyboard SEVERAL times}

You know (actually I know you DON'T but WTH) one time, a very loooooooooooooong time ago I got my weight back down to 135 pounds (from a whopping 150 pounds) by living on 4-8 ounce cartons of (loaded with sugar) yogurt a day. I happened to catch a glimpse of my (nude) self in a full length mirror and that image was burned into my brain for all time. I. WAS. TINY. I see that image superimposed over my big fat butt and I want to weep. I used to think that 165 pounds on me was obese. Frankly I'd do someone some serious hurt if I could get back to THAT weight, never mind the 130 I wish I still weighed.

All the pity party. Been a while since I had one. Hope you all enjoyed it with me. Sorry there wasn't any cake but I don't do wheat any more.

OK first try in the tub since my first abortive effort. Didn't do too much better this time. I started in the shower with the water set on cool. Once I got used to it I put the plug in and cut the shower sitting down as the water poured in. I need a bigger tub. With the tub nearly full the girls were still out of the water along with most of my front. I managed to scootch further in but then my legs were way out of the water.

I began shivering after just a few minutes. the crowd began chanting "weenie, weenie, weenie." Yeah yeah, shut up already. I gritted my teeth. "weenie, weenie weenie."

Oh for pete's sake. I didn't even manage to start the timer but I'd guess I lasted a total of under 10 minutes. Which means it was probably more like 5 minutes.

"weenie, weenie, weenie."

Up yours I'm getting out. Grabbed the ir thermometer and checked the water. 73. (louder now) "WEENIE WEENIE WEENIE"

sigh

Out of the water and feeling nice and toasty warm and if the peanut gallery doesn't shut it soon THEY're going in the tub. head first.

On another subject, had to go to town to run a couple off errands and have spent the afternoon wheezing away. I am so sick of this. But at least my sinuses haven't slammed shut. That's happened a couple of times. NOT pleasant. I was hoping if I started the CT baths it might help with the allergies. I did a face dunk this afternoon and plan on doing those twice a day along with alternating the ice on the torso thing. I just have to make myself do it. I'll try the tub again in a couple of days. Maybe the next time I can actually start the stupid timer.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-23T17:20:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-26671>

You all just crack me up! I have to come in here now to get my daily chuckle by reading all your responses. Never thought my juvenile sense of humor would be enjoyed by so many. And btw, I STILL have my 5th grade joke book. I used to LOVE getting my copy of My Weekly Reader during school. And then (OH THE JOY!) you could order books from them!!! That was when I got the joke book. I know I got lots more books but that one was my favorite. I LOVE to laugh. That's when I fell in love with DH. He makes me laugh and always so unexpectedly. His sense of humor is as twisted as mine is.

Well, nothing too entertaining this morning. I decided to forego eggs for a bit to see if they were causing issues. I found a nice big package of chuck steaks yesterday at the store and thought they'd be good for breakfast. Um. Not so much. Maybe had I cooked it just a wee bit longer 'cause all that blood first thing just didn't set well. Don't get me wrong, I like my steak med rare to rare. Just not so bloody it can be restored with liniment and a bandaid. Also the flavor was a bit too strong. I think I'll go back to just-done burgers.

I weighed the steak prior to cooking and it was just under 9 ounces. I cooked up 2 slices of bacon because ... well hell who needs a reason to eat bacon? I managed to get half the steak down and that was it. I'm satisfied. Not full, not stuffed. Just satiated. Nice feeling. Although right after my gut set up to gurgling and moaning and whining. I don't know WHAT that was about. Just noisy. Nothing else. It finally has quieted down. Kinda made me giggle though.

I found an interesting recipe over in the leptin RX section. Sardines and salmon mixed up with some celery and onion and fried up as patties. I think I'll try that for lunch (assuming I need to eat). Tilapia for dinner. I'm really trying to work more fishy into my meals instead of so much beef. The factor X webinar made it quite plain why that's A Good Thing.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-23T17:34:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-26673>

> **justjoan060;26668 wrote:** Your posts are always good for a laugh, Darlene! My laugh for the day. But I'm still trying figure out what pfbbt means??? hmmm?!
>
> At least you're trying! I haven't tried CT yet, but thought of that too - my tub is a small one. I can't get all immersed. Plus I don't have a big freezer, so trying to figure out how I'd do the ice on the torso thing. I will try the face dunks one of these days. Probably will be a weenie too! Getting things checked off the list; blood drawn for hormones, old root canal tooth pulled.

pfbbt ... the sound you make when you do a raspberry.

That old root canal may have been, no pun intended, the root of a lot of your problems. I had one done when I was in my late 20's. I'd say about 10 years later it started hurting. I ignored it because I thought ... how can it possibly hurt? No nerves left. Then I started having major problems with my sinuses. Actually just the ones on the right side. I thought it was allergies. Then, on 2 separate occasions I went to my dentist because of pain on that side. He couldn't find anything.

It got so bad that after many rounds of antibiotics to clear things up my doc sent me to the EENT guy who immediately wanted to do surgery. He had a CT scan done and was really surprised that ONLY the sinus on the right side showed evidence of damage. And just so you all know, I don't have the upper sinuses. You can call me a block head but I say it was so I had more room for my brain cause you all know I am just SO smart.

pfbbt

Anyway. At one point the right side of the roof of my mouth swelled up SO much that it was literally level with the bottom of my upper teeth. Still thought it was my sinuses.

More pain sent me on another trip my dentist (who is a GREAT dentist, btw) who had an AHA moment. Are you all ready?

It seems that when I had the original root canal done they (meaning the dentists at that time) were using SILVER pins in the roots. A few years later they all figured out that, contrary to what they had originally thought, silver does, indeed, react with the body. So for a good 25? years those pins had been causing all that trouble.

I was sent to an endodontist in Billings who jackhammered through the cement in the stupid thing and the INSTANT he pulled out those pins my sinus began to drain and all those problems I had been having simply disappeared. Now they use real rubber to fill the roots of the dead tooth and so far, so good, no problems. And I say that because the following year the tooth next to the old root canal croaked and I had to have a root canal done on THAT one. A couple years later another tooth on the lower jaw blew out and that was my 3rd and I hope FINAL root canal.

So. Long story I know but I wanted you to know just how much damage can be done JUST BY YOUR TEETH. Who ever would have thought that so much havoc could be wreaked by 4 tiny silver pins stuck in a dead tooth?

Side note: The last time I saw my doc about my sinus issues (prior to the fix) and he told me I would have to have another course of antibiotics ... I actually and literally burst into tears. All those antibiotics had played hell with my system. Later I found a fantastic probiotic online and used it to fix those issues.

So JustJoan be sure and LMK how it goes after you get that tooth taken care of ... I'm really interested to see how you do.

RESET

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-24T21:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-27128>

Yeah. I know it's a grain. I LOVE popcorn ... hot buttery NASTY stuff, LOL. I love the crunch the salt the butter ... I mean it's like bacon. What's NOT to like?

Interesting experience this morning after my shower. I was SO cold earlier I put on my heavy duty fluffy robe and a HAT and my slippers. It really isn't that cold but we're in that can't decide if it's gonna be summer, winter or spring thing. After a few days needing the AC then we need the heat. It's like mother nature is having hot/cold flashes.

So I'm freezing already. Get in the shower and have it set a bit warmer than usual. Then I cranked it up for about 2 seconds. Naw. Don't like it that warm any more. Seriously, I don't. turned it back down to warmish. Now I'm really chilled. Turned it down to where I usually have it (around 70ish). That's better. Finished off, shivering away, with a few seconds of even cooler. Hopped out of the shower, toweled off and started putting on lotion. did my face first cause at 60-something you have to fix the stuff that shows, right? As I was putting lotion on my legs I thought I noticed some unusual redness and blotchiness. I didn't 'think too much about it because it was my right thigh and that's the spot Three (wild cat that he is) likes to stick his hooks in when he wants me to get off my butt and get him something to eat. Little rascal. Point being I have a bunch of scabs and scratches there anyway. Then I got to my arms. HOLD the phone! Lots of blotchiness, red speckles and pure unadulterated ugliness (more so than usual). It was kind of rashy looking but it wasn't hive (no itching). And it's mostly gone now. Granted I have the kind of skin that shows the least little mark. You can write on my skin just by dragging a fingernail along it. Not scratching, just pressing.

I'm sitting here wondering what the heck that was about. Any ideas? Anyone?

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-25T17:30:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-27342>

Holy moly. I slept almost ten hours last night! I love it when it's overcast first thing in the morning. I could sleep until noon if it weren't for one mouthy little cat who thinks the universe revolves around him.

Yesterday I decided it's time to quit the eggs for a while. Lunch was a Protein Power style whey shake (3 scoops of Bioplex why protein with 4 TBS canned coconut milk) dinner was a tiny (like probably 4oz or less) chuck steak, some avocado and veggie chips.

Today's menu:

3 Protein Power style shake (see above) during the day and

dinner: baked tilapia

I'm going to go back to the 6 week cure for the middle aged middle and start over.

THEN we'll see how I'm doing. I feel like I'm just spinning my wheels and after nearly 7 months something needs to give.

I have never been able to stick to the 6 week program for more than a week so we'll see.

### justjoan060 — 2012-05-25T19:36:42+0300

I'm quit eggs, too, as an experiment although I've never had a problem with them before, then I had three a couple of mornings ago and I didn't notice any difference. ### DarleenMB — 2012-05-25T21:15:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-27410>

LOL you obviously haven't read the 6 week cure by the Drs. Eades. It's all good. And I lasted one half of the morning. I just cannot do nothing but shakes. My stomach has been upset all morning and I've been bloated so got real pissy. Now I'm thinking of a salmon patty for lunch.

The 6 week cure is a do=it-yourself liver cleanse. LOTS of people have had great success with it but I just cannot seem to get those shakes down. For me, at my current weight, it's 3 ... THREE ... shakes with about 60g of protein in each one. I can do 2. I can get by with one but I have only been able to do 3 a couple of times. It's just too much.

I keep telling myself that my body will heal and eventually drop all this flab. I quit getting on the scale so I wouldn't get super frustrated. Then I look at my belly in the mirror and I just want to scream. I still have NO energy. Well, next to none. I can manage a few household chores daily but that's it. I wish Jack would post a complete list of what he thinks are essential blood tests so I could give that to my doc with a reason why he needs to order them. I know he would. I had my HS CRP checked and while it's low-ish is should and could be better. My D levels are STILL in the basement and this time after I TOLD the PA D3 they gave me D2. asshats (pharmacist who argued the last time I requested D3). The H1AC is too high in my book. Never had a reverse T3 and I know I need to have my omega's checked. But other than that I don't know what else to do. And I don't know that any of that will help.

I just cannot make myself climb into a tub of ice water. I know I need to do face dunks daily and use the ice on the torso. But I fell off THAT wagon. So I have no one to blame but me.

I KNOW that ice baths destroy leptin and fat cells. So why can't I just DO IT? My excuse has been that the last few weeks the weather has been so up and down that I have been constantly chilled. I run around barefoot (as usual) and with summery clothing on so it's easy for me to get really cold. Then I REALLY don't want to get out the bag of ice or get the tub full of cold water and climb in 'cause I'm ALREADY COLD.

blah blah blah. yet another pity party. Did you wear your party hat?

I keep forgetting I WAS sick with some kinda bug for a good week and I haven't fully recovered from that. Woke up with leg cramps AGAIN because I forgot to take the potassium like I had planned. grrrrrrrr

I need a keeper. Or a rubber room.

### vkiernan — 2012-05-26T02:43:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-27513>

Darleen, Here is the blog that tells you which labs. Hope it is what you are looking for.

[http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/](http://http://jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

I have to tell you that when I am in the tub for CT'ing, the first 5 min. I am cold and start to shiver(this is new for me, wasn't for the 1st few months) and then after that I warm up. My body just heats up and I don't feel cold. I sit and read until I get cold which is usually around the 25 to 30 min. mark. Eat a very large meal, then start drinking the ice water he recommends and then get in the tub and put a smidgen of hot water in to trick your brain and then turn on the cold only and let it fill slowly. Give your body a chance to adapt and see how it goes. A bath is way easier than a shower.

When I first started I only did the bottom half up to the girls and wore a light jacket and a scarf on my head. Now I just wear the scarf and put a towel around my neck and get as much in as I can and eventually remove the towel.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-26T18:39:46+0300

did way better on the meals yesterday. Had a salmon burger for lunch with avocado. Dinner was some rotisserie chicken and sweet potato with butter. No snacking (which has become pretty much the norm with only a tiny slip here and there). Bed by 10 and slept until about 6:30.

I wish things would DO something. ANYthing. then I remind myself that it took me decades to get to this point so it may take a lot more time than I would wish to get things straightened out. I just HATE being so damned FAT.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-27T03:43:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-27780>

Just finished dinner which was WAY yummy.

Wild caught dover sole, layered in buttered pan with cherry tomato halves and some (sorta dried out) home grown mushrooms hubby produced out in the garage a week or so ago. sprinkled with dried basil but fresh would have been way better, smothered with sour cream as I didn't have any creme fraiche on hand, then topped with a heavy sprinkle of parmesan shreds. mmmmmmm it sure was tasty! Added in a few spears of asparagus (frozen, not fresh but it's still good!) and a few sweet potato fries and I am GOOD.

It's amazing to feel satisfied but not stuffed. Used to be I'd eat until I thought I'd pop but would (a lot of times) still feel like I needed to eat something. Not anymore.

Oh and lunch was an avocado with a few chunks of pickled herring. It tasted just too darned sweet for me. I don't know why they think they have to add so much sugar to stuff. won't be getting THAT brand again. Plus the herring was kinda tough.

ETA: 3 hours later dinner made a reappearance. I felt fine for about 1/2 hour after eating then started to feel awful. I strongly suspect the mushrooms. They were blue oyster mushrooms, a kind I have never had before. I should have known better as I know I am allergic to molds (household type but still). It felt like an allergic reaction. My tongue felt itchy, that's the only way I can describe it, my breathing was a little messed up and my stomach was NOT happy.

Well I'm better now except for the acid burning in the back of my throat. One more item on my do NOT eat list. blech.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-28T18:04:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-28042>

Gotta say that it appears, already, I may have to ditch the eggs permanently. I managed 8oz of ground chuck and 2 thick slices of applewood smoked bacon for breakfast this morning without batting an eye. plus my usual mug of bpc with 4 TBS butter. The mushroom incident of night before last appears to have finally jarred me loose in that regard. In looking back it may have been an issue with eggs that was causing all the nausea and inability to eat much in the morning. huh.

And here I always thought I really didn't like eggs because they came out of the back end of a chicken (true ... that's what I told my Mom many many eons ago when we lived with her parents. They raised chickens). Mayhap it was because of something else?

Was barely hungry yesterday after the 6oz of beef etc for BAB. Had half of half a chicken breast, half an avocado for lunch and the remainder for dinner.

Don't have any idea what's on the menu for today. Household chores await. Company coming later this week so need to get ready.

### DarleenMB — 2012-05-29T17:12:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-28173>

Thanks abetter me... I'm feeling MUCH better as well.

First things first. I had this crazy assed dream this morning which I'm sure relates to the Factor X webinar which I have been trying to process but cannot because I lack the education to put all the parts together. All the biologists on here need to speak "little" to folks like me who only had ONE biology class all through school. ONE. And one human physiology class. I liked earth science.

ANYway.

I dreamed I was looking at a photo pictorial in businessweek magazine (of all things). It showed all the world leaders swimming through dark, cold waters and they formed a sine wave. Then I spotted a rat (no pun intended. Or maybe it was. My subconscious has a wicked sense of humor) and a dog swimming with them. There was an article about something important which I can no longer remember but I was so excited to show it to Jack.

Then the magazine was gone and I was in the water watching the wave of humanity move away. There was a stone causeway nearby and I swam towards it where someone tried to help me out of the water.

THEN (and this is where it gets weird, lol) I was hovering above the earth. It was snowy and cold and there was a cave. I watched as a jet flew out of the opening and men ran out from the cave and aimed guns at the sky. Two views at once. The thought came to me that whoever was flying the jet was too busy with the business of flying it to defend themselves and that's why the guys on the ground were there.

OK. Interpret away. I have my own thoughts but want to hear what others think.

Didn't eat lunch yesterday but that may have been not only the BAB at work but I had a 2nd mug of BPC which resulted in a bit of caffeine overload. ick. Chicken and avocado for dinner.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-04T16:04:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-29343>

Well that was a wild weekend! Outlaws went home yesterday and we both collapsed. You wouldn't think a pair of 80-somethings and a pair of 60-somethings could wreak so much havoc. You'd be wrong.

We did sit on the patio a bit yesterday morning before they left and I managed a wee bit of sunburn on my arms and totally burned the back of my neck (it's not bad but it's red). vitamin D quota achieved yesterday, for sure.

I managed to avoid falling off the dietary cliff even though I did mess up my schedule a bit. Did get my BAB in every morning. So glad everyone sleeps later than I do!

I'm out of ground chuck so have to have a steak for breakfast today. wah wah. Poor me. AND it appears I have lost 0.6 pounds in the last couple of weeks. Since I only quit eating eggs about a week ago I think that's pretty good. I expect the scale to start dropping now especially since I am not very hungry the rest of the day. Hungry enough for a light dinner but that's about it.

Gotta run. Have to put some chipotle shredded beef into the slow cooker. Well, technically it's not shredded yet. It will be when I'm done and we're having it on romaine lettuce leaves. Can't wait!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-04T18:53:41+0300

I wanted to mention than in the last couple of days I have noticed a VAST improvement in my mood. I mean a REALLY BIG improvement. I don't quite know how to describe it except I'm happier? Not letting the little things bug me so much? Man. I don't know but it's pretty awesome from this side. I keep wondering who the hell IS this woman and what did she do with my regular persona? If this is the beginnings of LS then I gotta tell ya I'm LOVIN' IT!

I'm also noticing a bit more energy. Too bad it doesn't last long enough (yet). Who knows, one of these days I might be able to run all day without fading out into exhaustion partway through.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-04T19:51:56+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-06T18:56:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-29740>

Yesterday was ... interesting.

Had to go run errands and it was HOT .. 93 which is unusual for Wyoming this time of year. AND the wind was blowing. I mean REALLY blowing. Got my shopping done, fought the wind as I dumped stuff in the van, got home and unloaded everything. And then my energy just vanished. I was just plain pooped. Kept want to nibble carbs. I truly was not hungry but was SO tired all I wanted to do was eat.

I finally tried to take a nap. Not happening.

About 4 or so had a second mug of BPC which did nothing except curb my hunger. So when I finally got to dinner (2nd meal of the day) I just didn't want much. Ate a couple of chicken wings, a TBS (literally) of potato salad ... yes I know. And a half handful of sweet potato chips. AND woke up about 1A.M. with major hunger. Why am I not surprised. I told my body to just get over it, burn some excess flab and let me sleep.

Woke way too early as I left one of the drapes open so we could get some fresh air the bedroom. Oh well.

BAB was the usual 8 oz gf ground chuck and 2 strips THICK bacon, bpc

Probably no lunch as I really am not hungry these days.

Dinner ... fish.

Had planned on doing laundry yesterday but if I'd hung the sheets out in that wind they would have wound up in Montana. The wind was blowing from the South. weird. So get to do it today PLUS put my bedding plants in the planters. Got my work cut out for me.

Oh yeah, I got so overheated I went and sat on the bottom step and stuck my legs in the creek. oooooo it felt SO good. Snowmelt. Really COLD!!! I plan on doing it again today. I'll take a pic.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-06T21:03:49+0300

Was up at 6:30. It's now noon. Fed the cats, cleaned the litter pans, had breakfast, did 3 loads of sheets, one of towels, 3 loads of clothes and have them all hung out on the line.

Had my shower.

Planted one big planter, 2 window boxes, and put one big (deer resistant, so it says) plant in one of the barrels.

Sat with my feet in the creek for a few minutes.

I'm pooped.

that's the most I've moved in ages all at one time. Must be that BAB at work, LOL.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-08T02:33:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30018>

> **vkiernan;29972 wrote:** Darleen, got my book Sex, Lies and Menapause. This is not what expected it to be but is a very good book with a whole different twist on things. Thank you for recommending it. What I find very interesting is how she talks about "summer" all year long. She makes the very points Dr. K is talking about. She too says that carbs are fine in summer but in winter we should be not eating them and it should be dark and cold and we should want to sleep not be awake frollicking about. We have to be grateful to be alive today considering what women went thru medically way back in the day. Docs were learning and the things they did to people was crazy.
>
>
>
> Sounds like you were rockin' it yesterday. I need some of that energy. I hope you have another nice day!

I'd be curious to know what you DID expect from this book. And frankly, there's no need to look at what women went through "back in the day" (a phrase I simply loathe but that's just me). Look at what most docs do today.

A woman in her 40's goes to the doc and complains about being depressed. He immediately puts her on an antidepressant and shoves her out the door.

I want to bitch slap every one of them.

Depression is a hallmark of menopause. Instead of giving the woman a pill she will ultimately be (almost) addicted to, he should be checking her hormone levels and talking to her about BHRT. If he (or she) doesn't know enough about that he (or she) should send the patient to someone who DOES.

As for my energy burst ... it came and went. I'm still pooped today but did manage to get almost all the rest of the bedding plants into their containers, hauled home two more giganto bags of potting soil and and and Oh wait that was it.

I think I"m going to start doing just a weekly update unless something really exciting happens. Not much point to coming in and saying the same old same old.

ON THAT NOTE ... I have to tell you all how much I am enjoying my cool showers! I stand under that cool water and murmur little sounds of pleasure just like I used to do in those hot steamy showers I used to take. Hmmm. This is sounding X rated, LOL!

ANYhoo. The warmer weather is helping and I was busy which got me really warmed up (shall we call it CT foreplay?) so that the cooler the water the happier I became. This is just SO weird! I was thinking if this feels this good I think I'm ready for a long soak in a cool bath! Came out of the shower with my face glowing and pink cheeked -- as were those _other_ sticky-outy parts. I haven't seen this much color in my face since I was a kid! Or maybe it was the last time I blushed up a storm. Whatever.

Can't use the creek anymore. Either the runoff has slowed or the greedy ranchers up creek from us are taking our portion again. {insert naughty word}

Maybe when the current slows back down I'll wade out to one of the new shallow pools and enjoy a dunk.

So I'll check back in on Monday. that'll be weigh-in day. Oh yeah, here's one of those TMI bits ... I thought my breasts felt like they had gotten a wee bit smaller lately. Frankly they could get a WHOLE lot smaller and I would be thrilled. So I dug out one of my regular underwires and tried it on ... well huh. The cups are now a bit too big. DARN. See it's hard to tell wearing one of those fabulous genie bras. Man I just love those things. Like going bra-less but with plenty of support.

OKAY. See ya all on Monday!

I hope you enjoy your weekend.

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-08T19:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30168>

Darlene, AMEN AND AMEN on the _A woman in her 40's goes to the doc and complains about being depressed. He immediately puts her on an antidepressant and shoves her out the door. I want to bitch slap every one of them._ Been there, done that I complained of not being able to sleep and all they offered is an antidepressant. The drug of choice - that and Ambien!!

FYI, girls, years ago I read "The Wisdom of Menopause" by Dr. Christine Northrup. Amazing book with a wealth of information. It helped me a lot, and she recommended BHRT but of course nobody around here worked with it. They even looked at me funny up at Mayo when I asked about it. That was an endo in 2005! Not like 50 years ago!

Darlene, will you tell me how cold, how long you do your showers? I have to figure something out because my tub isn't big enough to get immersed, and I would have to stop and buy a bag of ice every time I did spot CT'ing, which I may do! Did you start with face dunking? Do you do it only after a high fat meal? I can do showers although our tap water is 70 degrees! Better than nothing, I figure and it would be a start. You're my hero, babe, so can you tell me about your showers?

It helped me a lot, and she recommended BHRT but of course nobody around here worked with it. They even looked at me funny up at Mayo when I asked about it. That was an endo in 2005! Not like 50 years ago!
>
> Darlene, will you tell me how cold, how long you do your showers? I have to figure something out because my tub isn't big enough to get immersed, and I would have to stop and buy a bag of ice every time I did spot CT'ing, which I may do! Did you start with face dunking? Do you do it only after a high fat meal? I can do showers although our tap water is 70 degrees! Better than nothing, I figure and it would be a start. You're my hero, babe, so can you tell me about your showers?

Well Joan, let's see. Yes I started with face dunking and progressed to the big bag o'ice on the belly. Worked up to an hour with the compression tee on then did a couple of times with it on my bare skin but not for more than 15 minutes. Then I kinda fell off the ice wagon.

The cool showers have been ongoing since, I dunno, last Feb? when Jack made me rethink the whole "long, hot, steamy shower" thing. I've gradually been lowering the temp until yesterday when I put it on full cold. And our cold tap water is still REALLY cold. Now I'm starting to have dreams about cold baths, LOL! Seriously! So I think it's time.

Since your tap water is so much warmer than mine I would offer the opinion that it's a great way to get (pardon the pun) your feet wet. Start with the water as cool as you can stand it and just try dropping it a little more each time. Just follow your comfort level. If you go too cold and are standing there turning blue and shaking like a leaf then I'd think that would be a bit much. I definitely think using the ice bag is a GREAT way to ease into it all and I'm glad I did it. I still have that 20# bag in the freezer and keep thinking I should go back to the bare skin bit.

HTH!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-09T20:50:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30303>

It now appears that eggs are definitely a problem for me.

We had friends from Washington stop by for a brief visit on their way home and we joined them for breakfast yesterday before they left. Without thinking I spooned out a bunch of scrambled eggs from the buffet and it wasn't until I finished them I realized I shouldn't have done that.

I was OK for most of yesterday but was feeling tummy upset by last night and again this morning. And then it hit ... they don't call it the "runs" for nothing. I had to RUN down the hallway to the bathroom and barely made it. Good thing our house isn't all that big!

so I need to engrave NO EGGS on my brain.

I did weigh this morning and was up 0.2 pounds. CRAP.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-09T21:14:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30306>

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-09T22:21:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30314>

Nope. Eggs at home do the same thing. I have to stay away from them.

also I forgot to mention an interesting little tidbit I found. All this diarrhea has resulted in dehydration, leg cramps and major fatigue. I've been trying to remember to take a potassium tab but kept forgetting. Last night I remembered reading something about potassium loss in the Drs. Eades's last book **The 6 Week Cure for the Middle-Aged Middle. It states:

> ... This prescribed regimen wil replace critical electrolytes and minerals, such as potassium, magnesium, and calcium, that the body may lose at a greater rate during this metabolic switchover period; it will boost levels of iodine and iodide, which are substances clinical testing has found to be low in 90% of tested subjects and critical to normal metabolism ... Magnesium helps to maintain the potassium balance, so look for a product that contains both in near equal amounts -- aim for about 400mg of each one ... We implore you to heed this advice; _unless you replenish the potassium you'll lose every day in the first two weeks as you lose fat and shed excess retained fluid, you may become very tired and could suffer muscle cramping or even heart rhythm disturbances._

**emphasis is mine

So potassium loss from all that diarrhea could explain a lot of what I have been through in the last several weeks and am STILL suffering with. Now I know to match the potassium to the mag and hopefully that will put an end to this strange fatigue I have been having off and on. It's been quite bad the last day or two with my legs feeling like lead. Last night I could barely move around the house. After I took 4 potassium tabs with one mag capsule I began to feel better.

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-10T18:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30422>

I have the same problems, Darlene. I sweat a lot when I'm active - always have. When I did the big bike ride across Iowa, I'd have to take salt pills and two bottles of water between towns. (95 and no shade on that ride) Lately I've been overly tired and getting cramps in my feet at night, AND my little heart arrythmias have been worse lately. So what he said makes sense. I haven't been doing that diet, but do think I get dehydrated easily. I recently started taking Potassium again, but don't always remember. I'm glad you posted that advice!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-10T19:45:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30427>

JustJoan I have to say I feel much better right after taking the mag and the 4 potassium tabs. I only had one of the potassium yesterday with my neuromag and by 8pm was just dragging. I took the 4+1 before bed but also took them again this morning at breakfast. I'll to the neuromag with dinner and take another potassium then. We'll see how I'm feeling at bedtime.

The lethargy really scares me. It's like my legs are lead weights and I can hardly move. If it doesn't improve with the potassium I guess I'll have to make another trip to the doc. darn it!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-11T17:40:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30525>

I decided to skip the cow this morning and had 2 Schwan's salmon filets. It wasn't as bad as I thought it would be ... having fish for breakfast ... and I did mange to get most of it down but now my stomach is upset. Just great. Now I suppose I'm either allergic to salmon or to coffee. Or maybe it's the bile acid factors capsule I take before every meal. A few unladylike belches and now it's not so bad. geez.

the weather here has turned cool/cold again. I suppose we're going to have one of those summers ... hot and cold and cold and hot and cold again. The joys of living in Wyoming. One of many too numerous to count. Makes it less inviting hopping into that cool shower.

Made a quite tasty shrimp salad for dinner last night, even DH commented on how good it was. Must have been all that mayo.

I know I keep slipping into ketosis ... cat pee indeed. Sure wish I'd stay there long enough to LOSE SOME DAMNED WEIGHT!!!

Still tired and was twitchy last night while trying to go to sleep. Had a hard time of it ... most unusual these days ... and woke up early. So I"m still taking the extra K with the Mg. Now if I can just figure out what I'm reacting to so I don't have all those lovely symptoms.

I think I need a nap. Already.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-11T20:19:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30559>

ANNND it's looking like I am allergic to fish. Same symptoms as from the Great Mushroom Incident (where I had Dover sole) ... upset tummy bordering on nausea, increased difficulty breathing, sneezing (a lot) and generally feeling like home made dog doo-doo.

(insert choice of 4 letter word with many exclamation points)

And NOW to add insult to injury, I;m reading [this September blog post from Jack](http://http://jackkruse.com/msg-your-gut-and-your-brain-post-trauma/) and am wondering if I have destroyed my hypothalamus. When aspartame first came out I was literally living on diet soda. I drank the darned stuff for 10 years, at least, before I finally just quit altogether. Then I read an article about how bad aspartame was by a doctor and I tossed EVERYthing in my pantry that contained the stuff.

I have NEVER (except inadvertently) used any food with MSG (or so I thought. had to rethink that after seeing the list of pseudonyms) because it gives me a 24 hour migraine=like headache (and it's 24 hours nearly to the minute), nausea and diarrhea. So I really REALLY make a major effort to avoid it at all costs. Much to the consternation of relatives and wait staff.

But maybe all this (leptin reset, etc) is going to be of no avail. Maybe I have permanently damaged myself and am now stuck.

I'd bang my head on the keyboard but I already don't feel so good.

I think I'm going to go cry.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-11T20:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30565>

Darleen, I just re-read that post because I used to use diet soda years ago. Although the post wasn't encouraging to past users of aspartame I didn't get the sense that we're totally screwed (for lack of a better word). "So if these neurons become damaged or rendered non functional your hormones will never be optimal with diet alone".

Which is why we need help via hormones and such??? IDK, a few years ago I'd be right into the science of all this but I feel like it's over my head lately, which I mainly blame on the brain fog!

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-11T21:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30576>

Well, Darlene, just DANG IT!!! I feel for you. I couldn't eat fish for breakfast if I had to - I generally don't like fish. I like shrimp, tuna, salmon, but not for breakfast!! Doesn't seem like it's a typical allergy, but what do I know! Maybe it is something else like ealachan says. I did look over the weekend, and my Potassium's only < 100 per capsule, so I started taking more of that. I did have more energy this weekend. I have 22 windows on my first floor and got them all washed!

I also have started back taking my Juice Plus capsules (you can Google it) which always seem to help my energy level and I don't get the colds/flu when everybody else at work has them. Haven't asked Dr. K an opinion on that.

I just went and read that September blog post (printed off the MSG list so I can have it with me shopping). I didn't read anything that sounded like your hypothalmus is "destroyed". It says it "may not be able to be managed with food only", unless I read it wrong. I do believe the body has amazing healing capabilities, so hang in there! Thanks for passing along info. It helps other people!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-12T01:20:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30622>

OK maybe I over reacted ... maybe "damaged beyond repair" is more accurate.

and the only thing I ate this morning was the salmon. Had another mug of BPC a bit ago and NONE of the symptoms I had earlier appeared. I googled food allergies and it turns out an allergy to fish is quite common.

Resupplied myself with kombucha from the health food store and this time they had L-glutamine but no FOS. Ditched the liquid Splenda (i quit using the packets long ago because of the maltodextrin) and bought a bottle (ye gads it's pricy!!) of liquid stevia. Tossing the Truvia as it is erythritol. I HATE sugar alcohols.

So I'm slowly trying to get my leaky gut healed (because I'm pretty sure that's BIG issue for me). And it's back to big burgers for breakfast.

Thanks ladies. I appreciate the input!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-12T01:45:43+0300

### justjoan060 — 2012-06-12T05:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30650>

Crap! I just read what Dr. K posted! I've used Sweet N Low in my coffee for years! I've FINALLY gotten used to Stevia, so maybe I can get less and less dependent on sweeteners at all. And yes, liquid Stevia is pricey!!

Also, was going to tell you I had bought some frozen Wild Alaskan Salmon patties at Sam's Club, read the label and it looked pretty legit! After seeing the MSG list today, I re-read the ingredients tonight before making some. Sure enough, "yeast extract" (MSG)! Insert 4 letter word here...!!!!!! They were really good though. I'm not gonna throw them out!

By the way, Darlene, I don't know if I've mentioned before, but I've been getting some therapy from a Physical Therapist that does some cranio sacral and other treatments that aren't real mainstream. I've been sleeping better since and have been able to wean down some on the dose sleep med I take. I did go grain and sugar free about the same time, so it's a little hard to tell what's helping, but I'm pretty sure the cranio sacral therapy has been helping me sleep better and stay generally calmer through this divorce. We do Reflexology, and some other alternative therapies at the clinic where I work and I had good luck with that also. You might try it. I believe in it! They work mainly on points on your feet. \*(maybe you've had it before) Just thought I'd throw out a few things I think are effective along with the dietary changes. For what it's worth.....

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-12T17:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30706>

JustJoan, my monthly treat is a visit to my massage therapist and she has done a lot of cranio-sacral work on me. I'm very open to alternative treatments and have tried acupuncture (now THAT was weird!) but cannot afford to continue that on a regular basis along with the massage. I used to go to the chiro on a regular basis (ours is a PEACH! He has a master's in sports med and his doctor of chiropractic or whatever you call it) but again, it's just too much with the massage. If I'd get back to making and selling beads I could afford both or even all three. And would NEED them! LOL

Ealachan, I hear ya on the hubs thing. Mine is still eating the occasional DQ blizzard. I did get him down to sugar free popsicles but now have to take THOSE away. He is not going to be happy. Although his weight, unlike mine, is easily fixed. He quit all things wheat in January and has lost (effortless, the butthead!) 20 pounds. He nibbles his way past dinner, crunches on nuts until I have to leave the room or else smack him with a heavy object and he keeps losing. It is so frustrating I cannot believe it.

Jack, thanks for the input. NOT. just kidding. This could explain why the hcg diet did not work very well for me. That and all the food allergies I am starting to uncover. I keep a list of current no-no's on the whiteboard on the fridge. Just in case I forget. Doesn't always help because yesterday I had a yen for some raw almonds. they don't seem to bother me as much as the processed ones. gee. I wonder why THAT is. duh.

So off to read through more of Jack's blog postings to see if I can uncover what I need to be doing. I'm starting to think that I need a phone consult with him. Better start saving my money because I AM going to do the 23andme thing first and I'll need all the blood tests done. This may take a while.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-13T22:24:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-30966>

I snagged this little template from paulalynn. Thanks!

**Breakfast within 30 min of waking: as close as I could get

**Breakfast 50 grams of protein: 8 oz ground sirloin 44g?? is that right? 2 slices ultra thick applewood smoked bacon mmmm

**No Snacking:

**5 hours between Breakfast & Lunch:

**5 hours between Lunch & Dinner:

Dinner within 4-5 hours of bedtime**:

Scale of 1 to 10, 10 high**

Energy?** 3

Mood?** 4

Cravings?** 0

I haven't said anything about this because, frankly, I've been too damned tired. It has been SO bad that a couple of nights ago I could literally barely get up off the couch and walk to the bedroom. I was starting to think something was really really wrong until 2 nights ago I was having the same thing happen and went and lay down for about 30 minutes. I didn't sleep, just closed my eyes and lay there. It helped. I realized I might not have been sleeping very well and I know I've been waking WAY too early the last week. So now I think it's been a lack of sleep although it has never ever affected me like this.

I also wondered if it was one of the new supplements I've added to the pile. Yesterday I skipped all of the supps except the Mg and the K. I take my supps twice a day. Once with breakfast and the remainder with lunch. Last night I got so bone weary AGAIN I had trouble moving around.

So I'm thinking maybe it's the extra potassium and I skipped THAT this morning and took all my regular supps. Had my breakfast and I'll be darned if 2 hours later my stomach didn't start to REALLY hurt AGAIN. (insert sound of creaky rusty mind trying to turn over)

hmmm

Could it _possibly_ be the cold water omega capsules? Well DUH you dummy! If you are, indeed, allergic to fish and esp salmon! WTH are you taking fish oil for???????

This might explain a lot. I'm ditching the fish oil (I'm also taking krill oil so we'll see if these nasty symptoms continue with that) for now. I have an appt with my doc next week who specializes in allergies. I'm scheduled for a blood draw friday for what our local hospital calls a "wellness screening." I'm confident almost every city and town does an annual health fair where they check your now-standard tests and tell you how close you are to dropping dead of a coronary because your cholesterol is 2 points over the latest low mark. Our local hospital now offers that test every week on Friday, same price as the health fair ($45). It's been over a year since I had this done and thought I'd like to see if there have been any significant changes.

I'm also MAKING myself drink lots of water because the last several months I've really fallen off the ol' wagon. I am STILL having horrible muscle craps. I woke up early this morning with a cramp on the side of my right leg that felt like it was pulling my foot sideways. A few hours later my left leg cramped the same way only higher up. My feet have been cramping (at night while I'm sleeping) as have my calves. WTh? The only other thing I can come up with as a cause is dehydration. Hence the water police.

Now I've worn myself out. And I'm feeling that stupid mouth thing that comes with the stomach ache. It's so weird. The only way I can describe it is to say it feels like ... tsk ... you know when you pour soda over ice and you get all those eensie bubbles that jet up off the surface? THAT'S what my mouth feels like. It's kind of tingly, kind of a little bit hurting and majorly annoying. I keep feeling like I need to scratch my tongue or the roof of my mouth. It's not bad right now but it's there. UGH!

Darleen, are you getting enough SALT? Potassium and magnesium are electrolytes, but so is sodium. If one of them gets out of whack it can throw the whole balance off and make you feel craptastic - tired, muscle cramps, the whole nine yards.

Just a thought. Hope you're feeling better soon!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-14T05:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-31068>

Thanks paulalynn. I'm pretty sure it's allergies. I couldn't figure out why I was feeling crappy again this afternoon and DH insists it must be some sort of issue with my supplements. Thank you, dear.

THEN I remembered I decided I could eat some raw almonds. um. Maybe that wasn't such a good idea. It IS on the top of my list followed by eggs, fish and mushrooms.

I suspect that by eating clean for so long the allergies which have always been there are now really beginning to flare. Enough that I now have to pay attention. At least, that's my theory and I'm stickin' to it for now.

By eating the almonds, btw, my appetite completely disappeared and I had to force myself to eat 2 thin but large slices of Amish deli ham, 6 beanitos (couldn't help myself, blood glucose levels be damned) and a tiny handful of sweet potato chips. It was all I could manage. That and a cup of plain kefir. I still feel crappy. but my stomach is getting better even though my tongue is still itching.

I notice a definite difference when I stay away from trigger foods. The change in feeling is like throwing a switch. Happened last evening so all the crap must have finally been cleared out of my system and the inflammation from eating the almonds fell below some threshold. Went to bed at 9pm as I was TIRED. Woke up about 6 but still need more sleep.

Am keeping my fingers crossed that my next weigh-in will show a drop since I'm really paying close attention to what I'm eating and how I feel.

3 days ago I was RAVENOUS. Ate lunch AND dinner.

next day I ate raw almonds and my appetite evaporated.

Felt better yesterday and was STARVING for lunch. At the 5 hour mark I was so hungry I could have eaten the cat. Raw. Good thing I had some ham in the fridge. FAMISHED again in about 4 hours.

So what I think is going on is that the food allergies have kept my real appetite at bay. Here I thought it was ME. I'm curious to see what will happen today but be assured I will be near the kitchen about 1 which will be closing in on the 5 hour mark.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-18T18:18:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-31938>

My stupid allergies have had me laid low for days now. It's so bad even HUBBY is complaining about itchy eyes and runny nose. My energy level is about 1 and I am sick and tired of hacking and hawing. I sound like a 90 year old man with sinusitis.

Am beginning to discover exactly what I should NOT be eating ... like anything with sugar alcohols in it no matter how minute. They wreak havoc with my blood glucose levels. When I eat ONLY proteins and fats and tiny bits of carbs my fasting BG tests out perfect. It was 79 the other morning. Last night I had 3 of those tiny sugar free Werther's and this morning my BG was 99. Guess what I'm giving up.

I have also stopped all supplements with the exception of the bile acid factors. If I don't take THAT I simply do not digest any fats I eat and probably don't get all the benefit of the protein. I cut way back on the butter and CO in my coffee. Not because I don't want it but because if I'm a pig about it then I'm just STUFFED at breakfast. Anyway, still having a little "tongue tingling" again this morning and am wondering WHAT is causing it. It just might be my seasonal allergies because they are SO bad right now that even medication isn't helping. But something I have been taking has been making it worse.

And on the TMI front, I was NOT paying attention again and am once again constipated. Stupid frakking allergies. GAAAAH! This is just MADDENING! No wondering I feel like crap. Yeah yeah, I know. I'm full of it. pfffbbbbbbtttt

I was hoping to get to the torch today so if I'm going to do that before the temps get too high I'd better skedaddle. Will try to remember to update:

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-19T17:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-32140>

Nothing new here. Same old same old. Went to see Prometheus ... good movie, fantastic visuals. Ate popcorn. Now here's the interesting part. Eating popcorn leaves me with a FBC the next morning of (this time) 86. Eating 4 or 5 of those tiny SF werthers spikes me up to as high as 123. Yeah yeah I shouldn't eat either. I'm having a tantrum. I eat the same BAB every day (going on 8 months now). Eat clean 99% of the time. have I lost anything? Only my temper. It's not like I'm only 10 pounds over weight. I'm frakking 90 pounds + over weight.

Never mind that I have discovered that food allergies either make me gorge on the trigger food or lose my appetite altogether. That's was a surprising revelation. sigh. I guess I'm getting PO'd over what few foods are left that I CAN eat. Which isn't really the case but it sure feels that way. I mean, nuts, eggs, dover sole, salmon all for sure. Chicken? Not sure about that one yet. Having Moroccan chicken tonight from one of my paleo cook books. We'll see how I tolerate that. Mushrooms. Short list but I'm in a whiny mood. Pass the cheese (to go with my whine).

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-22T05:51:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-32894>

Slow day today. HAd an appt with my Doc. He ordered a RAST to check food allergies. Will go in next Friday for pin test on pollens etc. Agreed that my CRP indicates low systemic inflammation and that it's possible unknown food allergies could be keeping me from losing weight. Then he mentioned "diet & exercise." LOL. I had given him Jack's intro page and the leptin RX pages that I had printed out. I sure hope he will go to Jack's blog and start reading!

Noticed the last couple of days my skin is unbelievably DRY. It looks like it belongs on a 90 year old woman!!! EEEEEEEK. I'm all dry and crinkly! Super deluxe moisturizing lotion didn't do much. WTH???

Had marinated chicken breast fried in CO for dinner with a nice fresh crunchy green salad with some of the last of my Suzanne Somers' ranch dressing on it. yum!

{{hangs head in shame}} ate popcorn again today. I am so bad. AND I had a mini milky way bar. Which made my stomach hurt although I don't know if it was from guilt or the chocolate. haven't had CANDY candy in 2 years. tsk! shame on me.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-23T04:33:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-33101>

Destiny I explained that in the post where you copied this from. Each number represents a line on the little plastic syringe. One is how much estradiol and the 2nd is how much progesterone. And yes ... twice a day.

Are you on the Wiley Protocol? Because if you aren't the numbers are meaningless in comparing to yours. The WP is a very specific program who's purpose is to put a woman's hormones back at the same level as when she was in her 20's. Which means I'm using a LOT of hormones. Most docs would probably come unglued at the numbers. But the whole point is to restore the protection provided by the hormones at their optimal level. Oooh. I used Jack's favorite word, LOL!

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-24T18:27:14+0300

The first time I did hcg was about a year after coming off that stupid jenny craig diet. I was still not eating right (paleo or low carb) and I'm hoping that means that my body just wasn't either leptin sensitive OR willing to give up any weight. I've said before how the last 6 months on that diet I only lost 8 pounds. EIGHT POUNDS in 6 months on 1200 calories a day. Something was just not right. I still had another 50 to go to goal. 40 if I was being reasonable. As it was it took me a year to drop 50 pounds. That's less than a pound a week when, in my 20's when everything was still working pretty well, that amount of caloric intake would have jettisoned 30 pounds in a couple of months.

SO. We will see. I know I'm sick to death of eating so much at breakfast and this morning cut back on the ground sirloin to 4 ounces (raw). Still had the 3 slices of bacon and the 4TBS of butter in the BPC. BTW I way miscalculated on the butter when I first started drinking BPC. I wonder if I did it on purpose. I was putting HALF A CUP (4 oz) of butter in a mug. And drinking it that way 3 times a day for about a week. Then I guess I topped off the ol' tank because my desire for BPC has dwindled to just one mug in the morning and then I'm done.

On another note, I got a sick headache last night (maybe it was that stupid movie DRIVE that I watched) and was up for a couple of hours in the middle of the night just plain sick. I felt clammy and a bit nauseous. Have NO idea what's going on but the stupid headache is still there. I took 2 8-hour tylenol about 4AM and I STILL have a headache. PLUS poor old Sammy cat had a nasty seizure about 2AM so I spent a couple hours worried about him. He really had a hard time coming back from it. That's 3 in about 6 weeks. WITH meds. Might be time to talk to the vet again. Geez.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-25T18:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-33464>

The heat is on and my appetite has withered to near nothing.

Yesterday for dinner I had the rest of the t-bone I'd had the day before. Not much meat left but it was enough. More of those yummy veggie chips from Terra and a small bowl of thawed berry blend with just a touch of HWC on them.

Breakfast this morning was about 6 oz of ground beef and 3 strips bacon with a mug of BPC which, 2 hours later, I am still not done with.

Supposed to be a cool 99 today. ;P Not sure what's for dinner. Maybe a fresh green salad with some shrimp.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-26T19:42:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-33705>

And yet another boring post. Went to see BRAVE yesterday and I'm SUCH a sap it made me cry. Got to hubby too although he'd never admit it. heh heh.

AND... NO POPCORN. Actually it was more because there were too many people at the counter but I didn't really miss it so however/whatever.

Made a nice green salad for dinner with homemade balsamic vinaigrette and lots of cooked shrimp. mmmm. And some thawed berry mix with a touch of HWC for dessert. That and breakfast were all I had (well, except for a handful of sunflower seeds).

I'm still waiting to hear on my order for hcg. I think it got lost in their system.

AND on that note I have some comments about all the flap about the protocol. First of all Simeons wrote that thing in the 1950s. For those of you who were not there here's a few thoughts on various subjects.

1\. Tuna back then came oil packed. I don't think water pack showed up for at least a couple of decades. I plan to eat it on protocol if I'm not allergic to it.

2\. Lotions and creams were different. And honestly I don't think you absorb fat or calories via their use.

3\. Given what I'm reading about hcg's effect on leptin I'm starting to think the whole strict food list is pretty much nonsense. The fact that hcg might assist the body in reading leptin levels and thus stave off hunger means you only need to restrict your calorie intake and only worry about getting adequate protein. Fat is avoided because THAT'S WHERE ALL THE CALORIES ARE. Although given how much butter I have in my coffee everyday and have NOT GAINED AN OUNCE I'm thinking that's probably a non-issue as well. But I'm not going to risk it.

Skip the breadstick! Wheat is off the menu! See [Dr. Davis's blog](http://http://www.wheatbellyblog.com) to find out why if you haven't read his book (and if you haven't, WHY NOT?). Watch the fruit. Apples in the 50s were SMALL compared to the gargantuan things in the market today. Broccoli ... why wasn't it on his list? Well as near as I remember broccoli didn't show up in the stores out in the boonies of Colorado where I grew up until the 70's. I have a Betty Crocker cookbook from the 40s or 50s and it doesn't even LIST broccoli! EAT it if you want it. It's good and has very few calories. Ditto with green beans. Filling up on salad (with zero calorie dressing) or steamed NONstarchy veggies is a good thing on this protocol.

Ok /lecturemode

Loving my cool showers in this heat. Have not gone back to using the bag o'ice although I keep thinking I should. thought about going to the public pool and soaking in the water for an hour IF it isn't heated. I need to call the rec dept to find out. Don't know why I didn't think of this sooner.

### DarleenMB — 2012-06-27T16:57:01+0300

But it tracks the food I eat with protein/fat/carb amounts as well as the calories. So I'm ready for the 21 days of hcg. which I hope jump starts the weight loss.

Breakfast this morning was 4 ounces (weighed raw) ground sirloin, 3 slices bacon, 1 mug BPC with 4 TBS butter. 731 calories.

that's what I had yesterday and was pretty much fine until about 5pm. We had garden salad again this time with diced ham and a sprinkling of pine nuts.

It's been gawdawful hot here ... up into the 100's the last few days. Today is supposed to be "cooler" and only the high 80's low 90's. Break out your fur underwear. There's a big fire up in Montana and the smoke has drifted down here so we got a lovely deep red sunset last night and an equally red sunrise this morning. ### [email redacted] — 2012-06-28T21:28:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-34219>

Hi darleen. Have you considered leaky gut as a reason for your new food allergies? The way it was explained to me is when you have a leaky gut, food passes through gut into immune system (especially if you have low stomach acid and food is not as digested as it should be) and then your immune system marks that food as a foreign substance and now you're allergic to it. Clear up leaky gut and it might help your allergies. Just a suggestion. Good luck to you. I admire your spirit and sense of humor!!
