Pretty sure it's the latter but can't figure out WHY as I've been taking those off and on for about a year with no problem until this Spring. There's really nothing in it that should/could be doing this which is why tomorrow I won't take ANY supplements again and we'll see if the itching occurs after I finish my coffee. Might also be the butter but it's Troyer brand hand rolled from GF cows so??? Unless it's the GRASS the cows have been eating ... I do have a lot of different grass allergies. hmmm. Something to ponder.

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T18:41:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-36848>

Darleen,

Yup, I'm on day 29 of my first ever HCG round. I would call it a success. Prior to undergoing, my weight was stable, and I have been eating paleo and epi paleo long enough that I don't worry about slipping back into SAD.

My approach was to really listen to and monkey those who have been successful with this on a long term basis. I look at journeys and behaviors of those who lost and kept it off for long periods of time.

What I found out was they were all very strict with WHAT foods and ate on protacol. Meaning no veggie mixing/swapping/messing.

I feel I'm in the groove on it now, and here is what I'm doing:

Coffee and Tea, no artificial sweetners, lots of lemon and vinegar.

Lunch: small serving of either spinach, lettuce, cucumber, asparagus and the remaining calories in protein.

Dinner: same, but never same protein or veggie as lunch.

Afternoon snack: about 40-80 calories in a fruit.

I want my protein going to protein things my body needs, not converting to glucose so I included a fruit.

I do exactly (or as close as one can get) 500 cals of protein. THis is way more than 100 grams weighed raw.

ONLY SIMEON FOODS. NO MIXING. NO DUPLICATING PROTIEN.

My protiens have been: chicken, cod, crab, shrimp, beef and once had buffalo.

I am not weighing daily. Last time I checked I was losing .08 lbds a day on average.

I messed with doses quite a bit. Lower, lower, lower works best. I'm doing about 125-150 iu's and adjusting as needed. I skip a day . .and my skip day is usually quite enjoyable. More than the other days!

The other advice I've seen from successes is don't do a short round. Harder to become stable after.

I plan to do the whole steak day thing.

I am all fat free, nothing, not a cream or a lotion and wash my hands immediately when preparing stuff for others. I planned ahead and brought all my food for vacations. I'm not taking any chances and I am doing this as perfectly as I can. I've gone through this much trouble, I don't want to risk anything by taking a chance a restaurant really didn't use oil - or accidently be too far off on calories.

I don't bother with "cooking" or preparing anything delish. Just eat it. It's a hormonal therapy not a real meal.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-11T19:45:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-36877>

Thanks for the input Cavemam. the original protocol calls for 21 days, period with SIX WEEKS between rounds 1 and 2. EIGHT weeks between 2 and 3, TWELVE weeks between 3 and 4, TWENTY weeks between 4 and 5 and SIX MONTHS between 5 and 6 if that many rounds are called for. I really think those who do double or more of the 21 days or do short time between rounds are asking for trouble in the long run. ANd it seems they are the ones who are hollering the most about sticking to protocol.

I also KNOW that some foods not on the original protocol were not even in the grocery stores in the early 50's when the protocol was written so the whole "broccoli" or "no broccoli" thing is a total non-starter. Beef was NOT LEAN back then. Ranchers would have laughed their backsides off had you asked for lean beef. Egg whites were used to make meringue for pies not omelets. Again, you'd have been laughed at had you asked for such a weird thing. times have changed, formulae have changed. Body lotions used to be made exclusively with mineral oil. And the only artificial sweetener around was saccharin. And I'm not sure it exactly when it became available.

/rant

Having said all that, I'm glad to see you're doing so well. I want to do a normal 21 day round to see if I can jar my body into proper action. I'm finding little pillows of fat on my legs (the 2nd one appeared on my left leg recently). ARGH. I've been icing the original one which was 3" x 2" when I first measured it and quite fluffy. I've got it down to about 2 x 1.5" now.  Now I have to start icing the OTHER leg. WHY is my body doing this? Is it because I"m not properly metabolizing the fats I'm eating? Geez. Bad enough to be FAT now I'm LUMPY fat. (insert swear words of choice)

My appetite is still nonexistent. AND I managed to get myself dehydrated enough that I had had a moment yesterday AM when I nearly blacked out. I was sitting at the computer (where else?) and took a deep breath and ... whoa!!! Remembering I was getting muscle cramps AGAIN the last several nights I smacked myself HARD on the forehead and went and filled up my water pitcher. I use it to track how much water I have or have not been drinking. I drank ALL of it plus part of another one PLUS a liter bottle in the car. Feel much better, TYVM

Had lunch which I don't normally do because a friend was here (she had her right knee replaced a couple of weeks ago and is now recuperating by coming out here to watch last season's GAME OF THRONES eps. So I fed her and ate as well so she wouldn't feel like I was doing something extra special. Then I didn't want dinner but did eat some junk (popcorn, what else).

Cut back on the ground beef this morning because 8 oz just seems too much. Cut back to 6 and changed the butter in my BPC to see if THAT makes a difference on the itchy tongue. If not then I'm looking at the coffee. NOOOOOOOOOOO

### [email redacted] — 2012-07-11T23:25:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-36950>

Dear god lumps of fat and no coffee? I'd rant too. I think it's great you are not wanting to eat! That's good right? Good luck on spurring the loss. I don't plan on doing another round any time soon, but I am eager to see what experience others are having with different stuff. Ultimately for me I just let go of the idea of the food being a pleasurable thing, and It's like medicine so I don't much care if it's broccoli or asparagus or salad. They all run together - no pleasure in them. That IS the idea though right?

For discussion (because I like exploring things to the Nth dgree, not because I'm contrary), the protocol says no more than 40 objections for the obese, and if you need to lose 15 or less do 23 injections. Or a max of 36 lbds. I wish!

You are probably right with the this/that veggie as long as calories and type of fiber are same. One thought I had on the meat, is wouldn't the beef in the 50's in italy have been normally raised grass fed types - thus lower in fat that we would have had here? Ultimately I'm sure your right it a little more fat in one meal probably won't RUIN all progress.

For me, it comes down to OCD + VOODOO. Since I don't really care much about the food anyways, I see no reason to NOT do it by the book. Although, come to think of it, I am not doing any melba and doing much more protein than suggested. I also have been skipping my daily "bow down to the pounds and inches book and dance by the light of the moon and summons simeon fairies" suggestions so we will see.

I think it's premature to feel "successful" on this yet. I think I need a good 6 months of no gains following to see. After looking at my specifics, doc thinks it's going to be tough to keep the weight down. It's probably good he said that as I'm going to be extra vigilant. I hate being told I can't do something

I have not weighed daily, as I really question if you can even judge if some combo or some specific food was the cause of a fluctuation. So, I may or may not weigh next week. I like the "try on skinny clothes" methods.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-11T23:33:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-36955>

LOL, love the imagery!

And now my rant about idiot clerks. I was at wallyworld. Went to the counter and politely asked for a 30cc syringe with a 1.5" 29g needle. She shows me regular insulin syringes. No, not right. She pulls out a 3/10ml syringe and continues to harangue me that it "says right on the package it's 30cc."

I cave and buy the damned things KNOWING in my gut they are too small.

then I get home and find the #\*(@&T%P clerk charged me for the package twice.

So I get everything out, clean it and begin to mix. (insert a very long string of nasty curse words here).

I was right. The blankety blank blank blank clerk was wrong. They are THREE TENTHS OF A ML or 30 UNITS total. I double and triple checked my arithmetic. Yup. I need to do THIRTY THREE DRAWS from the bac water to get to 10ml.

SNAP is right.

stoopid female dog know nothing clerk.

I'm going to assuage my irritation by going to see Magic Mike. 90 minutes of hunky guys disrobing should perk me right up.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-14T03:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-37666>

I'm trying to get this whole leptin thing straight in my head.

HcG stimulates leptin which lowers your appetite.

CT causes cell apoptosis which lowers the amount of leptin available to circulate in your blood. I'm assuming here that when the fat cells are disappeared (obliterated?) that the leptin goes with?

So if one is obese, then why wouldn't the two, in conjunction, work better than either alone?

with those questions ringing in my head I got out the old bag o'ice and did 15 minutes on my bare belly which has waaaay too much fat on it. I'd have done longer but I haven't used it in about 2 months. I figured since I'm roasting from the AWFUL heat we're having it would be most pleasant. I was right ... after the first 2 or 3 minutes. Belly is a nice cherry red. Cool/cold shower for the morning.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-16T16:48:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-38353>

Day 5 Hcg protocol

today's weight ... 223.6

change ... -2.0

total ... -4.2

I did NOT expect that big a drop again. figured 1 pound would be great.

Having some detox symptoms. got a headache last night and it's still here this morning.

Yesterday had shrimp and green salad with 0cal dressing and a small slice of melon (cantaloupe) for meal 1 and a halibut and about an oz of leftover shrimp with 1/2 small sweet potato (a tad less than 3 ounces but didn't eat the skin so probably 2+?) with a small slice of cantaloupe.

Drank a LOT of water. The entire pitcher and then some. So well over 100 ounces which is good since we're still up into the 90's here. Even with the AC in the living room, and all the fans it still gets to 80+ in the rest of the house. Cats are driving me nuts and had to get up about 4 times last night to untangle the boys. Missy announced her displeasure at them around 1 so had to drag her into the bedroom to shut her up. For such an old and tiny cat she has a BIG VOICE. And she's not afraid to use it.

Maybe chicken for lunch today for something different or maybe I'll do that tonight. DH isn't fond of fish.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-17T17:25:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-38719>

Thanks PaulaLynn!

Yesterday was little rough. I don't know if it was caffeine withdrawal (shouldn't have been) or if we had managed to pick up a bug (we aren't eating the same things so pretty sure it wasn't the food). I had a bad headache for over 24 hours, some nausea and loose stools (tmi?) Hubby had the runs. shrug.

So I caved early in the day and had a decaf mug of coffee with 2tbs butter. Well, there goes the protocol out the window. Stuck to it through lunch (diced chicken breast in a green salad with 0 cal dressing and a thin slice cantaloupe). Mid afternoon I was still feeling really lousy so fixed a regular cup of coffee with 2TBS butter. Dinner was a Costco/Trident salmon patty cooked in a scant tsp of coconut oil with a handful of Ranier cherries for dessert.

Sleep came late. Might it have been the coffee? And an hour long thunderboomie RAIN storm that had me up several times checking windows and covering up stuff in my studio where the roof has leaked recently. So another day with my butt draggin'.

Day 6 hcg protocol

weight ... 222.8

change ... 0.8

total ... 5.0

See that? I still dropped a little. 5 pounds in 3 days is awesome from where I sit. I'm actually surprised it dropped at all given how much has come off in the first 2 days of VLCD. Not to mention "cheating" as I did with all that FAT. So I"m pumped and I am having a mug of BPC with 2TBS buttah and 1TBS coconut oil for breakfast. Will continue the regular menu of 2 meals, no/low fat high protein with a kiss of carbs. And a cool/cold shower when I finish the coffee.

argh I just checked the weather widget. A "cool" 89 for today, 101 tomorrow and high to mid 90's the remainder of the week. My scandahoovian ancestors are whining.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-17T22:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-38832>

Ya know, Cavemam, even on 750 calories a day anyone will lose weight, hcg or not. I honestly don't believe that the list of approved foods makes one bit of difference as long as you keep your protein up, the carbs down and the total calorie count down. We'll see. I think the reason he banned fats from the diet is that's where all the calories are. But we all know that all calories are NOT equal. Right?

IF hcg stimulates leptin which then cause your hypothalamus to say ... oooo we;re full stop eating now! ... then keeping to 500 calories of protein keeps you from munching on your own organs and muscles -- and you. will. lose. weight.

Now I could just be full of it and it wouldn't be the first time. Being smart is no guarantee of having the right train of thought. I do know this. I have started a round of hcg, lasted 7 days, came off and just started eating and did not gain an ounce. So much for THAT hypothesis. Of course I wasn't eating lots of carbs or sugar or bread or pasta either. Had I been eating the "approved" SAD I probably would have PILED on the pounds. But I didn't.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-18T17:23:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-39006>

Day 6 -- hcg protocol

weight: 222.6

change: -0.2

total: -5.2

I'm surprised the scale went down at all after the first 3 days spectacular drop. But I'll take it.

Yesterday's first meal was diced chicken breast in a green salad with zero cal dressing and a handful of Ranier cherries.

2nd meal was the rest of the halibut from night before last, green beans and the (sob) last handful of cherries.

Got a good nights sleep last night. It cooled off enough I needed to use a throw on top of my summer weight blanket. Cats behaved themselves (unbelievable!) and I only had to get up twice to pee. Which is how I knew I wasn't going to see much, if any, of a drop on the scale this AM.

It's going to continue with the HEAT. 97 forecast for today, 101 tomorrow, 97 on friday. These are the summers I wish we had a basement and/or central air.

I'm trying to get some laundry done before the heat shuts everything down including me. I'm about out of clean underwear!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-20T22:48:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-39791>

If I had any lingering doubts that CT was effective in removing fat cells they have been blown away like dandelion fluff.

I have been taking cool/cold showers for several months now. I start with tepid water (which now feels too warm) so I can wash my hair without turning myself into a popsicle. Then I turn the water temp down and start washing. I continue to turn it down until it gets just this side of too chilly. When I've finished washing I stand with my back to the shower and just let the cold water run onto my back. It feels GREAT, btw. And given our balmy temps it's even better.

OK. I bought this handy dandy doodad for putting lotion on your back. I was putting the cream on my back and noticed it wasn't quite getting absorbed. So I got out my mirror to see where I had missed and there in the middle of my upper back was this rather large diamond shaped FLAT spot. WTH????

I looked again. Yup, definitely a flat spot in that FAT back of mine. What the heck is going on? Then I realized it is exactly where I let the cold water run on my back. Double checked it with hubby. I am not seeing things. Looked again. Holy cow. This WORKS! Time to get real and submerge the rest of my fat self in cold water.

Oh yeah, spot iced my fat belly yesterday for 30 minutes. aiming for 40 today and another 20 on my upper thighs which are now sporting LUMPS!!! What is going on with my body that these stupid lipomas are cropping up?! And they are relatively new (as in within the last couple of years).

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-21T01:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-39844>

How awesome is that!!! There, now I'm inspired to get in the cold tub!

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-21T02:25:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-39858>

Yeah PaulaLynn, me too! But since I had a cold shower this morning I opted for 40 minutes of spot icing on my belly. My skin was bright cherry red when I took the ice off and my skin temp was 46Â°F ... no wonder I was shivering!

I'm thinking tomorrow I'm just going to fill the tub with the temp set where I shower and just GET IN and get it over with.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-21T18:14:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40008>

Day 9 HCG

Weight 221.6

Change: -1.2

total: -6.2

Yesterday I had hunger starting about 7 and lasting until I went to sleep. Shouldn't be happening. So I'm trying yet another experiment (which is what this round of hcg has always been about). I'm going to do the leptin reset while using the hcg and see how that goes. Although I'm going to try to keep the fat intake under strong control because, again, that's where all the calories are.

so I had pseudo-BAB this morning, i.e., I had about 6 ounces ground beef. Cooked in my nuwave cooker that works out to about 4 ounces cooked ... all the fat (sob) falls through the grill into the pan at the bottom. DARN. It's GREAT for cooking bacon and then you can save all that lovely bacon grease for other things, but I wander. I also had a mug of BPC made with 2 TBS butter (can you believe the restraint?) and 1 TBS CO.

One hour later I checked my BG which was 88. So there's proof that putting fat in with the coffee keeps your insulin from spiking. Not good enough? OK I can check it again in about 30 minutes if you like.

Now then, I started having HUNGER issues again so grabbed my new jar of Artisana coconut butter (which yesterday was LIQUID from this heat) and gobbled a few spoonfuls of the now hardened stuff. And now I'm feeling hungry still AND a bit light headed. So much for keeping the fat intake under control.

What I'm learning from this little personal experiment is that I have been overeating while on the reset. I was using the reset as a license to eat a LOT without regard for true hunger. Granted I have not gained any weight but that wasn't the point. I mean, I've been on the reset for MONTHS now. Something is a bit hinky and I don't think it's necessarily my metabolism (although we'll see once the test results come back on my hormones. I still think my testosterone is flat lined and THAT is part of what is keeping the weight on).

But I really need to step back and assess where my hunger is before I start eating. I have a difficult time with noticing and interpreting hunger signals. I'll find myself cruising the kitchen or pantry and not really FEEL hungry but in fact, I am. I sometimes confuse thirst with hunger. I guess all those years of overriding my body's hunger signals has left me unable to read them at all any more. Which is why doing the hcg has been good for me. Limiting my caloric intake has forced me to notice true hunger.

Well anyway, that's my story and I'm stickin' to it. We'll see how the rest of today goes. So far it isn't looking promising. Maybe I should have had that bacon after all.

### chocolate — 2012-07-21T18:26:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40010>

I'm glad to know that coffee doesn't do that with fat. I was seeing that its a great source of boron. Its one of the simplest pleasure in life. Thanks for setting me straight and sharing. I guess I was using butter instead of ghee and I'm going to blame it on my genes that popped up out centuries past and conflicting information.

Just curious, though.... If you ate only fat, wouldn't the body scavenge for bad proteins first and eat the carbohydrate shells? That's kind of my goal when we get stable around my house. I think we may have the same issues, only mine caught up on the early end because of no fat storing capacity. I'm stuck ready to bury myself in the mud and then come out and lay eggs whilst pooping mucous, the frog. Maybe you're the penguin.

### Lanie — 2012-07-21T18:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40012>

That's really cool about the cold showers! Wow.

Re: insulin spiking after drinking coffee... if your blood sugar is 88 that doesn't mean your insulin didn't spike. It means your blood sugar didn't spike. Insulin actually brings blood sugar DOWN...so it may indeed be dropping your blood sugar and contributing to your hunger.

I'm enjoying the experimentation. Good for you for putting these different things to the test to see what works for you.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-21T19:33:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40018>

Hmm. Good point Lanie, as usual. Some days my head just doesn't get put on straight. But wouldn't there have been a rise in BG first? It had barely been an hour since I had finished breakfast and the coffee.

Well, hmmm.

chocolate, I'm not going to do a fat fast, LOL. That was Dr. Atkins method of last resort if his two week induction didn't work. and he did not recommend it lightly. He had many caveats against it, AAMOF. There isn't much food on his fat fast ... you'd be surprised how little there is in 1,000 calories of fat.

I'm trying to find a balance. The leptin reset is a great starting point but I must be doing something wrong (i.e. eating too much) or my hormones are blocking the road (zero testosterone for one thing -- my number one suspect). Or a little of both.

Oh yeah, I looked at my back in the mirror again this morning and there is that flattened kite shape surrounded by fluffy me just as plain as day. It is just WEIRD.

### Lanie — 2012-07-21T20:59:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40047>

> **DarleenMB;40205 wrote:** Hmm. Good point Lanie, as usual. Some days my head just doesn't get put on straight. But wouldn't there have been a rise in BG first? It had barely been an hour since I had finished breakfast and the coffee.

There can be a rise in insulin without a rise in BG. For example, I tested my blood sugar before and after eating an almond-flour cookie that was artificially sweetened. BG dropped 30 points in one hour. And of course, I was then hungry too.

I see two possible expeiments for you that might be telling. First, try no coffee or tea for a few days...see how your hunger feels then. If caffeine withdrawal is a problem take half a no-doz. Same amount of caffeine as a cup of coffee. Second experiment possibility: only eat to hunger for a few days, and only eat enough to feel satisfied but not full. I've envied your lack of appetite - lets put it to good use!

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-22T20:35:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-40253>

So what would happen if you combined hcg with the leptin reset? Ever wonder? Me too. I know that the CW says eating "normally" can cause massive weight gains either coming straight off the protocol or while on the hcg but I do not believe that. "normally" as defined by the "protocol" being the current SAD then yes, you would gain. But not because of the hcg but because the SAD causes you to gain. Or, as defined in the 50's/60's going back to eating starchy carbs. Which I no longer eat anyway.

I have, in the past, stopped in the middle of a round and gone right back to eating "normally" and not gained an ounce.

But what happens if you eat a seasonal ketogenic paleo diet (say that one 3 times fast)?

Let's find out.

Oh yeah, and I was back up 1.2 today. Gotta love fluid retention. I snacked. Shoot me. I'm trying to normalize today to 2 or 3 meals, plenty of protein, low carb (hard to do as it's summer and those cherries have my name on 'em AND the strawberries AND that honkin' huge pomegranate), MODEST (are you listening to yourself, Darleen? MODEST) good fats.

The ground beef I took out of the freezer yesterday was still a brick this morning so I wound up having a 3 scoop (60g) protein shake made with ice, water, cream and a coupla TBS of Ghiradelli cocoa. And a mug of BPC with (I am so proud of myself) only TWO, count 'em, TWO TBS butter and 1TBS CO.

Shoulda had the beef or something else. Man that shake blew through me so now I'm spooning out Artiana coconut butter. I really liked when it was liquid the other day. I may set it out on the porch this afternoon to get it nice and soupy. Now it's firmed up. Still good but I liked it soupy better.

OKOK it's technically a snack. But I needed to shut my stomach up.

Lunch: (cause I know I'm gonna want/need it) small green salad with small can of water=packed tuna (cause that's the only kind I every buy)

Dinner: Scallops and shrimp. ???

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-25T18:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41021>

Well I've wandered way off the "path" ... I got tired of sticking myself. The stuff does NOTHING as far as I'm concerned. I really truly believe it is all mental. My weight has stayed put no matter what I eat. So much for the fear of gaining massive amounts of weight if you stray from the VLCD. My hunger levels are exactly the same as when I wasn't using the hcg. So what's the point. What a waste of money.

I'm really pissed I missed yesterday's webinar. I got the email yesterday afternoon and went to sign up only to find that my internet was out. CenturyLink/Qwest/USWest/MaBell or whateverthehellthey'recallingthemselvestoday had yet another "outage." It was STILL out at 9:30 when I went to bed. stupid phone company.

Been yet another boring week.

Saw the PA yesterday and yeah, my testosterone is flatlined. So she's ordering up some cream from my friendly local compounding pharmacist to add to my BHRT. Maybe that will give me some energy which I am sorely lacking. Never mind my flatlined sex drive. It drove off a cliff when they yanked my parts. OKOK TMI but too bad. I'm in a MOOD.

Showed her AND my massage therapist the spot on my back which they both plainly saw. So I am not imagining things. I tried to take a picture using my iPad but it didn't show very well. My flab sure did. pfbbt.

My new resolution is to spot ice my big fat belly twice a day. I'll do the bath thing after I shower when I'm already adjusted somewhat to the cool/cold water. After I'm clean my plan is to cork the tub and wait till it's full enough to lie back in then switch off the shower, lie down and let the tub finish filling up. Next stop will be to have a bucket of ice ready and have hubby dump it in the tub with me but that's a bit down the road. First things first.

Man I wish this weight would go somewhere. Like DOWN.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-28T18:39:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41673>

this mornings discussion falls under the heading of TMI or EWWWWWWWW.

Everybody here talks about input (diet) constantly but I never see anything about output unless it's about an unexpected case of "the runs."

Well I have noticed a definite change in my bm's of late. Where the output used to be light to medium tan it is now deep, dark brown almost black. does this herald a change in the machinery? I suspect the answer is "yes" but I'll be durned if know what it means.

Fasten your seat belts so you don't get whiplash as I'm changing the topic now to something COMPLETELY off subject.

Anybody else watching Longmire on A&E? Of COURSE we're watching it ... the setting happens to be smack dab where we live in Northern Wyoming. I really REALLY like the character so I finally decided maybe I oughtta read one of them thar books the character is based on seeing as how Craig Johnson lives over in Clearmont (pronounced CLAREmont for the uninitiated) which is just a hop, skip and half a jump away from Sheridan.

So I mosied on over to Amazon and had a WTF moment when I saw the price of the Kindle edition. $12.99!!!??? for a 10 year old book? The friggin' paperback is TWO DOLLARS CHEAPER. I made a side commentary about the publisher's mothers and wandered over to the local library figuring I'd just read it for free. Well hell's bells it is naturally CHECKED OUT because the whole flappin' town is watching the series and I'm not the only one who hasn't read the books. poop (and that is not intended to signal a change back to my original topic).

I grumbled a bit more. Chewed on the price a bit more and finally decided WTH I could damn well afford to put a couple extra bucks in Johnson's pocket and if the book was as good as all my friends said it'd be worth it.

Ladies and gents I am here to tell you to RUN, do not walk, to Amazon and BUY [THE COLD DISH](http://http://amzn.to/N3zWeH).

Not only will you get a GREAT character in Walt Longmire, you get snappy patter (which I adore), a head-scratching who-done-it, giggles and grins along the way and a mashup of the state in which I live. OK OK that last part needs a bit of 'splainin. Craig took a mixmaster to our fair state. Which leaves me scratching my head in consternation about every third page. An example is his blithely moving Lame Deer from the Northern part of MONTANA down to Wyoming. At moments like that I want to revoke his poetic license but it's all good in the end.

Regarding the tv series my only complaints are (1) it's filmed in freakin' New Mexico (which I tipped to the instant they showed a motel with a Thunderbird on the the stepped wall of the exterior. Wyoming is way to proud of it's "Bucky" logo to use anything else.) and (2) the cast does not know how to pronounce ABSAROKA. It's pronounced abSORka just in case you wondered. Now you can sound like a native too! I"m waiting for them to mispronounce "Arvada." It is properly pronounced arVAYda here in the wild and wooly state of nowhere. Oh yeah, forgot there was a (3) the writers need to come take a guided tour of the area thus staving off any more horse laugh type mistakes like the one in the last episode whereupon our intrepid deputy pronounced that "room service at the hotel in Ranchester reported ..." BWAHAHAHAHAHAHA. ROOM FRACKING SERVICE? IN **RANCHESTER**??? We have ONE ... count 'em ONE motel in Sheridan which offers room service. But RANCHESTER? hahahahahaha

Ranchester is about 20 miles north of Sheridan and is on one of the prettiest drives (if not the most frightening) into Yellowstone which is "over the mountain" from us. Population is somewhere around 800 give or take (not counting cows and/or deer) and while it IS a tad bigger than Dayton (further up the same road) I don't believe that the MOTEL there offers anything approaching "room service."

whew. I think I hurt myself on that one.

OK. Can you tell I am bored with my non-movement on the leptin rx? It's been MONTHS. I'm still tired all the time. Although I am sleeping a lot better and people keep asking me if I've lost weight. No.

I finally got my testosterone cream and started it yesterday. In about 2 weeks I have decided to spring for the cortisol test to check that. Other than that I don't know where else to turn.

And that is that for today. Nothing else new to report. Hope you all enjoyed the book/tv report. I'm gonna go finish my book.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-28T18:50:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41676>

> Well I have noticed a definite change in my bm's of late. Where the output used to be light to medium tan it is now deep, dark brown almost black. does this herald a change in the machinery? I suspect the answer is "yes" but I'll be durned if know what it means.

I think this is related to liver/gallbladder function. If you don't have enough bile to digest your fats, your stools turn tan/lighter colored.

bother.

### vlynnb — 2012-07-28T19:37:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41688>

This is my first time reading your posts. Shoot, I should have been working on something this Sat. morning but I couldn't stop reading. Skipped a lot of the middle because grand kids need breakfast but the last few on Wyoming kept me chuckling. Husband grew up in Lovell/Powell area, live in S. East Idaho now. Had a friend appalled yesterday concerning the carry your wife race to win some beer contest at a Sheridan rodeo. I thought it was quite in character but assured her she would be safe living in Cody.

We love the drive over the Bear Tooth highway. Room service? Never heard of it outside of the big cities which I try to avoid.

I am sorry your experiments with hCg and the reset haven't worked out. I went right into leptin RX after second very successful round of hhcg and I think I am doing well. Lost another 10 pounds or so on leptin RX, have another ten to lose but have plateaued for a few weeks. I'll know just how well I am doing for sure after I get testing done this week. Keep up the good work on your quest and the writing, I'm loving it.

P.S. Being associated with Lovell stock I need to know the rating on the book and TV series, sounds like something I would love.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-28T19:49:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41694>

Vlynnb welcome! Always good to have someone else in my own little world who "gets" me!

I tend to avoid town altogether during rodeo weekend. A) it's just plain nutz with all the flatland touristers wandering the streets and b) it's usually hotter than the proverbial firecracker. Which, this year, it WAS. Sorry I missed the "carry your wife" race. Must have been a hoot given the size of most folks around here.

You know I "did" the protocol about 3 years ago and it took me nearly 9 months to drop 20 pounds. You'd think I'd have learned from that. But I am impatient. although I whine about not "getting" why I'm not losing, a part of me understands that the underlying mechanisms in me are so broken that it's just either going to take a lot more time or it may never happen at all unless I just keep my caloric intake low enough to force the weight loss. Since I am no longer a fan of starving myself thin I think I just need to learn a little patience and tough it out.

And I think you will LOVE The Cold Dish. Can't speak to any of the others because I haven't read them yet although my good friend and Sheridan native, Ariel, assures me I am now hopelessly hooked and might as well just lean back and enjoy the ride. Oh and if you get A&E they're rerunning a bunch of episodes this weekend including the pilot. Check your listings. Robert Taylor (not of old Hollywood but new Australia) is FAB in the role. He's rumply, like an unmade bed, has the Wyoming accent down (except for his continued mispronunciation of "Absaroka" which is an easy tenderfoot mistake and one I made many years ago much to the amusement and consternation of my radio audience) and is just plain "cowboy sexy." and I am NOT, in the parlance of the book, anywhere near being a "buckle bunny." But his easy manner, his entire demeanor, just says honest, hard working MAN. Enjoy!

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-29T19:24:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41815>

Was naughty and didn't go to sleep until nearly 11:30 as I was finishing up THE COLD DISH. Slept until 9 this morning! Would still be asleep except Three insisted that I get up and RIGHT NOW MOM!

Still waiting to feel the effects of the testosterone. OK it's been 2 days. I told you I was impatient. A little more energy, is that too much to ask?!

Other than that, nothing new to report. Except I ate a bunch of fruit last night for dinner. I mixed fresh blueberries and the last few strawberries with 4% Daisy cottage cheese and served it up on a slice of cantaloupe with a side wedge of watermelon. mmmmmm mmmmm mmmmm. Had a couple of spoonfuls of Artisana coconut butter to add some fat.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-30T17:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41945>

ungh .... I don't think my heart has started yet this morning. woke early and put on the sleep mask since we have to leave one of the curtains open for fresh air at night. Slept until a little past 8 and am feeling like a zombie.

Nothing new going on. Breakfast was my usual sortaBAB. GF ground beef patty and bacon. I don't think I'm likely to change anytime soon because I really like it. Fish for the other 2 meals is acceptable but I just cannot get down with it first thing in the A.M.

Lunch was microwaved pepperoni (like crispy little meat crackers) with a touch of humus (shhhh don't tell Jack!) and a double handful of cherries. Gotta be the last bag I buy 'cause I am such a little pig! Oh yeah and a tiny wedge of watermelon. Hubby has eaten most of it ... and it was a good sized one, not those tiny "just enough for 2" jobbies.

dinner rolled around and I wasn't particularly hungry (so I thought ... gotta watch that "thinkin'" stuff) but I made a small tuna salad and had most of it on lettuce and half an avocado. Hubby got the rest plus the leftover shrimp salad from 2 days ago. Way to force him to eat leftovers, Darleen!!  And more watermelon. 2 tiny wedges this time.

So that's it. Dentist this morning for teeth cleaning so need to be sure and get all the bacon out beforehand. And now I'm on the 2nd Walt Longmire book ... [Death Without Company.](http://http://amzn.to/MWIplh) Boy I sure do like that guy! Best character since Lucas Davenport. Gotta say though it's cuttin' in to my other reading. I'm almost finished with [The Dark Volume](http://http://amzn.to/MOtdKH) (you want STRANGE? Read the first book [The Glass Books of the Dream Eaters](http://http://amzn.to/OtO9GG)), just started James Rollins [Blood Line](http://http://amzn.to/T0SQHq) (and I swear everytime I start reading one of his books it will be the last, yet here I am again. argh) and have 3 books I picked up at the library the other day AND 2 bookcases FULL of stuff I haven't read yet.

You know what I want? I want to be able to press a book against my forehead and have the contents INSTANTLY transferred into my brain. Is that too much to ask???

### vlynnb — 2012-07-30T22:58:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-41966>

"So many books, so little time!" Do you really want it all transferred instantly? I think I wouldn't mind picking up pure knowledge or fact that way but I would miss the actually slow reading of a good book, the kind I really don't want to end. I haven't even convinced myself that I would love an e-reader. Something about having a library full of books and feeling it in my hand as I read.

I am wondering how you get along with all that fruit. Are you already Leptin Sensitive? I am craving some fruit right now myself.

### DarleenMB — 2012-07-31T06:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-42019>

vlynnb, YES! I love my kindle (which DH is currently using) AND my iPad because I can have a library full of books in something the size of a double postage stamp! I still have physical books and I still buy them but I LOVE ebooks. They save space, trees AND with my iPad I can look up words just by touching them so when I'm reading something and run across a word I don't know ... bingo! There it is!

ETA that plus all that my elibrary is ALPHABETIZED by author or title which means I can find that book that I know I had ... physical books require a major expedition. Not so with ebooks.

I'm not eating THAT much fruit. when I say a small wedge of watermelon I mean just that. I quartered the melon, then cut each quarter into slices about 1" thick. Don't know if leptin sensitivity has returned or not but I'm not passing up cherries OR watermelon or cantaloupe as they are in season for just a short time. I do know now, however, not to gorge myself. Not always easy.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-02T21:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-42546>

had out of town company last night and took them out to dine but it was way late. 9 or so before we actually ate. grrr.

Dinner was lovely though. Had mussels for appetizer (YUM YUM) and I had halibut with a mango/strawberry confit. The fish was beautifully cooked though a bit salty and I wasn't too crazy about the confit. Also had a cup of seafood chowder made with crab, lobster, cream and butter. mmmm mmmm mmmmmmmmmmmmmmm

Felt like the Hindenburg all day yesterday. Every time I caught a glimpse of my butt in a mirror I thought I was looking at the back end of a buick. Dont' know what that was about because all my clothes still fit as usual. Guess I'd better jump on the scale for a quick weigh-in tomorrow just to double check.

Missed taking my bile acid factors for 2 meals and didn't digest ANY of the fats I ate at those meals. BOTHER.

Am still on my "Longmire" reading kick. Am now on book 4 and am going to be majorly bummed when I finish the last one in the series so far. I think that's about 4 books away. Best darned books I've read (devoured) in ages. Can't believe I'm reading them this fast but cannot put them down.

We're still wilting from the heat but we are supposed to get a reprieve tomorrow. Weather widget says high of 76. I'll believe it when I see it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-04T18:44:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-42853>

Well I had a real pity party yesterday. I fixed popcorn and ate a HUGE bowlful. OK so it was Troyer purple popcorn not the nasty stuff from the local bigmart. AND I cooked it in lots of CO. But still. Had cottage cheese and blueberries & strawberries for dinner with a sprinkle of sliced almonds on top.

Oh yeah, I weighed yesterday. Still the same just FEELING fatter than ever.

Slept really well since it turned coolish yesterday and last night got down right cold. Had to put the winter blankie on the bed.

My resolution today is to get back on the reset track and EAT what and when I need to. My plan from a week or so ago fell apart the minute I thought about it. I still think if I have a SMALL green salad for lunch with a BIT of protein and then a bit of protein for dinner with a veggie I will do better than I have been. Plus I keep 'forgetting' to do the spot icing.

This summer has just sucked. I'm stupid from the heat, LAZY from the heat. I just can't make myself do anything. Except either read the Walt Longmire series or play on the computer. My house is pit. My brain left the building last June. I'm turning into Jabba the Hut.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-06T19:50:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43244>

Got ambitious yesterday and made a quiche with ham, eggs and raw milk cheddar. Had two slices for breakfast with 3 pieces of bacon and BPC. Wasn't sure I could finish it but managed to get it all down.

Did a whey protein shake for lunch and will do it again. Doesn't make me feel too full and I'm thinking now that I am still not eating ENOUGH. Had pork chops and asparagus for dinner. If I get my bills done and my shopping I plan on doing a spicy shrimp recipe I tore out of a magazine. It's full on paleo and looks/sounds yummy. Will probably do caulimash to go with. Or better yet a nice crisp green salad.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-07T19:20:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43449>

Lunch AND dinner yesterday was a protein shake. Lunch was a SF chai "latte" which was AWESOME. Chopped a cup of ice in the blender, added in one cup SF chai tea, 4 TBS Thai Kitchen coconut milk, 2 scoops vanilla cream whey protein from Bioplex. Just yummy!!

Went to see Batman Rises ... gotta say I wasn't all that keen on seeing Anne Hathaway as Cat Woman but she did an AMAZING job. Movie kinda drug in spots but when it reached the finale it was well worth the wait. And yes, I had popcorn ... the small bag (which in our neck o' the woods still means a 4 cup serving (almost)). We always go to the 4PM showing but Batman was quite long ... got out at 7:10 so I gulped another protein shake for dinner since I was already past the witching hour.

Off to the store today so looks like I'll be making that spicy shrimp for dinner.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-08T20:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43709>

Weighed in today. No change. As I've said before this is somewhat of a miracle. I cannot believe I'm not gaining given all I'm eating.

Breakfast was a slice of quiche, a GF ground beef patty, 3 sausage links and bpc with butter and CO.

Lunch will be a 2 scoop protein shake.

Dinner? Not sure yet. It's gonna be 99 again today and yesterday I spent what little energy I had going to the store, dragging in all the groceries and putting them away. I HATE the HEAT!!!

Speaking of which, I have discovered in the last couple of weeks that when I first get up in the A.M. I need to splash cold water on my face. And our tap water is still 60Â° ... love that snow melt. It just feels so goooooood. I guess because I'm HOT when I first wake up. This isn't like night sweats or hot flashes. I'm just HOT. Not that kind of hot either. Just overly warm. And that cold water is so soothing. I'd face dunk but don't have the patience to wait for the sink to fill. It's a deep sink.

Well, that's it. stupid from the heat. tired from the heat. Have sheets to hang out and ironing to do. Actually it's "pressing" but it's still hot. If I don't get to it now it won't get done for another day.

and then I'd have to sit around reading the last and latest Walt Longmire novel. It's already off to a great start!

### ealachan — 2012-08-08T20:55:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43714>

I feel your pain, Darleen! I can't stand waking up to it already being hot. Ugh! It just sets me up to be a miserable bitch all day long. More miserable than usual, anyway...

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-09T05:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43808>

Yeah but it did do one thing ... I just crawled out of my 2nd attempt at CT. Lasted about 10 minutes. Water was 61 when I went in and 64 when I came out. I just sat for a bit getting acclimated and then held my breath and lay back. WHOO! I'd been planning on doing this all day and after I brought hubby home from the weekly Legion "meeting" I just said to heck with it and ran the tub full. Started reading the last Robert Crais novel so that helped. His work is always engrossing. Three (my feral adoptee) did NOT like the sound of the water going into the tub and he REALLY didn't like it when I climbed in, LOL. I heard about it the entire time I was in there.

Well, the computer just informed me that it's sunset so I have to put on my blu-blockers. Means it's time to shut the 'puter down because I can't do much of anything at this point. boo.

Oh yeah, had a 2 scoop protein shake for lunch AND for dinner. Hubby was at the legion and it was just too damned hot to even think about food.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-09T20:37:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-43930>

Out of quiche and need to go get some good sausage from Sackett's so I can make another. Had 3 XL eggs scrambled in coconut oil, hunk o'ground beef, 2 slices bacon and 2 link sausage and a mug of BPC with CO for breakfast. I can't believe I ate all that.

Odd thing about the protein shakes. about 4-5 hours after i have one I get HUNGRY. I mean actual hunger pangs. If I skip lunch, or eat "food" instead of the shake, I don't get 'em. weird.

It was overcast this morning so didn't wake up until 8:45! HOLY MOLY. OTOH I did wake up a couple of times in the middle of the night. Last one was about 2:30 and I was HOTHOTHOTHOT. Wondering if it's the testosterone. NO NOT THAT KIND OF HOT. although I sure wouldn't complain too much about that. Temperature HOT. And again this morning splashed my face with COLD water. OOOOH it felt so good. I came real close to filling the tub with cold water and climbing in. Sure would have woke me up. Taking a cold shower in a bit instead. I am running WAY late today. Also, computer is acting up and it's something I can't figure out. grrrrrrr. That doesn't happen often but it pisses me off when it does. Evidently something (I'm thinking OpenOffice) has squirreled the works. Can't locate the missing piece. DAMN

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-12T19:34:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-44554>

YAY!!! we got a break from the heat! I mean a REAL break. Like 70 instead of 99. What a relief. It's going to climb a little the next few days but Wed forecast is for 67. WHAOO!

Have been late getting my blu-blockers on the last several nights and have paid for it by not being able to fall asleep as easily. gotta watch that.

Nothing new on the reset front. Still eating the BAB. 2 scoop protein shake for 2nd meal and am honestly not hungry past that. Forgot all about fixing dinner because of it and at 8 hubby was banging around in the kitchen looking for food. Had to go rescue him (which I'm sure was his plan all along). Me? Naw. Not hungry. I mean NOT HUNGRY. Don't even want to think about food. Maybe I have finally topped off THAT tank.

Jumped in the shower early yesterday morning instead of waiting until mid-late morning as usual. OH MY that felt good. Cranked the water all the way over to COLD (which is still 60Â° -- love it!) and just let it pour over me. Barely noticed it was cold. So WHAT is it with early morning overheat? Again, it doesn't feel like hot flashes (which I never really had, just bouts of power surges where I was uncomfortably warm) ... I don't even really notice I'm hot until I splash cold water on my face and then it just feels RIGHT. How did I go all those years taking hot showers? I can't even stand tepid water any more. I'm sure my scandahoovian ancestors are laughing.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-15T20:33:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45050>

I sure wish I could come here and report awesome and life changing news but it's still just the same old same old. With a couple of interesting additions.

1\. My skin has suddenly become incredibly soft. At first I thought it was the body cream I've been using, but nope. It's just wonderfully silky.

2\. My upper chest has lost a LOT of fat. My breasts used to extend up toward my neck in a sort of "foothills" if you will. That's almost all gone now. Very strange. I used to be able to grab a big handful of flab but not anymore. too bad I can't say the same for my belly.

3\. I don't seem to need or want much food anymore. Doesn't mean I don't occasionally eat just because, but to have that constant need gone is pretty amazing.

and 4. Boy howdy am I sleeping good. Except when my allergies flare which they've done AGAIN because of all the grass fires in the region. I cannot wait for it to snow and END this.

So I continue to slog forward telling myself to stop beating up on myself because of my weight. NOT my fault. Hormones. HORMONES. Try to keep that in the forefront. Menopause hit me way WAY early and the weight gain, lack of sleep, fibroid tumors that bled me dry for YEARS was a direct result of that. The stress from my childhood and adolescence coupled with own stupidity in my early 20's started all of this. Now I know better.

I plan to have my cortisol levels checked once my allergies are under control. I know they're stressing me because my blood pressure yesterday 110 over 65. That was up from the 100 over 60 it was the last 2 times. Yeah yeah. I know that's low for most peeps my age. But both my sister and I (unlike other family members) have LOWER blood pressure even though we're both overweight. OK both obese. There. whatever.

I did get my allergy drops yesterday. FINALLY. I've been waiting for weeks and weeks. I suspect they were at the office all this time and no one bothered to call me. WhatEVER. I'm also going to add in a daily teaspoon of local unpasteurized honey. I cannot take this anymore. It has been AWFUL. My mouth itched yesterday to the point I thought I'd scream! I washed my sinuses, took my OTC allergy meds and STILL was going nuts. I'm tired and yesterday I hated the whole WORLD. Which tells me I wasn't sleeping well the last few nights and am dead tired again.

blech

Just when my energy levels were showing signs of finally going up a bit. crap

So.

BAB was 5.5oz ground beef (measured raw), 4 slices bacon (the paper thin kind) and a mug of BPC with plenty of buttah and a heaping TBS of CO. Am now having another cup of decaf with cream. We'll call it lunch.

Speaking of which ... probably a protein shake about mid afternoon.

And that may be it unless I get super hungry. All that fresh fruit keeps calling me ... I have 5 Colorado peaches (OMG THE BEST!!) that are fully ripened and in the fridge along with another bag of cherries (I KNOW I KNOW) plus some watermelon and cantaloupe. OOOOO the temptation. Maybe I'll throw a peach in the protein shake. mmmm.

DH is off on a 3 day road trip with a friend. They're headed up around Salmon ID in said friends convertible Mercedes (vintage which he got way cheap). Hope it doesn't break down. So I don't have to worry about fixing his meals for a few days.

It's cool today (again) but the smoke from the fires is keeping me pretty much housebound. At least I can keep the windows closed without expiring from the heat. It's supposed to be 45 tonight YAY!!

### vlynnb — 2012-08-16T00:11:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45083>

Sorry you are not feeling well. I know how that fruit can call to you. Maybe another good book will take your mind off of it.

My allergies have been so good after going off of wheat, sugar, milk etc. that I only take an over the counter anti-histamine if I am going in for an allergy shot. The new diet is doing what allergy shots never have accomplished. I know because if I cheat and eat wheat etc. I am stuffed up or itching the next day. Hope the epi-paleo starts giving you this kind of relief soon.

Fires here too, close to Salmon, by Idaho standards anyway. We are getting a lot of smoke from the Salmon fires and a lot of others. Hope your hubby finds a canyon that is not affected. (effected? I never can get those two right!) Which reminds me, I think you write very well and descriptively. Maybe you should write a book.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-16T17:37:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45203>

Thanks vlynnb. My allergies are all grasses, trees, molds and mildew. I'm really allergic to timothy grass and since we live in the grasslands ... oh yeah and russian olives. Of course we have a whole LINE of the damned things up by the road so when the wind blows it scatters all that lovely pollen from those trees right straight at our house. pfbbbt.

Once upon a time I thought I wanted to write. Until I figured out I'd have to reveal too much of myself in my work. Oh well. I keep thinking I should write about my life because I really believe what I went through and survived might help someone but, again, I'm not real good at letting too much of my real self show. Which is probably why I love the internet so much. I can let fly and not worry too much. Love that anonymity.

REALLY off day yesterday. Just felt plain crappy all day. Wasn't all that hungry until way late. Had that peach protein shake for "lunch" and was good until about 8 or so. I sorta wanted something but nothing sounded good which probably means I should have left well enough alone but NOOOOO. I had a small bag of dried fruit and nuts and did a face plant in it. Didn't QUiTE eat it all but came close. Good thing it wasn't one of those horse sized jobbies.

Had a real sneezing fit a bit ago and now my mouth is feeling itchy. Not too bad. This awful summer just has seemed endless first with the non-stop heat and smoke and now more smoke. Maybe we should move to Antarctica. I hear they're always hiring down there.

vkiernan, if you can try to screen them over the phone before you invest your time and money. I had 3 doctors and the endo was the worst - the most dogmatic doctor, I've ever met (TSH needs to be over 10 to be of a concern; treats with T4 only; doesn't test 4 irons; my elevated FBG, thyroid, and low sex hormones are not connected - you get the drift...). So ask them what they treat, how they treat, etc. Good luck!

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-17T22:36:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45420>

I just picked one of the providers in Loveland. He seemed nice enough and was quite ready to give me what I needed. Since I have no parts and was 64 when I saw him there were no tests done at that time. However, AFTER I started on the BHRT then he did test to see where my levels were. Honestly, I wouldn't choose based on anything OTHER than what you're seeing them for which is to be put on the Wiley Protocol. One thing at a time.

Back to me.

Breakfast yesterday was 5 oz (weighed raw) ground beef, 4 slices bacon and a mug of BPC with butter and CO.

Lunch ... a bit of fruit

Dinner ... 2 nice slices of cold roast beef with cream cheese and avocado slices and the last of the cherries (sob).

TODAY: forgot to thaw any more ground beef so had 4 eggs and 4 slices bacon and a mug of BPC with butter and CO.

Lunch ... 2 slices of cold roast beef (I'm easy) w/cream cheese & avocado slices. And a peach. mmmmm. Only 2 left. sigh.

Dinner ... shrug. dunno. Probably should have fish.

Speaking of fish I had a tin of smoked baby clams for lunch the other day. It's OK as an appetizer but the smoke thing gets to be too much after a bit. Won't do that again. Am pining for mussels.

Waking up at 5:30 the last few mornings and am real TIRED OF IT NOW! It means that by 7:30 or so I'm starting to nod off and by 9 my body is FORCING me into sleep. I mean I can barely move/breathe/think. I crawl into bed and WHAM it's lights out. I did wake up a couple of times during the night ... just enough that my bladder decided it wanted attention. geez. NO problem dropping off again though. I'm back to sleeping to hard that I could probably sleep through a bomb run.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-18T18:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45555>

My personal opinion is that your hormones are not something you should just blithely mess around with. This is something that absolutely needs medical oversight. Makes me think of that adage ... which I am going to twist a bit ... about those who medicate themselves having a fool for a physician. Plus the only way to get the real deal is to ... wait for it ... see a physician. When it comes to your sex hormones, taking supplements cannot restore what time has taken away.

And that's my opinion and I'm stickin' to it.

breakfast: the usual

and I have been bloating up like the Hindenburg every morning when I drink my BPC. I know it's that TBS of coconut oil but I'll be durned if I can figure out why. I've been using CO for several years now and thought I was well past that bit of nastiness.

today I am going to MAKE myself fix a small green salad for lunch. I seem to do better all around if I eat a few raw veggies daily. I hate to admit to being lazy but I can't think of any good reason why I refuse to do this for myself. I LIKE salads. I know that my appetite is way down for some reason (OK so I think I'm constipated again and I blame those whey protein shakes I was downing last week. I manage to forget every single time that they do that to me. argh) but salads "move" me so I'm going to FORCE it today. I'll add a small amount of canned fish and call it good.

dinner? sigh. who knows. Last night I finally felt a little hungry about 6:45 so had some Daisy 4% cottage cheese and 1/4 of a cantaloupe. Then later ate a humongous handful of raw almonds. BAD DARLEEN!!!! Where's that rolled up newspaper when I need it?!

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-19T18:02:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45701>

I decided yesterday to start tracking everything I eat, my weight and my fasting blood glucose.

Day 1:

weight 227.2

FBG: 95

Breakfast, 8:30am

6.5 oz raw/4.5 oz cooked ground beef

mug of BPC with 1.5 oz butter and 1 TBS CO

4 slices bacon

total: 1012 calories!!!, 44g protein, 0g carbs, 75g fat

judas aitch.

Yesterday I didn't eat again until about 3:30 and by then was famished. Had a small green salad with a handful of cherry tomatoes, avocado, yellow pepper and small can of water packed tuna. Dressed with 0 calorie dressing because I was out of my homemade, and about 4 big green olives and a pepper on the side.

Late (like around 8) was hungry so had a handful of raw almonds.

Took half a bottle of mag citrate yesterday afternoon. Cleared the pipes and am feeling somewhat better.

Am starting to think about ditching the bacon and the ground beef and eating white fish for breakfast. Something has got to give on my weight. This just has got to stop. I am not prepared to just give up and accept that I am FAT/obese. I HATE the way I feel and the way I look.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-20T00:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45750>

TSH is "normal" and doc wouldn't do a reverse T3. Yes, sex hormones checked 2 weeks ago and within normal parameters. Don't know how that matches up with the wiley protocol but the PA was happy. And that's why she put me on testosterone ... it's flatlined which I already knew.

I had my cortisol checked a couple of years ago and it was ok. Been thinking about doing it again.

And yeah, most people would lose weight eating what I'm eating. Except for 30 years worth of ED ... NOT eating, then eating crap. We didn't have kids so when DH was off working (on the railroad and gone more than he was home) I simply didn't eat.

I have no proof but the evidence suggests I went into menopause in my late 20's -- early 30's. THAT'S when my periods became insane. I suspect I lost all my eggs at once because you do NOT develop a kilo's worth of fibroid tumors in just a couple of years. No eggs, no progesterone. No progesterone ... estrogen dominance anyone?

It's beyond frustrating. Which is why I intend to carefully monitor what I'm eating as well as watching my hunger levels. I still don't really know when I'm actually hungry.

(insert huge whiny sigh)

3 years ago I was down to 182. (see my avatar pic ... that's when I took that one) I felt better and looked OK for a change. I could wear "regular" sized clothes. I gave away ALL my "fat" clothes and now that I've gained back 40 pounds NOTHING fits. crap crap crap

Change of subject.

I'm baking tilapia for 2nd meal and nuking broccoli. Total calories after I eat will be about 1300. Unless I have a cup of decaf. Am giving up on the bpc for now and going back to just a couple of TBS of cream. That saves 300 calories right there. poop.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-20T18:06:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-45852>

Well I figure that if I log everything for about 2 months then take it to the doc/PA they will HAVE to listen. Of course, if I start losing weight that may not be of help.

I went back and looked at my records (which are hit and miss ... I"m not THAT anal) and in February of 2010 I weighed 197.2 pounds. I was still trying to stick to the hcg protocol. I had lost 20 pounds the previous YEAR doing that but had put 10 pounds back on. By the end of 2010 I was up another 10 pounds. Since I went "low carb" last year in January I have gained ANOTHER 20 pounds. Pardon me while I go shoot myself.

Last night about 9:45 I did get hungry and I know it's against the leptin reset "rules" but I did it anyway. I had 1/2 cup fresh blueberries (the last of them) and 2 TBS of HWC. It was enough. Total calories for yesterday approx 1600. I say approx because of the way I cook the beef and the bacon -- most of the fat stays in the drip pan. So I'm not sure I'm getting THAT many extra calories.

Day 2 ...

FBG 88

**Breakfast** was 3 7/8 oz ground beef (weighed cooked), 3 slices thick cut bacon, coffee with 3 TBS HWC for a total of 649 calories, 38g protein, 40g fat and 0g carbs. Cut the calories in HALF from yesterday. We'll see how long I can go on that. I know that yesterday by 3 I was hungry. No hunger pangs but knew I needed to EAT. I'm planning on having fish again. I'm sure DH will like that. NOT.

Oh and Lanie, thanks for the lab info. (and I've been reading your blog, just haven't had anything to say back!)

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-21T17:02:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46036>

Beats the hell out of me, Cavemam. I just know I'm sick of being FAT.

So to complete yesterdays log:

Had a venti SF latte bout mid morning. Later on had some raw walnuts and raw almonds. Went to the movie (dear god save yourself ... RUN AWAY if anyone tries to make you go see The Expendables 2) and had a small popcorn.

Got home a little after 6 and fixed bone-in pork chops for dinner. I had that plus 1/2 cup green beans and about 1/3 cup sweet potato with 2 tsp butter along with 2 TBS unsweetened applesauce.

Totals were 1498 calories; 94g protein, 71g fat, and 70 whopping grams of carbs. The result? this mornings FBG was 103. Insulin resistance anyone? Which means I am STILL leptin resistant. Big surprise there. So what am I to do? Keep those carbs under 50 and STOP EATING POPCORN. Not that eat all that much but even one little handful is now out of the question. So I think I can be a grownup about this.

Day 3. FBG 103 (the horror!)

Breakfast was the usual for a net 613 cal, 35g protein, 50.6g fat and zero grams carbs. good girl.

Hubby is off to the hills for a camp out with friends and to test drive his "new" little camper. He's been working hard to get it whipped into shape and after robbing the kitchen of half my utensils thinks he's ready for a shake down cruise. I get to stay home and babysit the cats and perhaps make a voodoo doll of myFATself and stick pins in it.

Switched my coffee from full on leaded to half and half. I've noticed the last couple of days/weeks I tend to be a bit shaky a couple hours after I drink my morning mug. since the weather is cooling off and I'm wanting to get back to work, shakes are NOT allowed. When you're working in a 1700Â° flame on a bit of glass the size of a pea it's best to have steady hands.

lunch/dinner: probably a bit of salmon and a green salad.

Am going to get the thyroid panel from DirectLabs but will have to drive 2+ hours to Casper to get it done. Oh well. Maybe I can do it on the way to colorado ... I'm 2 years out from my last visit to the outlaws and my sister. It's not that I don't trust hubby to take good care of the cats. I mean just because the last time he locked Three in the crawl space aND LEFT HIM THERE FOR NEARLY A WEEK with no food or water ("honest honey I don't know how he got out") ... grrrrr.

sigh

Men. Can't shoot 'em. Can't live with 'em. Can't live without 'em. Talk about an approach/avoidance conflict.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-22T16:07:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46293>

The Rest of Yesterday:

Had a mug of decaf w/hwc late morning.

Raw almonds early afternoon.

Dinner was at 5:15 and consisted of scallops sauteed in ghee (tsp) and CO and some canned green beans. Got hungry around 9-ish and yes I was BAD BAD BAD and ate some raw almonds.

Totals: 1406 calories; 94g protein, 104.9g fat, 19g carbs.

Day 4 -- no change in weight

FBG: 82

Breakfast is going to be a serving of sausage quiche I made yesterday and a mug of BPC made with just TWO TBS of butter instead of my usual 4. That would 1 ounce instead of 2. OK.

I've been trying to reread Jack's posts on reverse T3/leptin and am starting to wonder if that isn't, indeed, a big part of my problem. I used to be able to lose weight easily. BUT I have had thinning hair since I was 17 when half of it fell out from stress. Now it's so thin I have to wear it "pixie" short. I have NEVER EVER had good fingernails. They are thin, soft to brittle. They either bend and peel or snap off. And I am TIRED. I am finally sleeping well. No I haven't been tested for sleep apnea but I don't think that's the problem. I might be wrong. I just have NO energy. I'll get a spurt of energy occasionally but when I run out of gas I'm DONE. So I'm off to order the full thyroid panel from direct labs. Won't be able to get it done until first of next month but the August sale price is just too good to pass up.

Have no idea what I'll have later today. Need to get some fresh greens for salad. shrug. just dunno.
