>
>
> Dinner was at 5:15 and consisted of scallops sauteed in ghee (tsp) and CO and some canned green beans. Got hungry around 9-ish and yes I was BAD BAD BAD and ate some raw almonds.
>
>
>
> Totals: 1406 calories; 94g protein, 104.9g fat, 19g carbs.
>
>
>
> Day 4 -- no change in weight
>
>
>
> FBG: 82
>
>
>
> Breakfast is going to be a serving of sausage quiche I made yesterday and a mug of BPC made with just TWO TBS of butter instead of my usual 4. That would 1 ounce instead of 2. OK.
>
>
>
> I've been trying to reread Jack's posts on reverse T3/leptin and am starting to wonder if that isn't, indeed, a big part of my problem. I used to be able to lose weight easily. BUT I have had thinning hair since I was 17 when half of it fell out from stress. Now it's so thin I have to wear it "pixie" short. I have NEVER EVER had good fingernails. They are thin, soft to brittle. They either bend and peel or snap off. And I am TIRED. I am finally sleeping well. No I haven't been tested for sleep apnea but I don't think that's the problem. I might be wrong. I just have NO energy. I'll get a spurt of energy occasionally but when I run out of gas I'm DONE. So I'm off to order the full thyroid panel from direct labs. Won't be able to get it done until first of next month but the August sale price is just too good to pass up.
>
>
>
> Have no idea what I'll have later today. Need to get some fresh greens for salad. shrug. just dunno.

Yay for you and yay for science!

### vlynnb — 2012-08-22T20:30:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46388>

Lost my last post so I'll say it again. How frustrating to be trying but not see the numbers change and to still feel tired all of the time. It seems it takes energy to change but the change is what is needed to increase the energy.

I am wondering if increasing your BAB to closer to 50 grams would help. It might help you to avoid the snacking and cravings for popcorn. Can't hurt to try can it"

I think the BAB and close attention to going to bed early helped me to increase my energy and ability to avoid snacking.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-23T00:51:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46427>

Waving at Lanie ... aren't you moving???

vlynnb ... yes it's frustrating. I was just over at wheatbellyblog.com and there was guy who just gave up wheat and sugar and dropped like a hundred pounds. He looks AWEsome btw. My DH gave it up in January and has lost 30 pounds. grrrrrrrr

So I was back rereading some of Jack's blog posts and right there it was ... I should NOT be skipping meals. I know he says when you can you probably should but I'm working from an ED and skipping is the last thing I should be doing. I think I'm getting the reverse T3 connection to leptin resistance. Maybe. Sort of.

Oh yeah. I cannot EAT that much for breakfast. This mornings quiche had a nice lot of protein in it if I did my arithmetic correctly and THAT was easy to get down. So I may make some sort of quiche a breakfast staple for a while. But I need to go back and double check.

Had a ground beef burger for lunch with a small green side salad with 1/2 a small Florida avocado on it. 0cal dressing cause it was handy. Got a tub of pico de gallo at the store so had that on the burger with a slice of colby jack (small slice). Found some fish I've never tried/heard of so have a fillet thawing for maybe dinner, maybe not. We'll see how hungry I am about 6. If not very may just have a bit of CC with melon.

Can't believe how good my blood glucose is when I don't eat many carbs. I'd like to keep it there permanently.

So off to reread more of Jack's posts. Gotta find my brain first, though. I think it's here somewhere.

ETA I don't crave popcorn. I just LOVE it ... crunchy, buttery, salty. yum. I dont' usually snack either unless I don't eat enough at a meal. Which I have obviously been doing (not eating enough). Gotta watch that.

### chocolate — 2012-08-23T01:32:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46438>

Darlene, I love popcorn too. It used to be mindless and guilt free to me. I have a costco box of a hundred that are cellophane wrapped I'll give a way at Halloween. They must have a twenty year shelf life. I'll look first, of course. We get van loads of kids in our neighborhood. Some are so scary you want to answer the door packing heat.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-23T04:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46471>

Well I totally messed up the calculations on the quiche. One serving is only 27g protein, there was less fat and fewer carbs. duh. No wonder I've been a little hungry all day.

Too tired now to post much more ... chocolate I hear you. Only I pop mine fresh in my counter top "movie theater" popper. With CO it is fanTAStic. Of course I bought the stupid popper just a few months before I "discovered" Jack's leptin reset. Wouldn't you just know it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-23T18:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46547>

Catching up here ... yesterday:

breakfast was 1 slice of the sausage quiche and a mug of bpc with 7/8 oz butter

lunch was ground beef patty topped with a thin slice of colby jack and a small green salad with 1/2 avocado and 0cal dressing plus another mug of (decaf) npc with 2tbs butter

dinner was 1/2 cup Daisy 4% cottage cheese on half a medium cantaloupe topped with sliced almonds.

totals: 1862 cal; 86g protein, 139.4g fat and 56g carbs

**Day 5**

FBG: 102 OK I don't know if it was the melon or the CC or both. No more fruit after 3pm and no more cottage cheese for a while. Woke up feeling puffy as the marshmallow man.

Breakfast: 2 slices of the sausage quiche and a mug of bpc with 2tbs (approx) butter.

Have a fillet of barramundi in the fridge. have never had it and there's a yummy sounding recipe on the package so will have that for next meal with a small green salad.

Since I'm going to get the full thyroid panel done in a few weeks I have decided to stop fussing over calorie totals but am still going to log what I eat. I refuse to accept that I'm stuck being obese and I AM going to figure this out long before it's time for me to shuffle off this mortal coyle. Besides I just ordered a spectacular suede jacket from the King Ranch catalog online ... it's dark brown and heavily embroidered across the sleeves and shoulders with stunning blue flowers. It was ON SALE for $88!!!!! Shoot they had cotton tees "on sale" for more than that. Can't wait to get it although I'd bet the farm that I can't get into it just yet. I LOVE leather ... I have 4 simply gorgeous leather jackets that I bought -40 pounds ago. I want to WEAR THEM!!!

whew. OK that's enough ranting and raving. I need another cup of coffee but don't really want it if you know what I mean. Guess I'll finish off the glass of ice water.

I'm still plowing through the stuff on reverse T3 on Jack's blog and still can't make much sense of it all. I can't wait to get the results (even though I know I won't have them for at least a month). SOMEthing is not right. I'd sure like to figure it out.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-24T17:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-46719>

Had the barramundi last night, pan fried it in some ghee.

[](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/47129=433-barramundi.jpg)

topped it with some pico de gallo and added a side of green salad with avocado, greek olives and almost the last of the 0cal dressing.

The fish was tasty but pan frying is a little hard on it. It's delicate with a very slight sweet taste, nice texture but not a lot of flavor. Maybe next time I'll sautee some garlic in the ghee before I add the fish.

ANYway. This morning FBG was 80!!! I think besides tracking my food intake this is the most important thing I've done because I"m really seeing what I should and should NOT be eating.

Ordered the thyroid panel from DirectLabs. Won't get to the lab in Casper for a couple of weeks though. Am doing the salivary collection today for the cortisol test. yay me.

Ate the last of the quiche yesterday so am having ground beef for breakfast with 3 link sausages I found in the fridge. Hubby loves to bury stuff. then blames me when it turns into a science experiment.

later tomaters

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-29T18:21:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48098>

It has been gawdawful hot again and my brain is just fried. I hadn't been sleeping well either for the last couple of weeks and finally figured out that I didn't have enough air in my mattress. It felt way to hard a couple of weeks ago so I tried to let out some of the air. Nothing happened so I kept pushing the button on the remote and then all of a sudden I was flat on the platform. When I refilled it I didn't get enough air IN so my back and neck have been loudly complaining. sigh. Not the first time I've been through this.

Have finally figured out via my morning FBG that I need to eat dinner about 5 and then NOTHING ELSE AGAIN. Then it stays in the low 80's, otherwise it's low 90's.

Flathead cherries are in. Around here everyone waits for this time of year when some enterprising individuals bring in a couple of truckloads of the cherries and sell them from roadside stands out on Coffeen Avenue. Every year I swear I want to go to Flathead lakes and pick some myself. I'd probably waddle off the rez. Anyway, I finally caved and bought some. They are the size of a QUARTER and beyond delicious. A couple of big handfuls will clean the pipes but good! I'm trying not to overdo since the last thing I need is to put on more fat. But it's not easy. They also had some Ranier cherries, same humongous size and just as good.

Did some reading (very little) on reverse T3 and promptly went and bought some milk thistle. Unless I'm supposed to be living on 800 calories a day on the leptin reset there HAS to be some underlying condition that keeps me from losing weight unless I starve! I keep coming back to thyroid issues.

Of course, I do not have a medical degree so I really am just flying blind here. Hopefully the results of the cortisol and thyroid panel will shed some light on this.

On another note, my sister just got some not so good news. She is a mess, physically having had a hip replacement, surgery for spinal stenosis and she needs to get both knees replaced. She is retired now on disability but won't sit still. She fell off a ladder a while back and finally went to the doc. She has a bone splinter in her spine. That was as clear as she could make it to me. Her doc told her she needed to see a specialist but the soonest she could get in was sept 12. And it's a long drive from where she lives. She was planning on doing the driving. I suggested she might need to call the doc and see (a) if that long a wait was advisable and (b) if she should be driving at all. I know she's scared. But she won't do anything to help herself. I'm thinking of posting in the Ask Jack thread to see exactly what he prescribes for his patients prior to surgery. Is it full on CT and paleo?? Anything else. Not that she'll listen.

crap.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-31T15:56:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48559>

Here's my WTF moment for today ... FBG is 112. I repeat. **WTF**?!!!

Yesterday's menu:

breakfast: the usual 6oz (raw)/4oz (cooked) ground beef with 3 slices thick bacon and a mug of BPC with 2tbs butter

lunch: leftover shrimp scampi (about 3/4 cup med shrimp), a slice of braunschweiger and a handful of almond nut thins (yeah yeah I know they're made with rice and I shouldn't eat them)

dinner (about 6-ish) 4 oz (weighed raw) salmon with a small green salad (greens, radishes, handful of cherry tomatoes and 1/2 small avocado) with homemade vinaigrette (vinegar and oil, period), glass of kombucha.

that was IT.

#1 suspect is the kombucha. although WHY it would raise my BG is beyond me. It's kombucha and ginger juice with 7g carbs per 8 oz crap crap crap

On another note, I went back through my wellness tests and here's something interesting. My TSH:

8-31-98 ...1.78 (yeah my file drawers are like an archeological dig)

8-22-04 ... 0.42

6-23-06 ... 1.73

12-12-08 ... 0.61

4-15-11 ... 1.85

8-22-11 ... 1.50

6-15-12 ... 2.29

I had come off the Jenny diet in February of '08 (18 months of starvation beginning in September of '06). and I wondered why I wasn't losing weight on 1200 calories a day and why I was "tired" all the time. judas aitch. The increase this year is "suspiciously" timed to when I started the leptin reset. hmmmm.

I'm thinking I'll go ahead this month (september) and do the annual wellness thingy again just to see if it's gone up any more since June. I've been pretty good about that BAB.

In the meantime, back to my @\*#&%(#) blood glucose levels.

### DarleenMB — 2012-08-31T18:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48581>

Well ladies, that's good to know in that I will stop those drinks. Good thing I only have a couple left in the fridge.

also, happy to amuse!  If you met me in real life though, you wouldn't know I'm the same person. I was listening to "There goes rhymin' Simon" yesterday (one of my all time favorite Paul Simon albums) and the song "Somethin' so right" came up. Brought tears to my eyes AGAIN with the lines:

"they got a wall in China,

it's a thousand miles long.

To keep out the foreigners they made it strong.

And I've got a wall around me,

that you can't even see.

It took a little time to get close to me."

I'm always surprised at how I can just let the walls down here on the 'net and just be me. Of course, it may well be that this is just another invisible wall.

And that is my TMI for today.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-02T01:27:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48820>

Woke up feeling like home-made doo-doo. It was really smoky again yesterday ... could barely see the mountains through the haze. I've been taking allergy? or would that be anti-allergy? drops for nearly 3 weeks now so the whole tongue itching thing has pretty much disappeared. But I have had major sneeze attacks the last several days and first my left ear hurt and this morning my right ear felt plugged up and achey. I had gawdawful earaches as a child and that was long before they figured out a fix. I still have problems occasionally.

ANYhoo. Rinsed my sinuses and about passed out the salt water HURT!!! Did it again a bit ago and it wasn't quite as painful so evidently shoulda been doing it all along. geez.

FBG was still elevated at 94 this morning. would really like it to STAY around 80, permanently!

Am feeling like a beached whale and am terrified to get on the scale but really need to do it. So will weigh tomorrow. Man I knew I shouldn't have started eating those damned cherries.

Am still logging meals, nothing new to report.

Did a wee bit more reading/scanning on reverse T3 and am now back to where I started several years ago. I have a LOW basal temperature. When I quit the jenny diet it was hovering just below 96. The doc told me that that was fine, 98.6 is "just an average." unhuh. Don't see him anymore, either.

So I printed out a graph set up to record daily average. 2 readings today because I didn't find it until long after I'd been up for 3 hours. So 2 readings and the average is 97.7. 2nd reading I took after bussing around the house vacuuming the floor and then it was 97.6 so really I think the whole issue is my stupid thyroid/adrenals.

I mean, not to sound like a broken record (do any of you know what that means??? god I feel ancient some days) BUT when you go for 18 months eating 1200 calories a day (ok ok it was 1200 SAD calories) and you're 110 pounds overweight to start AND you only lose 52 pounds in that time with the last 6 months only dropping EIGHT FRAKKING POUNDS and the whole time you have the energy of a cooked noodle and when you quit your basal temp is hovering around 96 on a REGULAR BASIS, your nails are paper thin, you hair has begun to reach 'time to buy a wig' thinness what the heck else do you suppose is wrong???

I mean ... anybody? Anything???

grumble grumble

Waiting for the results of the spit test for cortisol and anxious to get the blood draw for the thyroid panel. I don't know what else to do.

sorry for the rant. I'm having a bad "I am so fat I hate myself" moment.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-02T17:35:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48950>

Morning, LinD! Yes. We can.

Got on the scale and it made me laugh. I was expecting to see it had gone up 5 or more pounds because my belly just feels like it's out to THERE. I'm up 1 pound. ONE pound. That was the cherries. Good thing I sent the last of them off to Colorado with hubby a few days ago.

FBG 91. No more pistachios for a while. I had baked tilapia with pesto and green beans for dinner last night. With pistachios for dessert. salty/crunchy is my downfall. And get this. I've lost my craving/desire for chocolate! I have 4 bars of dark chocolate in the cupboard. shrug. don't care. aMAzing.

OK, first-thing-in-the-morning basal temp ... 97.8 Given that a year ago it was 96.8 I think this is pretty incredible. Eating as much protein as I can choke down IS turning up the furnace. Of course I still can't hit the 50g mark unless I do 2 slices of quiche and then I still have to force the last couple of bites, but I'm getting there. Fat seems to be easier because I can just throw a couple extra TBS of butter in my morning cup. Which I did because 5oz(weighed raw)/3+oz cooked ground beef and 3 eggs is only 37g. DAMN. But I keep trying.

My plan is to go jump in the tub in about 15 more minutes so guess I'd better go turn the water on.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-02T19:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-48979>

Just a quickie ... filled the tub with the setting I normally use in my daily shower. Turns out its only 77Â°. And here I thought it was way colder. So I hopped in anyway figuring I'll drop the temp a little more tomorrow ... it was chilly but quite comfy. Watched (or should I say rewatched) S1E1 of True Blood on my iPad. I KNEW there was another good use for that! Stayed in about 23 minutes. will aim for 5-10Â° colder tomorrow and 30 minutes. I need to get some sort of cushion for the back of my neck. And I will definitely put some ice on my jelly belly partly so I don't have to LOOK at it rising up out of the water like some ivory island. Maybe I'll get some little plastic palm trees and stick 'em on there while I soak. And a tiny hula dancer.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-03T20:27:52+0300

LOL vlynnb you give me far more credit than I deserve.  My sense of humor is apparently stuck about age 9 or so. I still love the Three Stooges. I am such a geek.

Yesterday's menu:

Breakfast, ground beef patty, scrambled eggs and BPC (was out of bacon, sob)

Lunch: can of pink salmon, green salad with homegrown TINY ITTY BITTY heirloom cherry tomatoes and home made vinaigrette

Dinner: pork chop, sauerkraut, green beans

prebed snack of raw almonds. OK I know I'm not supposed to do that but I got hungry. Just a few almonds shut my stomach up.

This morning's FBG 82. Musta been the fishy.

Breakfast: ground beef patty, bacon (went to the store) and BPC

Have a screaming headache. Still don't have enough air in the bed so my lower back is tied up tighter than a tick's ass. It's now causing nausea it's so bad. Guess I'll go sit in the massager for a bit and see if it loosens up. Just took some aspirin hoping if I knock the headache and I can relax enough to get the back to calm down. wonder if hubby still has any muscle relaxants left from his back surgery ... they'd be kinda old but should still work.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-09T21:49:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50556>

I'm still working on both. DH doesn't call me "the princess & the pea" for nothing.

Other than that the only new thing I have to report is a slight uptick in energy. Ever since I started the milk thistle I find I have the energy to do small things ... make the bed, clean the kitchen, sweep the floor ... one chore right after the other, without needing to go take a short nap.

But nothing else is happening. Still don't have my cortisol results. thyroid panel will be taken when I go to visit the outlaws and my sister in Colorado in a couple of weeks.

OUt of sorts by a bunch yesterday. The day before I had a glass of malbec with dinner. It's been in the fridge for several months and I finally decided to crack it open. Was singularly unimpressed. not only that it made my face flush really BAD for several hours. We're talkin' bright red and a lot of heat. It's from Redwood Creek. And yesterday the flushy thing happened AGAIN for no apparent reason. I went and lay down for bit and felt petty icky. Had had a salmon fillet and some leftover cole slaw for lunch. Had NO appetite at all until, of course, 9 or so and even though I now know WHY I shouldn't eat anything that close to bedtime I knew if I didn't I'd have a hard time falling asleep and wouldn't sleep well because I was hungry. So I had a double handful of raw almonds.

I need to find something I can keep at hand for times when I don't feel like eating but need to ... like last night. sigh

Hi Darleen, I just read about you taking milk thistle and having the flushing experience you related to the wine. I suspect I built up enough in my body to start reacting. Don't know if that is an issue for you at all but wanted to mention it so you could be on the lookout.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-11T05:44:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50900>

Nothing really exciting to report the last several days BUT yesterday I only ate 2 meals, my usual ground beef patty and bacon for breakfast with a mug of BPC and about 2ish I had a green salad with a small can of tuna and homemade vinaigrette. I wasn't really hungry so skipped dinner but about 9 I felt a little hungry. I decided to just ignore it because it wasn't really much.

My temperature this morning was 96.6 at 10:15 and again at 1:30. So instead of skipping lunch I had a small protein shake, just one scoop, and then we ate dinner about 5:45... Boulder Sausage brats and cole slaw. Now the really interesting thing is that I took my tempt at 4:30 and it was 98.4!!

I am more convinced than ever that my issues are related to thyroid, specifically RT3.

### vkiernan — 2012-09-11T14:51:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-50938>

Darleen,

Been thinking about you lately and your trying to figure out what is wrong. My own n=1 is that had it not been for having a few consults with Jack, I still would be here completely struggling and not having a damn clue why. He has directed me with a few labs at first and then more specific ones. I have had 3 half hour consults with him and now I am finding my problem. Money usually is the issue for not testing but I can tell you that without it you will struggle forever most likely. I had what is called a GI Effects test by Metametrics, Jack recommended. It is a test to tell you exactly what is going on in the gut. Remember the gut and brain speak to each other and if something is off they can't speak correctly (**side note: you can have specific bacteria that inhibit weight loss no matter what you eat**). I more than likely have hashis and celiac according to my results as they were high. They are autoimmune diseases and ANYTHING I eat that I would have a reaction to causes imflammation and then disease. I also have fat malabsorption which is an indicator of celiac. This means I can eat all the good fat in the world and it won't do me a damn bit of good and will never reach my brain and I will continue to deteriorate. Jack has gotten this message thru to me loud and clear. He even told me things that my test results show and I answered yes, he was totally right. Amazing!

As for the rt3, yes, I have that too. I also have the pg/e2 imbalance. Until I fix some of these things, I will never get better. You have done the right thing to balance your hormones but that is only 1 step in this process. A consult with Jack is priceless; completely worth every penny you have to spend. I'm not saying you should go get these tests, all I am saying is you can quit guessing once you actually figure it out and Jack will help you get there and tell you which labs to do. Everyone is different and context is really important. I have a pretty clear path now. Completely sucks everything I cannot eat but at least I know now I will be slowing my own death down if I comply.

Ok, not saying anything really except that if you haven't had a consult (which I don't remember you saying) that it would completely benefit you if truly are serious about figuring this out.

I wonder if you should start with the e-mail consult. Don't know if it is expired or not(might be). You write up a very detailed history and he will tell you where to begin via e-mail. Then do what he suggests to the best of your ability and then have a real consult and download vodburner recording software, if you have skype, so you can record it (this is a 1 month free subscription that actually works quite well). ### DarleenMB — 2012-09-14T03:10:12+0300

Thanks vkiernan, I did but, like most of his posts, about 2/3rds went right over my head.

Got my new, more potent allergy drops today, took one drop of each and about an hour later my face looked like I had a sunburn! My lips hurt and felt like they were blistered. Checked in the mirror and they were fine. Wow.

Heard from my sister. The fall she took broke off those "spiny" protrusions on 2 of her cervical disks and she has a large bone spur as well. The doc scheduled her for surgery in a few weeks and she's now wearing a cervical collar. There's other stuff going on but this is the worst of it. Doc cautioned her about infection since she is prone to that anyway. Years ago she finally got someone to listen to her and they discovered her gallbladder needed to come out and it wasn't just that she's obese. Well boy howdy it needed out ... it was gangrenous.

ANYway. I have BEGGED her to show her doc the preop post Jack wrote. I cautioned her that she could be facing some horrible horrible pain from the surgery and if she starts NOW eating as much fish as she can choke down along with fat and doing some CT she might come through this ok.

If I won the lottery tomorrow I'd send her to TN. I'm scared witless for her. We had been estranged for nearly 20 years and just have reconnected. That nasty assed childhood and adolescence I mentioned wasn't suffered alone. We're both reaping the whirlwind as far as THAT goes. We both suffer from dissociative personality disorders AND we're both so out of touch with our physical selves that, when injured, it takes 3 days before either one of us notices. True.

crap on toast. To quote my favorite witch.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-14T15:48:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51631>

Boy did I sleep great! The automatic pet feeder I ordered arrived yesterday. Sammy, my 16lb male cat with cataracts and seizures needs his meds 3x a day. It's easiest to mix it with his wet food. But by 4 or 5 AM (even leaving out kibble) he wants me to get up and feed him. Sooooo. Last night I fixed his last medicated meal about 9:30, put a little something in one side of the feeder to tide him over until it was time for his regular meal and his A.M. dose/meal in the other. OMG they left me alone until sunrise! I never woke up once either. I'm a happy camper right now.

Meant to say I've been using Quelson's powerup shakes the last couple of days. We'll see if they can kick my lazy metabolism into gear.

gotta go fix my BAB ... later.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-15T20:14:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51832>

Sunrise today was a deep red ... there's a huge forest fire West of Buffalo which is about 40 miles South of here. The fire is up in the high country and is totally inaccessible so it's just ... burning. I don't understand why the smoke is drifting this way but the sky has been so hazy for a week now that you can barely see the mountains. It's really bad today. I steppd out onto the porch to open the windows and the door and now my mouth is driving me crazy it itches so badly. I've had the house completely shut up for a week and the air cleaner in the bedroom going 24/7. Just washed my sinuses in the hope it would calm things down.

Basal temp yesterday was 97.2!! Those power up shakes seem to be doing something. Sure wish the coconut milk wasn't so darned expensive. Oh well.

Worked on my medical history last night. That, coupled with talking to my sister yesterday, gave me a lovely little nightmare this morning. More forbidden territory that I"m not supposed to look at. At least bears and/or lions didn't show up to scare me off. This time it was a barbed wire fence topped with razor wire. hmmm.

My sister remembers a LOT more than I do about our childhood/adolescence. I'd rather she not remind me about it but she is insistent. darn her.

I'm trying very hard not to focus on my fat these days and am instead try to focus on healing my body. I stopped taking the milk thistle because I thought it was causing allergy issues (stupid forest fire smoke) and now because I"m getting the blood draw on Monday (FINALLY!) for the full thyroid panel. I didn't want it to be skewed. I'll start taking the MT again when I get back from visiting the outlaws and my sister next weekend.

I'll be leaving Monday (not looking forward to the 6 hour drive) so will probably not post anything at all until I get back home.

Positive thoughts and energy for my sister would be greatly appreciated. Her doc is, she said, confident he can FIX her neck but he's very worried about the possibility of infection. I sent her the link to Jack's post about pre/peri op and begged her to show it to her doc. I've sent her lots of other links and talked to her until I"m blue in the face. Right now she's so frightened she's like a deer in the headlights. She has NO time to get ready but I told her the least she can do is start icing the back of her neck with a gel pack inserted between her skin and the cervical collar and to start eating LOTS of CO and fish.

They're so broke they can't even pay attention and she doesn't have any credit cards so she's stuck waiting for payday next week. He's a retired school teacher and she just went on disability retirement last winter. I'm just beside myself. and trust me, that's a sight no one wants to see.

She's 63 and a physical wreck. Worse than me because her joints are all shot, she's riddled with arthritis, she's been morbidly obese her entire adult life, she lost one adrenal and part of the kidney years ago when they found a baseball sized cyst on that kidney. They finally did a nuclear scan on her and discovered her thyroid is almost nonexistent. I'm pretty sure we're all prone to liver issues ... I do know our brother is slowly dying of liver failure brought on by alcoholism. Mine has been giving me fits since I was in my late 20's and I'd bet dollars to holes in donuts I have non-alcoholic fatty liver syndrome and so does she.

Ain't we all just a big fat mess.

### Destiny — 2012-09-16T14:12:49+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-16T18:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-51937>

I'm not taking it because I don't want the results skewed. MT as I understand it, helps the liver to clear itself. What I read about RT3 is that it is made in the liver and if your levels are too high then taking MT helps clear it from the liver. THAT's why I have stopped it until after the test. don't know if I'm on the right track or not, just guessing from what I've read.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-23T16:01:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-53581>

chocolate, I hear you on the cost of utilities. when we bought this place in 1996, propane was 25CENTS a gallon. 10 years ago it shot up to $2.25 and has stayed there. This year the CoOp introduced "balanced billing" where they took your previous year's usage, divided by 12 and you pay that amount monthly, year round. They just came out and added more propane to our tank (which holds 1000 gallons) and the cost was $1.45!! so they must have been able to get a sweet deal from THEIR supplier since they knew ahead of time how much they were going to need. So no more $500 bills from them for a lousy 200 gallons. We regularly go through 1200 gallons annually in spite of all the weatherization improvements we have made to this place. And it's NOT that big ... around 2000 sq feet. I think what really hurts is the floor is not insulated. The house is on a 4' crawl space. Oh well.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-24T06:56:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-53754>

You might have noticed I'm back. My trip to Colorado was not good. First off I was sick by the time I got there. It felt like I was getting an ear infection, I was hoarse and just plain felt icky. Slept most of Tuesday, woke up looking and feeling feverish.

Wednesday my "SIL" dragged me off shopping. She likes to not eat all day long. I didn't fare so well. thursday I got a pedicure (LOVE the candy apple red color!) so my feet are nice and almost soft again. Poor Doen, she really had to work to get all that dead skin off my feet. Then I got a facial ... first one ever. I was late for both appointments. HEY! All those backroads did NOT exist when I was a kid! Thank god for GPS. The facial was quick because I was late but nice. Turns out I really didn't need it as I had very few clogged pores. But she got a bunch of those danged chin hairs. They're hard for me to see because they're blonde AND very thin. Plus I got to relax a bit. The vaporizer thingie sure helped my allergies. I forgot to mention to smoke from the forest fires went all the way from Sheridan to Denver and beyond. It was SO bad on Wed that you couldn't even see the foothills!!

My sister came over Friday and hand delivered the bad news. I'm still reeling. She has spinal stenosis so bad, and has had it for so long, that her spinal cord is dying. she has signed a DNR for this surgery.

I can't talk about it ...

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-27T00:27:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-54589>

So now I have infections in both ears. My brain feels as if it slid sideways. I hate taking antibiotics. And my sister, who is having surgery on her neck to repair the damage from her fall last summer, has signed a DNR. She is 62. I know she's terrified of becoming a paraplegic because of the damage and the spinal stenosis but I think the DNR is a mistake at this time. Her son and daughter are not so happy with her either, nor is her husband. I'm just plain scared out of my wits. We have been estranged pretty much since I left "home" at 17 and just this past year have reconnected.

Got my thyroid labs back. Did I say that already? I know I posted over in the optimal labs section. Turns out, big forking surprise, that my RT3 is about twice what it should be. Coupled with my low basal temps and low cortisol levels IT'S NO FREAKING WONDER I'M OBESE. Sometimes I just want to bitch slap the entire medical profession. Well, except for Jack. He gets it.

So now I'm shuffling paper to get ready for my consult. After that I will go see my doc with all this new info and may need to take a foam rubber bat with me to just smack him a few times for telling me that "RT3 isn't checked unless they suspect disease" and that regarding my weight "we need to talk about calories."

Did you all read the latest blog about iodine? It's AWEsome. I sent the link to my sister and told her if she wants to avoid infection (which is a real concern given her history) she needs to read it AND give it to her surgeon. I'm going to wait to order the liquid potassium iodide from Amazon until I've had my consult. AND I'm really trying to get into the whole "eat more SEAfood thing" .. which, living in landlocked Wyoming, is pretty forking expensive.

We went to Billings to Costco on Monday and bought a lot of meat. I really wanted to get some shrimp etc but at $45 for the bag I had to let it go. I did buy TWO of the packages of canned pink salmon and one of the packages of tuna. We hadn't been since early last spring so it was a very expensive trip. I'm hoping the weather will stay nice enough I can get back up there the first of November. That'd be the fish trip.

I guess I'll take my cranky lil self off to the kitchen now and make a mug of decaf. I gave up the leaded stuff when I got my cortisol results. I was only drinking a mug a day but figured even that probably wasn't a good idea. I've gone most of my entire adult life without caffeine so it wasn't to difficult to give it up again.

Here's to clear skies, a little RAIN and happy eardrums.

### chocolate — 2012-09-27T00:41:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-54598>

Darleen, real sorry about everything going on.

On a note that might help you, Trader Joes and WF are a third of costco's prices. Seriously. IDK why. I've heard that the restaurant suppliers are pretty good also.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-27T00:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-54606>

Thanks but I think the nearest Trader Joe's is probably a little more than 2 hours away. WF? 6 hours down to fort Collins. We live in the middle of nowhere.

Costco has great prices and great products plus they treat their employees like gold! We love 'em. That $45 bag of shrimp? It was a LOT of shrimp and they were the big ones. But we were already way over budget so I had to either put something back or skip it for now. As it was we spent WAY too much. Because we only go about every 3 months (and not at all in the winter) we usually spend around $300 per trip. We spent double that this time. I'm still reeling. But we were out of everything plus there's the usual, "oh look at this!" ... I must have spent nearly $80 on raw nuts alone and about that much on the meat. But it was 10 boneless pork loin chops (the big ones), 4 big strip steaks, 4+ pounds of chuck "ribs" which I cut up for a fantastic paleo beef stew AND 2 huge bags of sirloin burgers (which at $3.99 a pound already saved me a bunch over what I've been paying). When you consider how much you're getting, it's well worth it. I keep raw almonds, pecans and walnuts in the freezer. And I was all out of pine nuts. Plus EVERYTHING has gone up this summer. What I had in that cart would have been $500 last Spring. I'm trying not to get too depressed. At least we can afford decent food and a roof over our heads and heat in the winter. Not everyone is so lucky.

Darleen, I have a home remedy that works within hours for relieving the pain (and infection).

Take some warmed olive oil, crush a bunch of garlic in it for about 10 mins, then drip the oil in your ear. Let it go down to the drum. It can get in there quick and alleviate things within hours.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-27T19:06:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-54766>

> **ealachan;55573 wrote:** Hope you're feeling better soon, Darleen, and will be keeping your sister in my thoughts. When is the actual surgery?

surgery on her neck is October 2nd. The speed with which they scheduled it has me freaked. But they're not done. She has a "lump" on her side which no one would take seriously. She's concerned it's filled with infection from a previous surgery. I don't know how many years ago it was they found a baseball sized lump on her left kidney. When it was finally removed, along with half her kidney and her adrenal gland, it was found to be an OLD hematoma that had become a hard shell. She said the interior was lined with gills like a mushroom and it was FULL of infection. There's more to it but she's convinced they didn't get all of the infection cleaned out and that's why she's had other problems, including last year's episode with an infection in her jaw that came out of nowhere. It took weeks of IV antibiotics to clear up. Dr. Chung is very concerned and has already booked an appt for her with another doc to take care of THAT.

Also, her hip replacement has been really bothering her. Dr. Chung says it could be a loose screw which would have been pistoning up and down and causing her grief and if that's what it is it's easily fixed.

thanks for the good thoughts ealachan. I'm worried sick about her.

As for me ... I'm feeling somewhat better but my ear is hurting again. Guess I need to go wash out my sinuses again. It almost rained yesterday. sigh. I could spit more moisture than that. The smoke continues to create a horrible haze over Sheridan and my allergies are still driving me nuts. I swear I'm getting a mask and wearing it from now until it clears up.

Thanks paulalynn.

I tried to lie down for a bit because my ear is bothering me again. stooopid catz. Missy (who's 19 and I swear has lost some of her hearing) marched up and YELLED in my face. Then she tried to sit on my head. Well to be honest, she was trying to sit on the heating pad which I had gently laid over my aching ear. Then Three showed up. She doesn't like him. I figured there was going to be a beat down right then and there. But she finally decided she'd go under the blankets along my back while Three settled for pining me down in the front.

Then Sammy showed up.

bother.

I'm rethinking the "love potion" for my ear.

### DarleenMB — 2012-09-29T19:49:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-55241>

I see the mad spammers are hitting this forum REALLY hard. jerks.

Was feeling a little better when I finally got up this morning. Three woke me up at 3am talking to himself up and down the hallway. I was awake for 2 hours then finally dropped off again. Had truly strange dreams (like that's something unusual for me). Now my right ear is kinda bothering me again. You'd think it would get better once I'm up and it can drain. Thinking about putting some warm OO and garlic in it and lying back down for bit.

Reading other people's journals to see how everyone is doing ... I guess my cravings left a long time ago. Now if I could just figure out why my appetite signaling seems to be out of whack. In rereading the BG12 post last night I'm starting to wonder if my hypothalamus is totally fried. brain transplant, anyone?

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-01T17:16:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-55569>

I'm back. didja miss me?

My ears are much better today. finally. Good thing I was taking the antibiotics because Three hooked my arm a couple of nights ago and it developed a little infection. The redness got about as big around as a penny but the swelling went down quickly and now it just looks a bit like an old bruise. Live with cats, plan on getting hooked now and then.

So, still wading through BG13. Read BG12 for the 3rd time and it's slowly starting to make sense. I think I'm going to go find a good beginning text on cellular bio and start studying. I'm getting tired of this. Computers are much easier.

Nothing new on the diet front. I still don't "feel" hungry but do get slightly nauseated. Wonder if that's the antibiotics working. Had shrimp and OMG FRENCH brie last night. First bite of cheese was a little mouthgasm it was soooooooo good. And I'm surprised to report I did not get hungry again. Didn't eat all than many shrimp either. Planning on having the baramundi fillet for dinner tonight. Still having the ground sirloin burger from Costco for breakfast and some bacon. I know it should be fish. I'm workin' on it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-02T19:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-55866>

My sister is, right now, going under the knife. We spoke last night and I had a good, calm feeling about this. I think she's going to come through fine. She's worried about her attitude after. I kept reminding her she is 62, not 22; that not doing what the surgeon says after is being selfish as it could deprive those of us who love her of her company ... and on and on.

I'm trying to get myself motivated to get back in the studio. It's been way too long and I'll probably set fire to something I shouldn't. I am just so fatigued. man I'm sick of this.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-03T02:38:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-55985>

I'm so relieved about my sister! So it's time for a bit of humor. DH's SIL sent me this:

Ladies, this has to be read, laughed at and passed on. There is not a woman alive who won't crack up over this!

I was due for an appointment with the gynecologist later in the week. Early one morning, I received a call from the doctor's office to tell me that I had been rescheduled for that morning at 9:30 am. I had only just packed everyone off to work and school, and it was already around 8:45am. The trip to his office took about 35 minutes, so I didn't have any time to spare.

As most women do, I like to take a little extra effort over hygiene when making such visits, but this time I wasn't going to be able to make the full effort. So, I rushed upstairs, threw off my pajamas, wet the washcloth that was sitting next to the sink, and gave myself a quick wash in that area to make sure I was at least presentable. I threw the washcloth in the

clothes basket, donned some clothes, hopped in the car and raced to my appointment.

I was in the waiting room for only a few minutes when I was called in. Knowing the procedure, as I'm sure you do, I hopped up on the table, looked over at the other side of the room and pretended that I was in Paris or some other place a million miles away. I was a little surprised when the doctor said, 'My, we have made an extra effort this morning, haven't we?'

I didn't respond.

After the appointment, I heaved a sigh of relief and went home. The rest of the day was normal ... some shopping, cleaning, cooking.

After school when my 6-year-old daughter was playing, she called out from the bathroom, 'Mommy, where's my washcloth?'

I told her to get another one from the cupboard.

She replied, 'No, I need the one that was here by the sink, it had all my glitter and sparkles saved inside it.'

Never going back to that doctor. Ever.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-03T22:53:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-56115>

I made clam chowdah yesterday ... used 2 cups 1/2 and 1/2 and 1 cup HWC (thank you Costco!) Boy it is tasty. Had another SMALL bowl for lunch just now and added in the last handful of cooked shrimp. yummy!!!!!

Looking at fixing baked cod for dinner. Got the cod and some flounder on the last outing to the store. Am going to Albertson's fish counter tomorrow and see if they have any fresh oysters and/or mussels. I figure I can at least TRY raw oysters. Maybe if I hold my nose and shut my eyes. Mussels, OTOH, I could eat by the bucket full. mmmmmmmmmmm so good! And clams! ersters are just ugly looking.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-04T19:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-56255>

Missed the cod, forgot it was DH's night with the boyz so he's never home for dinner. I had a chilled small can of crab meat which I dumped onto some lettuce and dressed with a combo of mayo, seafood sauce, lime juice and a few drops of worcestershire for flavor. The crab was just boring. Last time I buy it that way.

had a cup of decaf a bit later and then had that stupid flush on my face AND my mouth started itching. Don't know what caused it.

Meat day today with the usual BAB. Which is never BIG enough. I think I'm still skirting the 50g of protein but I have lots of butter in my morning decaf so that should take care of it.

Lunch will probably be a small bowl of the clam chowdah.

Dinner is going to be meat loaf. I've thawed some of Sackett's yummy home made breakfast sausage and have a pound of ground sirloin sitting there. Gotta use it. so meat day today.

Had trouble falling asleep last night. Finally got up and put on some socks and fell asleep before my head hit the pillow again. When my feet are cold I cannot sleep. They didn't FEEL cold but yesterday's temps never got above 38 and it was about 62 in the house when we went to bed. It's AGONY crawling between icy sheets (I gave up pj's and other bed "clothes" when I was 19) but man I warm that bed up fast these days. But my feet need a little help. Somewhere in the middle of the night I shucked the socks. And slept until 9:30!!!!

gotta run ... the timer on my breakfast is beeping...

### Endless — 2012-10-04T20:11:10+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-04T23:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-56321>

> **Endless;57528 wrote:** I am a sock wearer in bed as well...usually I end up taking them off sometime in the night.

Got another call from my sister last night. She's already home! She said all of the pain she'd been experiencing is gone and only a small amount from the surgery. They put her in one of those electrical stimulators to stimulate bone growth. My BIL has one from his neck surgery last winter. Back to sis ... she's supposed to take it easy for 6 weeks. No NOTHING. HA that should be interesting.

so one hurdle down, 2 more to go. AND she said the CT for the concussion showed no damage so we both think her eyesight problems probably stemmed from the bone spur and the stenosis in her neck. Hopefully that means THAT should improve and when it does she has my old Kindle now with about 300 books on it to keep her busy.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-05T20:31:42+0300

Winter is coming. This is what we woke up to today:

 [](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert_files/attachments/57766=493-winter.jpg)Not much but it's a harbinger of what's to come (I hope).

Fish for dinner last night with a little broccoli on the side.

Usual BAB this morning ... ground sirloin patty and bacon with a mug of BPC

Lunch? Not sure at this point. Gotta run to town to replenish the bacon. Headed over to Albertson's fish counter to see what's fresh.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-06T02:26:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-56550>

Home again from the mkt ... no fresh oysters but they had a jar of 'em. Any suggestions? I was thinking oyster stew maybe? gak shudder (bad memories from living with the monsters). Anything else to do with 'em? anyone?

Also got a pound of mussels which were farmed in New Zealand. They're HUGE!! Can't wait to snarf 'em down. Yes Jack, I am still plowing through BG13 and I'm finally getting it. I still think you should let me be your editor.  You've improved a LOT but there are still little slips that make my brain skip like an old vinyl LP (you kiddies don't have a clue about that, now do ya?! LOL)

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-07T03:54:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-56663>

Thanks Diane! But they're in a jar! I think I'm gonna bake 'em.

That's ok though. Dinner tonight was mussels ... I sauteed some onion and garlic in a bit of ghee and CO, took it out of the pan and added in the mussels (they were already cooked and on the half shell) until they warmed up. Put the onion/garlic mix back in the pan and stirred a little bit longer. Had I some dry white wine I would have finished it with that. Had to settle for half a lemon (and not a very fresh one at that but it was still good). I steamed the crab legs while I was working on the mussels and that was dinner. OOOOOOO doggies! That was some fine eatin'!

I have to say those were also some big assed mussels. My taste buds are a little warped right now (probably from the antibiotics) but they still tasted gooood. And yeah, I chewed them puppies up good. Couldn't finish my crab so I have a bit left for tomorrow.

Jack would be so proud.

### Endless — 2012-10-09T18:57:05+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-10T23:43:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-57438>

Plowing through BG13. Now I'm a kinda "woo-woo" gal here but sometimes Jack out woo=woos me.

Have been getting "trembly" late morning/early afternoon for the last several weeks. YEsterday it happened again and I'm thinking WTH because I'm back on decaf. Then I ate a little lunch (braunschweiger and a small handful of sweet potato chips) and the trembling stopped.

crap.

Back to square one. I may be obese but in my heart of hearts I know that I am not eating ENOUGH. And you know what? I dont' wanna. Eating is a pita. There. I said it.

Today I was out running errands (voting and grocery shopping) and here came the tremblies again. Well fork.

Got home, unloaded the cat food and litter (yes my darling little furbabies eat well and on time as opposed to moi) and got Sammy his lunch/meds. Still trembling. okokokokokokok Fished (ahahahaha) the pack of water-packed sardines out of the fridge and spooned 'em out into a dish. BLEH! Oh. "no salt added" Well WTF

Salt.

Better

Am now finishing up a small handful of salted/oven roasted almonds. And my decaf latte AND my big glass of ice water.

There. Don't I feel better now?

pfbbbbt.

ETA my face has flushed bright red and my temp has spiked to 98.8!!! Holy fishcakes. could the sardines have had something to do with this? Do ya suppose??? Wonder what it all means.

### chocolate — 2012-10-13T20:56:04+0300

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-14T02:52:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58025>

I know better than to say "never" on any forum. It's been a long, stressful day but I'm still here. Sorry for the little snit I tossed earlier. sometimes the 5 year old gets hold of the controls and runs amok. I've sent her to her room to play with her crayons.

Just got done with my phone consult and have oodles and oodles to ponder. Good thing the recorder thing was working. Now if I can just get it to playback because I was so nervous I'm sure I missed half of what he said.

And then to add to the stress (pity my poor wrung out adrenals) I had a short visit with hubby about the consult. whew. I finally managed to share some things with him we've never discussed in over 35 years of marriage. My face is burning and I want to burst into tears but it's out. I cant' say I feel any better right now but you have to start somewhere.

Now that you're all scratching your heads I guess I had best explain just a little. And that's all it's going to be... a little.

My childhood and adolescence were marked by continued sexual, emotional and physical abuse. Parents divorced when I was 6, and I lost my Mother to a car accident when I was 13. The impact that part of my life had on me is not easy to even think about. BUT the good news is Jack believes that if I'm willing to pursue it I can fix my poor beleaguered brain. that the problems I am now experiencing are due to the fact the proper neuronal connections were not made when I was child. I think I got that part right. So if I rewire using epi-paleo, increase hormones and test test test as well as work on releasing lots of oxytocin I should be able to fix what's been broken for so long.

I'm excited. Yeah that's the neanderthal genes expressing delight at something new to tackle. I do SO love a challenge.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-14T02:56:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58027>

Glad to have you back and hearing you sound optimistic. Go Darleen!

Secrets can take many forms. They can be shocking or silly or soulful. They can connect us with our deepest humanity, or with people we'll never meet. How you use it to fuel your life is what courage is made of.

Sneaky mussels.

I've never cooked fresh ones before. I got them home, tucked them in a nice bowl with damp paper towels over them and a tight lid. Took them out a bit ago and a bunch had opened. Well hell. Into the trash.

Getting ready to fix seafood stew and thought to look and see how to clean the darned things.

Sneaky mussels.

I had to fish all but 2 out of the trash. TAP TAP TAP anybody home? errrrrrrk as they closed up tight.

bwahahahaha you're mine now you tasty little thangs.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-15T03:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58230>

so the fish stew is done. I tossed in oysters and shrimp. Just a simple base of tomatoes, onion, garlic, parsley and organic chicken broth.

It looks and smells yummy.

so why am I not hungry????

Had my usual BAB ... Costco ground sirloin patty, 3 slices bacon, mug of BPDC

About 3 I had 3 small slices of smoked cheddar with a bit of deviled ham on it and a handful of sweet potato chips. and some cream cheese stuff. I have no hunger right now. But I'll bet about 9-ish I get hungry again. foo.

Well hubby can test the stew ... he loves oysters and mussels and shrimp. I hope I got all the beards off the mussels.

Oh and I'm working on a rather long journal post. Man my head has been buzzing all day long with images and thoughts flitting around like fireflies on a hot summer night. sheesh.

### [email redacted] — 2012-10-15T04:31:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58238>

Darlene,

I love your posts! I think it's ok to be yourself and now and then actually even communicate negative things with people. I've never been accused of looking through rose colored glasses. Sometimes things actually suck, and sharing the suck with someone can be a great way to process and let it go.

On our call last night, Dr. K mentioned that there are some things that talk-therapy just makes worse. . .when you focus on negative things and go over them and keep them alive and keep dwelling on them. . . and I agree with this. Sometimes you just have to move on - but there is a place for sharing and letting the support you get help you jettison the bad juju.

I also have great respect for people who can go back and change their minds and adjust rather than sticking to some statement made in high emotions. I have a long history of cutting off my nose to spite my face, so I appreciate examples of slightly more mature behavior!

It must have been so difficult to talk to your husband after so long.

Good luck with re-programming!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-15T20:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58373>

I checked out some of the programs listed on the Skype site. The one I bought (it was $20) is eCamm recorder. Real easy to install (but then I drive a Mac so that kinda goes without saying) and use. When the call came in I pressed the button with the red dot on it and it started recording. I haven't looked at it yet but it appears to have gone straight to the correct folder.

Just a word of caution... be sure you have NOTHING ELSE RUNNING while you're on that call. I forgot that in my earlier panic that day I had set my email program to check every 5 minutes (I normally check it manually because I go through a filter program first to take out all the spam). so I had drop outs every 5 minutes and I did miss a lot at one point because evidently there was mail being d/l. STUPID STUPID STUPID But I was pretty much running in panic mode from the time I got up.

The things Jack discussed with me were very difficult for me to deal with because, I think I said this earlier, I have never EVER talked about them with anyone. And to discuss them with a man (even though he is a doctor) just made it about a billion times worse. I haven't even told my own physician (who I really really like and trust) the things I told Jack. It showed on my face too because it was bright red the whole time.

I'm seeing the PA today because (again) I just can't bring myself to tell Chris (my doc) the things I need to. I'm more comfortable talking to Deb about it because I've already broached part of it with her and survived to tell the tale.

god I am such a coward.

OK. I wanted to share my new journey and yesterday tried to write in my personal journal about all the thoughts banging around in my head. It was like trying to listen to Beethoven, Lady Gaga, Led Zepp, Dvorak and every horrible pseudo C&W song ever written ... all at freaking once.

Jack made me face some unpleasant truths. Unpleasant because I have been avoiding them for a very long time. So score a few points for the good doc.

What I distilled out of yesterday's mindmash was this. I have allowed fear to inform every single decision I have ever made and I am where I am today because of those choices and decisions. I have to own them if I'm going to fix them. My marriage is where it is partly because of actions on dh's part but I need to take responsibility for part of the sad state of affairs we have going on. It was choice I made. Now I have to work on changing it.

And that's as much as I can deal with right now.

You're no coward, not by a long shot. Brave and committed to moving forward is more like it.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-10-15T23:50:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58407>

> **DarleenMB;59885 wrote:** I
>
> god I am such a coward.
>
>
>
> What I distilled out of yesterday's mindmash was this. I have allowed fear to inform every single decision I have ever made and I am where I am today because of those choices and decisions. I have to own them if I'm going to fix them. My marriage is where it is partly because of actions on dh's part but I need to take responsibility for part of the sad state of affairs we have going on. It was choice I made. Now I have to work on changing it.
>
>
>
> And that's as much as I can deal with right now.

When a person grows up with constant abuse, fear is intelligent. Fear is essential to survive. To not have fear is to get more abuse, or even be killed. Fear is not cowardice in this situation, it is survival instincts.

What I hear you saying is that your fear no longer serves you. In fact, it is colouring your ability to see clearly when things are no longer abusive.

The same intelligence that helped you to see you needed to be afraid can help you to see what you need now. We all do this step-by-step. None of us do it all at once. If you treat yourself harshly, you are recreating the same thing that was done to you. Treat yourself kindly. Baby steps.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-16T00:15:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58422>

Thank you both for that.

Now, I sure wish I could rename this journal:

**Unleashing the Rock Goddess**

And that will take a bit of explaining but I'm not there yet.

Just thought I'd toss that out for grins and giggles.

The PA is prescribing cytomel for my thyroid. I gave her the 411 on my consult but we're taking one thing at a time. I gave her the list of suggested labs so we need to sort that all out. Just remembered I forgot one. I really need to watch the recording of the consult and make better notes.

AND I got almost as flustered talking to her as I did to Jack which makes me wonder if something else is going on. Like the deeply buried threat of what would happen if I ever told anyone. Life with a psychotic monster can be fun. /sarcasm

One step at a time. But now my brain is going haywire on me again.

Easier stuff ... breakfast was salmon, shrimp and bacon with a mug of BPDC

Just had 2nd meal which was tuna salad with celery, onion, HB egg and a bit of mayo and mustard on half an avocado with just a touch of black lumpfish roe (which I LIKE! but it's way salty and I do not care for the dextrose they added. ugh). Side of baby peppers and tiny dill pickles and a bit of dark chocolate with chili pepper in it. Love that kick.

### Lyndra — 2012-10-16T00:47:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58427>

Darleen, I hope you don't mind me saying this but I am getting a different vibe from your writings the last few days. There's so much hope and optimism, despite your fears. I'm really excited for you and where you're headed...

at the other end of your fears lives the freedoms you are craving.........to build the bridge to them requires action. Darleen is the dynamite........I just showed her a lit match. The next step is hers. She has all the keys for transformational change.......

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-16T21:37:27+0300

I'm not sure I'm alive today.

Wheeeeeee! Better living through batteries!

Adam & Eve usually has fun stuff at reasonable prices. I think the website is just adamandeve.com. I think they even have reviews on stuff. I'd check, but I'm at work...hahaha. They definitely have some good basic things, though.

And remember, it's supposed to be FUN. Feel free to giggle and point at things while you're shopping. That's half the fun.  Oh, and make sure you have extra batteries. There's nothing worse than batteries dying in the middle of...a critical moment. Trust me. LOL!

### ealachan — 2012-10-16T23:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58602>

Well see, there's two schools of thought there: you can spend a bunch of money for something state-of-the-art that does the trick, or you can spend little to nothing on something low-tech that does the trick. Either way, trick's getting done! I think a lot of women go the expensive route on their first foray into BOB's, because they maybe don't know quite what they want / need. Once you've gotten one and gotten a feel for it (sooo punny, I KNOW), you'll be able to then shop a little more efficiently. You'll know that the rabbit ears don't do it for you, or WHOA BUDDY bigger is better, or nope you just want a little $8.99 pocket rocket that gets you where you need to go quick fast and in a hurry.

Also, for what it's worth, there are cheaper versions of the one that you linked to. There are lots of them out there nowadays in that shape and style, not least of all because they look a hell of a lot more discrete than some of the big anatomically correct jelly monstrosities.

### Lanie — 2012-10-17T08:51:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58684>

I recommend the Hitachi Magic Wand...a bit on the pricy side, and not the quietest or the tiniest of toys, but other than that it's the best! Nothing else has come close. I tried a less expensive knockoff first - just as effective, but the product itself didn't last as long. Good luck, and I second the motion to have fun!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-17T20:21:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58743>

Thanks for the recs ... I'm still trying to narrow the field down.

FInally gave up waiting for my sister to get on her computer so we could talk via skype and I just called her. I was telling her some of the things I learned in my consult and the next thing I know she's sobbing. crap. She is just beaten so far down there isn't any more up for her. I can't talk about it any further than that because it's her story, not mine. But it just makes me feel so damned helpless.

and before the lectures start, let me share this with you. When I was 3 and Mom was pregnant with Louise, Mom went about getting me ready for the arrival of "MY baby." Now mind you I was 3 when she was born. She (my sisiter) got really really sick right after she was born and they kept her in the hospital for a week. She almost died. And I have a vivid memory of the day she finally came home. They made me sit on the couch and I can still see my Mother walk through the door with this huge bundle in her arms.

It was March so lots of blankets and Louise was a good sized infant ... she weighed almost 9 lbs when she was born.

Mom walked over and put that big bundle in my lap and I got to hold MY BABY for the first time.

I promised Mom I would always take care of MY BABY.

and here I am 62+ years later still trying to take care of her. And I can't. I can't fix her life. I can't fix what's broken in her because I'm having a hell of time fixing what's broken in me. And it's tearing me apart.

### vkiernan — 2012-10-17T20:32:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58753>

A whole lot of responsibility and guilt for a 3 year old!!!!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-17T20:35:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58755>

> **vkiernan;60289 wrote:** A whole lot of responsibility and guilt for a 3 year old!!!!

Nothing compared to the guilt heaped on me a couple years later by my dad. Doesn't matter. I love my sister and would move heaven and earth to make her life better. If only that would work.

I think the fact that you can admit this to yourself is a pretty big step in the right direction. So many people go through life trying to fix others' problems and never even realizing that's what they're doing or that it's fruitless.

Hugs to you.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-18T00:01:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58817>

Jack my intellect knows that and agrees. My emotions just want to hold her and make her well. I know I can't do it for her but it doesn't stop making me feel like I should. And that is precisely why I laughed when you told me that people close to me might say I'm a PITA.

I'm working on it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-18T17:48:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-58941>

Yesterday I only took 3/4 of a tab of the cyclomet. I had my usual bab of bacon, ground sirloin patty and mug of BPDC. Lunch was a BUNCH of shrimp. That was it. Later on I had a handful of almonds. I never got hungry again. I waited because usually if I eat a late lunch I don't get hungry again until about 8 or so. Nothing.

Got up this morning, not real hungry but I started breakfast and then checked my BG ... HOLY CRAP. It was 182! That is TWICE what it usually is.

I know I've read somewhere in here that someone else was having a similar problem with their BG bouncing all over the place. Anyone have any answers? Or do I need to go back to the PA? Why on earth would bumping up my T3 cause my BG to go nuts? And yes, I'm going to check it again in about an hour.

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-19T20:46:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59212>

so just what IS a rock goddess anyway?

This has been rumbling around in my head the last several days so I figured it was time to lay it all out.

First of all, the very fact I actually managed to get up on a stage once-upon-a-time and belt out rock tunes while fronting a band is pretty amazing in and of itself. My primary directive in life has always been to avoid the spotlight. Drawing attention to myself always brought punishment. Easier to hide. I learned chameleon-like behavior very early — mimic the predator and you stayed safe. I wonder what part of the reptilian brain controls THAT behavior.

ANYway. I saw a new album yesterday by Van Morrison entitled “Born to sing — no plan B” which pretty much sums me up. I wanted to sing for as long as I can remember. I sang to myself mostly. When I was 8 or 9 I conned my Mom into letting me take the lime green Crosly radio that was in the kitchen up to my room. I set it on the window sill next to my bed where, when I lay down, the speaker was right next to my ear. I would turn it on really low late at night and listen to the rock jocks on KIMN out of Denver. That was pre-head banging days btw. But it was still some great music. And Pogo Pogue was my fave DJ. Baby rock queen.

Later I dreamed of singing on stage with a band. At 30 I finally grabbed hold and went for it. It lasted 2 whole years and I was just getting comfortable with it and my voice was showing vast improvement (like any muscle, exercise strengthens) and then BAM it was over. (I just want to interject that a regional talent scout had already started booking gigs for us and we hadn't even signed the contract yet ... we were GOOD). I’m not going to go into it except to say it was like being divorced 5 times all at once. I shut down the music in my head and my heart. Turned my back on the thing I loved.

BUT (cue Joan Jett and the Blackhearts) I (STILL) LOVE ROCK ’N ROLL … my heart has a 4/4 backbeat. Give me pounding drums, screaming guitars and I’m a happy camper. Sex, (I don’t’ do drugs) and rock ’n roll. yup. That’s the life I live in my head.

When I read TS Wiley’s “Sex, lies & menopause” I found within the first couple of pages a very interesting tidbit. At least I found it interesting. Don’t know about anyone else. It’s called the sacculus and is part of the middle ear. It likes loud noise. Channels the vibes right through to (where the flock else?) the hypothalamus.

side note here … I only had one quarter of neuropsychology way back in my college days. I’m thinking I must have forgotten most of that class because although I remember gross anatomy of the brain I do not recall the hypothalamus getting a lot of attention. Why? Here’s this quote from Wiley, “ … the part of your brain that controls drives like hunger, sex and more than a few other hedonistic responses — the" (say it with me brothers & sisters) "HYPOTHALAMUS." CAN I GET AN AMEN? But then, it was only an introductory class.

Where was I?

Oh yeah, TS Wiley. She goes on to talk about how activating the sacculus causes feelings of movement or floating. Um. Well, maybe in YOU darlin’ but my reaction is a whole lot different. (see previous reference to sex, (I don’t do drugs) and rock and roll)

There you have the first half of the equation. Or at least a brief peek at it. Rock Queen + sex goddess = ??????? Will I get there? Is there any point. Well, other than feeling I finally pulled all the disparate parts of myself back together I guess not. But as long as I’m still breathin’ I’m not giving up.

### janagram — 2012-10-20T06:15:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59293>

this friend of mine who is a really shy older gal played a tape of herself singing with a band back in the day.........Sounded. just. like. Grace Slick.

It was awesome!!!!!!!!!

### Lanie — 2012-10-20T10:59:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-59301>

Darlene...I do believe a part of you has come unleashed!! Yay for freedom!!!
