### DarleenMB — 2012-11-28T22:49:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65813>

I was born and grew up in Fort Collins. Â My inlaws still live in Loveland, my sister and her husband in Brush. Â I positively HATE what has become of my once beautiful home. Â Where there was once open fields between Â Fort Collins and Denver there is now mile after mile after mile of subdivision. Â It literally makes me weep.

Â 

Once upon a time I wanted desperately to move back. Â I miss the front range. Â At least I always knew what direction I was facing, LOL, unlike here in Sheridan where we're huddled along a spur of the Rockies known as the Big Horns. Â It's pretty but I miss the foothills and the splendor of the mountains thrusting up behind them. Â Here you're either out on the flats or up on the mountain. Â No in between. Â Makes driving to the top very interesting, I'll say that. Â You get half way up to Sand turn and I swear you can see all the way to the South Dakota border. Â Just don't look down!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-29T13:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65843>

It is a giant PITA living with someone who just will not listen and doesn't want to get it.

Â 

DH complains constantly that the house is cold (I keep it around 68 just to be nice). Â At night he watches TV in the bedroom because he can close the door, turn up the little oil heater to max sweat Â and roast. Â By the time I'm ready to go to bed it's like 85 in there. It means I have a difficult time dropping off because it's too effing warm. Â And then I don't sleep well because first I throw off most of the covers and then I get chilled and my back muscles cramp up and then my neck hurts and I dream I'm slogging through the winter setting in Â GAME OF THRONES. Â I swear I am going to spend the rest of the winter in the guest room because it's nice and cool in there.

Â 

Then to add insult to injury, this morning he gets up early (for him) and I keep sleeping. Â He's up for a good solid 2 hours before I stagger out of bed, brush my teeth, empty the litter pans and check in here. Â Yes I know I shouldn't but I did. Â Shoot me. Â 

Â 

So now I"m at the 30 minute mark and need to get my breakfast done. Â HE saunters into the kitchen while I'm fishing out a ground beef patty and now I get to wait at least another 30 minutes while he dithers around fixing some eggs and bacon. Â A task I could accomplish in about 10 minutes but now I'm so pissed I'd likely crack his skull with the frying pan.

Â 

What a way to start the day.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-29T14:52:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65856>

> **Shijin13 said**
> Its definitely easier when all family members are on board.... I still struggle w/my mom - even though she's told me point blank she won't change... I'm thinking that if I could get her to read the dopamine RX she might consider changing. Â I think I'll print it out and mail it to her via snail mail...Â 

I wish you the best of luck. Â I have given up. Â Family tunes me out, friends diss me. Â I've learned just to shut my yap.

Â 

As I lay in bed this morning pondering away I realized that the majority of people are content to live underwater. Â It makes me crazy. Â How can you NOT be curious about the world around you and the world IN you?

Â 

I think I was born asking WHY? Â and will probably go out the same way. Â It's my favorite word. Â WHY????? Â I want to cram all the knowledge of the world into my head. I want to KNOW. Â How can you live and not ask questions? Â How can you not search and seek and try to understand? Â It baffles me.

Â 

It's part of why I chase around Jack's blog and this forum. Â It's a real big WHY? Â I keep pounding the information into my head in the hopes the light will one of these days break and understanding will descend to a chorus of hallelujahs. Â How can everyone around me be so complacent? Â Especially where your health is concerned?

Â 

(insert sound of me banging my head on the wall in frustration)

Like you Darleen - I have always wanted to know why. I think I have always been left of centre and nothing was ever too weird. I sometimes worry that I drive people crazy with all the questions. We had a restaurant for 35 years and I soon discovered - there was so many amazing people passing thru and if I didn't talk to them and ask questions I would never know.
Having said that - friends and family seem to be really wary like you are on some kind of crusade for something really weird and crazy and totally stupid. I certainly don't push anything - I barely say anything really. If they knew that I ate raw meat and MHS they would probably have me committed!
It really makes me stop and think........If JK's FIL didn't listen to him - and he has all the education and knowledge and only his best interests at heart - what chance do we have to make an impression on anyone? Although I am understanding more and more -I still can't really explain it in any kind of technical terms. Thankfully I have a great new Doc but I think I am going to drive him crazy as I try to understand all this and his time is so limited.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-11-29T20:14:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65897>

Then answer is in this blog post........modern life causes diminished brain dopamine from many avenues. Â So far I have given you three. Â When your dopamine levels are low you cant perceive the level of complacency of your decisions because your brain is working inefficiently.........and when someone points it out to you, you fly off the handle and attack instead of just thinking..........WHY might this be correct.

Â 

It's pretty simple. Â Bad choices are a sign of a modern sort of brain damage.

### Inger — 2012-11-30T06:31:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65925>

LOL.. I must laugh when reading all your stories about it beeing to warm! Must be the CT..huh! Me too feels so warm everywhere I do not like to have it warm around me anymore! I like cool.. And before Jacks cold thermogenesis posts I was the opposite.. always loved to have it hot!

Darleen I suggest to go sleep in the guest room too! Sleep really is important. Maybe you hubby starts missing you so much he will turn the heat down..lol amazing things might happend! But I believe we need to care for us first. Yes.

I tell everyone (when it comes/fits naturally) that I eat raw fishheads and raw meat and anything. It is not hard to explain / understand for anyone how a fresh raw fishheadsmoothie is so much healthier than some rancid processed cod liver oil everyone is choking down withouth a second thought... Or how vitamins and stuff gets destroyed by cooking and what benefit foods have in their raw state.

I am not one bit ashamed. Not ONE bit. Cause I know what good they do me. They save me my sanity and my health. I do not care one bit if someone thinks I am weird because of that. I have nothing to loose anymore anyways, that broken is this girl. How could anyone ever see, if I do not show/tell what treasures I know about..??? That is what I wish from anyone, to share their treasures with me! So I do it too. As long as someone is not forbidding me to speak about it. I am always respecting that people do different choises though, and I am not pushing anything on anyone. And guess what. There is so much love around.... that amazes me again and again.

### Inger — 2012-11-30T07:29:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65937>

> Yes - I can see talking when it fits naturally ...maybe in Europe this is all more acceptable. People I know think it is crazy not to eat bread/grains etc. They actually are worried that I am harming myself. I have been told so may times that I will make myself very sick if I don't have carbs. They tell me that carbs are absolutely necessary. Their is no doubt in their mind that they are right and I am wrong - even tho they have all kinds of health issues.

You know what I do then? I tell them I go regularely to the doctor and test just in case my diet would be not good for me because who can be sure about anything in life.. and that my tests are awesome! (in their or my docs eyes..lol to me they can be still much better but that I leave out..ha) Now, that always works! It needs to be scientific, always. So that is why I go to the doc and test even if I need to pay out of pocket. And.. they see my energy, my moods.. they always tell how healthy I am.. And I tell them why that is. And that I am actually very weak so I need to pay very strict attention to what I put in my mouth or I will feel not good. I say stuff like;

" oh.. I see you are eating this ice cream. Good for you.. lucky you you are strong and healthy to be able to eat this stuff. I am not unfortunately I would get sick eating these.. But hey I take it because I feel so great with my fishheadsmoothies..lol"

And they all see it is true! That way I do not come over as someone pushing them to change, but I hope when they have a silent moment in pain they might consider that they are weak too... and maybe want to change little by little!
There might be a much better way to do it but this is how I do so far and it works fine.. people do not get offended.. and I have a change to tell about my lifestyle too..
Like I always have my sunglasses on when somewhere out at night (only at work I cannot wear them for sure but after) and people ask why and I tell I am optimizing my hormones. Sound good, or?

When they ask how the glasses do that, I explain it further. Nature rules. When it is dark out we should have it dark too, when it is light out we should have it light too. The rules in nature are there for a reason. And I explain the signaling and stuff, how our brain works. It is all very easy to understand!

Â  Didn't Dr. K. say that someone was at his house and said that pasta wasn't wheat? Â I find that generally people aren't prepared to sacrifice the stuff they love - no matter what.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-30T08:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65943>

It only takes one small nugget to plant the seed w/ppl who are hesitant. Yesterday my Lactation consultant who worked w/me for both Chaos & Havoc called me b/c of the last time we spoke I told her I'd figured out why I had problems w/lactation. That little nugget stuck in her mind & kept coming back to her while working with a present client. She wanted to find out how I made my discovery. I broke down hormones 101, ct 7 & the BG series down in 15 mins. She asked me for the links & wants to sit down to learn more.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-30T11:30:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65957>

That little nugget stuck in her mind & kept coming back to her while working with a present client. She wanted to find out how I made my discovery. And that's part of my problem. Â I have trouble explaining these concepts because I don't have a knowledge base like you do. This is my daily struggle with a lot of this information. Â Lack of tools. Â I have had exactly one biology course and one class in human physiology. Â Oh yeah and the one lone course on intro to neuropsychology. Â So while I'm not intimidated by the terms you all bandy about I do have to spend an inordinate amount of time googling most of them and then trying to wade through all THAT information. Â 

Â 

It is the epitome of the blind leading the blind.

### Shijin13 — 2012-11-30T11:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-65959>

And that's part of my problem. Â I have trouble explaining these concepts because I don't have a knowledge base like you do. This is my daily struggle with a lot of this information. Â Lack of tools. Â I have had exactly one biology course and one class in human physiology. Â Oh yeah and the one lone course on intro to neuropsychology. Â So while I'm not intimidated by the terms you all bandy about I do have to spend an inordinate amount of time googling most of them and then trying to wade through all THAT information. Â 

Â 

It is the epitome of the blind leading the blind.

In my job I've had to teach myself how to learn something in short order and be able to explain it back to the experts w/in days of starting a job. Its a learned skill, not a lack of tools or knowledge base. I'd say 100% of the time I'm thrown into job I know nothing about - but I can learn it. you're learning too...add that to your online courses you're taking you'll be able to explain this as well. I think in the end you're going to understand it at a far different level than I do b/c of the courses you're taking and be able to translate it to someone else's perspective...where I'm only good at the n=1. your journal is amazing at actually explaining a lot of things... and your BHRT knowledge is amazing - don't sell yourself short my dear

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T12:28:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66029>

AUGH Â our internet was down ALL DAY LONG yesterday. Â They finally got it back up some time during the night. Â Talk about withdrawal! Â Stupid phone company. Â Long distance was out as well. Â I'm surprised every single business in town didn't shut down.

Â 

OK favorite movie line EVER ... "Never give up! Â Never surrender!" Â That's kinda my mantra.

So thanks for the ego strokes -- giving up was never in my vocabulary. Â Oh wait, there's that mule headed stubbornness Â again.

Â 

CT was tough yesterday because I had just settled into the tub when the internet went down. Â Double augh. Â I knew that if I got out to go investigate I wouldn't get back in because our water has dropped to 49. Â Yes, I'm a wuss unlike certain neurosurgeons who trek off to the 63rd parallel, climb into a deep, dark hole and hop in a tub of 42Â° water. Â pfffbt.

Â 

anyhoo, I had to have hubster check the modem which, since he is technologically challenged, is always a PITA. Â I finally gave up and had him bring me my iPod. Â Rocked the rest of CT and am thinking about adding to the 'talk sex' thread because of it. Â 

Â 

BAB was usual. Â Sardines for lunch. Baked tilapia for dinner with 1/4 of a honker of a sweet potato. Â I just like a little something extra on my plate. Â The fish looked lonely.

Â 

finally, snagged a new quote off the telly for my sig line. Â I love pithy, short commentary as long as it isn't telling me to put all my troubles in a little pink box, tie a pink bow around it, attach it to a pink balloon and let them float away. Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T12:56:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66030>

somebody talk me down off this ledge.

Â 

I just got off the scale and I gained FOUR POUNDS this last week.

Â 

The only thing that has changed has been the addition of a bit more fat. Â I'm going through a stick of butter in my coffee(s) daily ... cause not even I could do 4 ounces of butter in one mug. Â Other than that it's been the same routine as always. Â I quit putting salt in my morning ice water about a week ago so it cannot be simple fluid retention. Â My measurements have gone UP so you can't claim it's added muscle.

Â 

I am within 7 pounds of my highest weight ever and I'm about to jump. Â That little voice in my head keeps telling me to quit eating. Â It's the only way. Â damn damn DOUBLE DAMN.

I'm upping my morning cytomel to 1/2 tab with 2 more 1/4 tab doses. Â (runs screaming through the house)

### PaulaRichards — 2012-12-01T13:07:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66031>

Geez Darleen I wish I had words of wisdom for ya but with my history there is no rhyme nor reason for the numbers on the scale sometimes!Â  Do you seriously add 1/2 cup of butter to your coffee?Â  You must have a bubba mug!Â  I certainly am not a fat phobic and some dinners lately consist almost entirely of fat with some veggies.Â  I discovered I like asparagus (please don't tell me there is anything wrong with it lol!) and learned to make hollandaise sauce, yummy!Â  Not only am I drinking HWC with my bacon & eggs in the mornings, I'm covering it with hollandaise sauce...yup...no fear of fat here.Â 

Maybe this has been discussed somewhere on the forum but is there an opinion on measuring blood ketones?Â  I've been keeping up with Jimmy Moore's latest n=1 and seems it's been working for him.Â  I've always assumed I've been in ketosis when I eat like this but I ordered a blood ketone meter and some strips just to be sure.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T14:32:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66040>

WHOA! Â You actually drink bacon fat? Â WOW color me impressed. Â I actually had just increased the butter intake because of another thread. Â My mug holds about 1.75 cups. Â It's a "Stitch" mug done up a la Warhol. Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Â  I LOVE Stitch and one year for xmas DH sent his sister to their local Disney store and she bought everything she could find that was Stitch-related. I have baby Stitches and his fellow critters all along the top of my computer desk, big Stitch with the ray guns peeking out of my CD basket, fuzzy blue Stitch looking at me from a cubby, a Stitch water globe that lights up and beeps and my Stitch mug. Â He sorta took over Cinderella's place in my chlid's heart. Â And yes, I have been gifted with a couple of Cinderella water globes as well along with the honkin' big Fantasia water globe. Â I'm spoiled. Â Yes I am.

I've been thinking all morning about this and trying to figure out what I've done that's different. Â The answer is ... wine. Â The last couple of weeks I have indulged in a glass or two a couple of times a week. Â So I guess that fantastic bottle of Wild Horse pinot noir I just cracked open is going to have to just sit there for a bit while I give up the grape to see if that was the problem.

I've also not really been working at upping the cytomel. Â I'm still only at half the daily dose of 25mcg. Â After those couple of days of going bonkers at the full dose I got a little scared of it. Â And even though I did take that 2nd dose of d-ribose yesterday I was a little trembly this morning. Â Don't like that either.

I wouldn't have gotten on the scale at all this morning but it actually feels as if I've dropped a few pounds. My core is feeling rock hard these days (when I can find it through the flab). Â I decided that CT must be doing something besides making me shiver for hours even at this early stage (because I just started actually getting in the tub just a couple of weeks ago). Â I've been determined about it and have been doing 30 minutes on my back with 10-15 seated for my legs. Â Small tub which I thought was bigger but (shrug). Â First week I was in the tub every single day. Â Now I'm doing every other day.

sigh

Â 

I try not to get impatient. Â I try to tell myself I have lots of stuff to fix. Â Some days the problem seems insurmountable. Â Especially when I start looking through clothing catalogs. Â I get tired of living in stretchy pants and big t-shirts. Â bleh.

Â 

Ok. Â Pity party's over.

I'm with Kathylu on this. Â Take it back ... somethings rotten.

Just had to share. Â My (now retired) chiropractor, for whom I play computer guru, just called to sing "happy birthday" to me. Â Just cracked me up! I have definitely been there Darleen - It is so frustrating to be trying so hard and your weight is going up instead of down. Â I am finding more and more that my system is more sensitive I think. Â I for sure can't eat bacon - salt, nitrates? Â This last week I have discovered that anything from a can really bothers me - bloating etc. and my weight goes up by 1 Kilo. Â So I have a cupboard full of sardines, salmon etc that I will have to leave for now. Â I tried a little bit of stilton cheese the other day and I was up 1 and a half Kilos - that is craziness. Â  I do so much better if I eat lightly and mostly raw and lots of fat. Â I also think I have food sensitivities that I didn't know I had. Â I use to love eggs and ate them all the time but they are a problem now too. Â I get severe reactions in restaurants now too - MSG. Â That never used to happen either. Â I don't know what has changed but I feel so much better now. Â  Don't know if anything here helps but I guess if things aren't working you have to look at all the possibilities. Â I feel better too when I somewhat avoid salt - I seem to be highly sensitive - although everyone says you need salt in your diet. Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-01T17:40:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66062>

so there I am, ranting away to DH about my weight and when I finish up go to the kitchen to find a dozen red roses and ... wait for it ... a small box of chocolates. Â LOL -- I shouldn't because his heart was, as usual, in the right place. Â I had one bite of one piece and had to put it down because it was so sweet it made my mouth hurt.

Â 

Which brings me to the reason for THIS post. Â I came up with a new recipe for coco crack which I've been mulling over for weeks now. Â I nuked 1/2 jar or so of Artisana coconut butter just enough to soften it up. Â dumped it in a bowl with 2 squares of unsweetened Ghiradelli chocolate (the really thin bar), about 1/4 cup of 60% semi-sweet chocolate chips and nuked it for another minute. Â Stirred it up real good. Â Let it sit a bit then tested it. Â WOOO too bitter, too dark. Â Added half a dozen packets of splenda (next time will use pure sucralose to avoid the dextrose) and covered it up. Â Fast forward to this afternoon. It's now firm again so I dug out a spoonful. Â OM. Â I mean, seriously OMG. Â Is it good! Â I was afraid it would be too grainy-textured but it isn't. Â It is just plain flat out yummy. Â Pardon me while I go back for a 3rd spoonful.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T13:25:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66144>

I don't think I could do that (drink bacon fat). Â Just thinking about it upsets my tummy. Â I guess that pretty much means I am not a fat burner. Â yet. Â I love fat but I can only do so much.

DH took me to dinner last night at the Rib & Chop House. Â We went early (5:30) thinking we'd get there before the dinner crowd. ahahahahaha boy were we surprised. Â The parking lot was full there and there weren't any tables available so we sat at the bar. Which was OK. Â The food was just as good. Â 

We had mussels to start and I had the Dover sole with a Louisiana shrimp stuffing (no bread in it) and a lovely sauce, and squash casserole. Â I only had a few bites of the squash. Â It was good but I was more interested in the fish. Â Had a B&B for dessert and this morning discovered why I should not do alcohol. Â My FBG was 130. Â sigh. Â Of course I also took 2 - 1/2 tab doses of cytomel yesterday as well.

CT this morning in about 20 minutes. Â Just finished breakfast. Â Yeah, slept until just after 9. Â BUT I also had trouble falling asleep AGAIN. Â I'm starting to think I need to be in bed by 9. Â I was yawning and nodding off about 8 and could barely keep my eyes open. Â Lights out at 10:28 and then lay there for an hour before dropping off. Â THEN woke at about 3:30 and lay there for about another hour or so before dropping off again.

Haven't a clue what I'm fixing for dinner. Â I may do canned salmon later. Â Might have to do baked pork chops since I have some left from the last Costco visit.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T19:00:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66172>

I've pretty much demolished half of the coco crack fudge stuff I Â made. Â It's hard to stay out of it! LOL. Â Made it pretty tough to eat that canned salmon for lunch. Â I had to force the last 4 bites down my throat. Â 

Â 

Just realized why I'm sort of in the dog house. Â We are supposed to be headed to Disney World. Â I got "yelled" at a week ago because I never made the reservations and it got kind of tense last night at dinner because it came up again.

I don't know how to tell him I can't bring myself to go because I've gained back almost all the weight I lost 5 years ago. Â My ass is the size of Montana for cripes sake. Â I know I know the bulk (and I do not use that term lightly) of folks who wander DW are so fat that a lot of them rent scooters to get around. Â That's why DW has invested in kneeling buses. Â I'm not THAT fat but I'm still pretty much, well, ashamed, to be seen out in public. Â Which is why I've started hiding again.

So in an attempt to mollify him I said maybe we could do a Viking river cruise next May. Â Maybe by then I'll be losing some of the flab. Â And we''ll do DW NEXT December. Â He doesn't want to go any other time because he thinks that between turkey day and the middle of Dec is the slowest time and the weather is the best. Â He seems to forget it was pretty crowded in '07 when we went and it was so hot the natives were all whining. Â Whatever.

Â 

I was thinking today about starting my "Y" membership again. Â We have a fantastic "Y" here ... they received a lot of money over the years from Whitney Benefits and the facilities are amazing. Â I know I should be out walking in the cold but (a) I worry about running into critters, specifically a mountain lion and (b) the ice and mud. Â right now we have a bit of both and the last thing I need to do is take a flying header on the ice. Â So I was thinking I could use the treadmill at the "Y" because I'm starting to feel the need to get off my fat butt and move around some. Â Of course, it IS December and they close for 10 days? at the end of every year to do major maintenance. Â But there's still 3 full weeks I could get in. Â That's a start. Â And it would MAKE me do it instead of thinking, oh I can go out tomorrow. Â We'll see.

Â 

ok

/whining

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-02T23:38:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66199>

Um. Â Caroline, mountain lions are not adorable. Â They are major predators with sharp claws and fangs and yes they WILL take a rather large bite out of you. Â There were several people killed near Boulder, CO a few years back by cougars. Â Although they are normally shy of humans I'd rather be armed.

and yes, we figured he was maybe a year old and mom had given him the boot. I felt really bad when he got shot but also relieved. Â This may be wild 'n wooly Wyoming but we live in a valley with a pretty good sized human population with kids and pets. Â Better the cat than a kid.

Krista, I figure walking on the treadmill at a moderate pace is probably a good way to start. Â Then if things improve, in January I can start up with my 30 minute/once a week, heavy weight lifting program. Â But I'm not starting it until I see some positive movement.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-03T14:36:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66238>

Where to start.

Last night's dinner was a big ol' steak which I cooked 'rare.' Â It was flat out raw in the middle and I relished every bite. Â It was a good 8-10 ounces and that was all I ate. Â Had dinner around 6:30.

Early to bed (9:30) and light's out at 10. Â Almost dropped off when hubby and the cats came in and woke me up. Â From there it was toss and turn for several hours because the wind was blowing like crazy and for about the 3rd night in a row I was HOT. Â Not like a hot flash. Â this was more like my skin was running a fever. Â I side sleep and every part of me that was touching the pillow or the bed just fired up something awful. Â I kept tossing and turning to cool off, finally kicked off everything but the sheet and was STILL uncomfortable.

I finally fell asleep but guess who woke me up around 4:45. Â Dropped back off and didn't wake again until nearly 10. Â Checked my FBG to find it was 222.

so my question is, is it the cytomel? Â Is my metabolism revving up too much during the night? Â Is it the CT? Â What the heck is going on? Â I'm hesitant to stop the ctyomel because I want the RT3 cleared and the sooner the better. Â But I'm not going to continue if it's going to shove my BG through the roof.

Other than that there's nothing new or exciting to report. Â Either hubby has been sneaking "my" coco crack 'fudge' or I've been eating more of it than I thought because it's nearly gone. Â I am really working on putting more fat in my diet. Â Not ready to drink bacon fat though. Â Maybe some cream ... I could do that.Â

Oh... I get that HOT Thing... ughhh Â Its like your thyroid has turned on...but the rest of the body isn't quite listening... Best two analogies I've got for this... its like you flip a switch, but the relay doesn't connect properly giving off all this crazy heat. Â Â 

the other is the conductor to the orchestra is tapping away at the podium trying to get the attention of the orchestra - but they're not listening ...just making controlled chaos but even then they're making music even if it isn't all that good.

here's a better analogy.... its like stoking a steam engine... you keep feeding it until it actually catches....Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-04T13:32:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66300>

I guess my pilot light is still sputtering. Â I didn't CT yesterday but I still warmed up a bit when I went to bed. Â Not nearly as hot as the night before. Â I'm about ready to go run the cold water so am curious how tonight will be.

Finally got to see "The Life of Pi" and, as usual, they put all the really best stuff in the trailers. Â Which isn't to say the entire film wasn't 2 hours of eye candy ... because it definitely was. Â The movie paralleled the book very well. Â It was most enjoyable. Â It was nice to be reminded I'm not the only one with a hidden tiger lurking inside.Â 

Took a friend and she bought the popcorn but -- major news flash -- I ate about a 1/3 of it and tossed the rest (it was a small bag ... and here we still have small, med and large not large, larger and OMG-that's-enough-to-feed-10-people-HUGE. Â I simply didn't want it. Â Let me repeat that. Â I. Â Didn't. Â Want. Â It. Â Color me amazed.

Remembering how sky high Â my FBG was yesterday I had 1/4 of a leftover sweet potato (about 1/3 cup as measurements go) with dinner (which was a hefty helping of Costco pulled pork) so this A.M. FBG was 107. Â I'm still not sure I completely understand why going NO-carb causes FBG to skyrocket. Â Anybody have any ideas? Â And I did go to a blog post someone mentioned in another thread. Â Still don't get it. Â Does this mean I am definitely LR (after all this time?) or my metabolism was so fired up it drained my liver of glycogen stores? Â Color me confused but curious.

Well it's now time to go fill the tub. Â oh joy.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-04T18:59:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66330>

I have decided to just pretty much stick to writing in here. Â The forum seems to have died since the big switchover. Â I keep hoping people will find their way back but it just isn't happening. Â It's a real pity. Â I miss all the chit chat etc.

Oh well.

Just got back from the cat food expedition. Â Our shopping choices here are pretty limited. Â Wally world, Kmart and a couple of big grocery chains. Â I hate spending money at WW but what're you gonna do? Â They have the best prices in town on the cat food my spoiled little darlings will eat so hi ho off we go.

And just let me add that at this time of year I HATE HATE HATE HATE going there. Â It was ridiculously crowded for mid-afternoon on Tuesday. Â Stupidly so. Â And now the junk season is in full swing. Â I swear I want to puke having to weave my way through the piles of crap they bring in. Â And then there are the shoppers. Â You've been to "people of walmart.com" I trust. Â Well, we got 'em here. Â And I swear everyone in the store looks as unhealthy as can be. Â One woman was on her way in as I was circling the lot looking for a parking space. Â I wanted to weep. Â She was beyond enormous and I don't know how she was even walking.

Oops, almost lost where I was going with this.

Once upon a time I used to loved December. Â I'd put up the tree, decorate like a mad-woman, sing holiday songs at the top of my lungs, dance around the house. Â Now I have become Scroogette. Â The commercialization really began to get to me 20 years ago. Â That was the year we donated money in everyone's names to charity. No gifts that year. Â I sort of came out of it for a while but the annual crap merchandising really wears on me plus the fact I HATE HATE HATE the fact that wallyworld insists on playing religious music while I'm shopping. Â If I want that I will go to church (and that would be a cold day indeed given I finally tossed religion out the window and admitted to my atheistic bent). Â 

So I grind my teeth as I blast around picking up the stuff I cannot get elsewhere and hope for the best. Â But the holiday feeling is gone. Â Two years ago I threatened to start celebrating Saturnalia -- at least THAT holiday isn't worn into the dirt by the merchants.

/rant

Â 

OK I posted a dream I had over on another thread with a challenge/request for people to give me their interpretations. Â One person sort of bit so I can only surmise nobody cared. Â But since I happen to believe that an unexamined life is NOT worth living I'm gonna interpret it here (and yes I know I said it was intensely personal but not in the way people might have thought.

Here's the dream:

> _A tall, blonde, nicely built man was smiling at me withÂ thatÂ twinkle in his eye. Â He approached me as if to kiss me but instead, just the tip of his tongue grazed the tip of MY tongue. Â Then he stepped back, smiled and went back to talking with another man. Â They were sitting on a rooftop, smiling and laughing. Â He kept glancing my way, though.
>
> _Then I was in a room trying to corral a couple of unruly kids. Â There was a large window overlooking the rooftop and I could see the blonde man sitting there still talking to the other man. Â He saw me looking at him and smiled. Â There was a door next to the window and it had come unlatched. Â I reached to open it and saw a screen door behind it and then a few steps leading up to the rooftop. Â As I reached to open the door â€¦._
>
> Â_ 

First of all, I hold to the belief that every element in a dream is a representation of the dreamer. Â The tongue thing? Â Represents verbal communication. Â The tall blonde guy? Â That's my male self. Â He's tall and blond and nicely built because I like that sort of thingÂ 

Â (Leonardo DeCaprio, either of the Hemsworth brothers but esp the one who played "Thor," Chis Evans, Brad Pitt -- the list is endless). Â 

ANYway. Â the twinkle in his eye? Â that's the challenge I keep posing for myself to put myself out there, to join in the fun, to stop hiding! Â The unruly kids? Â Me again. Â Undisciplined. Â Unrestrained. Â And I'm always trying to put on the ladlylike face and to hide the real me. Â That's the room with the big window which I forgot to mention was dirty ... like looking through a dirty aquarium. Â The door to that other self is not locked. Â All I had to do was push open the screen door, climb the steps and join in the conversation.

Hesitation comes from the part of me that lives in fear. Â Fear of being found out, fear of being reprimanded, punished, humiliated. Â 

The tigress paces in the background, waiting to be set free. Â The guardian keeps a close watch on the door. Â I'm caught between.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-05T13:15:02+0200

Up late again this morning because I woke up about 1:30 and couldn't get back to sleep for a while. Â I thought I had this little issue all ironed out but evidently not.

Only ate 2 meals yesterday ... not enough hours in the day(light) to get in any more. Â Fixed a quick ham salad for dinner and enjoyed a glass of that YUMMY Wild Horse Pinot Noir 2004 Central Coast. If you can find it ... it's very very tasty.

Planning on fixing boneless country style pork "ribs" for dinner with a nice helping of sauerkraut and maybe some pureed cauli.

CT was good yesterday and I didn't get superheated last night when I went to sleep. Â Warmed up a bit yes, but not that uncomfortable HOT I experience the other night. Â So that's a plus.

It's currently about 62 in here and I'm running around in my house coat and barefooted while hubby has just put on his vest although he hasn't yet started whining about being cold.Â 

time for a shower. Â Looks like it's going to be a boring, overcast day. Â Oh except it IS Wednesday which is drive hubby to the Legion for his weekly tÃªte-Ã -tÃªteÂ with the guys and dodge all the deer going and coming. Â That's plenty of excitement for today I guess.

The only thing I worry about when wandering around the house sans clothing is somebody coming to the door. Â Our house is a bit of a fishbowl -- good thing we live out in the country surrounded by ... country cause I wander around nekkid quite a bit. Â Wouldn't do to terrify the Schwan's driver or the UPS guy, now would it. Â I'll stick to running bare through the halls of the forum during the day. Â Less chance of causing someone to lose their last meal.

As to grounding, well my feet have been cramping again which is a sign they are really cold. Â Last night I had to put some footies on when I went to bed because they were so cold. Â So it's pretty unlikely I will be doing much barefootin' outside. Â Grounding will have to be done inside during meditation.

Don't have much to relate this morning. Â Am supposed to do CT but scheduled a haircut for 11 (duh) and just got up (lazy bones ... can't get up when the sun doesn't show up until about now either. Â We live behind a row of hills so the sun, even though it's LIGHT out, doesn't show up until about 8:30-9:00 this time of year. Â I'm taking full advantage of it. Â Might get the CT in later, we'll see.

One perplexing issue has cropped up. Â I broke out in a small cluster of ugly, red, itchy spots on my chest about 2 weeks ago. There were 5 of 'em ... yes large enough to count them. Â This morning there are a few more tiny ones. Â They are really starting to itch! Â Guess it's time to call the doc's office. Â I was thinking maybe it's one of the creams I've been using. Â Whatever the cause I wish they'd go away.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-07T14:11:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66697>

Back again. Â Breakfast was inspired by Krista (I think) ... I chopped up 3 slices of thick bacon, sauteed them and then added in a tiny bit of minced onion. Â when the bacon was nice and crispy I added in a drained can of salmon. Â It was YUMMY. Â I also threw a few leftover shrimp on the plate and dumped 2oz of butter into my mug of decaf. Â 

You know, when the gas tank is full, trying to add more gas only dumps it on the ground. Â I could NOT finish it. Â could. Â not. Â Ate all the shrimp, am still working on the coffee but about 1/2 cup of the salmon/bacon went into the fridge for later.

Braved the scale this morning just for the hell of it and, no surprise, that huge jump the other day is long gone.

Cytomel ... day before yesterday I had a crash just before bedtime. Â It was weird. Â I had been in a GREAT mood all day long and it just evaporated. Â I think I must have run through all the T3. Â 

And today I realized that's why I used to get such a great feeling from drinking a mug of regular coffee. Â That caffeine high I would get did more than just wake me up. Â Since I'm back on full dose of 25mcg of the cytomel I'm feeling, not exactly euphoric, but just plain happy. Â I'm not grumpy or out of sorts and I have energy to do more than just sit in front of the computer all day. Â AAMOF I don't WANT to sit here at all. Â That's a real positive sign.

Random thoughts ... I know NO one on this forum will believe what I am about to say. Â But it is the truth. Â I am a very VERY private person. Â The fact I have been able to come in here and literally bare my heart and soul is astounding to me. Â It has not been easy. Â Giving anyone access to any information to my true self is like handing Jack the Ripper a scalpel and lying back on a bed and telling him to enjoy himself. Â It is terrifying like you cannot believe, painful in the extreme and something I simply cannot do out here on the other side of the computer screen.

Giving anyone access to the deepest parts of my personal being leaves the door wide open for them to hurt me, humiliate me, abuse me ... you name it. Â This is how it has been for me my whole life. Â Share a secret, get my throat cut while the person hurting me stands there and laughs at me.

so lest you think I am some freewheeling, let it all hang out, anything goes kinda person ... know that is actually the antithesis of who I really am.

And if I can do it ... why can't you?

Â  And yes, I took the pic this morning ... it snowed last night while we were sleeping. Â See all the deer tracks? Â they just big mice as far as I'm concerned.

Darleen - can't wait to see the haircut - I loved that movie. Â I took my grandsons and we had so much fun!

It is amazing Â - the things we are talking about - never would have dreamt that in a million years. Â Stuff that is kept inside is so hurtful/painful and kind of festers and does so much damage. Â Letting it out is very liberating and healing and I think - maybe - leaves room for joy. Â I also think that is why I want to go to Disney World now Â - there is all this inner joy and I want to be a kid again and let it all out!

Â guess we still have a long way to go - more secrets to tell - but we are doing a pretty good job. Â Like JK said - if we don't talk about it - he can't help us.

I have this amazing feeling of light - I don't really get where that is coming from.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-08T18:45:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66865>

Figured I'd better check in before the day is over with.

Last night was the annual UTU xmas party which I thought was NEXT weekend. Â And of course it freakin' snowed yesterday so we had the usual icy walk to get in the stupid building. Â They've been holding it at the Eagles out in the Commercial Park so the "street" is sorta paved. Â Add in a little snowfall and trucks pounding over it all day long and you have a skating rink. Â Last year I made the mistake of wearing heeled boots and almost didn't make it. Â 

I had wanted to wear my new twinkle toe shoes but since they are brand new had to wear my old(er) black flats. Â Nutz. Â  AND the top I had bought specifically to wear to the party was too low cut and those stupid red blotchy bumps are still there on my chest so ... oh well. Â And (almost done whining) my HAIR ... I had to wash it and discovered that my mousse wasn't enough to get it to spike like it should so it sorta just fell down and lay there limp as a cooked noodle. (HA thought I was gonna say something else, didn't ya!)

Fortunately I had the good sense to eat prior because the food is always atrocious. Lots of bread, overcooked beef, overcooked pork, mystery meat with some kinda barbecue sauce with probably an atrocious amount of sugar in it, pasta salad (getting the picture?) and two kinds of over sugared cake. Â shudder. Â Just cause it's "free" doesn't mean I have to eat it. Â Everyone had a fit because I wouldn't eat. Â I kept telling them I had had a meal earlier. Â sigh

Had a friend snap a pic of me and the hubby in front of the tree. Â Still haven't found where she left them on FB. Â IF her camera did it right (make me look 20 years younger and 50 pounds lighter) I'll post it.Â  { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

No more than fell asleep last night that DH woke me up because Sammy (the cat) was having a seizure. Â Why thank you dear. Â He's seizing, I can't do any more than you could which is pet him and try to calm him down but you HAD to wake me? Yes I love you. Â Good thing there aren't any heavy objects in the bedroom.

I lay there for at least 2 hours and finally FINALLY fell asleep again only to be awakened by a THUMP and thrashing sounds from the other bedroom. Â This time the seizure went full bore and I had to stay with the poor guy for about 5-10 minutes until his poor little brain reset itself and he was able to get up and walk. Â ANNNNNND I lay there for another 2 hours (until around 5:30) before I dozed off.

ANNNNND woke up about 7:30 because someone was running the VitaMix to make BPC. Â I'll give you a hint. Â It was not one of the cats. Â Since he had arranged with out of town friends to meet for breakfast before they began the trek back to Billings I hadda get up. Â good god.

I managed to make myself presentable and appear semi-lucid through breakfast. Â Forgot to take my cytomel before we left for town so was pretty well zombified. Â got home and tried to take a nap (stupid autocorrect!). Â No go. Â 2 hours and I finally gave up.

Am now listening to Mannheim Steamroller Christmas album and feeling tired but a bit festive.

Just had the last of the pork and sauerkraut so probably just some sort of light snack in lieu of dinner if I feel hungry.

And that's all I know.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-09T15:52:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-66944>

Slept really well until about 4 a.m. ... crap on toast. Â Finally gave in and gave up and GOT up about 6-ish. Â Haven't been up that early in a couple of months. Â AND it was -2 out in the great outdoors. Â 61 inside. Â holy mother of pearl.

Could have just wandered out in the all-together and parked my bare backside on the deck for and hour and called it CT. Â Could have. Â Didn't.

BAB was the usual. Â along about 9:30 just as DH was rousting himself out of bed I decided it was time to go BACK. Â slept until nearly noon when he woke me talking on the speakerphone. Â DAMN WILL YOU GET YOUR HEARING CHECKED ALREADY?! Â I swear to god I am going to trade him in for a couple of 35-year olds.

Missed CT yesterday and had thought I'd do it this morning but after the second sleep decided no way -- I needed to shower so made it a cold one. Â Here's an interesting observation (which I'm sure all the rest of you already noticed) but when the cold (and our tap water is COLD!) hit my back it didn't feel cold at all. Â Silly little cold receptors musta seen it coming and went into hibernation mode.

Second observation ... the girls are getting out of control. Â Yes, my cups suddenly runneth over. Â since they are already too big in my inestimable opinion, I'm just a wee bit disconcerted over this development. Â On a scale of you-just-need-a-bandaid to Dolly-Parton-frankenboobs they fall more towards the latter but not THAT big. Â I'm thinking (always a dangerous activity when I've not had enough sleep) it must be all those fat cells releasing stored estrogen. Â I'm taking DIM and thinking I may need to take a little more. Â Also silymarin so my liver oughtta be clearing things out properly. Â anyway, thank god for the genie bra cause it s-t-r-e-t-c-h-e-s.Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-10T12:43:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67006>

I dunno ... Â I used to be on the band-aid end as well. Â You're too young to remember the "pencil test." Â this was in the 70's ... you put a pencil under your breast, if it fell away you didn't need a bra. Â LOL. Â The only way I could have kept one in place then was if I used double stick tape. Â Then I gained weight and went from a AAA cup to a B to a C to a D. Â When I lost those 50 pounds starving myself on that stupid jenny-diet I was headed back into a B.

BUT I also know that the girls got WAY outta control from being estrogen dominant. Â Once I had the parts yanked and got onto just pitiful OTC progesterone cream I dropped nearly a full cup size. Â Which is why I'm wondering if I'm dumping stored estrogen. Â They're kinda getting in the way again.

I'm not really complaining too much though. Â Prior to getting on the BHRT they were pretty sad looking. Â Ick. Â Old lady boobs. Nothing like a boost to your hormones to get everybody awake again.

OK enough boob talk.

Here's something ELSE I've noticed of late that I keep forgetting to mention. Â My posture over the years has gotten really really BAD. Â You know that old lady slumping thing? Â My chiro told me it was common to women because of the weight of our breasts (thanks mother nature). Â I was having a lot neck pain and he told me I was hunching my shoulders. Â Straighten up, pull your elbows back and down and the pain stops. Â Who'da thunk.

Well, lately I find myself standing up really straight and tall with no effort or thought. Â It just is how I stand and walk. Â aMAzing. Â Who knows what's next. Â I'm feeling the need to dance so maybe I'll dust off that unopened set of ZUMBA dvd's.

CT this morning. Â Just about time to go fill the tub. Â It's overcast this morning, spitting snow, and about 33Â°. Â Winter has officially descended. Â Bother.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-10T17:32:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67039>

CT was brutal this morning. Don't know why. Â My legs ached the whole 15 minutes. Â On my back wasn't much better. Â Water temp was still reading 48 so it's not gotten any colder. Â Oh well.

Just started to warm up and had to go to town. Â It was around 30Â° and the wind has been blowing for what seems like days so wind chill is anyone's guess. Â I was wearing a turtleneck, my vest (not down, either), slacks and my Uggs. Â Oh and my gloves because my hands get so cold. Â My teeth chattered and I shook all the way to town as I cursed Jack and his ancestors all the way back to **10\[sup\]12\[/sup\]**.

Fixing clam chowdah for dinner. Â Seems like the right weather for it. Â Lots of chopped clams and half and half. Â I'll have to throw in a couple of potatoes for hubby. Â 

speaking of whom. Â I meant to share his last commentary about my eating habits. Â It was the first day I fixed bacon and salmon for breakfast and couldn't finish it. Â Later he asked what was in the little container. Â I told him and also told him why I couldn't quite finish it. Â His comment? Â "You're going to gain more weight!"

Really?

Well how do you explain the fact I've been eating like this for over a year (any small weight gain I attribute to the stupid RT3 and lack of real CT) and I'm NOT GAINING? Hmmm?

In spite of all my attempts to indoctrinate him (aka educate him) he still clings to the idiotic notion that fat will make you fat.

At the xmas party I tried to explain to a friend (who has gotten such a pot belly in the last few years he could easily play Santa without any padding) that bacon is your friend, sugar and grains will kill you. Â I left it at that because the look on his face told me he thought I was out of my mind.

so much for being a starfish.

Which brings me to today's question. Â How the heck do you know if you've become a fat burner? Â Is it the ketosis thing? Â Cause I don't think I'm there yet. Â Every time I skip or skimp on the carbs my FBG goes up. Â WTF?

Woke up last night about 1AM and lay there for a couple of hours. Â This time it was my sinuses. Â They had dried out to the point I could hardly swallow or breathe. Â Damn winter. Â I'm getting a little tired of this bs. Â I thought I was well past it.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-12T18:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67267>

Went to starbucks with a friend and we talked each others ears off ... 3 hours worth, LOL. Â Of course, part of it could be I forgot to specify decaf so was quite wired by the time I finished the Venti SF latte I'd ordered. Â And boy I was some kind of hungry by the time I got home. Only other thing of note is I discovered how the Genius thingie works on my iPod! Â I forget how much really good music I have and this is helping me rediscover it. Â Yesterdays song of the day: Â Bon Jovi, Lay your hand on me. Â rock it, boys!

Might go see FLIGHT this afternoon. Â Depends on hubby's mood.

Got a little bit of xmas shopping done. Â Debating about searching for the tree stand for my (old) aluminum tree. Â I LOVE those things! Â DH's SIL sent me a pair of "wood look" penguins for my bday gift. Â She's been at Costco again! LOL. Â they are adorable.

SO? Â Where is it? Â (what? Â You think I"m impatient? argh. Â No I'm not {shakes head emphatically} Â Not me. Â nuh-uh)

(puts away large, pointy stick)

Â 

Talked to my sister this morning. Â The good news is her "lump" is a result of multiple incisions along the same area. Â She finally had a doc explain it to her instead of just blowing her off. Â She did a lot of searching on WebMD and has finally relaxed about it BUT -- her weight, it seems, is making it worse so now she's talking about losing weight.

Want to take a guess what the next words were out of her mouth. Â G'wan. Â Guess.

ahem. Â "After looking at the fat content** in ground beef and turkey" (I'm paraphrasing here) she thinks she should get a grinder and do her own.

{insert sound of my head banging on my keyboard AGAIN}

Yeah, count your calories, limit your fat and you'll lose weight. Â unhuh.

She's also been "on a sugar binge" the last two weeks. Â sigh. Â AGAIN ... (paraphrasing) she thinks she's "trying to destroy her body" because she "hates" it because of all the abuse. Â But hey ... she's forgiven the evil monsters for what they did. Â yup. Â you betcha.

I JUST had this conversation with my friend yesterday (her childhood was another horror show). Â Maybe I'm explaining my position all wrong because they do. not. get. it.

Here's what I think. Â First off, in continually claiming she's subconsciously trying to destroy her body my sister is continuing to victimize herself (yes I mentioned the dopamine blog). Â claiming they have each "forgiven" their abusers both my friend and my sister set themselves up to continue to victimize themselves. Â It is also another version of the holier-than-thou-I'm-so-religious-you're-not set that it makes my teeth hurt. Â If you've forgiven them WHY THE HELL ARE YOU STILL TALKING ABOUT IT?

Forgiveness is such a loaded word. Â IMNSHO the ONLY person who can forgive you is ... YOU. Â True forgiveness does not come from the victim but the abuser who, once they face and accept what they did (and do it honestly) will be hard pressed to forgive themselves. Â But that is the only place true forgiveness can come from.

OK that probably doesn't make sense either.

Saying you "forgive" your abuser is a bandaid. Â It means nothing. Â Except that you are deluding yourself. Â Everything you do in Â life is a choice. Â YOUR choice. Â I wish I could get her to understand that.

OH well. Â I've urged her yet again to get over here and read the Leptin Rx quick start guide and the CT guide. Â You can lead a horse to water but you sure as HELL cannot make them drink.

Enough.

We had scrambled leftovers for dinner last night. Â I was lazy. Â Diced some ham, tossed in the last of the smoked pulled pork from Costco, diced some onion and red pepper and threw in some eggs. Â topped the whole thing with a bit of colby jack and part of an avocado that was languishing on the counter. Â Was actually pretty tasty.

BAB was bacon and onion and salmon. Â All but licked the plate, LOL. Â Seems I have not been eating nearly as much fat as I thought I was. Â mmmm mmmmm good.

Tossed a pot roast into the crock pot since DH and friends have moved their Wed. night bull session to thursday. Â Guess the dart teams got to be too much for them.

Up really REALLY late this morning ... only woke up once during the nightÂ 

Â last night and would probably still be in bed if sister hadn't called. Â Yes, thoughtful hubby brought me the phone and woke me up. Â Bless his pointed little head. Â Anyway, I'm still not showered or dressed so probably oughtta get off the 'puter and into the shower.

TTFN

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67697>

goood morning! Â Feeling great this morning except for that damned rash thingy on my chest. Â Still there. Â Still itching. Â Going to the PA this afternoon and have HER look at it.

Decided to switch back to eggs for breakfast since I'm almost out of canned salmon and not looking like any trips to Costco in the near future so ... Â Cooked up the bacon in the skillet, the better to keep all the baconyfatty goodness for the eggs. Â Slapped on some commercial guac I found hiding in the back of the freezer the other day, added the usual HUMONGOUS hunka' butter to my decaf and I'm good to go for most of the rest of the day.

FBG was only 85 this morning where it's been around 105 - 110 recently. Â Thought it might be because I had some extra carbs last night and then remembered I'd added 1/2 tsp of cinnamon to my morning coffee yesterday. Â But would the effects last that long? Â dunno. Â Seems the fewer carbs I eat the higher my FBG goes. Â I don't get that.

Today is also massage day. Â THANK YOU! Â My upper back is tied up in knots from sleeping with so few covers. Â Since I eschew bed clothes (why the heck you would wear CLOTHES to bed is beyond me!) my bare shoulders get really chilled by early morning and the muscles tend to cramp up. Â So once a month I get to see my massage therapist and SHE gets to take a sledge hammer to them and try to get them to loosen up.Â 

You would think that all that lying about in near-freezing water would make them immune to cold air but, alas, not yet it seems.

WHOA thought I just lost this post because of my sometimes-you-irritate-the-\*\*\*\*-out-of-me Mighty Mouse. Â It works with finger swipes across the surface and sometimes just the slightest movement of my finger makes it go back a page when I don't want it to. Â whew.

Accomplished about NOTHING this weekend and the workload is piling up. Â Well, as much as a workload can when you're mostly retired. Â Although I'm feeling perky I'm also feeling low energy again today. Â Sure wish this would clear up.

oh well.

TTFN!

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-18T11:54:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67815>

Just got up ... need to go fix breakfast but wanted to check in and see if anything new had developed. Â 

Am very VERY unhappy with hubby. Â He was watching tv in the bedroom last night with the door closed. Â I went in to get Missy so I could feed her before I went to bed. Â When I opened the door this BLAST of hot air smacked me in the face. Â He'd turned up the little heater to MAX WARP and it had to have been 85 in there. Â It's not that big of a room ... about 13 x 14 ... so it doesn't take much to overheat it. I have asked him before not to do that because I cannot sleep when the room is that hot.

sigh

so I grabbed my pillow, kindle, reading glasses and sleeping mask and went and slept in the guest room. Â I have always thought THAT room was really cold but last night it was perfect. Â Must be the CT. Â 

Speaking of which, yesterday I had to go to town and left wearing a VERY light, long sleeved cotton t shirt. Â No socks, just my summer canvas slip-ons. Â I didn't even feel the cold (it was 30Â°) until I got to town and even then it was just noticeable. Â Must be the CT.Â 

Today is my "little" brother's 59th birthday. Â Assuming he's still alive. Â I forgot to ask my sister if she'd heard from him recently. Â He and I haven't spoken for 2 years since he decided everything that was wrong in his life was my fault. Â sigh

Well now I'm depressed so guess I'd better go eat and then jump in the tub. Â Today is CT day. Â Wonder if the water temp has fallen any further. Â Two days ago it was a bone chilling 46 ...Â

### vkiernan — 2012-12-18T23:14:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67906>

**DarleenMB said**

I know it's not my fault Vickie. Â I don't care what he blames me for. Â That's irrelevant. Â It's just he's my brother and he's literally killing himself if he hasn't already succeeded. Â Kind of hard to let go of that little fact.Â 

Â 

I hear ya although I don't know what your brother is actually doing but, we, older sisters continue to worry even when they are adults.Â  Must be the mother in us.Â 

I have a whole family of bro's and sis's who are killing themselves.Â  Probably not quite like yours but none-the-less still doing everything they shouldn't be.Â  My one brother has a drinking problem and a very large belly to show for it; he also smokes.Â  He also has extreme inflammation and takes meds for it.Â  He told me he knows he shouldn't eat grains as they affect him but continues to anyway.Â  His fingernails look dead and disgusting and all he does is take drugs for it and his inflammation.Â  Another sis is a nurse who now has diabetes and takes premarin for her hot flashes.Â  Tell me she doesn't know better.Â  I am done teaching and if they ask, fine; otherwise, it is about me.Â  I am not angry at them anymore for not trying.Â  It is who they are and they have to make the change.Â Â  I have another brother who belittles me cuz I talk about all of this to anyone who will listen.Â  Unfortunately, if he is in the room, he makes a big deal about it even if I am not talking to him.Â  Go figure!Â  Some people are interested!!!

I sure hope you little brother wises up soon and realizes that big sisters usually know way more than they!

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-19T12:21:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-67960>

Vickie thanks for sharing. Â boy am I glad I only have one of each. Â My brother has been drinking himself to death for sometime. Â 2 years ago my sister and I went to his house which was left to him by our late evil aunt/adopted "mother." Â I use that word loosely because a rattlesnake has more mothering instincts that that woman did (how she could have been remotely related to my Mother \[her sister\] is beyond me). Â ANYway. Â It was a beautiful little place in a very nice neighborhood.

Have you ever watched "Hoarders" on A&E? Â It was worse. Â And not because my brother is a hoarder but because he is a drunk. Â I burst into tears when I saw the place. Â I was so angry! Â How could MY BROTHER be living in this cesspool?

It's a good thing he wasn't home (he had gone off to Denver and had left the door unlocked). Â Both my sister and I, and her husband and son were shocked beyond words. Â The STENCH alone was enough to knock you off your feet.

Last summer he wound up in the hospital. Â His liver is failing. Â I don't know how much longer he has but if he continues down this road (which I'm sure he is) he won't be around a lot longer. Â 

He found someone through his so-called girl friend who bought his house, cleaned it up and let him live there for 2 years (the rent was held back from the sale price). Â They finally had enough and tossed his sorry backside out into the street. Â He did find a place up near the Devil's Backbone West of Loveland. Â Out in the sticks so he can drink to his hearts content and not be bothered.

I cannot believe any of this, still. Â Both my sister and I have fought like mad to overcome everything we went through and to build decent lives. Â My brother is just as smart as we are but more lost, I guess.

There's nothing I can do. Â Nothing. Â Except ache for his loss.

### diane — 2012-12-22T13:34:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68187>

Paulalynn - that was a cool dream!Â 

I'm trying to focus on how I feel rather than the weight. Because if I did focus on the weight, I would definitely feel more frustrated. But when I am focusing on weight, it's usually me judging myself, which I'm really working on changing. So by focusing on how I feel, it keeps me motivated and moving forward, and more likely to keep changes happening because I can definitely see changes in how I feel happen faster than weight.

I also think that having days here and there where your attitude stinks are perfectly acceptable and I try to embrace them! { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Â It doesn't help that I'm also reading a book about Tantra that is telling me I need to love my body (and myself) no matter what size or shape. Â Why is it always someone thin who gives that advice?

I think my little pity party is done for the moment.

I also wonder if the meds I'm on for this stupid rash might not be causing some fluid retention. Â And I KNOW my chest hurts ... has for several days. Â Not a lot. Â Just enough to let me know a cold virus is lurking somewhere trying to wreak havoc and is fouling my mood.

Decided to try and up my daily cytomel by 1/2 a tab and see how it goes. ### Charlotte — 2012-12-23T09:20:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68235>

Merry Christmas, Darlene. OMG I spent all morning, reading your post. I still have a lot of catching up to do. You made my Sunday. I cannot help myself but admire your attitude and persistence despite all the issues you had to face. I am a pretty persistent woman, but I am not sure I would have stayed on track as you did. Even though your husband might be in a bad mood (which hopefully has lifted by now) he must love you for your wits and humour. Here is an educated woman who knows how to think and reflect on herself and be experimental about her issues.

It is hard for me to put into words how I feel about your story, but the essence of it is that that you hold gifts that are rare and most precious. You never buckled or gave up, you are a fighter and that is something I truly admire. You will succeed, I am sure because defeat is something you can never accept. And thank you for sharing your story and letting us know who you are. That shows how couragious you are.

When I started reading your post I was totally sucked in. But I had some urgent chores to do, which I would have loved to postpone but could not. So in order to get back to your post quickly I stuffed my nightgown into my leggins, put on my jacket and drove to work to check on my freezedryer which was supposed to be finished yesterday. I couldn't bother to waste time on getting dressed. I snuck out of the house, pulled my car out of the garage which is pretty tight hoping to be back in 15 minutes and above all not to catch any attention from my neighbours as I was definitely a sight I did not want anybody to see. On my way out I hit the garbage bag I had deposited there yesterday and the garbage spread all over the place. Because it is quite windy I had to pick it up immediately, so I got out of the car and started collecting. And that was the end of plan. Just as I was bending over to pick up a disgustingly fishy smelling paper wrapping my neighbours all came out of their house. They must have been getting ready for a sunday outing. It was definitely not the church they were heading to, at least not the kind of church we think of for a sunday morning. The husband is a scrawny looking guy who has acrylic fingernails, long and with patterns on them (like they do them in nailshops), the wife is 300 pounds for sure, blond with streaks of green in them and the daughters, OMG, had pink and blue hair, with tight black clothes around a waistline that fits their mother's. What you also need to know is that I had a long tear in the seam of the leggin in the back, which I had forgotten to remember when "getting dressed". I immediately remembered. So here I was exposing my big, naked ass to my neighbours picking up garbage. My nightgown had loosened and came down from under my jacket and there was no place to hide. The cascade of emotions that went through my body you can imagine. They looked at me as if I was some nightmarish apparition and then the guy started grinning. I then realized that I was just another member of an ongoing freakshow in a world full of people with damages. Some, or maybe even many, cultivate them and some of us are doing their best to fix them. I tend to think that you and I are among those who fix them (which I will do today by mending the seam of my leggins as a reminder of an outward fix to the inner) and what the heck, I have been carrying my damage all my life with me for everybody to see which has also been a constant reminder for myself what needs fixing. And just this makes me grateful, because there is nothing to hide which cannot be seen by everybody anyway. It sets me free to live my life the way I chose. Same goes for anyone else. The difference? WE KNOW! Aren't we just really lucky? We win so many times over.

And then I jammed my kitchen door which holds the pots and pans last night and I just cannot get the damn thing open. I tried everything short of just breaking it. So I cannot cook and it is Christmas, no chance of getting a handyman. At first I was totally frustrated then I resigned and thought: ok, then it is raw. Fortunately I still have access to my fridge and all the other kitchen utensils, so it is going to be carpaccio, minced raw meat and even raw tiger prawns and salad. It is an opportunity not a catastrophy.

And since my freezedryer needs my attention through the holidays I cannot travel as I had planned to visit friends which means I have to stay home and go to the factory every few hours. My first Christmas all alone, as my children do not come home this year. Catastrophy? No, opportunity to rest, sleep, eat well and do some things I never got around doing so far.

So all is well here at my end and it is true that there are many things beyond our control but we definitely control the way we act and feel about it. (Just don't write another Readers Digest version of your story to soon and get me into trouble)

So I wish for you this Christmas that all is well at your end too and that you have a wonderful Christmas with your family and friends.

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-23T16:38:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68257>

FINALLY. Â WHAT is going on with the forum? Â I've been unable to get it to allow me to post for hours now.

Still have 2 gifts to wrap, one to buy and waiting on one. Â Went on an archeological dig in the garage and found my OLD OLD aluminum tree. Â It's pretty forlorn ... think Charlie Brown and you've got it. Â So now we're "decorated." Â That was part of DH issue the other day.Â 

He's used to me doing all the work while he sits around and enjoys it. Â I'm tired of it and I'm done. Â Our garage is detached and it's a good 30 feet from door to door. Â YOU try dragging box after box after box of holiday ornaments across ice and snow, haul them indoors, unpack, decorate and then drag them back out to the garage only to rinse and repeat in a few weeks. Â Then there are the cards. Â I'm sick of it. Â So now he bellyaches about me not helping. Â Tough.

/grumpy

SOOOO

I'm reading another book on chakra meditations. Â Good thing. Â I decided to start doing my "color" meditations that I used to do all the time. Â Basically I breathe in white light and fill my body up from my toes to my head with light. Â When I exhale that breath takes out all the negative stuff. Â Weird things started happening when I did this regularly to the point I began to feel like I had lost the boundary between myself and everything else. Â It got scary so I quit.

So now I'm reading in the tantra book that this is a good thing. Â OOOOkay. Â If you all say so.

Yesterday I tried to get my mind back into the groove for meditating. Â Breathe white light in ... WTH??? Â Nothing but ash coming out. Â I'm serious here. Â Ashy gray tending towards brown. Â UGLY. Â I'm working on my first chakra which is closed up tight. Â Seems they're all shut down. Â Well beans. Â 

Looks like I have a lot of work ahead of me.

I'm thinking of trying to find my "buddy" the silversmith who found a way to "charge" water. Â He made the most interesting meditation tools. Â He'd come into my studio when I had it out of the house, wave his latest little concoction at me and laugh as my aura blew up. Â I'm very sensitive. Â That's me. Â A delicate little flower.

And as part of my new early resolutions for next year and the rest of my life I have decided to quit being so negative about my physical being. Â I stood nekkid in front of the mirror this morning with a new attitude. Â You know what? Â Not so bad. Â Not nearly as bad as I have been telling myself. Â Rubanesque, yes but not ugly. Â Being attractive starts in the mind. Â So I'm going to concentrate on one positive thing about my body each day. Â Today ... I have gorgeous skin. Â That's a plus, yes? Â Yes!

The only one who ever worries about what size my clothing is is me. Â So starting today I am stopping that. Â I am beautiful just as I am.

Merry Christmas everyone!

### Charlotte — 2012-12-24T05:51:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68290>

Hi everybody, some day... not too far away I will tell my story. It is long, mind you, because I had a very busy and evenful life and I am looking forward to a lot more. I am, what you call a busybody, always moving, always doing something. What I want to say is that at one point in my life I realized that a lot of me being miserable came from judgement. Judging other people is easy and even holds very funny moments, but judging others and me is only two sides of the same coin. Judging me (I am so ugly, I am so fat, I am not lovable, and so on and on) makes you miserable. Sure, because whatever you do, you never match up to your own ideal. And even if you are objectively right, the pure comparison with something we perceive as ideal will pull you down. So I started questioning my own thinking. Thoughts come and go, our brain is geared to thinking and that always within its limits of conditioning, we have received from the day we are born. So I think that what Dr. Kruse means by thinking out of the box is putting aside the conditioning and start being innovative in our own thinking. To me that means to question my own thought processes and ask myself if it is true what I am thinking. And sure enough, most of the time it is not.

And the mind is always repetitive in thinking that our experiences from the past will repeat themselves in the future. Like: oh, if I do this then that is what is going to happen, or, I always failed at this so I never succeed. Is that true? Sometimes, but often we get surprised. And that is also how fear is created. Fear is also just a thought process. Of course there are situations, when fear must happen, like just before a car accident or when in a situation that is simply dangerous, so one can react adequately, but this is naturally embedded in our bodies and brain. But why does our mind keep repeating such situations, projecting them in the future and making us fearful, negative, undecisive, reluctant, etc.? We compare situations from the past with what might happen in the future. And our imagination goes wild.

What I mean by all this (boy am I philosophical this morning, must be because it is Christmas and I haven't talked to anybody in a few days) we should not waste time pondering about all the thoughts we have about us and the world but make up our minds about what we want to change and DO it. Make a plan and go by it. And if that turns out to be the wrong route, then take another one or make the necessary adjustments. The brain has only one function and that is being a TOOL to achieve what you want to achieve. The mind is just a blabbering thinking machine, very repetitive and not useful. So lets use our brain by making it optimal, for which Dr. Kruse is providing the tools, to guide us to do all the things we truly want to do keeping it focused on our goals.

Darleen, how could you ever be ugly? Maybe you have some more pounds on you, but does that make you ugly? NEVER.

You are so beautiful inside, you have shown so much of you and there is so much in you that is adorable it must show on the outside I am sure. And you know what? I just love intelligent people and I do not mean people who have an academic degree. I mean people who have the intelligence to think for themselves, come to their own conclusions and do something about it. That to me is true intelligence. And something else, these are the people which whom you can have the best laughs. It is rare. And I just love laughing, it is the best remedy I know and in these moments I am at ease the most. Hopefully one day we can all laugh together and have a good time.

I think that Dr. Kruse should have his own conference one day where we can all meet.

Okay, now I must clean up the mess, the wild cats did on my terrasse. I put out some raw meat last night and they dragged it all over the place. Funny, but the birds also seem to like it.

And it is beautiful outside this morning. The sun is shining.
