### DarleenMB — 2012-12-26T14:03:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68434>

Thought I should check back in. Â I've been reading all the other journals, just don't have much to say today.

Slept like the proverbial rock last night. I started closing both sets of curtains in the bedroom and didn't wake up until 9:30 this morning! Â I feel like I'm hibernating. Â I don't want anything to eat except bacon and all the fat, butter and braunschweiger. Â I'll eat stuff but I'm just not into it. Â 

My BAB has devolved to 3 slices of thick sliced bacon plus all the fat, 2 eggs scrambled into the bacon and fat and a mug of BPC with 1/2 a stick of butter. Â Then I'm good till late afternoon when I might have a handful of raw almonds while I ponder what I want to eat which is generally nothing. Â So I have a bit of braunschweiger and another mug of BPDC with 1/2 a stick of butter. Â Maybe a few more raw almonds. Â meh. Â I'd rather sleep.

See what I mean?

I haven't been in the tub for a week now. Â In 15 minutes though I'm filling the tub. Â This should be interesting since it's been COLD here. Â 5 yesterday when we went to friends for dinner and -2 by the time we got home. I went without a coat but forgot my gloves so my hands got really cold and on the way home I wound up turning the heater on full blast. Â It was my hands that were really bothering me. Â The rest of me was ok. Â I was aware of the cold but, again, meh. Â No big deal.

Now if you knew how I have had to bundle up every winter since we moved here you would be stunned. Â I wasn't even wearing my boots last night! Â Just knee-highs and my dress loafers.

Hope everyone had a great christmas. Â In addition to the snow globe I got a meat/food grinder attachment for the Kitchen Aid ... yeehaw! Â Steak tartare here I come.

Final word of the day ... Â **WEBINAR**!!!!! Â

### DarleenMB — 2012-12-26T17:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68446>

I couldn't decide if this should go here or in my personal journal. Â both places I guess. Â I was dreaming away this morning just before I fully woke up. Â It was, as usual, pretty bizarre but it relates to my recent commentary here. Patience. Â You'll see how and why.

_In the dream I had entered St. Joseph's church which is, or was, THE Catholic church in Fort Collins when I was a kid. I attended parochial school there for 7th and 8th grade as my Mom thought the public school boys would be, I dunno, too "rowdy" or something. Â If only she had known. (insert snarky little giggle).Â _I walked up to one of the pews, genuflected and made the sign of the cross and then knelt. Â As I looked around I saw a few people here and there and I kept asking myself WTH I was doing in the church since I am anti-religion and an atheist. Â Not exactly in those thoughts but that was the gist. I was watching the priest at the alter and he was getting ready to offer communion. Â I still didn't understand WHY I was there. Â then the thought came to me, clear as day. Â **Comfort**.

Ah. Â 

I debated about taking communion then remembered that even though IN THE DREAM I was baptized in the Church I hadn't Â been confirmedÂ_ (Just for clarification I am a fallen away Episcopalian which is often referred to as first cousin to the Catholics or a Catholic who forgot their Latin. Â Any and all that I learned about Catholicism came from 2 years of indoctrination at St. Joseph's school. Â Sr. Olympia NEVER forgave me for being a "heathen" or for knowing the prayers better than any of my classmates.)

Instead I walked back out of the church. Â But my car was no where to be found. Â It had been stolen._

That's where I woke up ... not the first time in a dream my car has been stolen. Â I'm still not sure what that means. Â But the rest of the dream ties back to my getting back to my meditative practice. Â I realized last night that I have fallen far away from any kind of spiritual practice. Â And the result has been the state I currently find myself in. Â Completely closed up and receding further and further from life.

I'm not sure how this happened exactly but it has been accelerating the last few years. Â So I think it is a positive sign that I finally realized I need to get back on The Path. Â Life, for me, lived without any kind of spiritual connection is no life at all. Â Eating a healthy diet, getting enough sleep, studying & learning, being creative ... it's not enough. Â Not for me. Â 

There is comfort in some kind of spiritual practice no matter how you go about it. Â Mind, body, spirit all need attention. Â In neglecting my spiritual side I have also lost my creativity. Wonder of wonders, in just focusing on my intention to open my chakras and heal them that creativity has come roaring back. Â I read a story on HuffPo the other day about "the world's sexiest grandmother." Â Seems some 76 year old woman is writing hot novels for a publisher called Ellora's Cave. Â That story sparked something in me and in just thinking about inspiration for writing anything I came up with half a dozen possible plot lines. Â We watched the last Harry Potter movie on xmas eve and here came the bead ideas ... Inspiration is everywhere but if you are shut down you don't see it.

My point to all this is, for me, opening up to your heart, your true self, is not easy but it is necessary to heal all aspects of your life. Â Mind. Â Body. Â Spirit. Â All three need to be healthy to make a whole person.

judas aitch. Â Load ... refresh ... refresh ... refresh. Â FINALLY I can post. Â Either the server needs to be changed or ...

ANYway.

CT yesterday was a little tough. Â Water was 45Â°. Â Don't think don't think don't think just step in and SIT THE FRACK DOWN. Â Hugged myself for 15 minutes then took a deep breath and lay back ... OMFG ... gasp gasp. Â That's whatcha' get for skipping CT for a week. Â I did tough it out the full 30 minutes on my back with the water lapping at my nether regions and up around my breasts. Â Shivered for a good 3 hours off and on after that.

This morning it is 61Â° in the house and I'm sitting here as close to nekkid as I will get and am just peachy keen fine thank you all so very much. Â With my usual deviousness I bought DH TWO shirts from L.L. Bean for christmas that are guaranteed to shut him up about how cold the house is.

Â  And it's working. Â heh heh.

Had leftover prime rib for dinner last night (just a small piece ... but it was enough) and a little steamed broc and a small salad with olive oil dressing. Â Then I had some chocolate cream cheese. Â naughty naughty NAUGHTY. Â I swore last time I would never buy it again. Â Maybe I need a date with Buzz instead.

Hoohah. Â TMI, right?

It's overcast here again today. Â Makes me sleepy. Â Speaking of which I'm sleeping fine again but I have developed dark circles under my eyes AGAIN. Â Makes me wonder if I'm actually getting enough rest or if I'm dreaming so hard it's interfering with my rest. Â shrug.

I see the Dec webinar is still no where to be found. Â argh. Â That's all. Â Just ARGH.

ITM Jack's putting up pics of his CT on FB. Â show off.

Gotta run. Â Need to hop in the (cold) shower. CYA!

Krista, I'm actually finding the colder it is the less "painful" it is. Or maybe I'm finally making some forward progress.
I had a heckuvatime falling asleep last night. DH was watching football late into the night so I took a page out of Ealachan's book. Didn't help. Sure was fun though.

then the hubster woke me up somewhere in the middle of by yelling at the cats. They like to lie all over him because he turns his electric blanket up to max sweat. My side is unplugged and half the time I throw it off because I'm so warm. Like last night. I think I finally cooled down around 4A.M. with the rest of the house.

Somebody around the valley set off their annual NYD fireworks. I couldn't figure out what the noise was for about 10 minutes. Then I remembered. I miss Allie McDog who hated the noise and would set up barking so I always knew what was going on.
Feeling sorta OK this morning. Juries out. Speaking of which I drew jury pool from now until April so have to remember to check the website to see if I'm called in. Civic duty and all that jazz.

Got the laundry done yesterday but not the ironing. I wouldn't want to strain myself. So guess what I'm doing today. bleh.

Today's music recommend: Don Henley. "If Dirt Were Dollars" because he manages to work "uncomprehendingly" into the lyrics. As in "she stared at me uncomprehendingly like a cow at a passing train." bwahahahaha. Not only can he keep the beat he's smart! And "Building the Perfect Beast" with it's quick nod to evolution: "Ever since we crawled out of the ocean and stood upright on the land" ... DUDE! Who says rock 'n roll and intelligence are incompatible?!

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-01T13:49:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-68915>

I just looked up & downloaded some music from Sons of Anarchy, helps with my withdrawal lol, might have to start re-watching!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T12:37:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69012>

Woke up around 3A.M. and lay there for what was probably close to 2 hours. Ever since I started the cytomel in earnest it's been like this.

Nothing exciting to report. Well except for the fact I think I may need to cut back on the fat. My stomach has really REALLY been bothering me lately. Last night it just flat out HURT. Since I started sucking up all the bacon fat for breakfast I added in a bile acid factors capsule which sort of helps. Lunch yesterday was sardines and a small avocado. No pill and upset tummy. Last night we had beef short ribs which were very fatty (and very yummy) and my stomach hurt so bad at bedtime I couldn't sleep. I wound up scrounging for the mylanta tabs ... relief! So I'm evidently still not digesting well. pooh.

And today is CT. I decided to switch things up and start on my back for a change. Even with the drain cover a lot of water escapes while I lie there so I decided it makes more sense to start on my back and get better coverage and more cold than just sitting to start. We'll see how it goes.

I keep waiting for the magic to happen. Then realized maybe if I stopped eating so much beef and started chowing down on the fish that's in the freezer then (a) my stomach might quit hurting and (b) something in the ol' biochemistry might shift. You know what they say. Shift happens.

### janagram — 2013-01-02T13:17:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69022>

lol...I sometimes need to take a full spectrum enzyme...I don't have my GB anymore but have had no problem eating tons of fat, except that I will take the enzymes if I feel indigestion coming on. I hope that doesn't sound like an oxymoron, the fact is I can eat quite a bit of fat without trouble. But why not help your digestion out? Bromelain is good too.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T14:15:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69028>

Janagram, no GB here either. Maybe I'll try some enzymes instead of the bile acid factors. One seems to be not quite enough lately and 2 will give me unrelenting heart burn. (sigh) And I just placed a BIG order with vitacost and then this morning realized I"m out of maca. DANG it!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T19:13:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69063>

Just a quick checkin. CT was only on my back and a goodly portion of my front. Except for the girls and they're quite happy they didn't get the big freeze, thank you very much. 40 minutes and woulda gone 45 but HBOGO pooped out on my and Netflix wouldn't load and Spotify sounds like crap straight out on the iPad so BOO. I gave up. Skin was BRIGHT cherry red -- gold star for me today. I'm still trying to warm up.

Off to see Django Unchained ... I absolutely LOVE Tarantino's stuff.

Music of the day ... Led Zep ... nothing like a little head banging to get the ol' heart started.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-02T19:16:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69064>

I love the new singature!

### diane — 2013-01-02T19:20:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69065>

Darleen - you are a CT inspiration!

How was Django?

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T23:09:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69080>

Django Unchained was AWEsome! Jamie Fox was incredible. Not to mention sexy as hell. And unless they did some fancy computer stuff he can sit a horse and ride like nobody's business. WHO KNEW?! And, oh yeah. The scene where's bound, gagged and hanging upside down -- nekkid -- was a real eye opener.

The movie was a tad long but not boring at all. I really enjoyed it, DH was so-so. There was one scene where the whole audience (all 8 of us) were howling so loud I thought I'd wet myself. It reminded me of the campfire and beans scene in Blazing Saddles. See, there's my 5th grade sense of humor being tickled pink again. I'm SUCH a goofball.

So if you go ... DON'T DRINK anything before or during. LOL. Or you'll likely miss something when you go to pee.

### endless — 2013-01-03T00:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69092>

Oh, good. I'm looking forward to seeing that. Knowing me I won't see it till it's on video though...I hate sitting in theatres.

I've always loved that line about the cows too

Did you buy the drain cover, or just rig one up? I used a ziploc freezer bag and 'Tuck' tape (sheathing tape? It's red) to make one and it works pretty good!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T00:52:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69095>

Krista I bought one from Amazon ... was really cheap. It works sorta ok. I finally figured out I have to snug it up against the drain itself and then press firmly on the suction cups. It still leaks but not too badly. Barely lost any water today. Day before I got a pretty good drop. I tried duct taping across the drain hole before putting the suction cup thing on but it didn't really work all that well.

### diane — 2013-01-03T10:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69130>

Good to hear that Django was good! My significant other wants to go and see it, but I can't stomach gore in movies. And Quentin usually brings the gore. But hey, I've watched Pulp Fiction and Reservoir Dogs! I cannot make it through a movie without going to the bathroom, so I will have to plan and not drink all day! LOL.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T12:36:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69140>

Diane I'm not a fan of gore either but Tarantino splashes it around with such abandon you can only laugh. He has this thing for the color red and he uses it profligately in this flick. NO one is safe, LOL. Even the horse gets it. I dunno, there is something about his movies that carries a major undercurrent of humor. And it isn't even black humor. He does, as usual, put in an appearance and I was shocked at how much weight he has gained. Even Hollywood isn't immune to the fat factor.

Decided last night that maybe my tummy issues are being caused by the bile acid factors I've been taking. So I'm skipping it today and we'll see how it goes.

VERY interesting dream this morning. More later. Gotta run go brush my teeth. I'm really enjoying sleeping in.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T14:57:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69177>

I've just added a new note to my routine ... nude sunbathing! Yes, Jack I finally got good and naked out here in Wyoming.

The weather widget says it's 26 right now. I spent 19 minutes out in my chaise rockin' out with my iPod and it felt WONDERFUL. As long as the sun is shining and the wind isn't blowing I'll be doing this on non-CT days. It was GREAT!

Gotta run ... will post about the dream later. Don't want to forget it because it was pretty awesome.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T16:06:03+0200

>
> Go Darleen!!

### diane — 2013-01-03T16:11:39+0200

You know- I was thinking earlier that this is something that I would probably do. Except that I live downtown with no privacy lol.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-03T17:28:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69211>

OMG...nude sunbathing in the winter....awesome!!! That sounds more appealing than a tub of cold water...if only I had somewhere private I might join you! Now tell us about the dream!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T17:59:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69218>

Paulalynn that's the great thing about where we live. We have neighbors but no one can see me in my back yard unless they actually come INTO the yard.

OK, the dream. I was with a large group of people (I have just SO many facets to my personality ... sometimes the crowd is GINormous, LOL). Some stuff happened prior to this part but it isn't really relevant and I've lost that thread anyway. Big surprise ... a man came up to me. He was relatively nondescript which was kind of surprising. He took me in his arms and we began to dance. I closed my eyes and it was like I was floating. I felt my hand on his back as he held me close. All I could see was this bright golden light through my closed eyes. I sensed everyone around me was not very happy I was dancing with this guy. I didn't care. We danced and danced and at the end I wound up stepping in dog doo (WTH, LOL) so we stopped so I could clean things up. He went into the bathroom and I had words with some of the other people. When I went into the bathroom he was sitting in the tub, back to the shower head, with one of those large, light beach towels draped over his head and around his body.

More stuff ensued and the dream changed course. Not important.
\======================
And what do I make of this?

The male side of my/your brain is the logical, rational side ... the part that loves math but isn't very good verbally. Right side is creative as hell, verbal to the max, the exact opposite. Male and Female. Yin and Yang. My embracing of the man in my dream is me embracing and accepting the male (sexual) side of myself. the muttering was the peanut gallery trying to heap guilt on my head. The bright golden light? This is the second dream where that light has shown up and I still am not exactly sure what it represents. It's important, obviously, but what does it mean?!

My consult with Jack was very traumatic. But I asked for it. Yes, I did. I accept that. I needed to have someone in "authority" kick me in the butt and tell me to grow up and stop hiding from this very important aspect of myself. And here I am, a couple of months later, embracing that part of myself. It still is not easy. Maybe that was the dog doo-doo, LOL. and it's still scary. that sexy-as-hell rock goddess is still locked up in her gilded cage and she's rattling the bars on a daily basis. She still scares the crap out of me because who knows what she will do or what mischief she will get up to if I let her loose. She's uncontrollable. And she doesn't give a rat's backside, either.

So the work-in-progress continues. But the progress is remarkable. There is no WAY in hell I would have gone out into my yard before and sunbathed in the nude. NEVER EVER EVER. Who the heck IS this woman???

### diane — 2013-01-03T18:31:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69221>

Darleen it's just amazing how you're pushing yourself and facing even the scariest parts. You have been such an inspiration for us reading your journal and working alongside your journey. And just when you think you've reached your limits - you go further!

I would have never have gotten the same interpretation from your dream - that's so cool. In terms of the light - it's funny, when I do face dunks for a few days I start to see a light while I'm immersed in the water. I wonder if there's a connection?

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-03T20:02:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69229>

Hi Darleen...I lOVE the idea of nude sunbathing in winter...If you could work in grounding at the same time it would be ultra fantastic.

Here's my two cents on some of your dream....from a Jungian perspective...dog doo, basements, mud, getting sucked into dark tunnels etc...all can represent the primordial dark that we still carry with us...the old dark shadows and impulses that live in our unconscious. The while/golden light could be your integrated authentic self emerging...just as you said the male and female aspects of you are uniting for a quantum leap in being. The dog doo often shows up when we are shifting ourselves to a new level....it's the old stuff ready to try to suck us back down into the ooze. My thinking is that the ooze is important, but not as a controlling force...it's part of life...just like the light is. So when we are comfortable with all aspects of the light spectrum we are integrated. .... my humble opinion is that you are transforming in all ways!!!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T20:09:45+0200

OOOO SeaHorse!! thanks! that is really cool. I hadn't thought of either of those in that way but it makes a lot of sense.

OK Jack posted something on FB about mutant cucumbers causing baldness on the old Vajayjay. then me mentioned vajazzling. WTF? Of course I had to google it and have spent the last 5 minutes laughing hysterically at the definition and the PICTURES! OMG ... bedazzling taken to new heights. Or is that lows? Whatever, my only response, besides LMAO was OUCH!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-04T13:03:55+0200

Never heard of it but now have it on my Kindle ... thanks!

JUST as I was getting ready to go fill up the tub for today's CT DH crawled outta bed and into the shower. sounds like he's sitting on his little homemade shower seat and running hot water on his back. sigh. He refuses to listen to me about embracing the cold and how it would help with his pain. No one can diss you like loved ones.

Sun is shining and I"m thinking hmmmmm maybe a bit of nude sunbathing after CT? Seems I like running around (figuratively that is) outside sans clothing. WHO IS THIS WOMAN?

Actually was up about 8:15 this morning. Had tilapia for dinner last night and woke up about 7 with my stomach yelling at me for food but I was too lazy to get up. FBG was down to 92 (has been a little over 100 the last several day) so I think it was combo of the fish and no cytomel yesterday. I got my bottle of 50mg tabs of iodoral and took one of those yesterday instead. I know I do not eat nearly enough seafoods and was thinking this might be a better pathway than taking the synthetic T3 to help clear the RT3. We'll see.

Gonna whip up Charlotte's recipe for German Chocolate Mocha Torte today. Sounds divine!

Wow Darleen. When you embrace something, you really EMBRACE it! Look at you and the nude sunbathing!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69323>

45 minutes of CT proved to be too much. I lasted all of 2 minutes outside. SHaking really hard rightnow. brrrrrrrrrr

### janagram — 2013-01-04T16:07:30+0200

### Charlotte — 2013-01-04T16:50:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69343>

Gosh Darleen, everytime I read your post, I roll around laughing. You are so funny. I have this vision of you all pink and rosy running naked outside having a ball.

Now about the cake. I baked it yesterday and decorated today. This was my first try at this without any sugar. It smelled heavenly but it had some big holes in the cake. I don't know exactly why, maybe I did not fold the egg whites carefully enough. So do not make the batter too firm, Rather use a little less eggyolks, meaning, just separate maybe 12 and do only 4 whole eggs. That way the batter is a little more liquid and comes out more fluffy. I need to try it again. (Good reason to make another one soon)
The cream was perfect. One suggestion there also. Use just a little (very little) bit of hot water to dissolve the coffee and let it cool before adding it. Tomorrow I make a picture and post it (if I am not too stupide to do this.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-04T17:01:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69345>

Naked in Wyoming. Hmmmmm. Cowboys, Indians, main character with no clothes on. Hmmmm.

Charlotte, thanks for the tip!

Now then, it's a helluva note when one cannot differentiate between nausea and hunger. Evidently I burned off my entire breakfast while lounging in the tub. About 1 I finally figured it out. so I had an avocado, some braunschweiger, a tiny bit of a lovely English cheddar, a dill pickle spear, some Kombucha and for "dessert" a paper thin itty bitty square of Gjetost -- my new fave cheese. I feel better but my neck still is tight and I still have a tiny headache from it.

So I guess the only thing to do is wait just a few more minutes then go hang out in the massage chair. Funny, we bought it because of hubby's neck and back injuries (he was headed to get on a locomotive and slipped on the ice which the railroad had thoughtfully neglected to clear off the pathway). But I'm the one who uses the darn thing daily. Same with the Teeter Hangup I bought for him. They both help him but he won't use them. MEN.

### endless — 2013-01-04T17:18:29+0200

I would love to have a massage chair! I'd be in it all the time.

Woke about 8:30 when DH got up to leave for breakfast with the guys (his usual Sat. routine). Too tired for any oxytocin therapy. sigh.

Waiting for my bacon to finish crisping up so I can add in salmon and an egg. Groovin' to Stevie Ray Vaughn, again. TASTY tasty blues. With a rock edge, natch.

Music for the day .... ANYthing by Steely Dan. ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-05T15:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69472>

It's sunny and about 19Â° here right now. Nude sunbathing ... whoohooo. 20 minutes to toast the girls and about 10 for aunt fanny. Pesky breeze kept messing with me. I'd no more than warm up and here it came. Just enough to cool me right off.

My fingers are really cold, my feet chilled but the rest of me is ready to hit the showers.

Afternoon music recommends ... latin rhythms. Try out Bebel Giberto. Lovely Brazilian tunes.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T15:13:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69612>

gah. CT was BRUTAL today. Water same temp as always ... just under 43Â° but it took 2 minutes to get "acclimated" and I shivered the whole time. That was new. Toughed out the full 45 minutes though, skin bright cherry red. yay me.

Got dissed again about my breakfast. Hubby walked up, stuck his nose at my plate and sniffed. "What's THAT?"

"my usual .. bacon and eggs with the rest of yesterdays bacon & salmon."
"Bacon and FISH? EWWWWW."
"well why not ... you like bacon wrapped scallops."
tsk

My appetite yesterday just wasn't there. Left about 1/2 of cup of my breakfast on the plate (see above). Had a handful of nuts in the afternoon and yes YES I know I should NOT be eating them this time of year. I'm trying to stop. And yes, I know .. .I am VERY trying. Decided about 6 I might be a LITTLE hungry so had some braunschweiger, a couple/3 sweet potato chips, a dill pickle hunk, and a few slices of pepperoni sized hard salami. Or as it's spelled on the pkg ... salame. Was fine the rest of the evening.

Bed and asleep by about 10:30. Woke at midnight feeling so sick to my stomach I thought for minute there I was going to hurl. then decided maybe it was EXTREME hunger. Yeah, still have a little trouble telling the difference. Got up, went to the fridge (no lectures now ... I KNOW!) and rummaged around for a bite of anything to shut my stomach up. Finally settled on the tiny wedge of French brie lurking in the bottom drawer. Cut off 3 thin bits and inhaled them then drank a glass of water.

Oh dear, now it's even worse and I was about to get on my knees to the porcelain god. Finally decided everything was staying put so crawled back into bed in a position to make a sprint for the bathroom if needed. Fell sound asleep and didn't wake up until dawn.

Now it should be noted when I first went to bed it was like somebody turned my personal furnace on full blast. I don't know how to describe it ... it's not like the screwed-up=hormonal heat from menopause. My body just starts running hot. It heats up my entire side of the bed ... you can put your hand on the mattress where I have been lying and it is HOT. and then the twitches start. when my thighs heat up like that they get twitchy. It's NOT restless leg syndrome. OK I'm just weird, I already know that.

This morning I was STARVED. Ate my whole BAB ... 3 pieces of thick sliced bacon, all the fat, 2 eggs and the leftover 1/2 cup of BAB from the day before PLUS the usual BPDC made with 4 TBS of butter. Scarfed it right down.

Which makes me wonder about the effect the Iodoral is having on my metabolism already. 50mg coupled with regular CT and now nude sunbathing (when the sun is shining, that is which it is NOT today ... all overcast, boo hoo) in 20Â° weather. puhleeeeeeeeeeze let it be a positive sign.

I like your food. bacon, braunshweiger, brie, pickles, and all that stuff! Yum!
This heat thing you're experiencing is amazing.....something is going on...

### caroline — 2013-01-06T17:37:48+0200

Darleen - why did you start on idoral? maybe I missed that... ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T18:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69648>

Caroline, I am reading the book "Iodine, Why you need it, why you can't live without it" by David Brownstein, MD. I thought I'd try a big dose to see if it helped my thyroid as I'd rather not take synthetic anything. And I am not eating enough seafood. I'm working on it but am not where I should be at all. So I figured what the heck.

Was your iodine a urine test to see how much you've excreted overnight? According to the book, that's a good way to determine how much you need.

I'm just fumbling around here trying to see if I can fix things. And I'll tell you what ... I've only been on the Iodoral for a few days but today about an hour ago I checked my temp and it was 98!!!! I don't remember WHEN it has been that high. Somethings working.

Yeah yeah ... MORE FISH, thank you Jack. I got the message.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-06T21:43:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69678>

Just finished dinner ... 1/2 pound of mussels and 1/2 pound shrimp. MMMMMM. I had a packet of sardines for lunch with a couple bites of the French brie and a glass of kombucha. That was at 2 and by about 5 I was hungry again. Hopefully I got enough dinner that I'll sleep through the night tonight. I can't believe I'm suddenly eating this much!

Drank a liter of steeped green tea. Odd little thing ... I'm peeing a lot while I'm drinking it. Must have some kind of diuretic effect?

time for a mug of BPDC and then I'm done for the night. Adding in CO to this one. YAY fat!

### endless — 2013-01-06T22:02:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69684>

Green tea makes me pee, definitely. Unfortunately it also makes my heart do drum solos

Wish I could drink it more!

I've never heard of Iodoral. Must look it up! Sounds like something has got you fired up!

### janagram — 2013-01-07T14:06:39+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-07T14:07:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69758>

Saw a post on FB this morning that really explained a lot:

> On a very low carb diet one may experience higher than normal blood sugars, especially in the AM dawn phenomenon because you get simultaneously peripherally insulin resistant, (a normal healthy response to glucose deprivation) and more insulin sensitive to CNS tissue. The sugar arguably is higher for the CNS to have access to it. Thus you can have "high" fasting blood sugar but basement level A1Cs. I am not sure I accurately portrayed this process as I am no subject matter expert. If so, let me know how I am incorrect. I first read about it on Hyperlipid Peter's blog below after Brook Dubose informed me about it.

Last night, as I mentioned, well, last night, I had nothing but mussels and shrimp for dinner. My FBG this morning was up from 91 the day before to 123. EVERY time I skip or limit carbs at night, the next day my FBG is UP. Not that I understand AT ALL what the AM dawn phenomenon is but I'm working on it.

On another note, probably FA or it could be SO, the sun is shining and I need to ditch my clothes and go plop on the chaise for a little sun therapy.

Also have to pay the bills today and do laundry. snrrrkkkk. I hate mondays. Even in retirement they suck.

Oh yeah, had that heat thing again last night. Not quite as intense as the night before but it was there. I'm feeling ... mmm, how to describe it? Energetic is a bit too extreme cause I haven't felt that in about a decade. Slightly more HERE? More ready to get off my prodigious backside and DO something? Definitely a good sign. Must be all the fish and all the iodine.

Oh and my new sig line? Homage to Jack who not only stands alone but is head and shoulders above any crowd he might find himself in.

### endless — 2013-01-08T13:21:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69871>

I think I have the same problem with my FBG....it's almost always high in the morning. I should do a test and eat some carbs for dinner to see what happens.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T15:31:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69892>

OMFGHSMFDIATH ..... CT was beyond brutal this morning. I didn't bother to take the water temp before I hopped in but it has to have dropped another degree. If it's over 42 I'll eat my hat. It burned, it was so damned cold. I never did stop shivering ... 45 minutes and I'm feeling like a giant popsicle.

Yesterday I managed a whopping total of 20 minutes out in the sun. High cirrus clouds and 19Â° meant not a lot of sun warmth. I gritted my teeth and toughed it out. the last bit of open water on the creek has finally iced over. Must be January.

On the dream front, that little embrace with my higher self the other morning has resulted in issue ... I dreamed this morning that one of "us" gave birth to a baby girl. I immediately took her as my own, loving and cooing, Mommy's here. While I was busy getting dressed in a beautiful gown the rest of the crew took her outside to play where she was already growing by leaps and bounds. this should be interesting. I wonder what "she" is going to be like. Nothing like a little transformational change to brighten one's outlook.

Went to the movies yesterday afternoon ... please RUN away as fast as you can from Tom Cruise's new flick "Jack Reacher." That's 2 hours of my life I want back and right now. Got home late so fixed a quick tuna salad and had it on an avocado. done.

Still chugging that liter of steeped green tea daily. Really working on eating fish at least once, if not twice a day.

Got the chores done yesterday but now have to go to town to run errands. Big excitement out here in Wyoming. Yes indeed. My life is just the envy of all.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-08T15:39:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69896>

Hey Pink Popsicle, my doctor explained to me what happens when BG is up in the morning when not eating carbs. He said it is a counter reaction of the liver, producing glucose because it is low (something like that). He said it is normal and nothing to worry.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T15:54:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69902>

Thanks Charlotte. I had sort of had that explained to me before but I wanted more of an explanation than "oh it's just your liver, don't worry about it," LOL. doesn't mean I will understand anything past that, I just wanna hear it.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-08T17:02:00+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-69932>

Ashryn, You little dickens you! I wasn't really censoring anything. I just like to let people fill in their own choice vocab.

Sun is shining this morning so about ready to ditch the clothes and head out to see how long I can manage a little sunbathing.

I'm sure I'll get an eyeroll or two from DH as I stagger out the door with my cushion, iPod,speakers and timer.

Figured out this morning why I have probably felt so good recently. The coffee I have been drinking is NOT decaf. crap. I wasn't getting any buzz off it so no wonder I didn't realize. Or maybe it's because French Press brewed isn't as heavy on the caf as regular brew. shrug. Could also explain the difficulty I've had of late in sleeping. Nothing major just sometimes difficult to drop off and sometimes waking up. It was, however, beginning to bug me.

Rather than fill up my Optimal Journal with day-to-day stupid stuff, I'm MAKING myself post it on my own blog. I see all the fun HTML buttons are missing again so here's the addy:

www.darleenmb.wordpress.com

Off to catch a few rays.

Oh yeah, my new avatar ... taken 5 years ago at DisneyWorld. Nemo is hiding behind my back -- that is NOT my hair, LOL. 50 pounds lighter after starving myself on the jenny stupid SAD diet for a year. too bad it's all back. Even though my thighs were still big and I was still a bit thick through the middle I sure looked a lot better than I do right now.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-10T13:51:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70167>

You might want to cover your eyes as I'm sitting here nekkid as the proverbial jaybird. It's another gray, overcast day so no sunbathing. I may have to cave and go see how much the tanning beds charge. I have 4 more weeks of 50,000 iu Vit D to get through and then another week before testing again. I've been taking 10,000 iu's on the other days in the hopes of pushing those levels up to normal.

Crawled outta bed at about 9:40 this morning. I guess I'm going to have to start leaving one set of drapes open. I'm logging between 9 and 11 hours snooze time every night. Puts too big a dent in my day.

Today is Thursday (in case you all were wondering) which means about 90 minutes of uninterrupted, blissful ALONE time late this afternoon after I take hubby to the Legion for his weekly confab with the other vets.

Had a little trouble finishing my BAB this morning. OK it's not THAT big but it is FAT laden. Some days I can pound it down and lick the plate (that was yesterday) and some days, like today, I have to save part of it for lunch.

FBG was 81 this morning. Gotta remember to ask the new doc, when I find one, why the syn. T3 makes it go up.

Still miffed at myself for drinking regular coffee the last couple of weeks thinking it was decaf. I sure hope that wasn't the entire reason for the boost in mood and energy. Keeping my fingers crossed as I come back off the stuff. You'd think I could read. But noooooo.

Well I only have 30 minutes to CT. That screechy sound you'll likely hear followed by a string of undecipherable but blue words will be me as the cold water smacks me.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T14:39:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70311>

OK, I went back into my old photo albums and dug out some pics from a 1975 road trip to Taos. I weighed about 145 at that point in time:

New Year's Eve, 1979 I looked like this:

Well cannot post the link because of a stupid assed rule that says MAXIMUM NUMBER OF ALLOWED LINKS EXCEEDED: 2 Allowed. Who thought THAT up?

Now go look at my xmas pic again. Yes there is a definite difference which all the nice clothes and good skin and makeup cannot cover up. Ask Jack how he felt when he was at his highest weight. Ask anyone who is more than 20% overweight how they feel. I've said before it's hard NOT to let it get to me. 7 years ago I was an emotional mess. Today I am determined. No I don't like how I look, I never will while I'm at this weight. But this time around I don't hate myself. This time around I understand (pretty much) how and why this has happened. And instead of falling into the pity pool or worse yet, starting a restricted calorie diet, which was how I used to try and handle it, I'm working on repairing the damage.

While I appreciate all the kind words, please understand, I don't believe them. And yes, I know that is just me judging myself. Hard habit to break. But I have made some progress because previously I wouldn't have asked to have that picture taken in the first place let alone post it here for everyone to see. Now to press the post button and see if this looks ok. I sure wish we had that preview button or better yet, the "go advanced post" button. I like to check what I've written and am getting tired of having to edit posts because I typed too fast or spell something incorrectly (which I NEVER EVER used to do. WHERE did I put my brain?). anyhoo. Here goes.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T14:39:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70312>

http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert\_files/attachments/18327=140-me-band.jpg

Here's the link to the New Year's eve 1979 pic where I weighed in at about 132 pounds. THAT's where I want to be only healthy, not stupid.

### Inger — 2013-01-11T15:09:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70320>

Darleen, I thought you were much fatter than in the picture... You are no way looking obese at all..huh! You look so sweet you and your hubs!

Now.. If it was me suffering that much over those extra pounds.. I would go raw. 100%. I would bet my head you would soon have to worry about beeing too thin. To follow the epi paleo diet but raw, eat as much as you want.. raw fat.. lots of it, and protein too. You could still try it Darleen! Just for a week.. and then see..but I bet you would not stop.. you would not want to miss that feeling out.. and the pounds just disappearing.

And there are lots of delicious stuff you can make eating raw too!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T15:44:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70323>

Inger I have, in the last several weeks, taken to eating my steak very rare. And I have to say I really LIKE it. Got a food grinder attachment for my Kitchen Aid mixer for xmas but haven't yet used it. I was planning on doing steak tartare.

As to how I look, remember I'm almost 5'10" tall so that much weight isn't as apparent on me especially if I dress right. If you compare my face with the older pics, other than age, lol, you'll see a huge difference. Heck, even my previous avatar taken right after I lost 50+ pounds on that stupid diet looks pretty good. Right now I feel like a chipmunk.

Raw. hmmm. Not doing any kind of seafood raw yet. Or maybe I'll do some ceviche with those scallops I have in the freezer. THAT I could do now that I think about it. Gonna have to get some limes. What else. Raw beef ... yeah I could probably handle that. Might have to do one more baby step and just sear each side so I can pretend it's cooked. What else is there? Suggestions?

### caroline — 2013-01-11T21:36:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70362>

Darleen - the thing is this....since you are quite tall .....I have always thought that your goal weight was WAY too low......I am saying this really,really gently and with the utmost kindness. I am 5"5"
and am pretty good at 132 - you probably need to weigh about 160+ or so????? for your height? Have you ever tried looking at someone you think is an okay size and then ask them what they weigh? It is very often a real eye opener! Are you fixated on a number because it was a happy time? to me \[expert that I am!\] You look excruitiatingly thin at 132 - plus you are just the teeniest bit older

What if you picked a reasonable number and then re evaluate? I think this could be an "ask Jack" I have always had this self image problem - I am fixated on a number for some reason which has no bearing on my bone structure etc. When I was young 125 was the magic number - don't remember why - makes no sense. I seem to not understand what size I am - comes from verbal abuse when younger I am sure - and when I see myself in a pic I think - is that what I look like???? holy crap - I don't look too bad - but I can't seem to assess this on my own - no matter how old I get!.
I think maybe JK has to give us a talking to about our own body image - after all - like you said - he has been there. I wonder if it is different for a man????

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-11T22:16:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70366>

Caroline, when I weighed 130 pounds it was definitely NOT a happy time. I weighed 142 for a long time before my body rebelled and started packing on the pounds. I'm very comfortable at that weight. Now where my body decides it's happiest is totally out of my control and I know that. 160 though is just too fat. That pic from 1979 was a result of starving myself unmercifully. I was living on 750 calories a day. that's why I didn't look so good. 130 might be dreaming but I know that's where my body wanted, at one time, to be.

I guess that bears a bit of an explanation.

While living with my grandparents before my Mom died, the three of us were left pretty much to our own devices. We weren't exactly starving but you show me an unsupervised, wild 9 year old who knows anything at all about nutrition. Plus there wasn't much to eat. We did have lots of eggs (grandparents raised chickens) and raw milk (they had a couple of cows) so we did have that.

When Mom died and we went to live with the evil aunt and her husband we suddenly found ourselves getting real meals 3 times a day. I dropped 10 pounds in one short month without even trying. A couple years later when I started skipping meals again my weight went back up. There's a pattern there. And a lesson.

Again, it's just a number and I know it means nothing. I'm looking more at those pictures now and wishing.

### caroline — 2013-01-12T02:30:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70375>

At the end of the day we seem to be such slaves to our emotions - I guess we got screwed up years ago and it is pretty hard to change no matter how much we want to.......we have shed a lot of things lately tho - you especially!! ..... it is that guy with the flashlight ........

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-12T09:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70383>

Caroline you are not going to like this post so ... sorry.

I woke up at 4 this morning feeling like I was in the middle of a heat wave. I'd left the furnace on a bit higher than usual because the wind and the temps yesterday had the house like a freezer. So now I cannot go back to sleep because I"m hot. I can feel the room air is chilly but forget it. Now I'm wide awake and I'm starting to think. Never a good thing this early.

I'm wondering WTH is wrong with me that I cannot get out the door and DO the things I'd like to do. We haven't been on a trip together for 2 years because I just can't make myself get with the program. Once you get me out the door and to whatever destination then I'm fine and have a great time. I just don't like all the prep work and the actual getting there. This time, though, I"m wondering what's wrong with me that I cannot get off dead center about the movnat thing in Santa Fe. Part of me really REALLY wants to go play outside. But there's that other part, the one that keeps me home with an endless list of why I can't.

Why? Two things are going on. One is I am content right where I am. We do not have some fancy schmancy house ... in real estate parlance it is a 2 bedroom "cottage" although at 2,000 sq feet that seems a bit of an understatement. What we do have is 4 acres, privacy, quiet, live water bordering the property (right in the backyard actually) and I am HAPPY here. that one is easy to deal with.

The second one, well, it's kind of difficult to lie in bed at 5A.M. crying and trying not to wake up hubby. So I got up, fixed my coffee and here I am spilling my guts. Before you all start chiding me you need to understand and accept that I am talking about my emotions. Logic has no place here. I can stand in the left side of my head and lecture myself from sunup to sundown and agree with everything I say. won't do any good. Emotions are what they are. Trying to wrestle them to the ground with logic is a total waste of time.

It started because I couldn't figure out why I'm doing the whole approach/avoidance dance with the movnat thing in Santa Fe. It isn't that we don't have the money. We do. It is because I am loathe to spend that much on myself. DH would tell me to buy the plane ticket, sign up for the class if I wanted and go have fun. \*I'M\* the one who's telling me NO, not him. So WTH is it?

Never open a door if you aren't prepared to deal with what's behind it. 5:00A.M. ... What's behind Door #3, Monty? biiiig mistake.

I'm right back where I was in 2006, sitting at the kitchen counter with hubby and bursting into tears because I didn't want to go to Seattle to be with friends because I am ashamed of how I look. I hate myself for being so fat. It doesn't matter that now I know WHY and that it isn't some personal failing on my part. I'm broken but it doesn't matter because every single time I look at myself in the mirror all I see is bloat, fat, ugly. I KNOW how I look and I hate it.

When I was in my late 20's and my sister was morbidly obese and pregnant and I was a thin 140 pounds I remember thinking I would NEVER let myself wind up getting that fat. Never say never.

I try not to go there because down that road are the same behaviors that got me here in the first place. I try to be mindful that I'm eating enough which part of me finds hysterical given how much I weigh. But that's my danger zone ... that stinkin' thinkin' that whispers in the back of my head "you're fat because you eat too much. Stop it." The hidden self-bully that tells me I won't ever be thin if I don't stop eating. If I really want to beat myself up I could always climb on the scale. Boy that always adds to the fun.

So there's the demon I thought I'd exorcised. Holding me prisoner because I hate being seen in public by strangers because I'm so fat. It doesn't matter that I \*KNOW\* I"m judging myself far more harshly than anyone else. What matters is this is how I feel back in the dark corners of my heart where I don't want to look.

Please don't try and tell me it's OK and that I'm something I know I'm not. Again, this isn't about logic. This is about emotion. this is how I feel not about what I know.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-12T10:41:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70387>

Darleen, my heart goes out to you and you are so right, there is no logic and it is not about logic, it is about the deepest secrets we have about ourselves, which nobody can understand, except one has been in that place. And there is nothing to say, nothing to console only pure raw pain. I am crying here with you because it sounded so familiar so very much like the same place I have been practically all my life. It is so BIG to where nothing anybody could or would say matters, as it just does not match your feelings about oneself. So, I have no advice for you as there is none. But I am there with you right now.
So cry your heart out, go to that place inside of you, take that pain and use it for you.
And Darleen, I admire you for this post, you are an honest to God person who is not afraid to show to us who she is and take us to her darkest place, the most fearful thoughts one can have about oneself. To me, that is greatness and true grace.

### SeaHorse — 2013-01-12T10:53:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70388>

Darleen....something to think about:......emotions, as you say, don't really respond to logic, so one way of working with them is to listen to them (like you did this morning) and just sit with them. Don't try to "make" them go away, but get the message that they hold for you and THEN see if they will move on. In the work I do, it has been very useful for people to understand that we carry a "false self" around with us, but that each of us also has a innate "authentic self." So where would all these emotions of self loathing come from? They don't come from your authentic self...that's for sure. They are the accumulation of outside messages, experiences, survival patterns and coping strategies. They were responses to the outside world....not a recognition of your true self. They are part of you....but they don't have to control you. So listen to them and try to counterbalance all the un -useful stuff with some activating of your authentic self. I'd say each time you get in a bath you are tipping the balance...each time you take care of yourself...you are strengthening the muscle of your authentic being. It's ALL in there, you are finding your way to uncovering it.

So your authentic self is yearning to go to Santa Fe, but way the false works is to throw a fit if you start to change the norm. (Like an emotional detox) The fact that you were able to work through all of these emotions and then write about them allows you to see how strong that belief is for you (I'm too fat and too ugly to be seen) This is FANTASTIC!!! Give yourself a real thumbs up. Your well fed brain is working to unclog all the thinking and feeling that has been the way you've worked in the past. Don't try to reason your way out of those beliefs...just say thank you very much, that might have worked in the past, but I've got a bath to get into and some raw meat to eat and as I continue to feel alive and strong I will add some wonderful explorations and trips to my life.

Ripping too many emotional blankets off at once, doesn't serve us....but neither does staying in bed. I think you're finding your balance as you heal.

### vkiernan — 2013-01-12T11:34:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70392>

Wow Seahorse, what a message. Darleen, I'm borrowing your message if you don't mind sharing! I have never thought about anything in that manner.

### Inger — 2013-01-12T11:49:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70394>

Darleen, I am taking my hat off to you, beeing willing to try more raw. I am thinking... for people in your age coming from a culture where raw meat and fish was seen as dangerous, it is not easy at all. I see it in my parents (my brothers.. they just chow down raw chicken hearts from fresh killed own chickens.. all kind of weird stuff I tell them to try..lol my younger sisters too.. they have no issues with raw meat at all). They really struggle with eating meat raw, the do eat my salmon tartare though and love it, and chevice and marinated herring and stuff. They tried oysters first time at new year and my mom had big difficulties but they did it! So I so much understand it is not easy. I think you are so bold Darleen. You tan naked too. To read about how you try new things all the time makes me so warm and happy in my heart because I know you will feel better and better all the time! It makes me just so happy.

Your last post could have been me. I have had the same issues Darleen. I still have it at times but it is so minor now in comaprison with how it was.. Thinking I am so ugly I have to hide.
I can tell you what help me acutely, when a bout come over me like that. I concentrate on the other people.. that are present, that I was/am about to meet. I just consciously forget about myself, as if I were transparent. I suck in the impressions from my surroundings.. I learn to get intrested in others.. I forget about ugly me.
Then.. to do me as pretty as I can before leaving help too. Put on my favorit clothes and colors, do a nice make up, use some nice jewerely, make my hair. And then after that I just forgot about me. I had done my best about improving my outlook so I leave the worrying behind. one can only do ones best at the very monet, and that is enough. Then.. to concentrate on my surroundings.

It always helps me to eat the good stuff too. When I cheat it makes all worse. It is weird but my eating habits helps me tremendousy, they help my mind the most. They makes me feel not so scared! Yeah I know it sounds weird..lol They kind of makes me open up, or to get more transparent to my surroundings, so I feel more one with everything. This oneness just leaves the suffering we talk about above, behind! It is magic..?!

IDK I hope it does not sound like I have lost my mind.. because it is for real. it is very real. I just do not know why exactly it works like that but I am sure Jack could explain.

### Inger — 2013-01-12T12:04:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70395>

What SeaHorse said about not ripping off too many blankets at once is very wise me thinks. Be good to you, do it in your pace. When you feel you are ready, step by step. That is how I do. I am by far not done yet. I know so much I still need to do better but I am not stressing, I give me time.
Too much ripped off at once might just cause trauma maybe... we need to grow with the changes so they stay.
The only important thing IMO is, that we go forwards and change...

### caroline — 2013-01-12T17:18:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70436>

I am so very sorry Darleen if you thought I was judging you in any way ...I definitely am not .... that is my life story as well. So much of my life passed me by because I didn't want to go anywhere being the size that I was. I was an obese teenager \[with tons of pimples\] and ugly glasses and an even uglier haircut because my mother took me to a barber...... and carry a whole array of scars from that. Teenagers can be so cruel but nothing compared to the things we say to ourselves. I can remember so clearly - not wanting to live \[I wouldn't have done anything to myself - coward here\] but on a day to day basis my life was such crap. The very ugliest of clothes ... my mother thought you should have 2 sets of clothes - one to wear while the other is being washed. I guess in retrospect - I was doing C/T as a kid ...my mother made me wear an old men's coat to school....so I wouldn't wear it ...I would just hide it in my bag!! see - every cloud has a silver lining

I really think all of our terrible stories - give us definition and complexity and understanding and kindness. I think all of our stories make it hard for the people who love us - we aren't easy .....but we are real.....

### Inger — 2013-01-13T03:16:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70481>

The guys in school said to me I was ugly too. Such things you just never forget.
I had to use ugly clothes too all my childhood and never was allowed to keep my hair open, but always in two braids that was so thightly braided my head almost hurt.
I had pimples too and still have scars. I always had ugly skin. I always got read as a tomato because I was so ashamed for the smallest thing. I have weird white spots on my body (on parts that mostly are covered with a bikini) that I always was so ashamed to show.. no pigmentation at all and they got easily burnt too. I always wished I could be normal like others but hey.. I finally came to accept better and better who I am with my weird things and all. It really helps to focus more on others, how can I help and make their life easier and happier.. and then think.. oh my body is what it is because my mom ate almost only potatoes as she was pregnant with me.

and I stop comparing myself with the more beautiful ones and just concentrate on how I feel instead. To do the good things to me.
When I feel great everything is just beautiful! Every other person around is beautiful and I think I look so okay to when I check in the mirror and I really think it is all in the brain/heart!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-13T12:23:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70503>

You guys are just the BEST. thank you.

I was going to come in and claim my cats had snuck in and written yesterday's post without my knowledge but they claim they don't know my password.

ahem

Holy falling estrogen levels, batman. I haven't been THAT weepy in ages. Those of you who are certain age will understand. THAT kind of weepy meant I would sob uncontrollably at toilet paper commercials. Seriously. It would get that bad.

thank goodness Vickie mentioned that my complaint elsewhere about sore nipples meant too much progesterone (or the converse, too little estrogen which is what the OD who prescribed the Wiley Protocol BHRT for me told me). She reminded me (because I know I ran across this somewhere ... damn Jack do you have to be so cotton pickin' prolific? ... that CT will lower estrogen. It's a darn good thing that the night before the meltdown began I added an extra line of estrogen to my normal dose (which is what the OD told me to do previously when I complained about it). So extra 2 lines yesterday and extra one this morning. I skipped CT all together yesterday. I really wasn't in the mood anyway but somewhere in my poorly functioning brain it occurred to me that might not be the best thing to do until I got myself leveled back out.

Still a little sore this morning, physically and emotionally but feeling a bit more normal. god what a trip down the rabbit hole THAT was.

Speaking of the rabbit hole, every single time Jack mentions oysters my brain goes click click click:

"The time has come," the Walrus said,
"To talk of many things:
Of shoes--and ships--and sealing-wax--
Of cabbages--and kings--
And why the sea is boiling hot--
And whether pigs have wings."

"But wait a bit," the Oysters cried,
"Before we have our chat;
For some of us are out of breath,
And all of us are fat!"
"No hurry!" said the Carpenter.
They thanked him much for that.

EEEEEK. MAKE IT STOP, LOL

Back here in the real world, my new concern is that I am not eating enough. Seriously. Yesterday I had 3 strips of thick sliced bacon cooked in the pan then added in the other 5-6 oz of steak tartare that I hadn't finished the night before (I checked and I'd used about 10 ounces of meat but then couldn't eat it all) and an egg and then the usual BPDC with way too much butter. I"m thinking I'm probably tossing about 6 TBS or so because I'm getting the handrolled stuff in the 2 LB block and I just whack off a chunk in the morning. FAT I want FAT. Note that does not mean I want to BE fat, it means I want to EAT fat. Left about a half cup of the BAB or so because I couldn't finish THAT. During the day I had a couple of handfuls of raw almonds (yes yes I know ... NUTS OUT OF SEASON, make it stop!) but for dinner I had another mug of BPDC and then what looked like about 3-4 ounces of Costco's pulled pork. That was it.

I had no hunger other than just before dinner.

OK gotta run Sounds like the bacon is done cooking so need to finish up the BAB and EAT.

Later tomaters.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-13T14:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70520>

while I was in the midst of my little tantrum I did what any good, red-blooded woman would do.

I bought SHOES. TWO pair!

I've been jonesing for a pair of red shoes all winter long.

SQUEEEEEE ... I just hope I can WALK in them when I get them. We'll see.

And for giggles:

although I just spotted another pair I may have to get instead. But I like the ankle strap. The only part of my body that is skinny is my ankles. so maybe I should just stick with my first choice.

Whaddya think?

### caroline — 2013-01-13T17:37:10+0200

### caroline — 2013-01-13T17:39:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70569>

Why is it when you are fat ..... you always long for the really hot guy?...... that makes no sense......you should start with an ugly guy and work your way up - shouldn't you????

### ashryn — 2013-01-13T17:57:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70576>

Caroline.. No! You should always point yourself at the one you're attracted to.. Judging whether you'e going to speak to someone based on what you assume he will think of your place on the 'hotness' scale is doomed madness! People are attracted to each other for all kinds of reasons and physical beauty is way down in the scale of importance for all but the shallowest of beings.

Imnsho..

Darleen, I would love to be the person with the awesome words that will change how you feel about yourself, but i suspect even the most perfect, inspirational ones would be useless. So i just wanted to chime in and let you know i feel for you, and know what you're going through, and about myself at least, i have come to realise i am never going to look at myself and like what i see. Yes, when im fat i jump on the easiest thing and think everything would be awesomer if i was thin, but i remember being thin and actually, i didnt think i was thin, i still found something to dislike..

Perhaps it is the thing that stops us being 'read-only' people.. We are constantly striving for self improvement... Unfortunately sometimes the striving gets sabotaged by the evil emotional imp that makes us unreasonable and has an odd idea about what needs improvement the most.

I dont have an answer, most days i just go with it and do the best i can knowing with my intellectual brain that my emotional brain isnt really all that clever and maybe needs to just be smiled and nodded at as i continue on my way...

Hope youre feeling better today anyway..

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-13T19:22:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70597>

Oh yeah. Poor body image, I really get that.

Years ago, eons actually, I starve myself down to 130 pounds by eating 4 cartons of yogurt a day. That was it. 250 calories each. One day I was walking down the street and saw the reflection of a really REALLY slender woman in the window. I remember thinking, wow, she looks great! Yes. It was me.

I think I've gotten over that a bit because when I lost on that damned Jenny diet, even though I was at 185 which is STILL at least 40 pounds too much, I actually thought I looked pretty good. Dec 2007 at DisneyWorld sitting in front of Nemo ... hence the headdress.

### vkiernan — 2013-01-14T08:18:49+0200

### vkiernan — 2013-01-14T08:25:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70647>

> **caroline said**
>
> Why is it when you are fat ..... you always long for the really hot guy?...... that makes no sense......you should start with an ugly guy and work your way up - shouldn't you????

Actually it does make sense. You go for the one "you" are attracted to. You don't arbitrarily pick out some guy you don't think is attractive and go after him. That is what doesn't make sense. Beauty on the outside is only the first step. The guy could be "hot" and then you talk to him and he is a real dweeb.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-14T13:29:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70680>

Vickie that's definitely one way to see it.

Eye candy is fun to look at but that's about it. Most people who are THAT good looking tend (TEND) to be in love with themselves. Unless the guy is Mick Jagger or Keith Richards ugly (and who's to say they aren't swell guys when you get to know them) why would you make that kind of judgement?

When the evil aunt met my hubby (back when he and I were first dating) the bitch dragged me into my kitchen and in a PRONOUNCED stage whisper told me that "he was the ugliest man she'd ever seen" while laughing at me. Granted, at the time he weighed about 155 pounds (he's 6'2") and had long hair. So no, he was definitely not looking like Brad Pitt. But if I had only seen him the way she did we wouldn't have gotten past "hello."

What's on the inside is always ALWAYS far more important than what's on the outside. Remember never judge a book by its cover. And everyone is beautiful when seen from the right angle.

### caroline — 2013-01-14T17:38:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70728>

So true Darleen - It is funny developing relationships with people on line when you have no clue what they look like - mostly. So we are seeing someone's true essence I think. It is great to see pictures tho - makes us have a better connection I think. I have been trying to put up a pic - but as usual - can't really figure it out. It can't be reduced enough or something? The picture of you at Disney is lovely - how many pounds ago was that? you look perfect! You have an hour glass shape?
I think the weight gets dispersed more evenly when you do . When I am fat It all goes to my middle and I look like a beach ball - my legs and arms stay skinny.
Have you ever tried to buy pants to fit a beach ball?
That reminds me of something I was thinking about the other day - in regard to you. You have been talking about wearing stretch pants - have you tried on pants from before that weren't stretch? Maybe your body comp has changed and you aren't noticing.....Honestly - when my body comp changed I couldn't understand why my clothes were so big. I think when we have been fat and ugly and abused - everything is always our fault and I just thought I had bought stuff that was too big - stupid!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-14T18:27:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70738>

> **caroline said**
>
> Why is it when you are fat ..... you always long for the really hot guy?...... that makes no sense......you should start with an ugly guy and work your way up - shouldn't you????

### Jack Kruse — 2013-01-14T18:31:08+0200

Demand more of you. You are too good to waste any time. Been there and done it. It is time to sculpt.

You can only lose something that you have, but you cannot lose something that you are.

And what you are becoming is something rather special.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-14T18:35:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-70741>

Caroline, pictures are easy. You can use Preview to change the size. Open your pic, then go to Tools and chose Adjust Size. You need to make your avatar about 70 pixels x 70 pixels. You can just drag across what you want to crop ... be sure and hold down the shift key to keep it square.

The picture at DW was taken about 15 months into the jenny diet ... it was Dec 2007. And that was about 50 pounds less of me or halfway to where I want to go. And yeah, on the hourglass. At 130 pounds I was 36-26-36. We will not talk about what those measurements are today.

My body comp really has not changed at all. Fat is still fat in my case. sigh.

On another note, we're going to go see Gangster Squad which should make Jack have a nice hearty laugh. I need a tongue sticking out emoticon to aim his way. pfbbbt will have to do. Ryan Gosling is OK looking but there is something decidedly and deliciously MALE about him. shrug. My tastes never cease to amaze me.

My stomach is upset again today. This time it really wasn't hunger. I do NOT get this. I'm starting to wonder if I've picked up some h.pylori somewhere again. It feels a bit ulcerish.

On a positive note, just got back from the massage therapist. She hurt me but good today. I'm thinking it's probably because of the contorted position I have to be in in the tub when I do CT. 45 minutes all crumped up. I'm gonna start looking at salt water hot tubs (which I would keep at 50Â°... wouldn't DH just LOVE that, LOL). Don't think I'll spend the money but it's free to look. Sure wish he hadn't gotten a while hair a couple of years ago and sold our old one. Of course we were using bromine to keep it clean so maybe it's best it's gone.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-15T12:46:17+0200

> **caroline said**
>

FB doesn't have upload restrictions. This site does. I believe you are limited to either 50px wide or 10KB. If you open your FB avatar in Profile then use the tools to adjust the size it will tell you how big it is. Anything above the limits for this site will be rejected. You just have to keep making it smaller.

Jack, thanks for the tip about Staind... great tune! Now added to my spotify faves.

Checked the cherry angiomas on my belly this morning and they're almost all gone. Just the 2 larger ones are left. I'm now thinking they may have been an indicator of chlorine or bromine toxicity. I spent a couple of years in the chlorine laden pool at the Y, twice a week for an hour, doing water aerobics while I was on that stupid diet. I finally had to quit because when I'd get out of the pool my entire body would be covered with tiny red dots where the water had touched my skin. And our hot tub, which I never used all that much, was bromine laden. So I'm sticking on the iodoral for now. I think the upset stomach of late may well have been detoxing all that gunk.

At least that's my story for now until someone in authority changes my mind with facts that I don't have.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-16T17:40:12+0200

The upset tummy continues and now I have added (hmm, IS there a polite way to say this? guess not) a bad case of the runs. so either I have picked up a bug or I am detoxing like mad. Either way I a feeling not-so-hot. And a bit headachy as well.

Woke up to another ocular migraine so I guess it's time to get my eyes checked. Eye strain sucks.

Crawled outta bed around 9:40 (slugabed... that's me) to find a friend had called to have coffee. whoops. So it was a protein shake, Quelson style, real fast, jump into my jeans and head for town. We always have such a good time and it's a good thing Starbucks isn't real busy because we sat there for 2 and 1/2 hours!

so now I"m back home, my tummy is rolling, my eyes hurt and I think I'll go lie down for a bit because I want to be alert for the Q&A tonight. I already registered and bookmarked the site so I'm ready to go. This should be FUN!
