### DarleenMB — 2013-01-17T14:49:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71250>

Kirsta, I took a short break at about the 2 1/2 hour mark but came back for the finale.

Caroline, I can always use a hug but it is definitely detoxing symptoms. Yesterday my brain finally clicked into gear and I had a small aha moment. If I'm detoxing, DRINK MORE WATER you big dummy. I chugged a mug of green tea and 2 big glasses of water and felt amazingly better in a short time. Went to bed later and boom! Started feeling crappy again. Felt great this morning, took the iodoral and am waiting to see what transpires. I"m drinking a mug of green tea and big glass of water as I write this. I foresee a day spent running in and out of the bathroom.

Learned some very interesting tidbits during the Q&A but am now wondering what happened to the links and/or the optimal program.

On another note, the cats woke me up (AGAIN) at 3AM doing the bug tussle on the bed. Two (neutered) males and there's always a dominance play going on at some point during the day or night. BAD boys. So I lay there until about 5:30 while my mind did its usual monkey dance when that happens. I rehashed some of yesterdays events and came up with some more insights about moi. Here's hoping I remembered the HTML code for some things.

**The Art of Invisibility**

Somewhere about the time I uncovered all my memories of the abuse (I sometimes think I should capitalize that ... kind of like in "The Girl With the Dragon Tattoo" where the narrator talks about "When All the Evil Happened"and you automatically use a voice thick with drama as you say it to yourself ... but I wander. Again.) About that time I also started gaining weight. And it was at that exact moment I became invisible to men. I'm not saying I ever had to fight them off with a large club but there would be moments when a man would look my way and there'd be that little bt of crackling energy -- the stuff that makes you smile, makes you feel like you're made of light and air and are wholly the most desirable woman on the planet. Just for that moment. It stopped. Totally dead in its tracks. I remember going out for drinks one evening with a girlfriend. Some guy came over to our table and hit on her for like 20 minutes and it was literally as if I weren't even there.

Ok so it's slightly damaging to the ego but nothing I couldn't live with. Until you factor in the ego demolishing I was getting at home. When you love someone, you share a life, a home and a bed with them, and they spurn you (literally and figuratively) at every turn; when you have cried a river or two or three of tears; when you are told you are no longer desirable because you're too fat (and that was like 50 pounds less ago), the invisibility factor hardens up to the consistency of concrete and you stop caring that no one sees you. It becomes comforting and comfortable.

Then you get your parts yanked and your libido goes with them and peace at last. No more humiliating hints at intimacy that are soundly and roundly ignored. You are completely and totally phase shifted out of reality. My own little world.

And before you ask why I stayed I will simply say that the answer is as simple and as complex as a sigh. And not something I want to share.

Where is all this leading? Why am I, once again, baring my soul for all to see? Well something rather odd happened yesterday. Nothing earth shattering but it has left me wondering if my Phase Shifter needs to be sent to the quantum mechanic for a tuneup.

I met my friend for coffee at Starbucks yesterday. I was on time, she was late, as usual. This is a very small place with room for about half a dozen tables and a couple of overstuffed chairs. At some point last year they removed 3 of the tables and replaced them with one large table that seats 8. If all other tables are taken you wind up sharing the big one with strangers. And that was the scenario yesterday. A nice looking man had taken up residence with his laptop on one end of the table (I don't know how old he was .. probably somewhere between 25 and 35 -- these days anyone under 40 looks like a teenager to me). I took a seat at the other end, straddling the table leg so as to keep a goodly distance. Phase shifter ON.

When he got up to get his coffee, he came around behind me instead of going around the other way. He bumped into my purse which I had hung off the back of the chair. He made some funny little comment about it as he excused himself and I didn't think too much about it. (Checked the Phase Shifter again to make sure it was working. Huh. Lights are all green. Shake it a couple of times. Shrug and put it back in the holster.)

My friend showed up about then and we sat there for nearly 3 hours. She is a dear woman who is even bigger than I am (both taller and wider). She is a bit loud, LOL but we have the best times. She is bright and funny and at one point in her life she was one of 2 women engineers on the railroad. Yup. She drove those big ol' locomotives. The other woman was as petite as this one is oversized. They earned a lot of respect from the men because it is not easy to move a mile-long coal train up that hill out of Sheridan. It's not like stepping on the accelerator and just going.

ANYway. We laughed and told silly stories and she asked more about CT and is thinking this summer she might use the creek, or it might just be a ditch, behind her house to soak in. I told her I'd be up and we could CT together while dodging ticks, bears and mountain lions.

Right about then the guy with the laptop got up and started putting his stuff away. Now there had been this invisible wall between us the entire time. At least I thought it was there. But he made a point of saying something to me as he got ready to leave .. something about solving all the world's problems at this table. I don't know. It was just ... startling.

I think my Phase Shifter is definitely broken.

### diane — 2013-01-17T20:24:56+0200

Well, I'm not exactly sure what a Phase Shifter is - but it sounds to me like you're radiating electrons!

Darleen - you are getting so much insight into yourself - you are in motion girl

I keep meaning to ask you - are you going to Santa Fe???? Did anyone \[you know who I mean\] ever give details??
A friend wants me to go to India later this year with her -so I have to start thinking about what my one trip this year is actually going to be??? ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-18T14:17:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71424>

Caroline, the short answer to your first question is "no." I starved myself for over a decade in an attempt to keep thin. In the Fall of '79 I lived on 750 calories a day for several months and got my weight down to about 132 pounds. The MINUTE I ate any amount over that I started packing on the pounds. Reverse T3 at work.

No, no one every gave details about Santa Fe other than where to sign up which has the dates. And no one ever said "boo" about if they were going although I think I read in the original thread that a couple of the quieter members had signed up. I'm still toying with the idea of driving down but as Jack said, I'm a wuss.

It looks like the weather will be sunny and dry but it's two long days of driving down and then two back. Since I'm not much good a traveling I'd "have" to spend a day at the out-laws recovering from the drive. I swear we have driven all over the West in the last 15 years and have been in every single state. Of course when I say that you need to know that means \*I\* did ALL the driving. Now it takes an act of Congress to get me to drive any further than Billings.

I really love Santa Fe and basically all of NM. Although these days I'd guess SF is pretty much even more of a tourist trap than when I was there the last time. Taos used to be a sleepy little bump in the road. So was Albuquerque. Man, not any more. Being a stick in the mud I liked it better before when nobody even knew where it was.

OK, enough of the chit chat. I thought I had done pretty good yesterday with my eating. Had what passes for me as a BAB. And in case you missed it that means that I cook up 3 pieces of thick sliced CHOPPED bacon in the pan so I can keep all the fat, stir in a couple of eggs and call it good. Now I have a mug of decaf green tea and then a mug of BPDC with an obscene amount of butter in it. That was about 10:30? About 3 I realized I needed to eat so I had a packet of water packed sardines, a dill pickle and a few sweet potato chips. Hubby had fixed paleo stew so I had a bowl of that around 6-ish which OK was too close to lunch but I was now HUNGRY. I also had half an avocado and another mug of BPDC.

I woke up about 1:30 thinking I was in the tropics. Missy (my almost-20 year old cat) loves to snuggle up under the covers. She's only about 7 pounds but she is as hot as an atomic furnace. Three was snugged up against my chest and Sammy kept wandering up and over my head. Now you know why DH has taken to sleeping in the guest room this week with the door SHUT. ARGH. Nothing like being on the bottom of a cat pile.

I lay wide awake for about 3 hours when I couldn't take the complaining from my stomach another second. I snagged a couple of pieces of cold meat out of the stew and washed them down with half a glass of water. AH peace at last. Woke up about 9 when I heard the propane truck rumbling out in the driveway. got on here intending to do a quick update when my sister calls. AH it doesn't rain but it pours. So halfway through my post I spend a good 45 minutes on the phone and finally got to my breakfast. Yes, I was way late. Now back here for a quick finish and then CT! Haven't been in the tub for a week because of low estrogen issues ... we do NOT need another meltdown. But I miss it and today I'm getting back at it.

soooo. That's all I know for now. Waiting for updates on the Optimal challenge or whatever it is they're calling it.

I'm going to write this on my steering wheel......I can go all day without drinking if I get super busy at work!

### Charlotte — 2013-01-19T03:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71565>

Caroline, you don't need a snowsuit to rob a bank.. LOL
My suggestion would be to take a big jar of High meat, drop it on the counter and open it.

Darleen, thats a story I can relate to very well. My Dad used to look at me like an artist viewing his painting in progress and say: you could be so beautiful if you weren't so fat. Years later, I married someone like my Dad who used to say: I could love you so much if you weren't so fat. Needless to comment,.
But... the message I got was, it was all my fault.

In 1995 I was working in Melbourne calling the family every evening to see how they were doing and my husband was telling me again some story (I dont remember what it was) and something clicked in my head, as simple as that and I knrew that I was going to end this drama. First thing when I got back to Germany was calling the lawyer and filing for divorce. Then I went home and told my husband. I never forget his face. ME? Leaving HIM? If anything it should be him leaving me. (he did that a lot but always came back and I had told him before that one day I would be leaving, but I would not come back)
He even left me with a 2 month old baby all by myself in America and I still joined him again in Germany 2 years later, just so you know how screwed up I was. We had another child 8 years after the first and he was six, when I finally left.
Then for years, my youngest son told me that I should lose weight so his Dad would come back. That was heartbreaking, as it seemed that this story was repeating itself endlessly from generation to generation and that even my child should suffer from this distortion of truth. Fact was, I gained weight very early to protect myself from the physical and mental abuse and to create the selffullfilling prophecy that I was not lovable and ugly, to mention one of many ideas I had about myself. (including of feeling invisible for men who were a lot of cause for pain for me)

But like you, I am a rebel, and something (I realized only much later) kept me from losing as I wanted to be loved and accepted for the person I was and not because I looked as I did.
And now, something funny has happened. Because I lost all that weight, someone said to me: your sons must be so proud of you. And no... they didn't even notice (we talk over Skype a lot and they can see me). I told my oldest son this a few days ago and he said: you are my Mom and I see your person, I never see your weight. And when I told my youngest son, that I had lost all that weight he said: Mom, I didn't notice but I am so happy for you. Thats when I realized that healing has taken place for him too.

This is simply a little history of Charlotte and I do not want to imply anything, but only to let you know how very well I can relate.

I personally think that you are doing really great because you follow Dr. Kruses advice and hang in there patiently. Patience has never been a trait of mine and I learn a lot from your posts. Thank you for that.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-19T14:08:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71615>

Caroline, you don't need a snowsuit! silly woman. Although going straight from high summer to high winter might be a little challenging. My car has a good heater as well.

Charlotte thank you so much for sharing. You sound just like my sister on the weight thing. But weight gain is not so simple as saying you gained to hide your emotions. Sorry. I won't let her get away with that any more either. When you have run out of reasons why you gain weight that one seems to be the most obvious ... but that's just blaming the victim. Weight gain is a signal that something has gone awry with your biochemistry. If I've learned NOTHING else from Jack's posts I have learned that.

I'm glad you finally dumped the baggage. sounds like he deserved it. And thank you for sharing all of that!

BAB this morning was, as usual, about 30 minutes late. I pan fried 2 pieces of thick sliced bacon then cooked two salmon filets in the fat. They were about 4oz each or thereabouts. I ate almost all of it. Green tea and now sipping my BPDC.

45 minutes of CT yesterday and last night when I went to bed my nipples were, once again, REALLY sore. Crap. I stayed out of the tub an entire week, upped my estrogen by 2ml per day, had just gotten rid of the soreness and now it's back. Guess it's time to Ask Jack. My sleep was also affected.

It's taken me a week to realize that the sleep issues I've suddenly been having the last several weeks HAVE to be related to dropping estrogen levels. for the longest time my sleep was GREAT. I'd lie down, close my eyes, roll over and it would be morning. Lately I'm having trouble falling asleep, am waking at least once a night and many times I cannot get back to sleep for several hours. Sounds like menopause all over again.

Huh. One step forward, six back.

Oh, and Charlotte, patience comes easy when you have hit the end of the line. I have NO where else to go. Nothing else has worked or only worked for like a minute or two. I've said it before ... I trust Jack because HE HAS BEEN THERE. He knows what it's like and he fixed himself. THAT is why I follow him. It isn't blind faith either. He backs up what he teaches us with science. And he knows how I crave knowledge so keep feeding us the good stuff!

### endless — 2013-01-19T14:44:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71626>

Sore nipples is low estrogen? I didn't know that....

I get general breat tenderness from mid cycle onward, but I always thought it was rising progesterone.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-19T15:30:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71634>

Rising progesterone or low estrogen. Same thing. The OD who prescribed my WP told me to up my estrogen when I complained about sore nipples. I was quite surprised. But it makes sense. If your estrogen levels are low and your progesterone rises it's going to lower your estrogen even more.

So it's not the CT according to Ask Jack. Something else is going on. DAMN it. Now I have to try and figure out what happened about 2 months ago when all this nonsense started.

Guess the first step is to really tighten up the leptin RX and maybe cut back on the fat and eat more protein. I cannot believe when Jack said the other night that it can take up to 24 months to convert from being a sugar burner to being a fat burner. I'm pretty sure I heard him right. So maybe I need more protein for now. I guess I'll try that and see what happens. I sure miss my good sleep though.

I hope you get that figured out Darleen. I am just realizing I am feeling like crap - nauseated. I am wondering if I should back off all the Progesterone for a week and see what happens. My Pregnenolne is very high - don't have a clue what to do about all this.

I had a funny dream last night - I dreamt I was at Disney with all you guys and brought all the wrong shoes!!! How dreadful is that? Not just wrong shoes - but REALLY WRONG STUPID shoes. I was mortified! When we finally get to disney you will have to help me with a packing list ... ### DarleenMB — 2013-01-19T20:28:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71695>

Lyndra I am. sort of. I cut the dose way back because it was hitting me so hard. I worked up to a full tab and had even added in another 1/2 tab daily. When I started on the Iodoral about 2 weeks ago I quite the cytomel. 4 days ago I started again. I don't feel particularly energized and I take it first thing in the morning with breakfast so I don't think it's really causing sleep issues. I took full tab doses and yesterday started feeling a bit over caffeinated ... you know almost like grinding my teeth kind of amped up. It passed pretty quickly but this morning I chose to take just 1/2 tab. I'm really trying to go by how I feel.

The funny thing is I get sleepy right around 8pm and I can be yawning like crazy up to bedtime then seem to have trouble dropping off. Not so much last night but I also did wake up about 1:30 again.

Jack's on an electron kick (I'm waiting to read the next blog post so until I do ...) so I think I need to go find a bare patch of ground and stand barefoot as long as I can take it. The ground is frozen and we're due to get snow tomorrow. Probably shoulda done it today because it was sunny for quite a while.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-20T09:45:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71786>

Good Morning, Darleen ( I guess it is morning at your part of the planet)
The meat was great last night, but the greed did not cut it. I GAINED 2 pounds over night. That was a dinner I just did not need as I wasn't hungry at all. I had eaten a ton of liverwurst for a late lunch with 2 psyllium rolls and I was still stuffed. But I could not let my friend eat it all alone, so I joined. Oh so what, its just as well. You gain and lose and gain and lose. Sometime this rollercoaster will be over. Then on top I have some pretty heavy detox symptoms, so I wasn't all that good of a company last night (blind as a bat as my eyes are swollen, itchy and inflammed). After dinner, we were both so stuffed that we ended on the couches in the living room, so we went to bed early and found each other again at 5 in the morning in the living room, back on the couches with a blanket and started chatting. That was nice. Its great to have good company. Another thing I like about this forum, there are so many people and a common goal.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-20T14:14:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-71856>

Krista ... the image that brings to mind makes want to alternately weep with frustration and LMAO.

Caroline, I haven't danced for, well as long as I haven't _danced_ if you get my drift.

Charlotte that is TOO funny! The town I live in has a ton of artists and lot of them working in bronze. So, emulating Loveland, CO bronzes have been appearing all over town the last 8-10 years. Last year the life-size bronze of a fisherman (old world) was placed on the corner of the main intersection downtown. EVERY single time I went to town I'd be startled by it because that's where I turn right to go to the store and I always thought "he" was someone getting ready to cross the street, LOL. It was changed out a few months ago to a totem pole.

And Charlotte ... bit of advice ... put the scale in the closet and close the door. This isn't about that number on the scale and yes I know ... I still fall prey to that every now and then. I work on keeping to my own advice but I"m only human.

Lyndra ... I had the answer to the sleep thing then promptly spaced it off. I asked Jack about CT and falling estrogen levels and was told (if you missed it) that I'm losing electrons. See post above. So yesterday I decided to put the protein BACK full bore. BAB was 2 filets of salmon (about 7-8 oz) and some bacon and BPDC. "Lunch" was a Quelson protein shake with 2 scoops of WPI (40g protein) and dinner was the last of the paleo stew.

I slept really good, only waking up a couple of times to go pee and promptly fell back asleep. Woke about 9:30? but didn't get my lazy ass out of bed and would still be there except hubby got up and Three (my youngest and mouthiest cat) kept walking all over me yelling at me to GET OUT OF BED!

Soreness has lessened appreciably so am headed for the tub in about 20 minutes. BAB this morning was 2 pieces of thick sliced bacon, 4.5 oz of ground GF beef, 2 eggs and yes I poked it all down and am now STUFFED. A mug of green tea and now sipping a mug of BPDC with less butter than I usually dump in.

My current plan is to alternate the protein shakes with some kind of fishy little midday meal/snack. Today I'll probably just eat some sardines since I ate breakfast so late.

Also, even though it's frickin' cold today and snow is forecast I intend, once I'm outta the tub and dressed, to go stand outside my studio (fully dressed, however) in my bare feet on the patch of dirt next to the house that doesn't have snow on it. THAT will be a daily occurrence until/unless I can't get to the dirt. I'm not standing on icy snow. No way. No how.

One last thing. Some years ago a good friend from Toronto sent me magnetic mug. He swore drinking water that had been in the mug gave him more energy. I thought he was full of it. And I don't mean energy. So the mug has traveled from cupboard to pantry until I went hunting for it last night. I thought I'd thrown it out but I finally found it. Washed it and filled it with water. I let it sit overnight and put my D-ribose and MSM in it this morning. I figured WTH. If it does anything then I'm up one, if it doesn't, I needed the water anyway.

Would you all think I'm way strange if I go fondle my new shoes? LOL

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-21T13:19:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72057>

OKEYDOKEY Just polished off EMF2 (membership has it's privileges) and all I can say is holy shit.

I watched most of that video that Thor posted ... and Jack ... from the German doctor. Sent it off to all my cell phone loving friends and relatives, not that they will get it. When DH came home yesterday afternoon I announced I was getting rid of all the cordless phones (he got emailed the link to the vid as well). I got a snort and some snarky comment about what crazy thing I was up to now.

And from the same vid ... I know we have a smart meter. Now to figure out how to force MDU to remove it short of sabotage. Anyone have any bright ideas on shielding the damn thing if I can't make them remove it?

WiFi turned off at night but starting to think about hunting down the Cat3 cables and going back to direct hookup on the modem.

I will say I am now finally and happily planted forever in Wyoming. The nearest cell phone antennae are at least 1/2 a mile away from my house, there are NO cell towers anywhere near us, the radiation map says NONE anywhere near us. whew. There's more but I'll wait until the big unveiling to continue the discussion. This stuff came from the german docs video and some of Thor's links.

BAB choked down ... every last bite. Feeling WAY better, thanks, since I put the protein back in. I guess I am not nearly ready to dine primarily on fat no matter what I was craving. And wasn't that just weird? Craving FAT?! I don't usually crave things. I haven't eaten chocolate in months although yesterday I did finally open up that bar of 85% and had a couple of bites. It was ok. (did I just say that?!) Take it or leave it. Probably leave it again.

I had some canned mussels for "lunch" ... ewwwww. Sounded good, tasted blech. Too overdone. So I polished off a double handful of shrimp instead

Dinner was a hunk of the last of the pulled pork from Costco with a handful of lettuce w/homemade olive oil/balsamic vinegrette.

Slept pretty good .... hubs went back to the guest room and closed the door so I was at the bottom of the cat pile again. You wouldn't think a 7 pound cat could force a fully grown and overweight woman to wake up and let her under the blankets. 3 or 4 times. You'd be wrong. I'd lock them out of the bedroom but they just sit outside, whine and cry and scratch at the door. It's easier to let 'em snuggle.

ANNNND it's Monday. oh boy. Even in retirement monday's suck.

### caroline — 2013-01-22T01:52:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72252>

It has been a big day hasn't it? I guess - at least I think that I live in a good place. your spot sounds beautiful

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-22T14:35:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72325>

Woke up at 2AM to find myself the filling in a cat sandwich. How is it that two 7 pound cats can defy Newtonian physics? How do they manage to increase their mass to the point where they can pin me to the bed? Ever try to pick up a cat that didn't want to go? That's what I mean.

so I lay there for 3 hours pondering things and managed to write an entire post in my head. A LONG post. Came back in here this morning and decided not to post it. I will, however, share some of my insights from all that thinking.

Allow me to quote from an Eagles song to get the ball rolling ... "things in this life change very slowly if they ever change at all."

Words of wisdom when one is trying to change the world. Which all led to me to thinking about a book by Marilyn Ferguson titled "The Aquarian Conspiracy." I read it in the late 70's and it's now out of print. But the one take away I got was a phrase she borrowed from biology. She applied it to psychology and it is a very interesting concept. Paradigm shift. See the above quote.

If you aren't familiar with the concept or the book, I'll do my best to explain. In a closed system, as new thoughts and ideas are introduced it starts to get a little crowded. The more new information that comes in the more uncomfortable it gets. It's like your skin gets too tight or you fell back into your body while you were sleeping and didn't get it on quite right. This is where cognitive dissonance comes into play which only makes one even more uncomfortable.

Eventually the system has to expand to encompass all the new information. When that happens there is a _shift_ that takes place as all the information rearranges itself. I like to shorthand it by calling it the AHA moment.

I believe this is the uphill battle now facing Jack. Scientists (and I include physicians in that lot) tend to suffer from a hardening of the attitudes. It's easy to see why when you take into consideration all the years spent in education learning that things are one way. Their work lives are spent reinforcing this dogma. It is almost impossible for anyone caught in this mindset to accept new and untested ideas without a putting up a big fight. See the above quote again.

As an example I'm going to point you to the use of statins and low fat diets as a means to "cure" heart disease. How many of you buy into that? Now how many doctors out there believe this is the proper course of treatment. See. The "conventional wisdom" is nothing more than brainwashing and we, as a society including physicians and scientists and people who should KNOW better, have all bought into this nonsense. My husband is a perfect example. He has been on statins for 30+ years (I'll wait while some of you put 2 and 2 together and come up with AHA).

To say his job was stressful is an understatement. Rail freight does not have a schedule. He was ON CALL 24/7/50. He could get home, tie up (that's RR jargon for park the train and file the paperwork), get home and get called BACK to work 8 hours from the time he tied up. He was gone more than he was home living in motel rooms at the other end of the road and eating crap from local diners. Way back when we first moved here many of the guys self medicated to the point where management finally had to do something. Railroading is one of the most dangerous jobs there are. There's a reason for the 8 hour rest rule and it's not enough. ANYWAY. STRESS and hereditary factors had pushed his "cholesterol" up over 400. So he's been given what I consider to be a poison for 30 years and we have both suffered.

He won't quit. He's 71, retired, out of all that stress and home resting and enjoying life and eating mostly OK. I can't control his every movement. But he's still on the damned statins.

Hardening of the attitudes. It's almost impossible to fight until someone goes through a paradigm shift. Good luck with that. Closed minds don't take easily to new information if they take it in at all.

I hope that all made some sense. It kept me awake for a good 3 hours when I should have been sleeping.

BAB ... 8 ounces of salmon and a fistful of rice-paper-thin bacon, green tea, and a waiting mug of BPDC with CO in it.

Skipped lunch yesterday because of a late start and will be doing the same again today. No idea what to fix for dinner. Hubby is sick with a cold ... smacked him in the face yesterday. Good thing he's sleeping the guest room.

Time for CT and I need to read EMF 1 and 2 again. Later!

### diane — 2013-01-22T14:48:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72329>

AHA!

Very interesting post Darleen. And I agree with you about changing attitudes and fixed ideas. But someone has to do it! LOL. I work with someone who is doing the same in trying to change attitudes and behaviours towards dogs, and it is an uphill battle for sure. And the poison and vitriol that people spit out is shocking - and getting worse. I think I have more of an insight into why now. But he does it because he has a passion for it and has made it his mission so he doesn't let small minded people stop him. I assume that Jack will do the same.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-22T16:26:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72342>

I'm not saying he can't. What I'm saying it that, as my sig line says ... true pioneers are the ones with arrows in their backs. Scorn and derision are cheap commodities and are what small, closed minds traffic in. To expect anything else is to court trouble. I was a Girl Scout ... Be Prepared.

CT ... damn I still can't get that overflow drain stoppered up. The water level dropped 4" again in 45 minutes. Boy am I shaking. hard to type. Why does my temp drop? it was 95 when I got out of the water. sheesh. I thought it was supposed to go UP???

### caroline — 2013-01-22T17:18:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72348>

I have the same problem with my daughter - she is in terrible pain alot of the time - but simple will not listen to me. She looks at me like I am crazy! well that could be - but - still... She loves her pain Doc and simple says if there was something new he would have told her.....end of discussion. I have a son-in-law with Crohns and won't give up his wheat/grains and wine and beer - he has had three major re sections done. These are young people who should be out there turning over every stone to find answers and improve their health - the closed mindedness and inertia is appalling .....

### PaulaRichards — 2013-01-22T17:33:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72351>

Oh Caroline, that's gotta be tough having all this info that could help someone, esp like your SIL, having crohns and needing resections. I know ppl have to be willing to help themselves but I know ppl too that just don't do any research whatsoever and totally depend on their docs knowledge. Have you ever tried printing articles to give him? I have a hubby here and listens to some extent but sometimes it helps immensely if he hears what I'm telling him from a "credible" source lol. For instance I've been putting vit d drops in his water for a long time and one day I came home from work and he told me he heard on Dr. Oz (not saying he's credible) how good vit d is!!! (Insert smirk) "Really"!!! I said.

### diane — 2013-01-22T18:13:12+0200

Yes, I agree Darleen!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-22T18:39:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72373>

Caroline I'm sorry to hear about your daughter and SIL. I've said it before, no one can diss you like your family. Doesn't matter if you're a particle physicist, a neurosurgeon or the President of the US. Your family KNOWS you so you get no respect.

I have finally reached the point (rising dopamine levels?) where I can just casually hand off a bit of info "just FYI" and let it go. It isn't that I don't care ... of course I still do, but rather there's no point in trying to force the issue or explain why whatever it is you've recommended might be helpful. give 'em the info and change the subject, that's my new mantra.

Honestly I'm tired of trying to help my family. They tell me on the one hand "you are so smart" while on the other they dismiss everything I say with eye rolls and nasty comments. OKAY fine. Here ya go and I'll visit you in the hospital.

### diane — 2013-01-22T19:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72388>

Well, to both of you "twisted sisters" - many of us listen to you, and respect your opinion!

I don't try with my family. My mom believes that doctors know everything and worries about the supplements etc that I take. My response is have you ever seen the list of side effects in drugs? And you think that is better? Or her doctor who hasn't taken severe stomach problems (and bringing up foam in her mouth) seriously for 10 years. Then both she and my dad have colon cancer. But I try. I even suggested she tried giving up wheat for just 2 weeks to see if there is a difference - but she has no idea what she will eat. LOL. Oh well - I will continue to try different approaches. Maybe I'm wrong, and maybe I'm right. But I know enough to know that doing the same thing ain't working.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-23T13:52:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72529>

Diane you are definitely one smart woman!

Slept really well last night; only woke up once? Maybe twice but I'm not sure. So getting back to plenty of protein seems to be the right direction. How easy it is to lose my way.

Dinner last night was 7 oz of steak, half an avocado and some sweet potato. BAB this morning was a 5oz packet of pink salmon, heated in CO with 2 FF eggs scrambled in and a mug of green tea. Enjoying my mug of BPDC with less butter and a TBS of CO.

Hubby is still sick but is at least ambulatory this morning. So I know he's still alive.

No deep thoughts to share this morning (lucky you). Have more errands to run since Monday was a holiday and I didn't get to finish them then. I'm headed to Home Depot for a new light fixture for our computer room. It's the only room in the house with no windows. Yeah. Great, eh? And we have a fluorescent fixture in here which I have always hated. You know, I've all but lived in this room for the last 2.5 years and all that time kept thinking it was NOT healthy because of those two things PLUS the computer. Turns out I shoulda listened to my intuition.

I've thought about moving my computer out to my little studio because it's full of light. However, the ceiling needs work and I've been told I will have to move a lot of stuff out so DH can do it. So I think I'll wait until that gets done. Or maybe not. He won't be getting to it for several months and, come on DARLEEN, it's not like it's a major effort to move the damned computer a few feet. geez.

Off to buy that tri-meter from Amazon and wincing at the cost.

Well at least we've got the option for full featured posting. That's a plus.

Dinner ... shrimp and a pile of lightly sauteed bay scallops. I'm stuffed.

forgot to mention my EMF meter thingy arrived. I had no idea I was supposed to use it to hunt ghosts. pfbbbt.

ANYway. Only two major problems so far. The microwave (which I now have unplugged and am seriously thinking about getting rid of it) and my desk lamp! ### endless — 2013-01-26T04:06:33+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-26T22:41:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72753>

Krista it's already packed and back in the box with a return slip. And yeah, I ran around the house with it checking stuff. I don't know that I really learned anything useful from it though. Live and learn.

BAB was 5+oz of GF ground beef, 2 pieces thick bacon, 2 eggs and my mug of BPDC with CO and small amt of butter. Trying to cut back on the dairy.

Ct was ... weird. I thought I had turned the tap all the way over to cold but evidently didn't because when I sat down and lay back ... it felt oddly "warm." I was expecting the \*GASP\* and not being able to breathe for 2 minutes. I did shiver a lot though. When I got out at the 45 minute mark I checked the temp and it was 63Â° so must have started about 55Â°. I also tried duct taping over the edges of the over=flow cover. didn't work. Still leaked out at least 3" of water while I soaked. crap. I'd add more water as I go BUT the tap is like 4' up from the rim of the tub so (lazy person that I am) I'd have to stand up.

Found the following on FB this morning ... Now why can't \*I\* have this attitude? Because I KNOW that eventually the fat will be gone. It would be nice to like myself while I'm working on this.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-27T19:27:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72897>

I keep thinking I'm losing a little weight and when I get on the scale ... nope. Just your imagination. Nothing has budged. Then I get frustrated and want to yell 'WHY AREN'T I LOSING?!" and then I remember the RT3 and just want to bang my head on the nearest wall. sigh

Dinner last night was orange roughy again (I had forgotten just how yummy it is ... all it needs is a bit of S&P) and some shrimp and this time I remember the half of an avocado. And a glass of kombucha.

Slept fine after I finally fell asleep. I had dozed off when the cats decided to rumble. Took another hour after that to finally drift off. Woke up about 6-ish but lazed in bed for another couple of hours until Missy finally had her way and shoved me out of bed. Cats.

BAB as usual. It's overcast this morning so no sunbathing. boo. Going to the tanning salon tomorrow. 6 minutes and don't forget to ask about the ballast, Darleen!

Hubby is still fighting the cold. I hate to say it but having the bedroom all to myself (well, except for the cats) has been kind of nice. I'm sure he's thinking the same thing since they tend to pile on him otherwise.

Still bummed about going back to this format. I'm having trouble navigating again. I'd just finally gotten the hang of the other format ... sure wish they could have straightened it out.

Ennui. word of the day.

### diane — 2013-01-27T20:05:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-72899>

Could be body comp changing Darleen. I've felt that before - I've felt better and my pants fit better, but the scale didn't really move. So weird when that happens. And I don't think it's always because muscle weighs more than fat - but not saying I have a better explanation.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-27T20:51:43+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-28T18:09:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73010>

Boy today is definitely MONDAY. Got into a dustup over on FB with someone I do not know ... she said something hurtful and mean and I reacted. Nice little blood pressure spike there. I hate being laughed at. In my book, people who poke you with a sharp stick are bullies. ESPECIALLY people you don't know and who don't know you. Can you tell I'm still pissed?

ANYway. I'm now feeling low=estrogeny again. I was thinking maybe my pharmacist had screwed up my estrogen prescription but then I got to thinking that all this began right about the time I started supplementing with pregnenolone. My DHEA test had come back high and the preg. was flatlined. So I figured I probably need to supplement. Now I'm thinking that may not have been such a good idea. My sleep has suffered, I've been emotional (jeebus I have been emotional), parts have hurt, in short I feel like a small train wreck today. I guess I'll stop taking the stuff for a while and see if things improve.

Managed to choke down my BAB in spite of weeping into the bacon fat. See what I mean about being cranky? Last night's dinner was tuna salad and half an avocado. The 2 meals a day has become routine. Nothing I had planned, it's just sorta happened.

Still doing 25mcg of cytomel daily and 50mg of iodine + 300mg of selenium. Still need to find a doc to get my thyroid rechecked to see if there's been any improvement on the RT3 issue. My massage therapist gave me the name of a nurse practitioner she knows. I'm hesitant to call her, don't know why.

It snowed yesterday and everything is all pretty and clean and white again. We're due to have snow all week (YAY!) and temps in the low 20's during the day.

It's CT today and then the tanning salon for 6 minutes of toasting.

Boy I wish things would go back to where they were before I took this unexpected detour.

Probably because I've been noticing a bloated tummy sticking out of my dress pants today, I wonder if they'd fire me if I wore pj's to work?!!! Oh wth...

Well, OK so that probably won't fix it but I'll sure as hell feel better.

Went over to the Life Extension page and am reading up on pregnenolone. The only thing I can think is it's converting to progesterone and causing a drop in my estrogen. But WTH do I know.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73354>

You don't know who Patrick Stewart is??? Seriously? Most famous for Star Trek: The Next Generation tv series and movies. YOU MUST watch!

speaking of SF ... watched "The Fifth Element" last night for about the 6th time. It is a pretty silly little flick but the subtext is rich and thought provoking. I have to admit that while watching it I kept having this rather heated reaction to Bruce Willis ... and I am not a fan believe me. Must be the progesterone, LOL, cannot think of any other reason for it.

Went to bed last night with a headache and woke up with a worse one that is getting worse by the minute. Not sure if it's a sympathy reaction to Gretchen's 2 day migraine (which I sincerely hope has left her by now), muscle tension in my lower back (I'm thinking of having DH hammer at those muscles with the meat tenderizer), or if I have succumbed to his bug. crap. On toast.

Speaking of that, I wonder if my favorite fictional witch has a new adventure out. I'll have to go check Amazon. I hate paying the same frickkin' price for an ebook that those greedy assed publishers charge for a PRINT book but I may have to cave in and just do it. Charlaine Harris should have a new Sookie Stackhouse out by now. There goes my monthly book allowance. I just bought "The Joy of Sexus" which is an historical look at sex 2,000 years ago. Looks to be ... hmmm ... interesting if nothing else. DAMN I have sex on the brain lately.

ANYhoo. In talking to my sister the other day she mentioned our brother had called her and she was upset, as usual. I had to remind her ... he lies. About everything. He is a master manipulator as well. But the bottom line is he lies. And once again she admitted that was true but she didn't understand it. So once again I had to explain it to her. Denial is a tough nut to crack.

She and I had the benefit of our Mom during those crucial formative years. Our brother did not. She divorced that that that ... I don't know what to call him ... lazy shiftless bum of a husband (and my father) before our brother was born. We were living with her parents and she had to work. She HAD to work. So the three of us were left pretty much on our own on a daily basis. No one taught our brother the difference between truth and lies (or make believe shit). Every word he uttered was taken as absolute gospel. So here he is ... 59 years old and he just makes shit up. He's wily enough to figure out which of your buttons to push to get you on his side so he does that too.

I'm not saying he lies with malicious intent. I just do not believe he knows the difference. The manipulation is another story. I'm neither justifying nor condoning his behavior. It is what it is. And it's difficult to accept. But in the end, he is our blood and as angry as he makes me I still feel love for him. I just have to step waaaay back and try and keep proper perspective.

Ah the soap opera that is my life. Ain't it fun.

Dentist appt in 90 minutes to get my teeth cleaned. Oh boy. Hope I got all the salmon out from between my teeth.

Weighed myself this morning just to punish myself. Just kidding. I'm not a masochist but sometimes I have to wonder. Here's the thing. My weight has not budged so much as a tenth of a pound in weeks. My pants are feeling loose through the thighs and the waistband feels loose as well. hmmm.

Hope springs eternal or some such bullshit.

forgot to mention yesterday that my drive to town (we live 7 miles out) was solid ice all the way in. That's always so much fun.

Well I have medium underwear that's not quite bikini but sort of ... low enough to be annoying when you have even a bit of a belly because it pushes the band down it drives me nuts. ### DarleenMB — 2013-02-02T18:36:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73805>

Gregory House: â€œEverybody lies.â€

If you've been to my blog you know what I've been wrestling with for 2 days now. Late last night (or early this morning, Iâ€™m not sure what time it was) I lay awake putting pieces together. So if you feel like it, put on a diving mask and come with me into the murky depths of my subconscious where all the pain and angst lie in wait.

All through my childhood and adolescence I was lied to by family and friends but mostly lied about. My brother still lies. I really should have known better because that goes all the way back to when we were children. One incident always comes to mind. He might have been 2, I was probably 7. All three of us were out in the front driveway playing, no where near one another. He fell down and, as children do, immediately began wailing as if he were being murdered. Grandpa was near by and he hollered out â€œWhat happened?â€ My brother screamed â€œshe pushed me!â€

I didnâ€™t hesitate. I jumped up and ran for the house, my sister hot on my heels. I ran into Momâ€™s bedroom and threw myself under her bed. My sister hid under the other. I lay there, heart pounding and shaking with fear as my Grandfather stormed through the house yelling for ME. I donâ€™t remember how long I stayed there. Long enough I guess.

As a teenager I was victimized by two of my fellow classmates who could have written the book on mean girls. Long story short, they destroyed my reputation our Senior year. I found out, too late, they had spent the winter calling boys (one in particular) and claiming to be me. God only knows what they told them but I have a very good idea. You can probably figure it out as well.

As an adult I have lived for nearly 40 years with a man who constantly fibs. Usually about me. I gave up long ago trying to get him to stop.

This next part is gonna be long and requires a great deal of finesse. I have to be extremely careful because (paranoia aside) I am probably still being stalked on the interwebs. Bear with me.

Somewhere around 4-5 years ago the bead making community was ablaze with outrage and indignity. A person (Iâ€™m going to call them â€œMadameXâ€) was found out to be buying bead sets from well known bead makers on ebay then reselling them for outrageous prices on another online venue. THAT was not the problem. The problem was MadameX was claiming to have made the beads. That pretty much falls under â€œintellectual property theft.â€ People were incensed. The torches and pitchfork crowd came out with a vengeance.

MadameX refused to stop, claiming that having bought them they were hers to do with as she pleased. The mob was having none of it. I stood on the sidelines calling for calm and was promptly mowed down by residual anger. So I picked up my beads and left.

The mob didnâ€™t quit. They hounded TPTB on both selling venues until MadameX was banned for violating the TOS. I guess there was quite the dustup on forums that I completely missed. I did, however, make a blog post at the time ABOUT the dustup. I did not name names but did make the mistake of putting MadameXâ€™s name in the Tags. WTF was I thinking?!

In retaliation, MadameX put up a â€œfraud blogâ€ where she claimed to be a â€œgroup of concerned citizensâ€ who were certified IRS tax preparers and then she began naming names. I think Iâ€™m number 7 or 8 on the list. Each of us was deemed a tax fraud and a cheat and that we were running businesses without proper licensing and that we didnâ€™t pay our taxes. She even called the Wyoming Department of Taxation on me, probably the county as well and we know she was calling the IRS daily. (PLEASE FOR GODS SAKE DO NOT GO LOOKING FOR THE BLOG because itâ€™s still up. I do not need X back in my life)

Before she was done I think she had several hundred people listed on her little blog. So a few people in her home state got together and sued her to force her to remove the blog. I was asked if I wanted my name taken off and of course I said YES. It was very unorganized and they quickly ran out of funds and had to drop the suit. Whereupon MadameX crowed on another blog about how she had defeated all of us meanies and that justice had prevailed or some such bullshit nonsense.

Three years ago she hired a lawyer and sued 30 of us for the most vile and imaginary activities. She was asking for $100,000 in damages, each. None of what she said was even remotely within the realm of reality. It was, however, a perfect example of projection. All her bad behavior was being projected onto us. THIS time there was organization. A non-profit fund-raising team was formed, we held organized fund raisers and billed ourselves monthly dues. We got a great lawyer. We spent hours worrying ourselves sick over this mess. We worried about money. We did get 5 or 6 of the litigants removed from the suit for various reasons but the rest of us were stuck, even though the majority were out-of-state, because of the previous suit.

A year ago her attorney asked to be released. Iâ€™m not going to list details because I donâ€™t want to risk getting sued AGAIN by this person. A couple of months later our attorney asked for, and got, No Evidence Summary Judgements for us. In two years she had never EVER presented a shred of evidence to back up her case. Her goal, as you may have guessed, was simply to spend us all into the poor house and then have us give up so she could get judgements against us. She miscalculated.

It cost us, as a group, over $100,000 to defend ourselves agains these spurious and outrageous accusations. She appealed the decision and was roundly smacked down by the Appellate Court. The state Supreme Court has yet to do the same, but they will. Sheâ€™s been acting as her own attorney and itâ€™s been a cluster-you-know-what. She cannot dazzle with truth or brilliance so she buries the courts in bullshit. Itâ€™s been a real education. One Iâ€™d rather not have had.

There is an upside. One of our number filed a suit against MadameX before she was served with xâ€™s suit. It goes to trial this month. Our buddy is asking for close to 3 million in damages. Our friend is also acting as her own attorney with some help from a couple other members and has probably attained enough experience to go practice law on her own. The presiding judge was supposed to have retired last Fall but has stayed on to run this case. LIke every other legal professional who has had dealings with Madame X, heâ€™s pretty tired of her bullshit. I hear she got yelled at and threatened with jail the other day in yet another suit she has filed. Wrongfully, of course but who cares when you can use the justice system to harass innocent people.

Hereâ€™s the kicker. She told our friend who is suing HER that her stated goal was to make her homeless. Nice, eh?

Lies. Two years. $100,000. Complete loss of almost all of us to get back into our little studios and create. I still havenâ€™t recovered. And I still donâ€™t understand why she went after me.

So this is why I have been so depressed. Pick up the thread and follow it to the source. Learn something about yourself. Itâ€™s painful. Iâ€™d rather get angry â€¦ thatâ€™s easier. Instead I choose to dig. Canâ€™t heal a wound until all the infection is cleared out.

Long story short, I finally saw her for what she was (yet another big fat liar) I did something I rarely do. I warned family and close friends to be wary.

A few years after I left the radio station my inlaws confessed to me that they hadn't believe what I told them about her and they apologized. they learned the hard way. Darleen I hope things get better soon. It is unfortuneate about your life with all stress throughout. ### diane — 2013-02-02T22:01:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73835>

Darleen, that's a whole crapload of stuff to go through. And it really hurts when it's something that you're passionate about - and it takes the passion away. That would be the hardest part for me. And if I remember correctly, you also had a stressful situation over your singing too.

I just don't get people who put other people through things like that. So not in my nature. But like you, my values are truth and honesty. So lying is something I have a hard time accepting too. I give people all sorts of leeway until I catch them lying or they lie about me, then it's over.

I really feel for what you're going through.

### caroline — 2013-02-02T22:43:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73838>

sending you big hugs ... xoxo

### ashryn — 2013-02-03T00:43:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73860>

I thought people on forums and blogs had grown up a bit since the nineties.. Apparently I have just been insulated from it all. Glad the suit got chucked out.. And good luck digging into your new theme.. Liars all around you, some of them your best friends.. That's going to be an interesting can of worms to get untangled in your head.

I had a similar tendency to surround myself with people who ultimately ended up hurting me, and it came down to trust for me... I just trust that people I come to love won't hurt me... Then they do, and I think I have learned my lesson, until I meet the next person and I give my trust and so on.. The thing I realised after this whole cycle in which the same thing happened over and over was that I have a choice.. Simon and Garfunkle style.. A rock feels no pain, and island never cries.. And shutting yourself off works as far as preventing people from hurting you.. But being disengaged is lonely, and to lock out one part of your emotional life is to lock it all out.. I realised one day that I had no passion for ANYTHING I found it difficult to hive a shit about anyone, and weighing the two states up, have come to the conclusion that deciding to trust is scary, but its also the spark that makes a relationship work on more than the surface level.

These days I have my passion for things back to an extent.. Never so much as before, and I suspect I will never meet another person who I put so much faith in that they destroy me when they don't live up to my belief in them, but at least I can let some people in. And I can focus on the people close to me, and consciously give them my trust.

I know your demons are different to this example, but I think digging through it is the only way you will be able to heal, but you're clever, you will line up all the threads and see the patterns soon enough, then you will know what you have to do about it.. Good luck!

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-03T03:24:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73889>

Ashryn you are right on target. The last decade or so I have insulated myself to the point that even DH is getting exasperated. It's easy to do since we live out in the country and our nearest neighbors are about 200 yards away. Makes for optimal nude sunbathing, though. Only ones I scare are the deer, the raccoon, the birds and the squirrels.

And you're right. There's a heavy price to pay for isolation. I figured out a long time ago that it's not that people are hurting me it's my expectations of them that hurts me. So to avoid expecting anything (other than hurt) from anyone I just shut myself off.

At heart I am a regular pollyanna. got the rose colored glasses to prove it. So when people are "mean" to me, or hurtful or cutting or just plain nasty I tend to fold in on myself even more. I think I feel things far too deeply for my own good. Right now I"m like a raw wound. Oh, I'll get over it. The pain will ease, I'll move on. But the trust thing. You know I may wind up living a loooong time because I cannot seem to work through the issues the dominate my life. Fear of abandonment and failure to trust people. EVERY single time I do (trust someone) I get hurt. Which makes me wonder ... why am letting these people into my life? It's a long track record of failure and wrong choices. And no one to hold responsible but myself.

bleh.

### ashryn — 2013-02-03T05:09:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73894>

Heh.. You know I was going to call you Pollyanna the other day in response to a post you made..

This is going to sound a bit Freudian, and I suspect someone with quals in psych will jump in and tell me its rubbish, but I have a feeling the reason I keep picking the wrong kinds of people to trust is because I am trying to replay a scene from being a kid and getting hurt by people I SHOULD have been able to trust.. Like, if I can trust this person and they don't hurt me, then things that happened in the past wont hurt me as much either.. I dunno. The subconscious is a strange beast. i didnt have any major traumas, it's just that the way I understood and responded to things as a child was perhaps a bit misguided, (catholic guilt made me have a shame reaction to everything - everything was my fault) and so I was easily hurt. Thus when the people who should have realised this and shielded me from my own reactions and taught me resilience didn't, I thought it was something bad in me. I seem to keep on giving people the chance to stick up for me to try to prove to something in myself that's how things should be and are..

### Aussie Nana — 2013-02-03T07:40:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73898>

Darleen, my heart went out to you when I read your story. I too have had some bad stress. Fortunately its over 15 years ago now, though it continues to affect me if I think about it. It took over five years to clean the mess up and it wasn't primarily of our own making, though we did go along with some things for a while which we shouldn't have. If we hadn't been so trusting and naive we might have seen the fact that someone was being crooked earlier. We became whistle blowers. I would never be a whistle blower again. The cost is too high.

I can understand why you withdrew as I've done the same. I'm re-established now and have started to find some nice people to be around, though its taken a lot of time. I've found it productive to focus on doing some volunteer work and also on improving my home aesthetically. When I'm volunteering I spend time being nice and building people up with no expectations at all of anything in return. That has helped a lot and one place in particular has become a place to go to by choice. I am in the process of letting another place go as the people there aren't as nice to be around.

Look after yourself. My thoughts are with you.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-03T18:10:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73957>

Ashryn you are exactly on target. It's called trying to finish the story. My now and then therapist (mostly then because I think it's been nearly 2 years since I talked to her last ... she's damned sharp) explained it thusly. And bear with me because she only told me this stuff once and I have not, to date, done any digging into it which I really need to do.

when a traumatic event occurs and doesn't get stored properly into memory it forms a loop. That loop will replay itself as the brain tries to store it but it can't because ... it's looped. EFT, aka tapping, allows the brain to put that memory away so it stops the loop. The catch-22 is you have to get into the loop and feel the emotion that accompanied the event which is what caused the loop and I don't wanna do it because goddamnitall I already WENT through it. Or I'm just lazy or I just enjoy torturing myself.

god I love science

She mentioned some Harvard Doc (I don't know if he's a physician or neurobiologist or what and I've long since forgotten his name. I ought to email her and get that info.) has studied the phenomenon and EFT is not some kinda woo-woo stuff. It is real.

Aussie Nana, GOOD FOR YOU. I'm in awe. Doing the right thing is never easy but what are you gonna do? If it's the right thing it has to be done. I'm glad you've been able to overcome all of that. I will never understand why, when people take the moral high ground, they are castigated and shunned. Makes absolutely no sense to me.

I'm feeling somewhat better today. Time passes, things change. It helps to be able to come here and just let it all hang out and know I won't be judged or ridiculed. At least, not to my face.

The reset continues with nothing new to report. I'm sleeping better again since I upped the hormones and the protein intake. I'm almost back to where I was a couple of months ago so that's good.

I haven't returned to the tanning salon since my first foray. 8 minutes left my back pink. I didn't pull the lid all the way down which was smart or I'da been pink all over. I"m going to go tomorrow FOR SURE and do 6 minutes. I may try 3 sessions next week depending on on that goes. If nothing else it makes me feel better about myself. Like I'm up off my butt and making an effort.

Salmon for breakfast which I need to go fix. That's it. Boring now that the dust has once again settled. Drama Queen? MOI? never.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-03T19:18:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73966>

Darleen that is interesting what you say about 'the brain store bad event'. I always tried to determine how some people with similar trauamas (of course everyone is different and situations are different. But I always couldn't help but notice one person with a REALLY bad incident(s) manage just fine in life and stress is not a hurdle for them (outside the normal confines of stress). Yet on the other hand I know others with MUCH less of a trauma but their lives are riddled with stress and they are continually affected by it for years and years. I always feel bad for the ones that can't get over it and I admire the ones that can...and then feel blessed for my own life. lol

Darleen, I hope the tanning salon went well. It DOES feel good when the actual sun hits your skin, so I can't see why not a tanning salon would not feel the same?

### diane — 2013-02-03T20:28:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-73981>

Darleen I did EFT with a therapist since the summer. I am amazed at how it helped and how much my thoughts and attitudes have changed. Not just from that - also from getting another perspective and learning how to deal with certain things. Like Ashryn, I wasn't taught or supported when I was younger. No ones fault - I don't think they know how to either.

Yes, you do have to go through it again, but you will do it in your own time. Or not. For me, an emergency trip home brought up a bunch of stuff that I didn't realize existed - so that was my prompt for getting the big stuff out. And I'm really glad I did. I hold so much in that it's really hard for me to let it out. I'm really good at burying stuff.

Yes, some people probably think it's woo - but actually, I kinda like the woo sometimes. LOL. Just depends what kind of woo it is.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-04T20:06:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-74102>

Lanie, yes. My testosterone levels were flat lined. Big surprise as they yanked EVERYthing. I was on 3mg per day but my libido was still no where to be found. The pharmacist had told me when he originally filled the prescription that I could go up 1 more mg if I felt I needed it. So about 2 weeks ago I increased it to 4mg per day. That plus the extra E2 has kinda kicked things into gear a bit.

Nothing really new to report via the reset. Although I swear I'm losing inches around my belly. Not a lot but I'm starting to feel very hopeful that the SCALE will soon follow suit.

Yesterday's menu was the usual BAB ... I had salmon though instead of the ground beef. Lunch was the rest of the tin of wild smoked herring, a pickle, some blue cheese stuffed olives (OMG I LOVE those things!) and a couple of ounces of pomegranate juice since I was out of kombucha.
Dinner was the rest of the chuck roast/soup. Um. It was LOT, LOL. I was HUNGRY.

Didn't get to use my blue blockers enough because I had to go pickup DH from town. It was, as you all know unless you were deep underground yesterday, stupid bowl sunday. HEY. I LIKE football. I just don't like to watch it on TV. It puts me to sleep. Let me get out in the stadium in the fresh air with a bottle of wine and some rowdy friends and I have a GREAT time. ANYway. I finally gave up about 7pm and put 'em on anyway because the light was hurting my eyes. Didn't give me a problem getting to sleep though. And boy did I. Except I had some patented "Darleen weird" dreams. Jeebus. Stir up the old memory cesspool and you never know what tentacles are going to come looking to drag you back under.

Don't know what's going to be on the menu for the rest of the day. I have some Boulder Sausage german links in the fridge (no chemicals, no msg ... only brand of sausage I can eat and I LOVE it) and was thinking about sautÃ©ing it was some shrimp and peppers.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-05T22:06:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-74321>

Just crawled out of the tub. I don't know why the water seems so much colder all of a sudden. It's 30 seconds of I-simply-cannot-breathe to 90 seconds of gasping for breath before the cold sensors shut off. I've finally been able to almost stop the water leakage out of the tub. Got it to where it almost overflowed this morning so was able to get more of me underwater. Good news is the belly island is definitely getting smaller. Palm trees and hula girls will soon be ankle deep.

Sleep has returned to where it was a few months back. Out like a light, sleep until 9. woo hoo. Woke up about 4=ish but fell back into dreamland. And boy did I give Stephen King a run for his money this morning. A giant albino man-like creature that could shoot straight up into the stratosphere, execute a double loop-de-loop and then come straight back down to earth looking to make a one-point landing on MY head. It got stranger.

It's mighty weird out in the swamp.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-06T02:18:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-74367>

I don't bother with tub kind of CT these days: at all. It is not worth it for me. I was getting a 'tan' in my skin (after losing it in Nov./Dec.) just from either Mg, D3 and/or both. I asked why I was getting a tan and someone said CT. I said that I have been 'bad' with CT.

I only like outdoors CT like walk in snow with bare feet (b/c it feels good and stimulating and maybe some grounding). I swim in lakes and rivers from about March to November (before I wuss out). I would of course like to be able to go in lakes and rivers year round and maybe one day, but again I won't force myself in a torture in order to do it. I do what I can.

I just don't feel like tub CT is very natural. Cold showers feel good when it's hot out though. lol

I am trying a different approach/strategy to these things now a days in case if people notice a bit of a shift in my attitude/writing. I am not eschewing these protocols but instead I am either a. questioning everything b. tweaking based on my own feelings and/or practicality.

Btw, from my observation so far the ONLY thing (I know I know it's only been a few weeks) that seems to be helping EVERYBODY is the mito support supps. Now THAT is something that makes 'sense' to me!! I have already gained a benefit just from Mg. Mg oil and malate. So simple, yet so helpful (already). I am now looking at d-ribose....etc.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-06T04:01:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-74383>

The shoes arrived this afternoon, thank goodness while hubby was napping, LOL.

I had the pedi done last Friday. couldn't stand my ugly feet one more second. Wore my flip-flops out of the salon. HA HA that was a sight, I'll tell you. But people all over town wear sandals all year long so I guess I just looked like on eof them.

I never thought I could wear heels ever again. My poor right knee just didn't like them. This is my second pair of sky high wedges and the small amount of platform really makes a difference PLUS the sole is a big help. It's not like you're teetering on a spike and your toes. You might want to check your local shoe stores and just try some on to see. I find them so unbelievably easy to walk in. CANNOT wait to wear them somewhere. Besides my living room, that is.

CT in water gets you where you want to go faster than just walking out in the cold air, esp if you're fully clothed outside which I'm assuming you are. I switched to coldish showers a year ago when Jack told me the reason I was addicted to hot showers was because my omega3/omega6 levels were out of whack. Never say things like that to me. I love a challenge. The cold showers started as tepid and progressed to cold. Even now I take a tepid shower then blast myself with cold water when I'm done. Feels good. I also splash icy cold water on my face every single morning. I'm usually overly warm when I get up so it feels fantastic.

For those of you keeping up with the soap opera that is my life, MadameX has filed for a rehearing with the state supreme court and has called the judges and staff everything but white. Our soon=to-be ex-attorney says it looks like she's setting up for a run at the US supreme court. OMFG will this never end?!

I also ran across some "words of wisdom" today that I really needed to hear. Something along the lines of not judging other people who may have cause me grief because I don't know what their path is. You would think that, given my life philosophy, I would not have needed to be slapped upside the head by the universe but such is not the case. Alas, I am still a puny human. That one bit of truth set me back on my heels and I have been able to let go of the crap that was dragging me down the last week or so. yay me.

Please I hope you are recovering from your migraines this week. I hope that you can one day have ZERO migraines ever!! You're awesome Gretch and I cheer for you (and others) on the side lines/on this forum.

I am currently trying to find some watering holes in my parent's location so that I can start up some polar dips. I wussed out after November (last time I went for a dip). I probably should have kept going since I had some declines since then (but am on recovering mode and I 'believe' on the upswing---yay).

I am just so resistant to a bath tub (I did do this last year though) b/c I just don't feel like it's natural. I prefer being outdoors in nature. I go outside in bare feet every morning as part of my AM morning ritual/sun gaze (if it's even there lol). I hug a tree too. I think in winter it's at least good since I can't rely on the sun (although it's getting earlier and earlier now woo hoo). This Fall coming up I might do a mix of light box + go out in bare feet and hug a tree. As in I'll go outside and ground and then maybe while eating breakfast, eat it under a light box.

\*I know light box is not natural just like a bath tub but I guess we pick and chose what we individually want as 'natural' versus 'artificial'. lol I just want to feel good and I don't want a land slide down AGAIN this Fall. Second slide down was of course not even .00000000001% as bad as a CRASH in 2011, but still, I was still shocked and surprised at my body given what I was eating/doing etc. hehe Gotta mix a bit of Asprey with Kruse me thinks...see where that will get me. LoL

Caroline: You say you are working to be kinder, more passionate etc. Me too, but more for myself. lol I am always too hard on myself and of course it's easy to be compassionate towards others, b/c I always care. But alas I must be more gentle on myself (but without that letting me 'make excuses').

Darleen: That pic of the sandals...is that your foot? This might sound weird, but you have really nice feet!! I am a bit weird about feet and legs in particular, rather obsessed... Hence why I complain about body composition...I only like good legs (Note: It doesn't have to do with SIZE but rather COMPOSITION). I have several examples to draw from where the person is no where near slim BUT THEY HAVE FREAKING AMAZING LEGS!! Actually, Marilyn Monroe is a pretty good example. Check out her legs and how you can actually see her knees. LOL Yup...my own weirdness.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-07T00:09:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-74510>

Ashryn ... "everything but white" means being called every single not nice name in the book.

Gretchen .... hee hee I knew I'd get you with these. I LOVE LOVE LOVE the tiny bit of animal print.

Zorica ... my feet?! Good thing I didn't get a shot of my toes. DH says my feet are ugly. My 2nd toe is longer than my big toe so it's bent kinda weird. My little toe is turned sideways. On both feet. And they WIDE ... Well at least my toes aren't webbed. No offense to anyone here with webbed toes.

Got an early start this morning because I left the drapes on one of the windows open. So was outta bed by 7:30. Grabbed my BAB then a shower. Coffee with my friend this morning. And as usual we yakked for 3 solid hours. Good thing Starbucks wasn't busy as they mighta thrown us out otherwise. She's a hoot.

7 minutes on the tanning bed. Had to stop at Walgreen's for a light SPF lotion ... um, TMI? but the nips on Monday were like YAHHHHH WTH ARE YOU DOING TO US?! whiners. 7 minutes again but thinking about going to 8 on Friday. I'm getting used to the noise ... man it's loud. The fan is blowing and whatever is running all those bulbs is making a racket. 73 minutes left and then I"m done and out in the sun toasting my parts. We will not think about nor discuss what all that EMF is doing. (covering ears and hollering I CAN'T HEAR YOU!!!!) somedays denial is useful.

Still seriously considering going to PaleoFX. Off to read up on it some more to see if it's gonna be worth the money. Also I need to go photograph my AlphaCentauri bead ... Lark books is doing another book of 1000 beads and I"m going to enter this one. Maybe it will jar me enough to get back to the torch.

Just got home from errands and realized something as I was driving home. It's a short commute (7 miles one way) but I"m a fast thinker so there ya go. You all may have figured this out already. Or at least noticed it. I am, at heart, a lurker. I let fear rule my life. (really, Darleen? I have decided the time has come to stop hiding. Let the tigress out of her cage, take off the shackles and just let 'er rip. Hell I'm already pretty much doing that now. don't expect a lot more. I'm just going to let go of my fears and take whatever comes.

All the people who put me in that box are long dead. And I'm still here. Why should I continue to let them victimize me (which is exactly what I've been doing) when I have this precious life to live.
