### DarleenMB — 2013-02-08T18:38:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-74882>

OK, getting back on topic here

Last night's dinner was orange roughy, shrimp, a spoonful of leftover lump crab in homemade cocktail sauce and a small avocado (had a BIG seed so wasn't a lot) with balsamic vinegar in it. Didn't eat until 6 so was pretty darned hungry since the only other meal was a BAB. Scarfed it all down save for 4 of the shrimp. Couldn't. Quite. Manage. It.

Wasn't very hungry this morning so ditched most of my 2 eggs. Need to eat dinner a little sooner.

Today is tanning salon day. Oh boy. Cold and cloudy with more snow forecast for the weekend.

CT yesterday was COLD. WHY does it suddenly feel so much colder?

Oh yeah, on the PaleoFX thing in Austin. I asked 2 people who's opinion I trust and have decided not to bother. I have better things to do and can most of what they're selling on the interwebs. So I'll be avoiding Texas. yay. I AM, however, seriously thinking about actually doing the bead show in Denver this April. Gotta contact some of beady buddies and see how the market has been and if anyone wants to share a booth space. At $500 a pop I really cannot do it alone. OR I could simply attend in April and then sell my wares in September when they do it again. Gotta investigate before I make a decision.

Which pretty much tells me I'm still not ready to do it otherwise I'd just DO it. I'm not one to ponder things for very long. Maybe I'm just getting old and stuck in my ways. OMG I hope not.

And on that note ... I return the conversation back to where we last left it. A malbec soak eh? Not sure I want to try that. I think I'd rather sip the wine instead of bathe in it.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-09T19:43:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75046>

Pictures coming in about an hour of me wearing my new red shoes (just my feet, so don't get too excited). I figured I'd better get the picture now before my pedi needs redoing.

Why am I starting to feel like Dr. Ruth around here?! LOL. Well, I'm a lot taller and (no ego here just go look at her) quite a bit prettier. Don't follow me you guys. I'm still lost!

Forgot to thaw the ground beef last night so it was salmon and bacon for breakfast. Yesterday about midday I had some shrimp and the last of the lump crab in the homemade cocktail sauce. Gotta get a better chili sauce. I had thai chili sauce and it was just toooooooo sweet. ick. Dinner was Aidell's chicken sausages (I find I really don't like them any more but I still had some in the freezer so that's what we had) with peppers, onions and mushrooms.

Sleep was good although the cats woke me up a couple of times which meant I wound up getting up to pee. WHY is that? Didn't really need to but the ol' bladder said GET UP NOW.

Leaving one window uncovered so I wake up with the light otherwise I'd be sleeping until 10.

Oh yeah. Almost forgot. Went to Amazon yesterday and found and bought this:

\[h=1\]Schumann's Resonance - 7.83 Hz (just do a search, it'll pop right up)\[/h\]
It was a whopping $1.98 and is 2-38 minute meditation tracks. I d/l to my iPod then went and sat in the massage chair. I was good for about 20 minutes and then I went somewhere else. I wasn't sleeping. Just gone. When Missy jumped on me came back and I have to tell you I was SO relaxed for about an hour afterward I didn't think I would be able to move.

AND my energy level yesterday was good. Not spectacular but good enough to get chores done, run some errands, do more chores and fix dinner. WOW. color me impressed.

One of the things I'm doing, which I may or may not have mentioned is ... keeping my bare feet on my earthing mat whenever I'm at my computer. It's been a slow change but I'm starting to feel so much better. Or maybe my stupid dopamine levels are starting to go up. Who knows. I'm just glad I'm feeling some steady, albeit small, increases.

Off to climb in the tub.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-10T21:29:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75262>

Another dull and snowy day. BUt not nearly as much as the East Coast got.

Skipped a midday snack/meal yestereday as I got busy. Dinner last night was pork loin "country style ribs" with sauerkraut. MMMM. Thought I had had plenty (had two good sized pieces) but about 2 hours later my stomach demanded to know when I was planning on dinner. A couple of spoonfuls of coconut butter shut it up.

Sleep was just ok. Woke about 3AM and lay there for about an hour. Fell into a deep sleep after and didn't wake until 9:40. So much for leaving a window uncovered.

BAB as usual. Too much FB as am still sitting here in my pj's. Guess I should get off my backside and go take a shower.

Thinking about visiting the Taylor Gym tomorrow to check prices. They have Nautilus equipment and NO kids allowed. Want to check on availability of treadmills. I'm feeling like getting back to Fredrick Hahn's slow burn workout. 30 minutes once a week. Taylor made for a wuss like me.

### janagram — 2013-02-10T21:58:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75266>

hmm, that must be the "super slow" workout...? How do you do it on a treadmill? (walk real slow?...naw, don't think so...)

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-11T02:27:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75324>

No. Walking normally on the treadmill. Strength training using the heaviest weight you can manage to a slow 10second count up and the same back down. Object is to reach muscle exhaustion in 8-10 reps. There are 10 different exercises and it should take you about 30 minutes. Do it once a week. His book is "The slow burn fitness revolution."

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-11T18:16:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75396>

I've been feeling down in the dumps the last several days. I find it difficult to even come in here and post and, other than FB, this is pretty much where I currently "live" on the 'net. So, as usual, I went dumpster diving in my subconscious. Didn't have to look very far, either.

First of all I'm still (big surprise) having issues with the whole MadameX thing. The final chapter is about to be written and it's been on my mind a lot. Here's hoping she goes down in flames and burns out completely. That's all I can say. My fear is she will track me here and try and retaliate.

And the second thing is a little more subtle but it's been demanding I stop denying it and deal with it.

About 6 weeks ago hubby pretty much moved into the guest room. He has legitimate reasons for doing so. The cats like to pin him to the bed. I've told him it's because he turns his side of the electric blanket on max sweat and they like the warmth. MY side is unplugged. I have NEVER liked electric blankets.

AND he likes to lie in bed and read for HOURS on end. I put and end to that a couple of years ago when I read one of Suzanne Somer's books where she talked about the study showing that even the tiniest dot of light on your skin in a completely darkened room screws with your melatonin production. He's been in a huff ever since.

He also has physical issues that make sleep difficult. The docs at the VA put him through a battery of tests and finally concluded (or so he told me) that the pain in his leg and his other symptoms are the result of a lesion on his brain stem. It's evidently near the area that causes MS so he has symptoms but NOT MS. At least, that's what he told me. I do know the pain in his leg and foot have bothered him for as long as we've known each other. I should note he still carries shrapnel from getting shot up in 'Nam.

So we now, at long last, have his parents' marriage. To be fair we took a lot longer getting there than they did, but here we are.

And when the subject of oxytocin came up in another thread, I cratered. I realized years ago that sex (with a partner) was done for me. The lack thereof was not something I wanted, nor do I still want. But it was not, no matter how you slice it, my choice.

I've been asked why I stayed. Simple sweet answer is I made a commitment. Long before we ever even began living together, he basically verbalized our marriage contract. He stood in my room and told me that as long as we held each other first in our hearts nothing else mattered. I realized then he was leaving a door open for himself (it was pretty obvious he was asking for an open relationship ... I might be naive but I am not now, nor have I ever, been stupid). I accepted the commitment and as long as both of us are still breathing I will honor it. He has always been first in my heart. There is no room for any other.

Second part isn't so sweet. When the great drought started I blamed myself. It obviously HAD to be something I had done and if I could just figure it out we would be OK. Naive. Not stupid.

The really sad thing about a childhood/adolescence like mine is that even when you work at it, your choices as an adult are still informed by what happened to you as a child. And throughout those years, anything that went wrong was ALWAYS my fault. I was the family garbage dump.

The third part is, sad? Ugly? Deep down, in the place where no light ever goes ... I believed then and still do, that I am not worth loving and that I am undesirable.

There. Now you have it all.

jeebus.

So when people talk about sex I run and hide. I don't even like to discuss self-administered oxytocin therapy because it just plain hurts too much. too many years all by myself and then finally just giving up. and you wonder why that thread depressed me. Well, there ya go.

And another week off to a stellar start. More stress, more angst. More whining.

Weighed myself yesterday and was happily stunned to see I was down a whole 1.2 pounds. Yeah right. Today was up 1.6. 2 years doing low carb, 18 month no grains, a full year on the leptin Rx, 3 months of CT every other day, 3 months on cytomel and a month on high dose iodine and where am I? Spinning my wheels.

I'd give up but there's no where else to go. Some days I hate my life, some days I hate my body. Today I get a double dose.

I wish my stupid 23andme results would come in and I gotta get off my duff and go find a doc because I need to do some lab work. or I could just crawl into my cave, roll the boulder in front of the opening and go take a nap.

### endless — 2013-02-11T18:38:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75399>

I feel exactly the same way today Darleen! I'll be in the adjoining cave, just knock on the wall if you want company

> The third part is, sad? Ugly? Deep down, in the place where no light ever goes ... I believed then and still do, that I am not worth loving and that I am undesirable.

Hmm. This has recently come up for me, too. I should probably look into it at some point.

### Inger — 2013-02-11T18:44:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75401>

Darleen. I feel with you. I gone through the same stuff.. less and less sex in marriage and then no. And weird.. I knew to save myself having a amazing lover that made me feel perfect.. but I so well know what you mean when thinking it is all your fault. I so know this state of mind.
I can have moments of them still but I never let it for long because guess what I do then? I kick myself in my ass, stand up, wash my hair, make me pretty, look into the mirror and say I am beautiful. And if I feel fat I just put on some clothes that looks great and makes me feel not fat and I tell myself I am just fine. Perfectly fine.
You know what Darleen? I have a broken heart still. But I am not too sad about it because it is a blessing to. Now I have a good reason to say, I must heal. I allow myself to hide in a cold and dark cave.. and just.. rest. Rest and heal. Do nice stuff that I like. Healing stuff. I am allowed to hide, to heal. Because we must go cold, dark and boring..lol

And during all this, I know soon comes summer. And my heart is open. I know there are thousend of possibilities, men to love, love to share.. all beautiful things out there. But right now, I enjoy this "vacation". I can feel sad if I want, but I like to be alone. I guess it is the grieve in my heart and it is ok. One day it will be gone. I give me much time and it feels just good.

Guess what Darleen. You are lucky your hubby friend moved to the guest room because now you can have all windows open...! Yay! And do whatever you want!

We are blessed, as we can consentrate only on ourselves right now, on our healing only! To me it helps huge to think, no ones know what the future brings.. it might be really exiting! Have an open heart always, it does wonders to me. And then.. go on and enjoy what beautiful things you have in your life... with every. single. breath.

And then.. I use to think if I never had the "bad days" I would not enjoy the good ones that much! The sun will shine soon enough.. it always do.

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-11T19:43:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75410>

Darleen

I think we've all been where your at. Some of us get out of that pit, only to discover we're right back down in it, having to struggle to get out yet again. We've all got baggage we're struggling with, all of it different and all of it impacts where we are at this given moment.

I was feeling very similar on Saturday evening - when I offered but DH said not now there's this movie on I want to watch. I told him point blank - got it, watching a Effing movie, is far more important than spending quality time with me. Got the memo. Thanks. of to conduct Self Oxytocin therapy myself. 20 mins later he comes up and wants to "play". I was pissed. I told him - you're on your own tonight buddy, and here's why - not only did you piss me off, but you made me feel bad b/c you decided a movie was more important than me. thanks for making me feel un -loved.... I finished taking care of myself and slept great... don't know about him. I'm sure I made him feel like crap -and the response was totally based in a bad place of self preservation, trying to protect myself from getting hurt. Not the best move on my part - but I was pissed, and he needed to know that.

Damn I totally went off the rails. IDK - but I think sometimes we get so wrapped up in our own shit - we miss the fact that others have stuff going on. I have those days where I'm wallowing right with you. I don't have any recommendations for getting out of it... some how I just pick myself up and move on, I really don't have any other choice.

And if it makes you feel better about the weightloss & improved health I've been at this 13yrs, Gluten free since 2004, Paleo since 2010, Leptin Reset since 2011... and while I've had some wins, its seems my environment has more control over me than I ever thought. I'm still trying to figure out how to pull this all together...

maybe I'll got put a $1 on a lottery ticket just incase...  hang in there, get your daily CT in say EFF you to the rest of the world -

for all the verbal hugs, sharing and thoughts I thank all of you from the bottom of my heart. I need to go pull my head outta my back pocket. I just am not up to replying to everyone right now.

### diane — 2013-02-12T02:19:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75473>

Definitely have all been there Darleen. And even though I didn't go through in childhood what you went through - I still came to the same conclusion about myself, and have made a lot of choices along the way that reflected that. Although I didn't realize it at the time. Slowly working my way out of it though.

And have been there trying to initiate with someone who rejects you, and then cried myself to sleep. For many years.

I'm also a master at suppressing all of those feelings and have a great capacity for ignoring them. So when I actually feel something, for me it signals it's time for me to care. Which, when I reflect back, is definitely a positive step forward.

But we're all entitled to our pity party days! Lord knows I have mine. Hope yours passes soon.

### ashryn — 2013-02-12T03:10:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75478>

Fwiw, things in marriages change when people change. Change is GOOD.

i feel a bit dumb saying this when you have lots more practice at marriage than me, but I have seen complete turnarounds in our relationship following one or other of us healing some long held lump that was holding us back from being our best version of ourselves.

The commitment, as you say is the thing. So long as you choose to remain committed, you choose to grow with each other and make the adjustments to change as they come along. Perhaps your DH is struggling to adjust out of the rut, and he needs some guest room time. It isn't always easy for the partner not living the change to understand what's going on.. If he is like any other human, he is probably afraid.. That he's not good enough, that you're going to leave, that there is something wrong with him.. The response by 99% of humans is to hide, ignore it and hope it goes away, deny deny deny... And sleep in the guest room. (Fear comes from within, there is nothing you could do to prevent that by the way)

Let him take his time, eventually he will come out of hiding and meet the new improved wife he has.. More empowered, more courageous, more assertive about her needs.. And he will probably see that this is a woman he always knew was there, but forgot.. Or only saw rarely..

Or not.
I can't really guess what the results will be to change, no one can. But it is essential to keep growing, keep changing, just remember that not everyone will be ready for the journey when you are.. That's not to say they won't be ready eventually, or that they won't take a journey of their own, but when the time is right for you you must travel the path as far as you can. Commitment to each other is the reason this thing is possible. So keep going woman!

### Lanie — 2013-02-12T08:09:44+0200

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-12T20:12:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75573>

I cannot believe I have had two meltdowns in about 10 days. It makes me wonder if the machinery that has been rusted in place for years is finally firing back up. My estrogen will fall off a cliff in 2 weeks and one day and then the Pg starts back up. If I even get a hint I'm going to do this Weeping Wilma routine again I will unplug the damned computer and lock myself in the bedroom for 2 weeks and do nothing but watch the shopping channel.

This is just NOT LIKE ME. I mean I'm pretty damned good at ignoring this crap or at least dealing with it in the privacy of my own head. I honestly do not understand WTH has gotten in to me that I wind up sharing all this garbage.

Shoes, OK. Personal angst ... NOT OK.

Krista, dealing with all that self loathing, and that's what it is, is never easy. I now have a little mantra I try to remember to say to myself every single day (and several times a day, at that). Reprogramming your thoughts takes time but it can be done. Don't know if that will help or not.

Inger, you are absolutely right. focusing on the positive is much preferable to only seeing the negative.

Lyndra, yes, friends are good to have. She and I will be having coffee again tomorrow. That should help.

Gretchen, all I can say is you are so much braver than I am. I am in awe.

Jana, I'm invisible. Men simply do not see me. so there it is ... what's the use. Ah yes, yet another of my favorite pity party themes.

PaulaLynn, it sounded exactly like what it is. You're right. It is a dilemma.

Diane, although I wish it weren't so, thanks for the company. I know I"m not alone. I just appear to have lost all my filters on this forum. Maybe it's time for another annual checkup with my once-in-a-while therapist.

Ashryn, you voiced part of what I was feeling. I think his move was mostly prompted by his poor sleep because he is feeling and looking better since he locked the cats out. But I also figured it was the changes I've been trying to make. I don't think he's scared, per se, but I do think he has either just given up or doesn't care anymore.

Lanie, Thanks! With all you're going through it means a lot to have you stop in here with a hug. Here's one back! {{{{{Lanie}}}}}}

OK, trying to get myself emotionally back on track here. We went to see Identity Thief yesterday because I needed a good laugh. It was stupid (which means I laughed myself silly) and banal and just what I needed. I am coming to adore Melissa McCarthy. She is hilarious and I cannot wait to see her teamed up with Sandra Bullock.

I was naughty and had popcorn for the first time in months. Didn't make me feel any better but sometimes I just gotta say WTH and be bad. I. Don't. Care.

I couldn't have picked a better day to have a meltdown either. I had a haircut (ODG it is just so short I could almost pass for a skin head) and then 8 minutes in the tanning bed and then a massage. My massage therapist (who I have been seeing for 10 years?) said apropos of nothing and just out of the blue "You have beautiful skin" and I replied by pointing out all the ugly spots. TSK. Can't take a compliment. Still.

Pg cycle is done for this round so my mood should even right back out. thank god.

I'm not getting on the scale for another month. I may put it out in the garage so I am not tempted. The interesting thing is my pants are getting really baggy through the hips and thighs. I just washed, dried and ironed them and they're hanging on me. That's not right. Putting them through the dryer tends to make them smaller, not bigger. (insert me scratching my head in confusion here)

And that's it. I'm going to go look for my rubber leash because I really need to be restrained.

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-12T23:03:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75603>

Darleen I'm no braver than anyone else here on this board, no braver than you. maybe the difference in how I respond is due to the fact I tend to put myself into situations that test my personal fortitude... look at the craziness in my entire life - up until 2000 - I moved around every 2-4years, I chose to go to a university that required a lot of moral and personal fortitude - I can't tell you how much a school of only 150 girls/women = MEAN GIRLS x10000000000000000 - their treatment was far worse than the day-to-day military crap. Every day I'd stand outside and look at where I was and Couldn't leave even though I desperately wanted too b/c of MEAN GIRLS. ONe of my best friends there who helped me through difficult times there became a Navy SEAL. I am no quitter. look tenacious in the dictionary and you'll see my picture  (at least that's what my dad says)

how we handle adversity shows our true character. you my dear, IMO have far more character than I'll ever have for what you've come through in your life - I am in awe of you, and inspired by you.

At the Zoo - we're required to read the story of Capt Lance P. Sijian. He is a tragic hero, who's courage and character carried him through dark times. I aspire to face things with the courage and character he did, for what burdens he carried are far less than what I carry. If he can carry his, I can carry mine, even in the face of fear and despair. You have the same thing he has, you may not see it, but I do.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-13T01:22:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75624>

> [Zorica Vuletic said:](/goto/post?id=75591)
>
> You think it's the Pg that makes for small hips and thighs? Probably a good thing.
>
> Still confused about the low/no E and crying as opposed to high E causing crying...or maybe it's the two 'ends' Low = crying high = crying , in the middle = normal. lol
>
> Wish I could figure it out on my own....

Read TS Wiley's "Sex, Lies and Menopause" or any of the late Dr. John R. Lee's books about menopause and "what your doctor may not tell you." The #1 symptom of menopause (low estrogen) is depression. And no, I don't think the Pg has anything to do with the my apparently tightening up hips and thighs. I think that's a consequence of 2 years eating low carb and a full year on the leptin Rx (mostly).

When you have your period your estrogen is at its lowest and Pg has been heightened because of the release of an egg. Low E makes ya weepy. I just seem to have forgotten that.

Aw Gretch, we should form a "mutual admiration society" here. And I will never understand why wimmen can be so cruel to one another. And a Navy SEAL?! Wow. I met one once (a guy). He works on the railroad here. This part is no joke or exaggeration. We were at a picnic at another friend's house. I felt this WAVE of energy coming at me, looked up and it was this guy who was a good 50' away from me. I found out from hubby he was a former SEAL. Guys like that could impregnate a fertile woman from a 100 yards off, LOL. I never felt anything like it, ever.

And we all have our burdens. Doesn't matter what they are and there's no use comparing them. I just wish I could be as honest and forthright in my relationship as you apparently are in yours. {{{hugs}}}

Jana. No poety clubs (good god woman, this is WY...forking...OMING. Cows. Cowboys. Men in trucks. Rifles. deer hunting. Baseball caps. POETRY? Seriously? Ditto for a piano bar. bwahahaha. Years ago there was this great writer working for the local "newspaper." He wrote once (and I'm sure I mentioned this once already but it does bear repeating) that here in Wyoming ambiance means "a swell place to slug 'em down." So much for culture.

OK, got some of my 23andme results and just ran the data through genetic genie. Now I need a little help.

I have no MTHFR mutations.

Here are your homozygous mutations as indicated in your SNP gene table above (not including MTHFR):​

-   COMT V158M
-   COMT H62H
-   MAO-A R297R
-   MTRR A664A

Here are your heterozygous mutations as indicated in your SNP gene table above (not including MTHFR):​

-   VDR Bsm
-   VDR Taq
-   ACAT1-02
-   MTRR A66G
-   BHMT-08
-   CBS C699T
-   SHMT1 C1420T

VERY interesting results. I have ALL you have in the same mutation except for two - MTRR A664A, of which I am hetero, and BHMT, of which I'm a homo. My MTHFR 667/1298 status is negative as well.

Diane, are you my long-lost sister?! Did my mama..... never mind.

You have CBS mutations, which may mean you don't clear sulfur foods well.

Your VDR vitamin D receptors are +/- which may mean that you need a water-based Vit D sup ona counta it may not absorb well. I took 24,000 IU's a day for five months and my levels went from 25 to only 52.

And other snips that do all kinds of weird stuff.

What to do?

VERY complicated strategy to bypass the mutations that we have. I did some sulfur food elimination on my own and got hammered with detoxing. Ugh. Backed off and waiting for consult with Dr Tim Jackson to discuss getting some relief from this hole I'm in. Meanwhile, doing all I can to reduce EMF's and stickin' my feet in the mud to ground, huggin' trees.

Doc K has some interesting info coming down the pike with this EMF thing, so perhaps you can wait and read/listen and decide then?

Your decision, sis.

### freshveggies — 2013-02-13T16:51:13+0200

Like Martin said, Dr. K has some things coming up. I would have liked them months ago as I feel some of the protocol for methylation has set me back. Just because we have a snip does not mean it has expressed itself. I was put on a low sulfur diet and it was 7 months. Sulfur --I think is one of the 9 essential minerals. I was getting sicker so I just pulled back from all that. It is deeper than methylation. Methylation is like a branch of what is going on in our body. We can work branches all we want and spin our wheels. I have been taking down so many rabbit holes it makes me sick to think of all the damage done and the money and health deterioration. GLad to know Dr. K has more ideas. I just can't get them fast enough. Just some of my thoughts, hope you don't mind.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-13T17:42:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75703>

Just finished reading all the text that came with the genetic genie. Krista you might try running it through again. Did you get lots of reds and yellows on your table? If so you should have had explanatory text with it.

My initial response is that I'm going to keep on doing what I've been doing but stop the NAC for now and cut back on the sulfur stuff. I've been taking a tsp of MSM daily so am going to stop that for a bit. I'm also going to try to eat a lot more fish. I'm giving up eggs for now and I haven't eaten veggies at all this winter so that's not an issue right now.

My low vitamin D levels are explained by this (that and the fact I've avoided the sun like forever) so I think I'm going to continue what I've been doing. Just need to find water-based D for now.

I'm also going to add in gingko and 5HTP to my daily supps. Just from the first thorough read-through I don't think I"m in all that bad a shape. About the 20th time I go through this I might finally have a glimmer of understanding. But right now I don't think I need to make a lot of changes.

thanks for the input!

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-14T21:06:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75847>

_m\*th@rf&ck#r defects_ aside, and back to my journaling attempts ...

Went out to [The Powderhorn club house](http://www.thepowderhorn.com) for dinner last night. Sort of early VD and sort of "thank you" to friends who have lent us their ATV for the winter so hubs can keep the driveway cleared. His ATV is on the fritz.

I had the NY strip with 3 grilled tiger prawns. That cow musta put up quite a fight because it had so much connective tissue and stringy icky stuff in it that even my Jack-sized steak knife couldn't hack most of it off. Flavor was good, though. It came with roasted potatoes and baby carrots which I didn't eat. I did eat the iceberg lettuce wedge though ... raspberry vinegrette and blue cheese (yummmy) and then I did a BAD BAD THING (cue Chris Isaacs).

I have been so good for so long I don't know why I do this stuff.

sigh

chocolate peanut butter cheesecake.

OKOK I did NOT touch the cookie crumb crust. I only ate about half the cheesecake but I ate ALL the chocolate ganache.

So then my tummy was upset. (SERVES YOU RIGHT YOU PINHEAD). GAH!!!!!

(bangs head on desk in frustration)

What else to do but pick myself up, dust myself off, go read EMF 5 (happy VD to you too dude!) and then read it about 60 more times.

ON THE GOOD NEWS FRONT!!!!!

My "friend's" suit against madameX is headed to closing statements and she got all the damning documentation entered into evidence. PLEASE keep your fingers crossed. We're all hoping for a verdict from the jury before the day is out. The bad news is the bitch has threatened in public to the judge that she isn't done suing people yet and she actually named 2 names (mine was not one thank god). Never mind no one ever did anything TO her. Quite the contrary. She is hell bent on driving all of us into poverty and making us all homeless. (insert pause while I hyperventilate and call her EVERY SINGLE BAD WORD in the book).

I have to tell you I am so disappointed in how our legal system DOESN'T WORK. To top it all off I'm reading John Grisham's latest and it's sorely pissing me off. There is no justice in this country. Probably never has been. But to get ensnared in it is a real eye opener. I hope NONE of you ever has to experience this shit.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-15T20:06:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-75997>

Back to business as usual. Just finished my sorta BAB. Since I ran my 23andme raw data through genetic genie I'm thinking I may need to back off the protein just a smidge. My last labs showed a high uric acid result and now I know why. I need to get with a doc soon and rerun those labs and show them the GG data. Also need to get them to order an ASI for me as well. BEcause ...

I debated about putting this post over in the oxytocin thread in The Cave but decided to put it here because because because.

After I did my little "poll" it really got me thinking. So I put on my deerstalker cap and went sleuthing out on the interwebs. And I found this [little tidbit](http://med.monash.edu.au/sphpm/womenshealth/docs/testosterone-and-androgens-in-women.pdf).

I've included their graphic in this post. I like visual stuff.

From what I read in the article my testosterone levels have been cratered for years and when they yanked my parts they flatlined. Since my adrenals have been stressed most of my life and my last test (last Fall) showed very low A.M. and P.M. cortisol levels) I now understand ... I think ... where my problem originates.

It does no good to tell a woman with my issues to "think like a man." That's kinda (pardon the phrase) beating a dead horse. NO matter how hard you try it is NOT gonna get up and run.

It isn't a matter of "wanting" to, either. It's a matter of CANNOT. Would you tell a man with ED to just try harder? Didn't think so. My hormones are still a train wreck. The question is what do I do to fix it?

I increased my testosterone cream about a month? ago from 3 "lines" to 4. Today I bumped it to 5. The reason is that about a week ago my libido roused itself from its decades long slumber, looked around and went "whassup?" and proceeded to punch it. Zero to Warp 8 in about 60 seconds. LITERALLY. Then she went back to sleep. dammit.

The doc who originally prescribed the wiley protocol for me told me that "in his experience" women (even those of us without parts) did NOT need to supplement testosterone and that my libido would come back on line. It hasn't. Guess he never heard of adrenal fatigue.

So. It's back to the blog to hunt up and read again and again and again the adrenal Rx. Off to find a doc and discuss this most delicate subject. I'm looking for a woman 'cause there's no freaking way I'm talking about this with a man. It's humiliating.

In the meantime, any suggestions, advice, references what have you would be greatly appreciated. Lady Libido is snoring in the background and I'd love to kick her ass awake. Permanently.

forgot to include that I had my DHEA and pregnenolone levels check a couple months back and my DHEA was HIGH as I'd been supplementing so I quit that. The pregnenolone was way low so I've started supplementing that.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-16T19:03:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76121>

Einstein. Einstein. Hmmm. Damn that name sounds familiar.

I love this place. I manage to learn something new every day. OK then. Next time the sun is out at noon and the wind isn't blowing too hard ... off with the clothes and out onto the lounger.

fitness I posted one pic a while back. I have a few still in [my Etsy shop](http://www.darleenmb.etsy.com) and will be relisting a bunch more pretty soon.

[I entered this bead](http://www.darleenmb.com/alphacentauri.jpg) in the Lark Books call for entries for their newest book "1000 Glass Beads." I was lucky enough to take a class from the woman who is judging the entries. That's the one I posted here. It's the only bead I've made (other than the very first one I ever made) that I won't sell. It's mine. Mine I tell you.

CT yesterday ... the usual 47Â° or so at 45 minutes. I wish I could get completely submerged but I do the best I can right now with what I've got. I mentioned, kinda casual like, to DH the other day that I had found a hot tub from Costco that uses an ionizer to clean the water instead of chemicals. I need to go read the owners manual again to make sure. It's pretty darned pricey but if I get off my backside and start working again, assuming there's still a market for my beads, I could probably pay for it myself with bead money. Of course, then there'd be the argument about the water temp because \*I\* want it at 50Â°.

One step at a time.

Speaking of that ... I've been up puttering around and haven't eaten yet. I don't normally do that so don't scold me. I just got side tracked.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-17T20:31:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76238>

Thank you!

BAB this morning which is supposed to be followed by CT but I'm dragging my feet for some reason. I woke up about 5:30 groused at the cats then fell asleep again and didn't wake up again until nearly 9:30. I feel like I need a nap.

We had orange roughy last night for dinner and hubby surprised me by telling me how good it was. He doesn't like fish very much. I cooked it in CO and added some lemon pepper to it. That was it. He said the "sauce" was tasty, LOL.

Tonight we're having tri-tip. I found a recipe for a rub that sounds pretty darn good. And then there's a [pan sauce](http://www.justapinch.com/recipes/main-course/beef/oven-roasted-tri-tip.html) to go with it.

It's snowing, off and on, again. Must be February. But the days are getting a wee bit longer and Spring is around the corner. YAY. I suppose if I spent my winters skiing I'd be sad but haven't been on the slopes since I was in college. About a zillion years ago. I'm looking forward to some real sunbathing instead of hanging out in a tanning booth.

And speaking of that. In December I had a handful of small, ugly, scaly red spots appear on my upper chest. The PA didn't know what they were but she gave me some stuff to take which did nothing. They didn't get worse but didn't get better. Since I started the tanning thing they have all but disappeared. Coincidence? shrug. who knows. But they're GONE. Thank you very much.

Well it's either go get dressed or fill the tub with cold water. Maybe I'll toss a coin.

ETA I forgot. Had a discussion with hubby last night about his move to the guest room and how permanent it's likely to be. He hemmed and hawed and sorta allowed as how maybe it would be good because he woke up the other night and had trouble sleeping etc. Which is his way of saying YES. So pretty soon I guess I'll clear out the closets in there so he can move his clothes in there too.

I've been using those cloests to store stuff since our house has NO storage space whatsoever. We have a 4' crawl space which is right on the dirt. So can't use that for storage. There's a storage shed out past the garage but he's filled it up with the lawn tractor and his ATV and it stinks of gasoline now so ...

I guess the cats and I will get used to sleeping without him. OTOH it does afford a lot of privacy for SO if I ever get in the mood.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-18T19:21:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76331>

So. Dream time. Don't get all excited. This was just plain sweet and wonderful and fully clothed.

I had a difficult time falling asleep last night. Finally drifted off but woke up around 2A.M. after dreaming of The Hug.

And yes, it features you know who. Seems he's finally crashed through the door to my subconscious. "Jack" was waiting for something (I don't remember what) and I came up to talk to him. We hugged. That's it. BUT it was one of those hugs that last forever where you put your all into it. I closed my eyes as we stood there, arms wrapped around each other. That golden light encompassed everything and the longer we stood that way the more relaxed I became. to the point I almost felt drugged (cue Bob Dylan "Everybody Must Get Stoned"). He finally said he had to go but we'd hug again later in the afternoon.

I woke up from that with a smile on my face and feeling so peaceful.

So no interpretations on this one. I just want to enjoy that peaceful feeling without picking it apart. I already get most of it. I just want to let it be. It was just the nicest dream ever. Sweet, really, and short and to the point. No wandering around alien landscapes and dodging nightmares. Just ... a hug. OK just a little interpretation. Evidently the right side of my brain (me) and the left side of my brain ("Jack" in this instance) have decided to make peace with one another. Yay for the grounding mat.

Hey! The tri-tip was DEEELicious. Although I thought it was a bit too garlicky (if that's even possible). I didn't have garlic SALT so used the same amount of garlic POWDER. Um, don't do that again. The meat was so good and the pan sauce added just the right touch. I fixed cauli-mash to go with. Oh and at 25 minutes the meat was blood rare. mmmmmm.

Sausages and the rest of the cauli tonight with an acorn squash for hubby.

I had planned on going to the tanning salon today for my 8 minutes of sunlight burst but it's been snowing all night and it looks the driveway is only passable with the truck. Bother. Maybe I'll go anyway ... have to get DH to put it in 4 wheel for me. shoot.

### PaulaRichards — 2013-02-19T01:16:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76380>

That tri-tip sounds delicious! I can't believe it but I love my steaks bloody now. When I was much, much younger everything had to be well done then I learned how much better they were when they were "medium". Now, if they're not oozing blood I don't enjoy them!

### janagram — 2013-02-19T21:03:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76523>

I've gotten even more that way too! (lovin meat super rare)...I guess I just have permission! DH just stares at me.
Your dream was beautiful...a melding, or melting....
Yesterday DH and I had wine with lunch and I started talking....(not that unusual)...but I mean I just got down with some soul stuff...spirituality, and things that don't really fly with him...I just "let it flow"....why not? Ah, wine.

Oh, wait! This is Darleen's journal.....(see what I mean about talking?)

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-19T22:12:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76538>

LOL ... keep going Jana ... I don't care where you post, just post!

OK gang. Got an N=1 for ya today. Yesterday I was feeling just so fine. Then we went to the movies to see the new Die Hard (run away as fast as you can ... it was so dreadful I give it a -5 rating). ANYway. By the time we got home I was feeling OFF. Kinda twitchy and just not quite right. And today I'm cranky.

Sooo I'm wondering. Intellectually I know all that violence is just pretend but does my body know that. I think not. I find myself tensing up during particularly uncomfortable scenes so I'm thinking that I'm not doing my poor adrenals any good by watching this crap. Maybe I should just start watching only porn, LOL. Good for oxytocin and not so hard on the adrenals, eh?

I just finally finished slogging my way through EMF5 for the first time. It took a while. I do have one observation. Kindly remember I only took one year of basic freshman physics about a million years ago. But the first thing that came to mind was why can't someone come up with a [wave interference](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interference_\(wave_propagation\)) device that would cancel out the ELF we are generating. A small model that would work for your home and a personal model you could wear when you go out. I'm not immigrating to Mars. So we need to FIX what we've screwed up. Technology got us here and I don't see why it can't get us out of this mess. But then, what do I know.

And a note to Jack ('cause I know you read everything on this forum) ... You missed a spot. You traveled all over the map this winter doing a biohack but neglected (I'm pretty sure) the Rocky Mountain West and I do not mean New Mexico or Colorado (which has been so Californicated it is beyond repair). I'm talking Northern Wyoming and/or South to Southwestern Montana. I wouldn't wish the Dakotas on anyone (no offense to anyone here who lives there but you know what I mean) You get back into some of these places and ELF aside, you don't get much junk being thrown at you.

Our house BARELY gets satellite signals for the TV. We would need a 6-8' antenna on the ROOF to pick up regular TV transmissions and they're still snowy and grainy. Gee, I'm so sad about that.  And we cannot get a cell signal at the house at all. darn. In town it's a soup like most places but not quite as bad as some. I'm glad we're out of town and I plan on staying here until they carry me out in a pine box.

I'm canceling netflix and have already disconnected the roku and the appletv. Ebay here we come. The microwave is next on my list. I've been keeping it unplugged except for the few times a day we use it (mostly to reheat a cup of coffee). Hubby grumbled "whatever" when I told him it was headed out the door. A pan on the stove works quite well and I'm sure we can get used to that again.

I'm still real concerned about the damned smart meter on the side of the house and I forgot there's another on the power pole out there. It belongs to the neighbors and is connected to their water pump down in the creek. WHY it's on our property I do not understand. So I"m going to do some research about ways to block the emissions from the house. There has to be something I can do. Chaps my backside that MDU is charging EVERYone $25 a month for the damned thing which basically means we pay them to have put people out of work. I just love privatization. It's just SO good for everyone in this society.

OK that's enough grousing. I told you I was cranky today. Feet on the earthing mat! It's overcast and cold (16Â° when I went to town). But Spring is around the corner. The evidence was on the road in front of our house. Cattle drive. Already. Yup, the joys of living in Wyoming. Cow flop all over the road. The late, lamented Allie McDog used to get SO excited when the drives started. You could see and hear them headed down Beaver Creek Road then they turn the herd down Big Goose and go right past our place. She'd be dancing and barking and running around. Then she'd have to settle for herding the cats. Never as much fun.

### Shijin13 — 2013-02-19T22:40:40+0200

Can I come live with you? hehehe

yup. we're going to be getting rid of most everything.. .next investment is going to be a wired router and I'm dropping cable down to where we use the computers.. actually - maybe we'll just clean the office and start using the computers up there...

as for shielding - yes you can do it... but it will take a little work... on your walls - put copper mesh on them/weld the copper mesh sheets together and either slap another sheet of drywall up or wallpaper over copper mesh sheets - note you're going to have to do this to the ceiling, walls, floors etc. Fortunately DH knows how to do this... so next house we're going to do that...I think right now - intermediate remedy will be emf paint on our current house... which of course will piss off who ever buys the thing...

As far as your neighbors smart meter why can't you complain that its on your property and requests its removal - claim eye sore - claim you've spotted golden/bald eagles looking for nesting sites... I bet the power company will remove the thing ASAP if you tell them that... might even remove yours too!

Oh BTW - Suddenly I've been dreaming like crazy - not sure if its the increased Pg, or if its the magnetico sleep pad... but damn am I having weird dreams

can I say I'm disappointed to hear about your review of Die Hard ... may have to re-think going... and I love Bruce Willis...

He's still younger than us, LOL!

Lanie, it's the whole EMF series. I saw no point in continuing netflix without the roku or Apple TV (OTOH I see they have a new series which I could just watch on the computer so maybe I'll keep it for another month and see if it's worth watching). also I bought (but then returned) and EMF meter. I was shocked when I put it next to the microwave, which was OFF, and it pegged the needle. My desk lamp pegged it as well when I turned it on so it's OFF permanently.

I'm not at the point of throwing the breaker at night. It's just too damned cold. And buying a generator sounds like a solution to keeping the pump on the hot water heat going but right now it's baby steps. Hubby thinks I've slipped a cog anyway.

Honestly so many of our friends and acquaintances are developing cancer ... and they're all avid cell phone users and users of other tech AND bad diets. I was at a friend's apartment a while back ... her cancer has recurred. I had turned on Airport on her Mac to see what wifi was available and she's swimming in it. I counted at least a dozen networks. She's on the 3rd floor and there are power lines right outside her windows.

I know that a lot of what Jack is writing about currently may seem really out there but he's backing it up with info from scientific studies and putting it all together in such a way that if you can't see the handwriting on the wall you just are not paying attention.

My health is actually pretty good, all things considered, and I'd like to keep it that way.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-20T17:25:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76668>

> [Lanie said:](/goto/post?id=76640)
>
> Ok, but how many of your friends who are NOT developing cancer are using cell phones? Cuz everyone I know uses them. And how many people who are NOT developing cancer have a bzillion wifi networks in the neighborhood? I don't have cancer and there are 28 wifi networks in and around my home (just counted them).
>
> I think Jack Kruse has some good ideas. However, I just implore you and others to use some critical thinking skills before turning your worlds upside down.
>
> Copper mesh? Doesn't that have holes in it?
>
> EMF paint? Really?
>
> I am becoming truly bothered by this.

Lanie, as in all things in this life YMMV (your mileage may vary for the acronym-challenged). Not everyone has genetic mutations or defects that lead down the road to cancer. And just because most people are (apparently) using cell phones constantly doesn't mean they are healthy or are going to stay that way.

There is just so much that we don't know about what we are currently doing to ourselves. As Jack keeps pointing out ... we've only been in this soup for a little over 100 years. Compare that to the 3 million years we have been evolving into modern humans. Plus he's cited plenty of evidence to back up what he's saying. My challenge to you would be to start reading and reach your own conclusions.

And what's your point on the copper mesh? Holes (and they are quite fine) don't necessarily mean that EMF will pass through. I don't know anything about metallurgy so cannot answer your question. I can hazard a guess that the waves will simply bounce off the metal threads or that maybe it sets up some sort of interference pattern. I dunno.

EMF paint is a reality (although it may not actually be called that) as I've been to the website.

As I said earlier, my physics knowledge is limited to one year of freshman physics so long ago I barely remember taking the class. I'm sure there are others here on the forum with far more current knowledge who can answer these questions. I'd suggest going to one of the other forums and starting a new thread. You'll get much better answers than I can give.

And lastly, my world isn't being turned upside down. Unlike a lot of people here I'm already in a good physical location. Giving up cordless phones, the microwave and the wifi is no hardship. I got along quite well long before they came on the scene.

As to cell phones ... I've NEVER liked the damn things. They are so misused and abused that if they were outlawed tomorrow I would have a party to celebrate. We had a bag phone AGES ago when they first started becoming popular. DH insisted we get it so when I drove to Colorado I would have a way to get hold of someone in an emergency. I never once used it. We went to one of those big ol' "flip" phones ... the kind that weighed nearly a pound. Never used it. About 6 or 7 years ago we got our first tracfone. Now we each have one and they are seldom on. Once again it's mainly for emergency use when we go on road trips. Living out here in Wyoming it is an absolute necessity. We have nothing but miles and miles of empty miles and miles. A breakdown out here could mean a darned long wait for the HP to finally come by.

I do think, however, that a number of people here are panicking since the EMF series began. I'm sure that was NOT Jack's intent but there's always that part of a group that does the chicken little thing no matter what. They will calm down. Yes, what he has laid out IS frightening. But as he says ... when you know better, you do better. Good enough for me.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-20T22:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76746>

Back from the salon and coffee with my friend. and here we go with yet another TMI post.

My first EVER bikini wax. And may I just say

OUCH

I had timidly thought about a full Brazilian but chickened out at the last minute opting instead for a landing strip (not that anyone other than BUZZ will be landing there any time soon but WTH). The woman who did the deed was funny and so she and I and my stylist (who was doing some painting in the next area) had a good time while I winced and tried not to levitate off the table.

during the course of THE PROCEDURE the subject of piercings came up. Clitoral piercings to be exact. I tried not to cross my legs and squeal and then the waxer informed us SHE had had it done. OUCH OUCH She said she'd always wanted to do it and after she gave birth to her first child figured WTH and did it. uhhhhh ... Well TECHNICALLY, she confided, it was not the clitoris but the hood that was pierced and yeah it was worth it.

Things that make you go hmmm.

I didn't have the ovaries (hahaha little post-hysterectomy joke there) to ask WHY it was worth it but I can sorta guess. When I go back for the touch up and the rest of the Brazilian I will ask. IN the meantime I just will have to use my imagination.

I did allow as how I would probably rather get a tattoo and she said THAT hurt more than the piercing did. hmmmm.

OK SHOW OF HANDS ... who would consider a piercing of that most delicate area?

Oh yeah, the wax job. We were doing fine until she accidentally dropped a glob of wax at the top of the landing strip. Oh well, she allowed as how since I"m blonde it isn't much of landing strip anyway. And it took most of the painful part of the full Brazilian right then and there. But I'd had enough ripping, thank you very much, so we just left it at that. She can get the rest next time I go back.

Now, if only the rest of me was bikini ready I'd be real happy. I think I'll go internet shopping now and see if I can find that hot pink hibiscus print job Gretchen thought up for me.

### ashryn — 2013-02-21T14:43:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76870>

I'd consider a piercing there... A small barbell would create some interesting effects I should think.. A "secret lady rub" when wearing jeans - as a very good friend of mine described it once... The only thing that is stopping me is piercings are a pita to keep clean whilst they're healing.. But my nipple piercing was such a rush I almost got the other one done at the same time... You know how nipple tweaking is supposed to help produce oxytocin? That good.

i have a pink tattoo of an ohm (symbolising balance and the interconnected ness of things) on my shoulder blade. It hardly hurt getting it. But it is only about 3 inches tall. My nose piercing on the other hand was the most painful thing I have voluntarily done to myself.

I used to get brazillians occasionally, but it always disturbed me to look so prepubescent. And I had several nasty cases of barthic cysts which I eventually worked out had something to do with getting ripped. So I stopped and have been a hairy feral for a few years until just recently (pool.. Beach.. Don't want to frighten people) I will probably eventually get IPL done on the bikini line so I don't ever have to think about it again. I suspect the cysts have contributed to my not being all that interested in SO since it is the glands that produce the lubricant that get blocked and eventually burst (one was the size of a small apple) sorry for the tmi Darleen, but I just wanted to pass on the warning.. Just in case.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-21T17:39:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76894>

Oh Ashryn I so do feel your pain. When I was in my early 20's one of my Bartholin glands developed not one but TWO cysts. The doc I was seeing turned out to be next to useless (long story not worth bothering with). She inserted a balloon cath into the gland and told me I had to leave it there for a week. THEN she left town. Do you know what it's like to have a piece of rubber tubing sticking out of your labia? It is NOT pleasant. Neither was the procedure.

ANYway. About 5 days in the pain became so awful I couldn't take it. I called her office and she had left town for a long weekend. I finally called the doc who had correctly diagnosed my appendicitis (which SHE had decided was an ovarian cyst and had then proceeded to pounce on my right ovary several times) and HE removed the cath. That's when he found there were two cysts not one and the cath had been rubbing on the 2nd one causing all the pain.

I think I'd rather have had that than one that got so huge. And just to make things interesting about 6 months ago I found a swelling right about where that cyst was. I had a moment of pure panic. I finally figured out it was a swollen lymph node. Odd spot but it went away almost as quickly as it appeared. stupid hoohaw.

I was thinking the same think re the piercing. A tiny barbell. and for the same reason. heh heh. Great minds and all that. Ah the nipple thing. Um. Seriously? I would have thought it would hurt like a sumbich. No thanks on the nose, still.

DIANE! You Amazon, you! That sounds gorgeous! Stars and hearts for moi. Skulls? Maybe it's my age but they're starting to have some appeal for some reason. shrug. I was actually thinking of trying my hand at skull beads. If I ever get off the computer and back in front of the torch.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-21T23:52:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-76941>

Audrey, me either. But I am thinking about it.

CT this morning was COLD. I missed another 2 sessions this week and almost skipped today but grabbed myself by the scruff of the neck and went and did it anyway. I thought I'd never warm up. Fortunately the tanning salon was BUSY so by the time I got to a bed it was already warm. Ok OK probably NOT a good thing. Sometimes even when you know better you fail to do better. Shoot me.

Oh and while I was at the tanning salon/body piercing place I spent some time eyeballing (from a discrete distance) the body jewelry they have on display plus the motorcycle mama clothing lines.  They had one jacket (all the stuff is BLACK) with printed skulls and 3D roses on the shoulder. I immediately thought of YOU diane.

And while I was thinking on all this stuff I realized that I like the idea of being a 60-something hotty. Only undercover. I LOVE LOVE LOVE [Chico's](http://www.chicos.com) clothing line. It's hip without being juvenile, trendy and slightly flashy. And I can see myself dressed in some of their clothing which would be hiding sexy tattoos and a couple of body piercings. No one would be the wiser. Kinda describes my entire life, I guess. Sexy as hell without anyone being aware of it. And I wonder why I think I'm invisible to the men out there. ha. I'm not sure that's a good thing or a boring thing or something I need to take up with my sometimes therapist, LOL.

Still haven't had a chance to watch the webinar and I just started EMF 7 and already my brain is hurting. I am not slow but I don't see how everyone manages to just plow through these things so damned fast. I have to take them in small bites, go think about what I've read, think some more and then go read some more. Not to mention time spent looking shit up. I just FINISHED EMF 5 and haven't done EMF 6 yet. geez.

I tried to....apparently I am anatomically unsuitable. Both piercers I approached said my hood was too small. They'd never seen that before. Trust me to be a freak of nature with an abnormally small clitoris I have a large tattoo that covers most of my upper back. In spite of not actually having the hood piercing myself, I know for an absolute fact it is far less painful than the tattoo. My sister in law is a body piercer and I've seen people get it done and be totally fine a couple hours later. It's very thin skin and it heals quickly. She keeps threatening to do my nipples instead but THAT one hurts. Not sure I'm up for it.

Waxing, on the other hand, is great! I've been waxing for 20 years....I usually don't bother with the bikini area this time of year, unless I'm seeing someone, but I do my pits and legs myself. I hate shaving.

I'm still on EMF 5/6 too....I can't process it all at once.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-26T18:27:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-77798>

The Schumann resonance recordings I got from Amazon ... they were a grand total of $1.98 for both. Between using those and the grounding mat whenever I sit at the computer I'm starting to feel more myself again? I'm not sure how to describe it without sounding completely woo-woo. It ties to the dreams I've had recently where my left and right brain are coming together again. A feeling of wholeness? I have to think on this some more to get the language straight.

Woke up about 2:30 and my brain took off. I am definitely feeling agitated about all the nastiness going on in this forum right now. I've seen it on other forums and it does not bode well.

What I have observed elsewhere is there are 3 main groups in any forum. You have the early adopters (those who do the chicken little dance whenever something unpleasant is observed), the torches & pitchforks crowd (the mob mentality that will mow down any and all who get in their way) and the VAST bulk which is the lurkers/observers.

And yes, this all ties to the EMF series. I am nonplussed at all the geniuses who frequent this forum. I am in awe of their massive brainpower. I just finished reading EMF 6 ... FINALLY ... and am now starting to slog through EMF 7 while EMF 8 is waiting in the wings. I BARELY can comprehend what Jack is saying. And I am not a stupid woman. So praises to all those who actually understand it all and are even now picking the series and Jack completely apart.

See, all I have managed to glean so far is this:

**TIMING IS EVERYTHING**

That's it. If your timing is off then everything else goes to hell in a hand basket. Diet doesn't matter, carbs don't matter, exercise doesn't matter. NOTHING else matters when your timing is off. And man-made EMF is screwing with everyone's timing. The result over the last 100 years has been a huge spike in neolithic disease which has now reached down into children and young adults when it has previously occurred mainly in the older segment of the population.

But WTH do I know? Evidently not much at all since I cannot follow the conversations regarding this AT ALL. Pseudo-intellectuals puffing out their chests and throwing word salad everywhere. That's what I see. It is creating discord instead of discussion. I hesitate to even come here to my journal and post because the bad vibes are everywhere. I'm not sure I should even post this here given how many viewings my silly little journal logs on any given day. (god am I really THAT much of a train wreck?)

Well, anyway. Back to personal matters. Crawled outta bed about 8:10 and ran out to the deck. It was mostly overcast but the sun was forcing itself through the clouds as it rose over the hill to the East of here. So I got some photons for my eyeballs. Followed the remainder of the prescription and am now cooking my BAB which is 30 minutes past the mark because I had to clean out the litter pans and then feed my little Center of the Universe. BAB is almost ready so need to wrap this up.

Had a packet of sardines and an avocado for lunch yesterday. Had bratwurst, sauerkraut and pureed cauli last night.

Trips to the tanning salon are slowly turning me a light, golden toasty brown. Got an appt with the nurse prac next week to get new labs ordered. Will see if my vitamin D levels have come up any. I've been popping 10,000 iu daily along with 3 - 10 minute sessions per week at the tanning place.

I am saddened beyond measure that Jack is thinking of shutting this down. ### DarleenMB — 2013-02-27T19:47:01+0200

Diane, electronic ballast not the magnetic. I did 8 minutes the first time and it was a bit too much. Depends on your D levels, according to Jack. If they're good you should be able to tolerate more. I'm pretty fair ... strawberry blonde ... 99.9% Northern European forbears (German and Swedish mostly). And I burned myself quite badly several times when I was younger. So I was pretty apprehensive. I could tell the first time I went that I was gonna be pink. I can always FEEL when I'm getting too much. anyway. Enjoy! First time was kinda scary because the beds Ruby Rays has are very noisy. You can hear things spinning up as the bed comes on line. Now I enjoy it. Just make sure you've got those eye protectors on good.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-27T23:21:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-78123>

Krista last time I had it checked (I think I'm remembering correctly) it was 33. Still way low. So I've been slamming the supps and now the tanning bed. I stopped the pills this morning as I'm going for a blood test next week. Here's hoping they've come up. Both my VDR Bsm and VDR Taq were heterozygous and the VDR Fok-I was "not found." And I do not have a clue what this means esp since I don't have the MTHFR mutation. shrug. geek to me.

Diane, no reaction from CT? How cold is your water? My skin gets bright red when I go full bore cold but it doesn't stay red once I get out of the water.

Picked up a price sheet from the salon ... nipple piercing is $60 each and the hood ornament is $100. They have a whole list of other stuff that I have NO idea what it means, LOL. Oh yeah, the piercings include "premium jewelry and H2OCEAN aftercare products."

I'm down to my last 10 minutes on my tanning pkg so am gonna get 200 minutes for $65 this time. I'm moving towards 15 minutes 3x a week. That's the max and I'm doing well with the 10 so WTH.

Picked up the last jar of oysters at Albertson's and they also had some wild caught shrimp so bought a pound of that.

I have some snow crab legs in the freezer and another bag of scallops so I'm good for a bit. Last night we had tilapia. yummy. And out-of-season asparagus. I know I know.

Forgot to mention I'm really getting into Jack's book. Great stuff if you haven't started it. VERY well written as well. Don't forget if you don't have a Kindle you can also read it on your computer with the Kindle app ... just sayin'.

Thinking it's time to book DisneyWorld. If \*I\* do it now instead of waiting until the last minute I won't have to buy a pkg from United Airlines so hopefully will save some money. Of course I just looked and Thanksgiving is the last week of Nov this year. CRAP. I really wanted to go for my birthday (I want a special pin ... whine). Oh well. There's always next time I suppose.

### DarleenMB — 2013-02-28T22:53:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-78392>

I know. clip ons. Huh. I'd rather get pierced.

It's been an interesting morning. Woke late because I lay awake for a couple of hours starting about 3:30. Well, not really awake, more like semi-awake. I wrote a long diatribe to Jack about putting his blog posts IN ENGLISH PLEASE. You are giving me a permanent migraine! Now I don't want to bother with actually writing it all out. Maybe later.

Got my morning dose of sunshine ... amazes me how little it takes to get my engine started. I cleaned out one of the 2 closets in what is now hubby's bedroom and moved his stuff over. I've already dumped two boxes of junk that has been lurking in there for about 4 or 5 years and hadn't seen the light of day since I put it IN the closet. Spring housecleaning is evidently starting early at my house. Looked at my clothes that I was moving out of there and into what is now MY room. The last few things I actually made for myself before I gained all this weight and quit sewing. Stuff I'll never wear and I cannot believe how TINY they are. Once upon a time they fit perfectly.

So there's my perennial, nay DAILY, battle. Wanting the excess weight to be gone, knowing that the old ways DID NOT WORK and that I just have to be patient. Rome wasn't built in a day and neither was my big fat ass. I have to repeat and rinse ... REVERSE T3 REVERSE T3 REVERSE T3. When my T3 levels overwhelm the RT3 then the weight will start to come off. It has to. My eating has improved so much over the last 2 years it amazes me.

When I started the leptin Rx I had to FORCE the BAB down and then I couldn't eat anything for another 24 hours. I was physically sick from eating "all that food." It was too much and I stopped for a couple of months but stuck it out here trying to wrap my head around Jack's blog posts (PUT IT IN FRACKING ENGLISH JACK!@!!!!). I read other people's journals and started my own. I got back on the reset.

I stumbled. I fell down. I picked myself up and kept going. I bared my soul to Jack in my first consult. Things changed. BOY HOWDY did they change.

The BAB was no longer troublesome and I was eating a couple more small meals each day. Now, mostly because I'm a slug this winter and don't get up at 4:30 or 5 like I do in the summer, I only eat a couple of times a day. I've trimmed the butter in my coffee to a reasonable amount and have replaced it with a hunk of coconut oil.

I wander outside naked. HA! That one still never ceases to amaze me. You really need to actually know me to understand how unfreakingbelievable that is. I use the tanning salon 3 times a week! This! From the woman who has hidden from the sun for her entire adult life. And now I'm actually getting TAN. NOT burning!

My sleep is getting better, again. I'm still not sure what caused the reversal the last couple of months. I suspect I need to just turn off all the lights at sunset and live by candle light. Good luck with that. Hubby has reluctantly followed, sort of, as I've trailed after Jack but there are still some things he flat out refuses to do. Well, I do the best I can with what I've got. That may change next winter. We'll have to see.

And now I have a jar of shucked oysters sitting in the fridge waiting for me to gulp them down. Will wonders never blinking cease?

I am not nearly as broken as a lot of people here and thus my progress appears to have been negligible. It has not. MY changes have been, outwardly speaking, primarily -- emotional? The physical changes ARE occurring. And I'm hoping the next round of labs will show a positive forward movement.

So to some who, I have heard via the grapevine, have pointed to my "lack of progress" as another way to pillory Jack and his methods I say this. As in all things in this life YMMV. I'm 66 years old. My changes are going to be slower because I have spent many MANY years breaking down. It cannot be fixed overnight. If I had had all this information when I was in my late 20's I wouldn't have suffered as I did for the next 30 years. It took me a very long time to get this messed up.

Oh and one last thing. For the first time in the last 30 years -- I am no longer GAINING weight. That alone was worth the price of admission.

forgot a coupla things in my little diatribe. And btw, thanks for the support everyone.

The last 3 or 4 nights I have gone to bed and I just lie there feeling ... unsettled. Next thing I knew it was 3:30 in the morning and I was mentally writing my little tirade to Jack about SPEAKING ENGLISH. In thinking back I realize that late afternoons for a while now I've been getting that feeling. So I"m hoping it;s a good sign and means that my T3 is starting to take over. I hope. Also, a few days ago my right knee (the one I've had the 2 surgeries on) started giving me fits. It's a little swollen and hot and I'm having trouble bending it. ### DarleenMB — 2013-03-01T18:30:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-78510>

Caroline, the clock is always ticking. Unless I have missed something specific there isn't going to be any overnight Armageddon. It is a cumulative effect that is accelerating which is definitely cause for concern especially for those with children. Those babies need to be protected and an immediate course correction taken. Also for those whose health is poor. You can't fix it if you don't know where it's broken and what's keeping it that way.

I'm not saying I'm ignoring any of what I've read so far. But it isn't as urgent for me as for some. It IS for hubby but he won't listen. the docs at the VA (and his late dad's health) have him convinced he'll fall over dead if he stops the statins.

As to the nastiness around here last week ... when I saw what was happening I ran back here to my little journal and buried my head in the sand. You should have done the same. But a bottle of wine sounds like a good 'scrip to me. GO for it! Shovel down an oyster or two instead of the cheese. I have a large mutant one lurking in my fridge ... want it?

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-02T01:13:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-78547>

NEWS FLASH!

Wyoming woman eats raw oysters, lives to tell the tale

Today was my day to play crash test dummy for my coffee friend who is a (sometimes) Reiki practitioner. She has a friend who is an NP who does cranio-sacral massage and wants to incorporate Reiki. We finally all got on the same page (one without a ton of snow) and off I went to Story. The plan was to meet at the Wagon Box restaurant for lunch then go back to my friend's house for the practice session.

I checked. They had oysters on the half shell ... 6 for $12. I told the waitress to make sure the cook picked out the smallest ones they had.

Yup. I slurped all of 'em. First one didn't slurp because I forgot about the stupid "foot" so got a mouthful of tabasco. When I finally got it into my mouth the ol' gag reflex about spit it back out but I persevered. DOWN THE HATCH. It got easier. Last one took just a split second from "in my mouth" to "down my gullet." Yes there is a definite, faint metallic tang to them. A glass of good pouilly fuisse would have been marvelous right about then.

And just so you all know they were 1/4 the size of the monster in the fridge. SO THERE.

Inger ... sunlight is good I have decided, even if you don't get a tan from it. Just the photoelectric effect is worth stripping for.

ANYWAY.

Got my tanning session in and asked the young woman at the desk (who has Raggedy Ann Red hair, 1" ear plugs and full sleeve, full color, tats on both arms -- to her tribe I am sure she is considered quite the beauty. I try really hard not to judge.) about a picture book. They're all on the piercer's phone and they're going to put them on a card and stick it in the tv at the front of the shop. She said it was a "quick" pain and within a week she was all healed up. But that was "a long time ago."

Yeah. I'm still pondering. It appears I'm willing to try just about anything to get the machinery MOVING again.

Oh yeah.

Last night when I went to bed I had that "HEAT" thing happen again. Because of some things I read over in the dehydration thread I am now thinking it's the Pg. Yesterday was day 2 of the Pg part of my cycle. So I'm thinking that if I'm heating up like that with the minimal application of Pg that my own levels must be coming up. And that the heat is FAT BURNING trying to take off. ohpleaseohpleaseohpleaseohplease

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-03T02:15:02+0200

I wanted to share this with everyone who is looking at that hideously expensive emf meter. My coffee buddy (CB for short) has [this meter](http://www.amazon.com/Technology-Alternatives-7021-Sensor-Detection/dp/B0013P6ZJQ/ref=sr_1_5?ie=UTF8&qid=1362180748&sr=8-5&keywords=EMF+meter) and it gives the same readings I got with the expensive one. at less than a third the cost. I borrowed hers yesterday and have been playing with it today. It has two settings via a switch ... normal and high. I've checked all the stuff I did with the other one and get the same readings so I'm going to buy THIS one.

Checked my earthing mat ... it's fine. Needle didn't even budge.

### Martin — 2013-03-03T17:11:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-78745>

I've been researching EMF detection devices for weeks now and have a few thoughts on selecting one.

I have come to think that the cheaper ones show activity, but lack the ability to differentiate enough to make an accurate diagnosis of the area being checked. This is just an opinion right now, but the more I check into it...

I've looked at schematics of various devices and they are actually simple in the basic design, but accuracy of the EMF levels are revealed by more sensitive circuits in the more expensive models. Professional models cost upwards of a grand!

These $30 devices are kinda neat, but what are the ranges in Hz, GHz, distance from source, cross-readings from many sources, etc., and are they useful enough to make a million-dollar decision to perhaps move to another location?

Still, I just know there is an inexpensive (relatively!) device or two that will give us enough to make a decision. The TriField meter looks interesting, but I think it lacks sufficient RF cellphone signal-gathering abilities. It looks good for house wiring field measuring, though.

I'm still looking... gathering data... horning in on Darleen's journal...
