### DarleenMB — 2013-03-03T18:38:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-78756>

The one I borrowed measures cellular as mW/cm2 (1-1/1-10)
Power (EMF) as milliGauss (1-5/1-50)

I used it on the computer last night while it was connecting via wifi (yes I still run it during the day. Am too lazy right now to drag everything out of the living room and into the room with the computers and find the right cables blah blah blah). The probe had to be right ON the computer to measure anything but it flashed off the scale when it did. I had it on the N setting.

It would probably peg permanently where most of you live. Out here in the sticks the damn thing appears to be not functioning. Eat your hearts out. ;P

### endless — 2013-03-03T20:02:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-78763>

Does that meter have the same settings as the trifield? I had to use the expanded magnetic setting to get readings on some of the electronics. The laptop puts out more at different spots, so make sure you see which directions it is highest in. I found the readings jumped when the computer was 'thinking', too.

I personally would not trust the cellular readings on something that cheap....but that's just me. I KNOW the wifi/cell is bad anyway, so I avoid it when I can.

A blossoming lotus is a symbol of rebirth in a number of cultures. I'm a HUGE fan of ancient Egyptian "stuff." And I just like stars. Stars and hearts. Since I got my parts yanked I kinda lost myself for a while. But I slowly have been coming back to who I am and who I'm supposed to be. That's why I want it over my scar ... to remind myself of that journey.

Boy howdy. Mention chocolate and/or sex around here and everyone comes outta the woodwork.

Gretch ... I had the same idea but then I forget about it. Do try the commercial ones first. They are to DIE for and will give you an idea of ingredient proportions. I figure if I make my own I can eliminate the maple syrup and sub in stevia and pure sucralose (which I happen to have in my "bread" box. BTW they don't bake their's -- they dehydrate them which leaves them way moist and chewy. Do you have one of those mini-cupcake pans? That's about the size of the commercial ones. They're maybe an inch in diameter but they look like they were popped out of a pastry bag with big tip. Know what I mean?

On another note, it has been snowing horizontally off and on since late yesterday afternoon. pffbt. And it's laundry day. AND I made oyster stew last night for DH who was disappointed that I didn't put potatoes in the stew. Then he sorta whined that it was "bland" ... like potatoes would have zipped it up any. Well it got rid of the monster oyster that tried to do me in the other day. I did notice on the lid that they were tagged "medium." Well hells bells I'd like to see what they consider LARGE.

Laundry day, oh joy oh boy. And bill day. even more fun. Plus one of the cats barfed on the bed last night. judas aitch it doesn't rain but it pours. Or blizzards in this case.

> **Boy howdy. Mention chocolate and/or sex around here and everyone comes outta the woodwork.**
>
> Gretch ... I had the same idea but then I forget about it. Do try the commercial ones first. They are to DIE for and will give you an idea of ingredient proportions. I figure if I make my own I can eliminate the maple syrup and sub in stevia and pure sucralose (which I happen to have in my "bread" box. BTW they don't bake their's -- they dehydrate them which leaves them way moist and chewy. Do you have one of those mini-cupcake pans? That's about the size of the commercial ones. They're maybe an inch in diameter but they look like they were popped out of a pastry bag with big tip. Know what I mean?
>
> On another note, it has been snowing horizontally off and on since late yesterday afternoon. pffbt. And it's laundry day. AND I made oyster stew last night for DH who was disappointed that I didn't put potatoes in the stew. Then he sorta whined that it was "bland" ... like potatoes would have zipped it up any. Well it got rid of the monster oyster that tried to do me in the other day. I did notice on the lid that they were tagged "medium." Well hells bells I'd like to see what they consider LARGE.
>
> Laundry day, oh joy oh boy. And bill day. even more fun. Plus one of the cats barfed on the bed last night. judas aitch it doesn't rain but it pours. Or blizzards in this case.

LOL to what I bolded... Love the idea you've got for the tattoo... I really want another one - but I've been holding off for while now...

I do have small little silicone cupcake tins - and a low temp food dehydrator... I've got my eyes on the gift pack tins - If I like them then I may venture forth to make them!

speaking of oysters Chaos and Havoc noshed down 3 each on Saturday for dinner. then complained b/c they wanted more and what was left was gone! they like them with some horseradish and lemon juice - then they just slurp them down! LOL!

### endless — 2013-03-05T06:37:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-79030>

> You all thought I was joking about a meet-up at a nude beach? I was not. come on you guys. Think of all the mischief we could cause ...

I have the perfect spot...I kid you not. Our area has a rich heritage....first were the Dukabores from Russia (pacifists) who were all vegetarians but prone to randomly burning their houses down to prove their non -attachment to material objects.

Next came the draft dodgers.....we are off the beaten path so it was a good area to settle in.We have a lot of children (my age now) named Karma and Rainbow. Nudity is not even an issue around here. There are beaches along the river that are nude-friendly, and some closer to 'civilization' that are not.

I guess the thing is, that nobody here is shocked or dismayed by nudity....they might curse about the 'damn hippies' or something, but overall people just tolerate each other peacefully.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-05T21:18:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-79171>

Well I'm not laughing now. My blood pressure is probably normal I'm so pissed.

Over an hour spent doing tedious paperwork. The NP finally comes in and one look should have been enough of a clue. Mid-50's, not particularly attractive, very "East Coast" in her dress and trying to appear the total professional. Doctor-wanna be.

She starts going through the paperwork I brought with me ... short medical history, recent labs. Guess what the first thing was that she remarked on. go ahead.

"Your cholesterol is high." (it was 238 last time)

"Yeah but my hscrp is 0.05. Cholesterol has nothing to do with heart disease and I won't take statins, don't go there."

Then she tried to argue with me about the RT3 ... well your numbers are in the normal range. Yes they are, it's not the range that is significant IT'S THE DAMNED RATIO. I made the mistake of mentioning stopthetyroidmadness.com and the ratio calculator and "well we don't do anything on the internet" except of course put all my medical records out there.

Then in response to my comment about the BHRT and that Chris (last doc) had signed off on it -- "Well, we won't sign off on it. It's very damaging."

My response was, and I QUOTE "I would suggest you read TS Wiley's book ..." and that was as far as I got.

**"DON'T TELL ME HOW TO PRACTICE MEDICINE, I HAVE TWO MASTER'S DEGREES"

**I thought her head was going to explode. Now mind you I didn't SUGGEST in a snarky way, it was meant as I said it, merely a suggestion. And frankly I don't give a flying fuck how many master's degrees you have I'm trying to explain to you how it works for me. She practically threw me out of the office.

I'm still pissed. You don't want to know what I'm thinking because it's a string of 4 letter words and a few MTHFRs thrown in for good measure.

stupid effing you-know-what

So I stomped down to my friendly compounding pharmacist who suggested I try the NP who bought out Dr. Wilson's practice. Who was my doc before Chris came back to town. Wilson and I butted heads often but he usually listened to me. So I'll be seeing Deb on Thursday.

All I want is some new labs so I can check my progress. I don't need or want prescriptions for pharmaceuticals that are going to destroy my health and I don't care WHAT you think about my cholesterol levels.

I want to punch something.

### Shijin13 — 2013-03-05T21:24:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-79173>

then PUNCH SOMETHING!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I really wish I could have hubby's attitude. Someone can be screaming at him and turning purple and DH will simply sit there, smile and toss off a bon mot. He hardly ever gets rattled. Me? I either go all Donald Duck on yer ass or I turn tail and run for the hills at warp speed. Usually the latter.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-06T19:44:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-79319>

Just looked at the count again. WHO THE HECK is reading my stupid little journal? I feel like I have a horde following me, LOL. DON'T FOLLOW ME CAUSE I DON'T KNOW WHERE I'M GOING!

Still freaking about yesterday's incident. I don't know why. I know she was completely in the wrong and totally unprofessional to boot. Still bothers me no end. Somebody shoot me with a tranquilizer dart. Please.

Had to get up at sunrise (darn) to get Sammy to the vet for a dental appt. He has SUCH a dirty mouth. I swear from now on I'm brushing his damned teeth once a day whether he wants it or not. Even if I have to fix a box up so that only his HEAD is sticking out and he can't get away from me. damned cat. This is gonna be at least $250. crap.

Headed out for coffee with my friend in a bit. Hope she didn't get snowed in again or that she forgets. Going for 8 sessions of red light therapy at the tanning salon (no piercings this month. Oh shoot.) in addition to my regular sessions. We'll see if it helps the redness on my throat and the itty bitty scaly patches on my back. Yeah, the dermatologist told me what they're called and they're caused by age (pffbtt like what ISN'T?!). I keep hoping they'll go away. They're ugly. I hate 'em.

My N=1 on the water "thing:" for years now I have kept a pitcher of water on the counter so I could monitor my water intake. The last 18 months or so I've pretty much stopped drinking more than a couple of glasses a day. My little emergency room episode from LAST winter turned up the fact I was, at that point, dehydrated. Or maybe it was just and excuse to stick me with another needle. I dunno. ANYway. I tried another 3 gallons of so-called ionized water this weekend. shrug. Either the stuff is just snake oil or I'm either beyond broken or super woman. It has no effect.

So yesterday I was at the loathed and loathsome WallyWorld and checked out the bottled water section. Low and behold Arrowhead water, bottled from mountain spring water in Colorado was on "sale." The gallon jugs were all gone so I bought a case of pint bottles. It's half gone already. I cannot -- CANNOT -- stop chugging it. It feels different in my mouth than tap water (and our local water is ok ... no fluoride (yet) but definitely chlorine). I'm suddenly THIRSTY. this is just weird.

### caroline — 2013-03-06T21:20:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-79326>

Great news on the water Darleen! Seems like dehydration is a huge problem - gotta figure this out!

OK so these aren't thousand dollar Lucchese all leather boots. But they're still sexy as all get out. couldn't get a side shot so you can't see the way the top of the boot curves down in the back nor the nice heel height. Bonus ... they make my size 10 feet look small. whoo hoo.

Also, got 2 pairs of jeans TWO sizes smaller than the ones I've been wearing and they fit. Gotta love stretch denim.

The red light therapy was ... interesting. 20 minutes is a long time and even with the protective goggles and my eyes closed the light was way bright. Warmed up just a little. then 8" in the standing regular tanning booth. Boy was I hot by the time I was done.

The woman at the register announced when I arrived that "the piercer" was on the premises. Oh darn. Well I'm doing 8 sessions of red light so no piercings this month. whew. Escaped with my parts intact.

i've been feeling kinda of ... I dunno, out of it the last couple of days. My zing must have zanged.

Came to the conclusion I need to get post leptin rx ... Been at this for over a year. So for breakfast I made paleo coconut pancakes with bacon and eggs. They were yummy but VERY heavy. I'm going to cut the coconut flour in half next time to see if that makes a diff.

Had a good visit with the NP yesterday. She was very very nice and more than willing to accommodate my lab requests. She's an itty bitty little thing. would probably fit in someone's pocket with no trouble. I had to kind of bend down while on the examining table so she could get to me, LOL. Anyway, getting the labs drawn on Monday, got the kit for the cortisol, just have to get her to sign off on it so they will bill the insurance. Then I get to do the spit thing.

Meh

not much else going on. I don't know why I'm in such a funk.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-09T18:17:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-79807>

Ha. You make it sound like I should change my name to Imelda, LOL.

No graphs. Some labs yes.

Spring is definitely here because the Canada Geese have arrived and are squawking and honking out on the creek though it's still mostly frozen. They just crack me up. First year we were out here I heard this racket and went out on the deck to see what was going on. A pair of geese were standing on the topmost branch of this huge cottonwood across the creek and checking out the lay of the land. You could just about hear them:

her: "Well I think we should put the nest over there by that sandy beach"
him: "whatever you say dear"
her: "on the other hand, it might get more protection if we put it over there"
him: "whatever you say dear"
her: "my best friend is nesting further up the creek, maybe we should go there"
him: "whatever you say dear"

### diane — 2013-03-09T18:22:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-79809>

I don't like the Canada geese- they scare the crap out of me. I've seen them walking in a parking lot angry and chasing everyone around. So I steer way clear of them, creepy things. Then again, I'm not much of a bird person. Although to get myself over it, I had someone put their parrot (or one of those huge birds that sit on people's shoulders) on my arm at a pet show. Actually did it with 2 different birds. Even though I was scared crapless, I took a deep breath (cause I couldn't let the damn bird know) and tried to enjoy it. It really did help with my bird phobia!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-09T20:45:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-79830>

LOL DIANE! That's too funny.

When we were kids our grandparents bought a chinese goose ... they have a distinctive bump on their beak. We named him Honk-Honk. That (expletive deleted) HATED us kids. He'd see us coming and make a bee line right for us, neck and wings extended and hissing to beat the band. If he caught you you'd get a pinch that would leave a mark.

My sister is a bird person. Always has been. She had a parakeet when she was about 7 and she thought it was hilarious that I was afraid of it. We shared a bedroom and she loved to bring that damned bird up to our room early in the morning and let it parade all over me while I was asleep. She's down to just her parrot now and because she hasn't handled him enough over the years he punishes her whenever he gets the chance. Now THAT would terrify me. They have a beak that can take a hunk out of you!

### diane — 2013-03-09T21:25:01+0200

My vet let me watch him file a bird's beak and talons. I think it was a parrot, or a grey something. It was pretty cool to watch though. But I kept pretty far back.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-11T17:52:04+0200

Last couple of nights I've awakened to find I've kicked off all the covers and am so hot I can barely stand it. Night before I couldn't get back to sleep because of it. This time I did fall asleep again. Both nights I've slept like the dead. I mean GONE. I think I'm staying in the same position because my knee is giving me fits and so is my neck. argh.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-12T20:37:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80223>

I've been over at Marksdailyapple.com reading through some of the threads. One of them caught my eye ... Eat More Fat ... It's a challenge thread. I'm not going to officially join it but I like what they're doing.

Using their calculations (although I'm ignoring the one for my weight since using it I _should_ weigh 168 pounds. I DON'T THINK SO) I come up with a daily BMR of 1676 calories multiplied by 1.2 (since I'm an inactive slug) puts me at just a hair over 2000 calories per day. The breakout of protein/carbs/fat came out about 400 calories higher that the above calculation so I'm cutting back on the fat slightly. totals work out to 53g protein per day, 30g carbs per day, 113g fat per day (instead of 150g fat). (protein and carbs = 9 calories per gram and fat = 11 calories per gram).

I've already ordered some MCT oil and bullet proof cocoa butter and cocoa from the BPE website. I'm recharging my old Palm which has a nifty little food tracker program on it. Best one I've ever found and it was discontinued ages ago so I cannot just toss the Palm as it won't work on anything else. And all the calorie trackers I've found in the app store want to chide you for not exercising and for eating "too much fat!"

So by combining this challenge with the post-leptin Rx I'm hoping that maybe I can start dropping some of this weight. I've written out the guidelines for the post=leptin Rx because by handwriting out my notes instead of just printing out the page from the blog it makes it stick in my head better.

Something needs to give besides my temper. I mean WTH did I get waxed and buffed and tan if I'm still looking like Jabba the Huts little sister?

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-13T17:28:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80365>

Jana I do ok with the same thing for meals ... for a while. I'm willing to try this "eat more fat" for a few weeks and see if anything changes. Since I'll also be watching calories (but not obsessively) by counting grams it should shake something loose.

Cannot believe I screwed up on my calculations. I don't know where I got those numbers from (probably outta my back pocket) ... recalculations show that to hit 2000 calories per day I have to up my fat intake to 180g. Now THAT'S more like it.

Hopefully my Palm (yeah yeah yeah) is gonna work. Every time I let the battery run down it gets an error trying to restart. I finally figured out if I hook it up to my old Mac (even though it's OSX it's stuck back in the dark ages because it's a PowerPC chip, not an Intel) I can get it back on track. That little program (Balance Log) takes into acct your BMR and automatically calculates percentages of P, C and F but also total calories and grams. It's a wonderful little program and I was so sad when they stopped supporting it. It's a super easy interface, too. You can add items in, quickly run a recipe through the works to figure out totals ... and it's "ancient" in tech terms.

Coffee and tanning today.

Got the last of the bedroom furniture swapped around. Hubby got his Granddad's wardrobe and the flat screen (actually I wasn't sorry to see either go to him). I got my old dresser and dressing table back so there's a little more room in "my" room now. I have the king size air bed (well, the cats have most of it) and he got the queen size regular mattress and NO cats. I think that's a fair trade. It still feels really lonely but I suppose I'll get used to it.

We're headed off to Billings tomorrow since it's supposed to be near 60 today and Thursday. Costco run. I'm almost out of toilet paper.  Next week, my luck ran out, I have to report for jury duty on Wednesday. We aren't allowed to even bring in our PURSES! Craptastic. Two more lousy weeks and my "term" would have run out. nuts.

Just got back from Billings and I am EXHAUSTED.

Figured out my knee problem and then wore the wrong inserts to shop. My knee has been hurting big time all day long. so pissed at myself.

Will write more later but need to go lie down.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-15T18:59:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80689>

I was absolutely totaled yesterday.

My knee. Somewhere around 1974 I tore the cartilage in my right knee. It was where a tendon attaches and it wouldn't heal. I'd be walking along and BAM my knee would give out. The ortho finally decided it need to be removed. They took out the whole piece which resulted, about 13 years later, in the joint collapsing since there was nothing to support it. So ... back to surgery where they straightened out the joint and then removed a wedge of bone from my leg to force me to pronate thus removing strain on the lateral portion of the joint.

I was told it could last the rest of my life or I'd wind up having a knee replacement. My personal nightmare. So whenever I start getting knee pain I panic.

The pain has been going on for over a week now. You can imagine what I've been thinking. BUT I have noticed in the last few years that when I stand barefoot my ankles both "slump" down and to the inside flattening my arches. I thought my arches had fallen and that I had "flat feet." So anyhoo, there I am watching QVC and here comes the presentation on Orhthoheel sandals. Again. This time I paid attention. The guy had drawn X's connected with straight lines on the models legs. When she was barefoot the line was crooked and when she put on the sandals, the line was straight. Well, WTH? So I ordered a pair. I think they are horribly overpriced since they ARE MADE IN EFFING CHINA but when you are desperate ...

They arrived day before yesterday. I put them on and not only did the knee pain vanish but my back adjusted itself in 3 places ... pop pop pop. My neck suddenly felt better. WTH X three!!

So yesterday, knowing I would be walking all over Billings (figuratively ... Costco and Lowes aren't THAT big) I dug out my running shoes with the NB ultra arch inserts and wore those. Well. They're were not enough and the pain in my knee was nonstop. I gave in and took a 300mg motrin I had with me and gritted my teeth. We started into Costco and I had to use the cart as a crutch. The motrin finally took hold so my gimping wasn't so bad but it was still wearing me down. PLUS it was HOT in there! It was 70 yesterday. By the time I got to the checkout my face was beet red. God I hate that. Plus would someone please explain to me HTF I managed to drop nearly $600 every flippin' time I now go to Costco? It used to be $300 then it was $400 and then it jumped. THERE'S ONLY TWO OF US! It isn't like we buy shit we don't use or need, either. gah.

Oh yeah, speaking of spending ... the Bodega malbec was not to be found but I did find two others that both came from Argentina. We'll see how they compare. DH decided he wanted a bottle of Yellowtail Shiraz. He said he likes it but I think he likes the cheapassed price more than anything. Then I found a bottle of pinot noir with, literally, his name on it. LOL ... Wine by Joe. cracked me up. Even came with a twist off cap although at $16+ COSTCO price it shoulda had a cork. I bought it just for the label.

So there's my knee story. Now for the travel bits. It was good.

Just over the Montana border we encountered the widest load I've ever seen outside a dressing room. I kid you not, this asshat was over a third of the way into the passing lane. I had to drive OVER TO THE LEFT of the rumble strip to get around him. Freaked me out. DH of course was all "You can get past him ... keep going" while I'm freaking out about the giant hangyoutty things that look like they're going to rip the lid off the van. I finally got around him although I think I gained a couple dozen new gray hairs in the process.

THEN here comes a white van with a white sedan practically stuck on its back bumper. I glance over and see there's some sort of CAGE inside the van. I look again ... yup, metal walls with large holes perforating the surface. WTH? Then I see the logo on the door ... Montana Department of Corrections Prisoner Transport. EEEEEEEEEEEEKKKK

It's like a bad Nicolas Cage movie.

The sedan had the same logo. I had visions of felons lurking in wait to cause an accident to bust their buddy out. Guns blazing (Jack would enjoy that part) ... blood on the pavement. Cars flying through the air. Innocent citizens (me and hubby) caught in the crossfire. Screaming headlines nationwide as the manhunt begins.

Damn I really should be writing screenplays.

Either that or I need to get a life. or laid. Can't decide which at this point. Maybe both.

Did you all hear that yesterday (according to a post I saw on facebook ... thanks Diane) was national steak and blowjob day?

And with that, I'm done for today. Red light therapy and tanning session on tap later.

### diane — 2013-03-15T23:13:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80702>

Today sounds like an enjoyable day!

It's amazing how shoes can impact knee and back pain. I was getting pain in my lower back walking the dogs in early January - so much so that it wasn't enjoyable. And I hated that! Realized I had bought a new pair of goretex runners over Christmas for slushy day walking, and even though I have orthotics in them, they were killing my back. As soon as I changed shoes, back pain gone! Glad to hear your sandals help.

### marthabob2u — 2013-03-16T04:55:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80726>

Well, maybe the knee replacements would be a good idea after all. Surgery sucks and so does physiotherapy afterwards, but I had two knee replacements in 2009 two months apart. Had one in May and one in July 2009 and now I am a happy camper.

Before surgery I, to quote my orthopaedic surgeon, was "so bow-legged I couldn't catch a pig" and now my legs are straight as an arrow and trouble free. After surgery one would bend OK but not straighten without a fight, the other would straighten but not bend without a fight. Now they are both just fine. Today I painted two exterior sides of our Mexican house and was up and down the ladder a gazillion times.

When you need surgery, get surgery.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-16T18:38:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80785>

Krista I got them from QVC ... orthAheel. I misspelled. it. I like them but the part that goes between your toes has rubbed me raw on both feet. OUCH. So I have bandaids stuck between my toes this morning. AND the really high arch part is super hard and my feet hurt today but I'll get used to it. Better to suffer a little than keep going and have to get my knee replaced. I AM NOT GOING THERE. I figure if I wait long enough they can repair it like on Star Trek ... just run a tiny little beepy thing across my knee and voila ... All better.

Coconut pancakes cooking as I type. In bacon fat from the bacon I'm having with. Louisiana style shrimp with scallops on the menu for dinner with green salad and cauli puree. mmmm.

Got my MCT oil yesterday so put 2 TBS in my mug of coffee. Hope it wasn't too much. I've been using about 3TBS of coconut oil so replaced it with the MCT.

Oh yeah, I'm turning into a bronze goddess. MAN I am brown. Of course, compared to regular folk I still look pale but WTH. I'm liking the red light therapy and debating about continuing it through the year. REAL sun after the first of april. Can't wait. Better warn the neighbors.

### endless — 2013-03-16T21:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80809>

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-16T23:54:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80812>

Krista, I'm fiddling with the recipe from Mark Sisson's cookbook. 3 eggs, whisked in a bowl. Add 1 tsp vanilla, 1/2 tsp baking powder, some salt and a bit of sweetener (I used 5 drops of liquid sucralose). then whisk in 1 to 2 TBS of coconut flour. It absorbs all the liquid so I just did one and gave it a minute then added the second. It was a bit much so next time will try 1.5TBS of coconut flour. I have faux maple syrup made with sucralose and acesulfame (?) I also stirred in 1 TBS flaked dried coconut ... they were yummy. Oh and I cooked 'em in the bacon fat. That's important. It made 3 small-med pancakes. I ate 'em all but it felt like too much so next time will use 2 eggs and a wee bit less flour. They sure are tasty!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-17T18:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80868>

Another gray day. A bit of snow in the air. Can't wait for Spring. The equinox may be in a week but we lag behind by 2 weeks. Darn.

My knee is still hurting and I'm really starting to worry about it. The orthaheel sandals I bought have made my feet hurt so badly I ache everywhere. So I'm taking the day off of them and limiting my walking around. Aspirin helps which makes me think it's not bone-on-bone but the twisting of my leg from the excess pronating that is causing the pain. I hope. My leg muscles are also sore. The sandals are a good thing but trying to wear them too much early on is not.

Not much else going on. The 2 TBS of MCT oil got burned right up (i.e. no "issues" from taking that much) yesterday. I felt, not exactly energized, but a little more with it than usual.

Hope to get the lab results back tomorrow. Curious as to where my D levels are these days. Since the lab didn't even know what HS-CRP was I guess I'll have to shell out $40 to the hospital for their wellness panel to get that done. honest to pete. It was 0.05 last time so wondering "how low can it go?"

### endless — 2013-03-17T18:57:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80871>

That's a little concerning about your leg....do you think it could be from CT? Didn't Dr. K say that he had pain from an old injury recur after starting? I've been reading lots and watching videos about proper foot position for running and all that. We sure screwed ourselves up with shoes! I hope your pain goes away and you figure out what works best to keep it away.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-17T20:45:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80881>

Krista ...nope. See my previous post about the injuries and surgeries. When they repaired the damage caused by the menisectomy they jammed the joint together and squished what was left of the cartilage. By removing the wedge of bone from my leg they forced me to pronate where I used to supinate (I always used to wear out the outer edge of my shoes). The reason was to take the weight off the lateral side of the joint where the cartilage was completely gone. I haven't been able to completely bend my knee ever since. My biggest fear has been that that tiny bit of cartilage would get worn down and i'd be bone-on-bone again.

Right now I'm hoping it's just pain caused by the over-pronating.

### janagram — 2013-03-17T21:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-80884>

let's hold that thought, Darleen. hoping for the best outcome, easiest to solve.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-18T21:11:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81010>

Well, so far so good. I put my NB ultra arch supports in my too-big slippers hubby bought me a year ago and it makes them fit plus they don't hurt my feet. I wore them around the house yesterday and my knees (yes, both of them) pretty much stopped hurting. We did go out for dinner to friends so I put my custom orthotics is my running shoes and did pretty good.

However, when I went to bed they both started hurting again. I'm a side sleeper and the pressure ?? on both knees was a bit much. I tried putting a small pillow between them but that seemed to make it worse. I finally gave up and took 2 aspirin and they fixed it.

Feeling pretty good so far today. I think most of the pain is from my thigh muscles. They really HURT when I massage them. So I'm going to gently massage them off and on today and see if that gives me some relief.

I also need to find that website of that outfit in Denver that is doing stem cell work to rebuild cartilage.

### Inger — 2013-03-19T07:42:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81058>

Darleen,
have you tried to limit your coffee and wine consumption? Hubs gets pain in he's hip if he drinks those just a little bit more. He have figured out himself the connection is there. To me it is plausible as alcohol and coffe both are dehydrating... so I would not drink more than 1-2 cups of coffee and zero wine for a while and see if it gets better? Now IDK if you drink wine but if you could try this, at least hubbys pain went away as he stopped the 2-3 glasses a few times / week... now he figured the coffee is bad too as he had yesterday had many cups of it and the pain come back...
IDK but somehow I feel it is good to be very careful with alcohol living in a environment satiated with EMFs.. as they dry our cells out too it must be not good at all.... exept one glass but that is the thing it is hard to not have two.. at least for me as I love the taste of wine.. and women are even more sensible than men...

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-19T17:07:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81095>

Don't drink regular coffee and limit my mug of decaf to ONE a day, drink wine about every 6 months. The knee pain is strictly biomechanical. I'm thinking about going to therapy to see if they can help. when I stand bare foot my ankles overpronate and my arches go flat. THAT's what's causing the pain.

The orthotics really are helping. I went all day with minimal pain. I did take 2 aspirin about 8PM and had no problems with knee pain when I went to bed. It's feeling better today but I can tell I'm not ever going to be able to run around barefoot or wear cute sexy shoes again until or unless I get this pronating thing fixed.

Yesterday's menu:

B: 2 pieces thick sliced bacon, 2 eggs cooked in the bacon fat; mug of BPDC with 1oz butter and 2 TBS BP MCT oil
L: sardines in spring water, one small avocado, 2 bites of what was left of the FRENCH brie (OMG it is SO good)
D: Costco's pulled pork, green salad, almonds

Don't think I even came close to the amt of fat I calculated. My Palm Tungsten is dead. I can recharge it but it won't start up, keeps giving me some stupid error message. crap. I d/l an app for my iPad and right off the bat I wanted to fling it across the room. Stupid program. All I want is to calculate fat grams and totals. No it's going to lecture me on what and how much of what I should be eating and of course it's typical SAD. Guess I'll drag out my book on how to write apps and do some studying.

### endless — 2013-03-19T17:13:56+0200

I do it the old fashioned way...I have Corinne Netzers complete book of food counts from my Atkins days, and I use that, and the nutritional panel on what I buy (if there is one). It's a very complete book, and it doesn't emit EMF...lol.

I hope you can resolve your knee issue, since you are not open to surgery as an option. I know my extremely active ex who had a crippling foot issue (I know, not the same as yours but your description of foot position made me think of it) finally had to go with reconstruction. It involved permanent metal implants but it totally restored his function and I know he'd do it again in a minute.

The stem cell thing sounds promising...did you find anything out?

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-19T22:21:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81124>

Just got my labs back. Not worth much so I guess I'll shell out $40 to the hospital this week for their wellness panel. What I did get was this:

triglycerides 158 (was 99 last June)
cholesterol 208 (was 238 last June)
HDL 72 (was 85 last June)
VLDL 32
LDL 104 (was 130 last June)

TSH 1.88 (ref range 0.2 to 4.0) -- last September was 1.460 so it's gone up
T4 5.06 (ref range 4.0 - 11) -- last September was 7.5 so it's gone down
T3 uptake 36.5% (ref 32-47) -- last September was 31% so it's gone up a fitch

no free T3 or RT3 which is what I WANTED.So now I get to go back for another draw to the lab. oh boy.

vitamin D 41.5 ng/ml (last time I think it was like 30?)

She was pleased with the D (I'm not) but it HAS come up. I have a couple of refills of the 50,000 iu of D3 so will refill that and add in the 10,000 iu I got from Vitacost on the other days of the week AND will continue the tanning booth until I can get outside for some real rays. April 1st we get past the 50Â° mark so if the sun is out I will be too.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-20T17:23:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81184>

On a personal note this morning, I spoke with my brother yesterday. We haven't spoken in nearly 2 years and things were pretty ugly that time. He'd called twice the day before and once yesterday morning. I figured he either wanted something or was dying. So I called our sister. He's dying. But we knew that already. He told her (and then me) that he's "end stage" liver failure. Then he said the docs have told him that, best case scenario, he's got maybe 5 years. Well, I'm not sure that's "end stage" but yes, he is dying. As are we all. He just knows what's killing him. It was difficult to be civil and not sound like the snow queen but he really hurt me with all his lying bullshit. Doesn't mean I still am not already mourning him. Oh well.

On the whole diet front (diet is easier than typing out "losing weight because I'm sick of being fat" front) ... I've been mulling over something I had read in Atkins ages ago and it was something akin to "you still have to watch how much you eat, this is not a pig-out diet." Then I look at my lipids and wonder WTH is going on because last year and this they are sky high compared to previous years. I _suspect_ it is all the beef I've been eating with all the fat that goes with it PLUS the fat I've added on top.

So. I've cut way back on the butter (which I've noted before) and am using MCT oil instead. Still cooking with small amounts of CO. Refigured my caloric intake from the fat challenge at MDA using my GOAL weight instead of my current weight. Daily intake should be 1500 calories. Yet more reason to get a decent calorie tracker.

I know that a few people have followed the leptin Rx and gotten stellar results. I'm not one of them. My nails are still crap, my energy level is still low, my mood IS better overall but when I miss a little sleep I become the dragon lady again. Since we're going to Disney World in December and I want to look halfway decent it's time to get serious.

Yesterday:

B: 2 pieces thick sliced bacon, packet of pink salmon heated in the bacon fat; mug of BPDC with 1oz butter + 2TBS MCT oil
L: cup of tomato soup, last of the chemical free (small) Italian sausage slices, veggie chips (small handful)
S: apple and almonds
D: BIG pork chop fried in CO, green salad with homemade vinaigrette, piece of 90% Lindt chocolate

Still probably way too many calories. I need to sit down and figure out a decent menu.

Red light therapy and tanning on the schedule for today. Sun was shining when I got up so got to stand outside and enjoy the early morning. I'm always so warm when I first get up that even 20Â° feels good.

Side note ... I've stopped CT. I'm sick of trying to scrunch down in the bathtub in the hopes of getting most of my fat self under the water, which I never manage. My neck always hurts and it's done nothing. I still take tepid showers followed by 5 minutes of icy cold. That will have to do. Maybe we'll get a stock tank for the summer and I can soak outside.

Just about done with "the book." Have notes for my review, just need to write it. In case you hadn't heard it looks like the print version may now be available and I heard via the grapevine the price of the ebook was dropped to $9.99 so if THAT was holding you back, time to go get it.

### endless — 2013-03-20T17:32:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81186>

I hear you about the leptin reset...I am not one with stellar results either. I have been changing things up and hoping to hit something that works better for me. A year is a long time, and if it hasn't 'worked' by now. At least I figure I should have had these mythical 'smooth heels' I've been hearing about! lol

Sorry to hear about your brother. It's an odd thing to be estranged from someone you grew up with....especially when something like that happens.

Your menu sounds ok to me....I was having an apple or two a week up until recently...cut them out to see if the carbs were making a difference. I love that I crave them instead of crap, though!

>
> Hi Darleen. So sorry about your brother. I believe you still have some underlying issue that is holding you back. Eating the BAB and taking Cytomel wont be enough to get your leptin reset if you have other things wrong. I have been doing this for 2 years and I have finally found some answers. It is a slow process for sure for some of us. I still don't know that I have them all but am continuing to do the LRx along with everything else for now. I have to knock all of the bad out that I know of and then do more testing if necessary. I have all those symptoms that you still have too. Crappy nails, not a lot of energy and yes, the dragon lady still rears her ugly head. You might have autoimmune issues and if so, you won't get magically better eating things that you aren't supposed to. You might be surprised if you did the autoimmune test for the dairy, eggs, etc. When I dropped dairy, eggs and coffee, that was a good day for me. You could try this on your own by eating only meat and fat for about a month. Take out eggs, dairy, corn, rice and soy and see how you feel.
>
> You might still have an upside down cortisol level. Have you done an ASI lately? Have you done a gut test to see if you are absorbing the good food and if you have the right bacteria? Crappy nails and low energy tells me you might not be getting what you think you are.
>
> Another thought is bad trigs are from carbs, not fat from what I understand.
>
> Not trying to preach. You sound pretty frustrated. Just trying to offer up some other thoughts for you.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-20T20:21:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81208>

Vickie, I have all but cut out carbs this winter. For about two months the only ones I got were from eggs, if I even ate those. So no, the high trig has to be from all the meat. There was no other source.

My cortisol, last Fall, was still low both AM and PM. I have the kit here and just need to remember to do the spit thing and get it sent off. I'm hoping they have leveled out near to where there supposed to be.

I simply cannot go on eating all that protein, most of it in the AM.

I have had really lousy fingernails my entire adult life and I blame that, and the low energy on too high a level of RT3 (now that I know what that is). I haven't done any gut testing because honestly, I'm pretty confident it's good. Visual test ... stools are dark and well formed (eww tmi) I've been doing Kombucha and pickles and kraut this winter and I have a bottle of Primal Defense on the counter if I feel I need it.

I don't eat rice or soy ... gave them up ages ago. Popcorn? Maybe once every couple of months now. The only dairy I eat is butter and occasionally good cheese but not very much of that. I don't gorge. If I want a bite of cheese, an ounce, if that will do. My diet most of this past winter has been bacon, GF beef, fish, shrimp. I quit HWC months ago.

I think I have just been eating too much. Unless I want to continue to weigh over 200 pounds. Since I don't, something has to go and that's about 500-750 calories a day that I don't really need.

Sammy the cat has had 4? or maybe 5 seizures since the first of the month. Soooo I set the damned alarm for 6:15 so I could get up and make SURE he ate his medicated food. I hope that's all it's been because of they're going to start up and get really bad it's the end. I can't even think about it.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-23T17:49:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81542>

Not only have I found it super easy to stay within the boundary of ~1500 calories/day but for the first time in ages I am waking up hungry. That's telling, right there.

Yesterday:

B: 1 piece thick sliced bacon, coconut "pancakes" (made with 2 eggs and 2 TBS coconut flour) cooked in the bacon fat, mug of BPDC with 1 oz butter and 2TBS upgraded MCT oil
L: water packed sardines, <1 ounce veggie chips, dill pickle
S: apple, handful raw almonds
D: ~4 ounces t-bone steak with ~1/2 TBS herbed butter, steamed broccoli

done. Under 1600 for the day according to livestrong app.

Sleep has been good except for having to get up the last 3 mornings about 6ish to give Sammy his meds. I'm done with that and we're back to the automagic pet feeder.

Had an interesting conversation with the woman who owns the tanning salon yesterday. They also do body piercing and, of course, sell the accompanying jewelry. I had mentioned I make jewelry then clarified by explaining I actually make lampworked beads and occasionally make jewelry. She's desperate for larger sized ear plugs and wanted to know if I could make them. She gave me a list of sizes and I'm going to make a few test ones for her employee to see how it goes. So now I HAVE to get back to work!

### diane — 2013-03-23T18:27:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81545>

Opportunities happen in the most interesting places, don't they? Sounds like good incentive to get to work!

Interesting about the hungry feeling. I've had that the past few days as well. Maybe it's a spring feeling? I'm having a hard time eating breakfast, and wonder if it's because I take the Diatomaceous earth with my supplements, right before I eat breakfast.

Are you really tracking calories or grams of nutrients?

### SeaHorse — 2013-03-23T19:21:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81553>

Sounds like the world is telling you it needs some of your beads!

### endless — 2013-03-23T20:25:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81556>

I haven't counted calories in ages....maybe I'll do it for a few days just to see where I am at. I still have to try those coconut pancakes, they sound so good!

That would be great if you got into custom body jewellery! Bet you could trade for free piercings......

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-23T20:46:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81558>

Yeah it's funny about the earplugs as I had been thinking about making some. I have some ideas for something NO one has done (that I have found) so maybe I can stay ahead of the curve on this. I was making "Troll" beads 2 years before it caught on in the lampworking community. But I stopped and by the time I pulled my head out of my back pocket I was completely at the rear of the trend.

Krista, counting calories is just an exercise to keep me from overeating right now.

coconut pancakes. Adjusted recipe: 2 xtra lg eggs (cause that's what they had at Costco) with 2 scant TBS coconut flour, heaping 1/2 tsp baking powder, heaping 1/2 tsp of vanilla, sweetener to taste, 1/4 cup coconut milk (full fat) or HWC. YUM. I got a little too much flour in them this time so they were a bit dry. have to watch that.

### endless — 2013-03-23T20:48:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81559>

What kind of flour do you have? I've been reading recipes on different sites and there seems to be wild variation in results that they are attributing to the brand of flour! I'm going to try that for lunch.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-23T20:53:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81560>

Bob's Red Mill high fiber organic coconut flour. Just add the flour in small amounts until you get the consistency you want. Pancake batter should be a bit runny ... too thick and you've got waffle batter. or crumbly pancakes.

### endless — 2013-03-23T21:08:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81564>

Thanks. Mine is Tropical Traditions. I'll monkey with it and see how it turns out. I notice that when I use it to bread fish fillets if I don't mix it 50/50 with almond meal it is very dry, so I might not need much.

### diane — 2013-03-24T16:16:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81610>

The pancakes are delicious!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-24T17:11:58+0200

Diane ... aren't they? I can't take credit though. Got the basic recipe out of Mark Sisson's cookbook. I just adjusted the amounts to make it more pancakey.

Cold gray Sunday. bleh. Waiting for summer when our front yard will look like this. Cows are always optional 'cause it IS Wyoming, after all.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-25T16:58:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81691>

LOL, Martin that was almost as funny as the cartoon!

Definite mood improvement since I stepped down off the reset. Small amounts of protein 3x a day and limiting carbs (but not eliminating them) and bumping the fat seems to be having a positive effect.

Yesterday:

B: 1 piece thick sliced bacon, 2 eggs scrambled in the bacon fat, 1 mug BPDC with 1oz of butter and 2TBS upgraded MCT oil
L: leftover roast beast with a few bites of potato (!yes potato!), 3 or 4 baby carrots, a bite or 2 of onion and the broth, handful of raw almonds for dessert
S: apple and a few more raw almonds.
D: small tin of mackerel fillets (packed in sunflower oil ... ick but that's all they had), sweet potato chips (about an ounce)

Lots of spring water (which has hubby in a snit as he thinks our tap water is just fine).

Slept ok except for the damned cats. Between Missy pawing at my neck to be let under the covers, Sammy hopping up and down off the bed (and walking over me, of course ... 16 pound cat who is NOT at all graceful), and Three trying to snuggle without disturbing Missy ... well you get the idea. Woke early (like about 5:30) and lay there about an hour before giving up and getting up. Sunrise always is 20 minutes AFTER actual sunrise because of that ridge we're behind but it was spectacular nonetheless.

Weighed in ... will weigh once a week from now on. Scale was up 2 pounds (probably from getting rehydrated) but I'm not worried as I am now eating WAY less than I was before.

### endless — 2013-03-25T17:19:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81698>

Ah yes, sleeping with cats. I have three on my bed as well, needless to say I don't change position much!

How on earth can you handle eating only ONE piece of bacon?? It's like teasing...I can't stop at just one.

### janagram — 2013-03-25T17:21:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81700>

trying a new approach is a damn good idea. I've tried a potatohack...(last wk...1 day)...its hard to do but I do lose about a lb a day if I stick to it. No fat.
Hell, Darleen...I just don't know anymore. Just want a diet that lets you live your life! Went out to dinner last night and gained a pound! why? I had ahi tuna!
Oh, sorry, this is your journal!!

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-25T18:44:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81714>

Krista ... look at all the fat I'm getting in the morning. Makes it real easy to stop at just one piece.

Jana, I hear ya. I really like the force fat approach. Honestly 60g of protein is plenty. I'm getting enough carbs to keep my FBG in a good range and I am NOT hungry. As I said, my mood has improved -- the one marker I've been waiting a whole damned year to see and here it is! I also firmly believe that paleo is a good template. I will continue to add as much fish as I can and keep starches to a minimum. Doesn't get much easier than that. And I think there's room for an occasional treat. I also think that weighing every day is a big mistake. This from the woman who used to start every day by doing just that. Your weight can vary so much in a 24 hour period it isn't even funny.

That's all I know. Not much. But I'm working on it.

### endless — 2013-03-25T18:46:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-81715>

I wonder if your mood has improved from adding the carbs in...

I don't weigh myself even every week anymore. waiting till I see/feel some difference in my clothes.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-31T19:50:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-82362>

Jana, I'm back on low carb, modest protein, plenty of good fats and on a regular schedule. No more BAB. I'm using livestrong.com to track my calories and attempting to keep them around 12-1400. And btw I do NOT count the calories in my BPDC ... they come from fat and if I count them then I don't have much left for food-food. I know that sounds kind of stupid but the MCT oil gets used immediately and I'm now very strict on the butter. Looks something like this:

B: 2 regular slices pan fried bacon, 2 eggs scrambled in the bacon fat, mug of BPDC, 2 ounces pomegranate juice
L: water packed sardines, 1 ounce sweet potato chips, dill pickle spear, handful raw almonds
S: apple and more raw almonds if I feel hungry
D: baked tilapia, green salad with homemade balsamic vinaigrette (usually 2TBS or less)

### Hope — 2013-04-01T20:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-82493>

Darleen, that is awesome about your new T labs!!!!! Way to go!! I have to check out your new site as well!

### lioness7 — 2013-04-01T22:07:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-82509>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=82238)
>
> Oh man. Someone posted a link to the sun's distance from the earth based on your lat and long. You could enter in a day, then it had degrees from the sun by hour. It's in one of the threads here!

The link is [http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php](http://aa.usno.navy.mil/data/docs/AltAz.php)

But, I always had a problem using the international form (though have not tried it for a while), so I used to use the U.S. form and entered Niagara Falls N.Y. to get a pretty good approximation for Toronto.

The other site is [http://nadir.nilu.no/~olaeng/fastrt/VitD\_quartMEDandMED\_v2.html](http://nadir.nilu.no/~olaeng/fastrt/VitD_quartMEDandMED_v2.html) where you can input more variables.

### DarleenMB — 2013-04-02T04:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-82562>

>
> And....did you ever start red light therapy? If so do you like it?

I've done 10 sessions so far. And my lips are back! My upper lip had disappeared quite a while back. Age sucks. But I noticed a couple of weeks ago it felt like I had a fat lip and sure enough, they're plumping up. Well, as much as mine ever do. So yeah, I'm really liking it.

> [Lanie said:](/goto/post?id=82515)
>
> Darleen, can you say more about this? Even if the MCT oil gets used right away as opposed to being stored, shouldn't those calories be counted since your body is NOT burning off other calories since the MCT oil is being burned? I mean, if you're going to count calories why wouldn't you count all of them?

Lanie I want to make sure I get enough protein so I decided to ignore those two counts. It's a game I play with my head like entering deductions in the checkbook that never actually exist. I've managed to build up quite a nice little cushion doing that so now I never ever worry about having enough money to pay the bills or bouncing a check. It's silly, I know but it works for me.

and I forgot ... about the RT3 ... the NP told me wrong. That number is 157 NOT 1.57. WOW what a difference. It means my ratio IS 22.3!! I stopped the cytomel Saturday and just take the iodoral + selenium now. I did notice a little bit of trembling in my left hand again about 3pm. Gotta remember to take some d-ribose at lunch. That stops it. So YAY for me on the thyroid. On the right track.

Lanie, good to know. I thought I was just being weird. Something new is I now pay for everything (except the monthly bills) with the cc. We're racking up air miles for our next trip. yay! All I'd really like at this point is for that person to acknowledge what she did and 'fess up to the guys she used to destroy my reputation. Fat chance. I still cringe when I see a post from one of my high school friends wondering if they still think I'm some kind of slut.

Inger, thanks. I am trying to cut out the tanning booth and get the real thing. The weather hasn't been real cooperative.

### DarleenMB — 2013-04-07T17:47:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-83231>

Lanie, I just checked. This theme uses an iPhone-like appearance. To leave a comment, look next to the title of the post. You'll see a "conversation balloon" over to the right. Click on that to leave a comment.

### DarleenMB — 2013-04-08T01:36:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-83290>

Since things are not going anywhere I've decided to do a once-weekly checkin starting today.

My weight has not budged in over a year which, in and of itself, is actually a very good thing. But being stuck at 231 sucks. However, I have decided that I've set my goals way too high (or low, depending on your viewpoint). Why? Well, my age for one thing. For another, there was an article in one of the fashion magazines I read that showed real women getting their jean-thing on.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/jeans-jpg.85/) [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/jeans2-jpg.86/)

On the left, 5'8", 162 pounds. On the right, 5'10", 200 pounds.

I'm 5'9.5" and was aiming for 130. Well, not gonna happen. So I'm thinking 160-165 might be a better goal. Especially since I'm starting back to doing heavy lifting. Muscle and all that.

On another note I was steered to a book by Ridha Arem entitled "The thyroid solution diet." Dr. Arem is an endocrinologist in Houston with a spectacular website and a forum which no one posts in. Too bad. He pretty much lost me when he mentioned "healthy grains" and he DID lose me at insisting on cardio. Um, no thanks doc. I may yet buy the book (I just downloaded a sample to my Kindle, that's as far as I've gotten) but after reading some of the review, probably not. It seems EVERYone has an answer but I've yet to find one that really works. And before the howls of protest begin, let me point out the only reason I started on THIS journey with Jack was because I want/need to LOSE WEIGHT.

My health is pretty decent given how much extra weight I'm packing around.

Well anyway. That's all I have for this week. See ya around the water cooler.

### fitness@home — 2013-04-15T00:28:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-84098>

> [DarleenMB said:](/goto/post?id=83290)
>
> I'm 5'9.5" and was aiming for 130. Well, not gonna happen. So I'm thinking 160-165 might be a better goal. Especially since I'm starting back to doing heavy lifting. Muscle and all that.

Darlene, I am 5'7.5" and have never been considered petite. Have been this height since beginning of 7th grade when I towered over the rest of my peers, especially the boys . I would love to see 130 again but it is also not likely...my weight doesn't go down that far unless I am dealing with IBS and/or high anxiety.

Right now I am just barely under 145 and carry it well. I feel good. Not going to beat myself up over it. I think your goal is good for your height. Would LOVE to be be 5'9"!

### DarleenMB — 2013-05-06T23:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-lets-try-this-darleenmb.627/post-85536>

It's been an interesting several weeks. The sun finally decided to quit playing hide and go seek with Mother Nature and I've spent a couple of blissful afternoons (well, OK really only 15-20 minutes per side each time but still) getting solar powered vitamin D therapy! Man it was HOT out there today. I am a pansy? or is it candy? ass when it comes to summer heat. I wilt. Pansy it is.

Sleep has been pretty good but when you're sharing your bed (even if it IS king sized) with three cats it can be problematic when the little darlings want the warm spot, or want you to get your ass outta bed because the SUN IS UP DAMMIT AND YOU SHOULD BE AS WELL! Or maybe they think you should get their breakfast ready because, you know, dry kibble is just not what they wanted.

I finally managed to get my big ol' ass to the gym last week. The owner took me through all the machines and wrote down the settings for me. I think he was trying to kill me. The next day was fine but the day after that I was a hurtin' unit. And the next. But I went today when my massage therapist changed my appointment on me and I got through all of them. Want to know what I hate the most? The wall to wall frickin' mirrors. I've gotten very adept at only looking at my face but when you're sitting 3 feet from the mirror it's kinda hard not to see your/my fat belly, the batwings on my arms, ugh ugh ugh. Well, that's not exactly what I said to myself.

I may yet hit the treadmills he has. The place is pretty well empty or it has been each time I've been there. Much nicer than the crowds at the Y and no kids. yay!

And finally, a little something to get everyone stirred up. We WERE talking about oxytocin weren't we?

I still haven't done the piercings. Doesn't seem to be much point since I'm the only one playing with my parts. Oh well. I'm still thinking about it though.

I have discovered, quite unintentionally and totally by accident (drum roll) that female ejaculation is a real deal. See it all started when I spotted an interesting blog entitled "[aroused woman](http://arousedwomanblog.com)" and naturally had to go check it out. Well she just doesn't pull any punches. She recommended [this](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00893XJMQ/ref=oh_details_o03_s00_i00?ie=UTF8&psc=1) so naturally I ran right over to Amazon and bought it. Let me just add here that glass is wonderful. She recommended another toy but I haven't gotten the nerve up yet to order it. I'm sure you'll understand when you find it.

short pause while I ponder the wonder that is glass

OK.

So anyhoo I was giving it a good workout the other day when I finally FOUND the damned spot. Leave it to me to have things not be where they are supposed to be. Did I ever mention that when I had my hyst I decided to do a spinal block but after 30 minutes of trying the anesthesiologist had to give up because he couldn't block me? So OF COURSE something as important as the OMG-spot isn't where it should be. That explains a lot.

But it's there.

Pause for a while as I ponder the wonders of the female operating system.

Back on track. There I was enjoying the sensation when ... WTF was THAT?

[This is what THAT was.](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Female_ejaculation)

I have been wandering around like a hormone amped 16 year old since then. I've caught myself eyeballing every male who crosses my path. holy macaroni. Nothing like being in your mid-60's before you finally discover WHAT is what.

# Thread: you know how i feel
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 99
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### .lina — 2012-04-03T18:28:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-10806>

_fish in the sea - you know how i feel

river runnin' free - you know how i feel

blossom on the tree - you know how I feel

it's a new dawn

it's a new day

its a new life

for me

and i'm feeling good

THIS. this has become my theme song. what a YEAR it has been. oh my oh my oh my.

please excuse me, i am feeling very shy and quite n00bish here amongst all you experts,

but i shall jump in the deep end of the cold pool and carry on introducing myself:

i am a swedish female of 33 years. january last year i weighed 111 kilos / 245 lbs.

that was after 15 years dedicated to low fat regime and exercise. i dieted myself fat.

an online friend in Iceland inspired me to do the opposite - her results with LCHF, which

is what we call it here in Sweden, Low Carb High Fat - gave amazing results. her weight

and her winter depression (welcome to the cold dark north) both vanished. i wanted me

some of that! so off carbs i went in march.

after some amazing success with my lifelong stomach problems instantly vanishing, my

weight still felt a bit stubborn so i searched around for possible ways to budge it, and i

came across the Leptin Reset. so THIS was why i always had been constantly hungry!?

i could literally have dinner and stuff myself to bursting point and then shortly after be

ravenous. it had eased off a bit with with LCHF but not entirely.

so on the reset I went in june.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

(my weight chart for the last few years)

things have gone so nicely - a bit up and down, sure, but overall down. and my health up.

**so** why wouldn't i try CT as well!  it's an adventure and i am full of trust. and so _inspired.

i am following the discussions here, Jack's facebook and blog, and consuming all the links

to other blogs, articles and research that i can lay my eyes upon. my eyes are wider open

than ever before, i see red threads going all over_ the place. connecting the dots. and being

considered all kinds of crazy by people around me  but i will let my results speak for me.

i still have a long_ way to go, probably 20kg / 44lbs more, but i am starting to feel like it can

be done, and people are commenting on my amazing energy and mood and improving figure.

thank you all for bringing this new paradigm on, and thank you Jack for changing my life.

### .lina — 2012-04-04T18:29:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-11326>

oh wow, that's a lot of sweetness all at once - i'm blushing!

**Jack, thank you kindly! will keep posting :}

**Marsha**, thanks! oh the image posting thing is a debacle isn't it! hehe. i just use the \[/img\*\] (sans the \*) and it always works. also no need for any elaborate answer to the photography lighting thing - all natural daylight (Jack would approve, non? hehe)

AbetterME\[/b\], you know, i can only take credit for taking pics of it and eating it up ;D i am an outright horrible cook! the one who stands for the magic in the kitchen is my better half. he just gave me permission to post his success-story with lowcarbing and his own first steps into CT, so that will come in a future blog post. but omg, his before/after photos are \[i\]something else\[/i\].

my own CT:ing is all babysteps - i have done the facedunking and the cold showers and even today i have sat here with ice packs strapped to my body, haha. which makes me wonder - if they are applied to areas you want to slim down a little extra, would it work to have one strapped to your chin? \[img\]http://4um.fromamouth.com/images/smilies/icon\_shifty.gif

i am working up courage to do the baths, but am a bit hesitant as i have felt a little off these last few days - i guess it could be the detox thingy from the CT maybe? i feel a little lightheaded and like my heart races. maybe just a cold coming on. right now i feel pretty awesome

i am longing for when the weather gets better so we can start enjoying the outdoors a bit more, and head to the lake for refreshing cool dips in the setting sun like last year!

**

### .lina — 2012-04-05T12:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-11659>

**davek, thanks  it's a magical photo indeed, hope to take many more this summer!

**ealachan, i agree!

elin**, thank you. just ask for recipes and thou shall receive ;D

kiwilauren**, hehehe thank you, i shall lead you into temptation

last night i cooked because my chef was out at a dinner with coworkers. i decided to make

it hard for myself and test new recipes - also to cook fish since he's allergic to all seafood.

so i started out by baking something that people had written about as a perfect pasta substitute.

not sure if i did it correctly, but it turned out... well, like a cross between pasta and omelet, hehe.

it was pretty simple to do - mix 6 eggs with 200g philadelphia and 0.75 deciliter psyllium husk,

let it set for 10 minutes, pour thinly in a baking pan, bake for 10 minutes then take out, let chill

and start cutting strips. i'm not sure if paleo people eat cream cheese though? LCHF allows it

then i ovenbaked a fillet of cod (wild-caught, i was looking for salmon but they only had farmed

ones and i read they get fed chickenpoop and liquidized feathers, which if true is just _so_ sad & gross.)

with some lemon- and parsley butter.

to add another level of chef-i-ness, i fried up some shrimp in olive oil and lime zest. et voilÃ !

though i have discovered that i am no longer a fan of olive oil (i tried 3 different), it tastes oddly

like candle wax!? haha. i guess my tastebuds have gotten more sensitive since i quit eating junk...

### .lina — 2012-04-08T12:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-12616>

i confess, i've been quite naughty! my parents are visiting for easter and that

means wine, lots of wine. i've had mostly red delicous wine, but also some

glasses of rosÃ©. i usually don't snack inbetween meals but they brought out

the roasted almonds and dark chocolate. we haven't had any proper easter

food so nothing to gorge on, which is a relief. so. my weight?

**thursday -500 gram / -1.1 lbs

**friday -400 gram / -0.9 lbs

**saturday -100 gram / -0.2 lbs

sunday** -600 gram / -1.3 lbs

what kind of black magic is this? :O hehe. well i don't complain... i guess it'll bounce

up soon, probably tomorrow as we are tasting some variations wedding cakes today.

(we're getting hitched in june on our 16-year anniversary together).

and some food pics:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

roast with fresh mushroom sauce and cauliflower mash

taco seasoned minced meat with fake pasta, avocado, creme fraiche, shredded cheese and tacosauce

oh by the way, my mom tasted the fake pasta and burst out "but it tastes just

like regular pasta!", so here's the recipe for those of you who want to try it, or

when you have friends over who are the kind that say "i could NEVER give up

pasta!", to show them there are viable alternatives sans gluten & carbs

> FAKE

**

> PASTA

**

> for 2 oven pans
>
>
>
> \- 6 eggs
>
> \- 200 grams / 1 cup of cream cheese (f.x Philadelphia)
>
> \- 0,75 deciliter / almost half a cup of psyllium husk
>
> \- a pinch of salt
>
>
>
> Put the oven on 150Â°c / 302 f.
>
> Whisk the eggs fluffy, add cream cheese and psyllium husk and whisk it all together.
>
> Let the "dough" set for 10 minutes.
>
> Cover an oven pan in a cookie dough sheet and pour in the dough, make sure it is as thick as you want the pasta to be.
>
> Bake it for 10 minutes.
>
> Take it out and let it cool off. Then you can either slice it thinly with a pizza cutter, cut out bigger pieces for lasagna or canelloni. You could also try to roll it up and cut or slice it for easier strips. For me, it was a bit tricky to get it off the baking sheet, but i think mine were too thin.
>
>
>
> Finished! You can eat it immediately, keep it in the fridge for a few days or freeze it.
>
>
>

### [email redacted] — 2012-04-13T16:17:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-14682>

### .lina — 2012-04-14T15:02:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-15036>

ok! this sauce is made with button mushrooms, this kind:

dice half of the mushrooms finely, slice the others in 2-4 slices.

start by panfrying the diced ones in butter,

then add the sliced ones and keep panfrying.

season with black pepper and the herb savory.

if you want, you can add some stock now.

add heavy cream and let it storm-boil until it thickens.

then take off the stove and let it settle.

this should do it

### .lina — 2012-04-14T23:47:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/you-know-how-i-feel.628/post-15141>

my pleasure, **AbetterME**!

so hey, i was looking through photos on my harddrive and stumbled across these pics from 2 years ago taken at my niece's baptism, that made me absolutely cringe when i saw them! so naturally, i wanted to post them online for everyone to see  and pasted it together with some newer pics from yesterday and today to compare progress since i cut carbs.

i still have a loooong way to go but what a _shock_ i got from seeing how much it showed on my face! :O you know, from day to day looking in the mirror, it's so gradual that i didn't pay much notice to it - but like this side by side it's quite a difference. i can hardly imagine what i'll look like 10 more kilos down the road, let alone 20 or 30!

oh! and! haha, i got a comment at work yesterday that i looked "fit", bless 'er!

i still weigh twice as much as the girl who said it, but it still warmed ye ol' cockles ;D

### .lina — 2012-04-15T00:31:55+0300

other things that have happened the last few days include my first intermittent fast,

and my first cold bath. hoping to see results on the scale/measurements soon, so sick of plateauing.
