Just a few weeks ago I was doing great with my CT. I was increasing to 30 minute baths in 53 degree water. My constantly cold feet were improving, dry skin was getting better, sleep was more restful and energy levels were increasing. In the last week it seems like everything has taken a nosedive.
>
>
>
> My feet are freezing again and my hands are so dry they're cracking. I'm having trouble falling asleep and am waking at least once throughout the night unable to fall back to sleep for hours. I never feel rested or refreshed in the mornings any longer. I also just feel "down" in both attitude and energy level. I feel like I have no motivation to do anything, especially CT. My constipation is back and I'm nearly a month late on my cycle. Could all this just be related to detox and hormone balancing?!?!
>
>
>
> I'm on 1 grain (65 mg) Naturethroid and am wondering if I need to increase it. I have a lab slip to get re-tested, but I was holding off until after a few months of CT so I could see how it was affecting things, but now I think I need to get it checked sooner. I'll probably head in this weekend.

Check your diet maybe some excess omega 6 has slipped. Dry cracked skin is a sign of omega 6 excess.

### vkiernan — 2012-04-15T02:51:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypothyroid-worsening.819/post-15179>

I am doing the same as you. Up and down and all over the place with everything. My skin was doing great for awhile too and now it is worse and it has been 7 weeks or so of CT'ing. I have had nausea with headaches, hormone issues, bad pms, bad TOM, moodiness, etc. Dr. K says this isn't linear. I am not concerned and will keep on a doing. I know over the course of time things will right itself. Some of us have a lot of issues to fix and I think that is why this is so difficult. The poor body can only do so much at a time. I have read some of these threads about bad symptoms and we are not alone in this. Anything bad outside the norm and different has to be good or it wouldn't be happening. Hang in there.

### Kiera2 — 2012-04-12T09:55:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snacking-is-coconut-oil-snacking.823/post-14146>

Yes, and yes. But some people who were really struggling in the beginning of their Leptin Rx, had a small between meal snack of CO just because it was the lesser evil. But we're not supposed to snack at all, and CO is a snack.

### freesia — 2012-04-12T11:17:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snacking-is-coconut-oil-snacking.823/post-14151>

I needed CO between lunch and dinner for the first 4 or 5 days. Then I made sure I got some of extra oil at lunch, plus the LeptinRX worked it's charm, and I haven't needed it since.

### Petra — 2012-04-12T12:34:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snacking-is-coconut-oil-snacking.823/post-14161>

Given the structure of CO, I thought it would help with the fat burning part of the diet.

I only use it when my stomach tells my brain I need lunch. That rumble makes people around me scared.

So it is a snack, yet one of the better ones in my opinion. About coco bark I don't know any thing.

Petra

### Glamorama — 2012-04-12T12:45:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snacking-is-coconut-oil-snacking.823/post-14164>

### Patty Cakes — 2012-04-12T19:09:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snacking-is-coconut-oil-snacking.823/post-14282>

It's probably on the MDA thread from a while ago.... I'd say probably pre-December. Or maybe his blogs.

Snacking is not supposed to be done because it stresses the pancreas. Every time you put calories in the body the pancreas has to work. Now I have used CO as a meal when not hungry enough for a full meal but still needed something. I'd eat a couple of Tbsp for lunch and be fine until dinner.

### janagram — 2012-04-12T19:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snacking-is-coconut-oil-snacking.823/post-14284>

Ohhhh, thanks guys! Been having a T. or two in evening or aft. but don't really haaaave to, so won't.

August, support thread would be great!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-04-12T20:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/snacking-is-coconut-oil-snacking.823/post-14313>

Its a snack sadly......I use it a lot especially now in spring and summer.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dog-tags-saying-im-cold-adapted.824/post-14158>

My mind is playing with me, and gave me this insight for the future:

Because being cold adapted is a game changer in the medical world. And medical care can be handled differently on cold adapted patients: I suggest we should were a dog tag saying that, so that when we are in need medical staff can edit their care.

How long will it take before we can get those?

Just feeling WOW-ed,

Petra

# Thread: Cheers from Spain
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheers-from-spain.828/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AndrÃ©s — 2012-04-12T17:06:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cheers-from-spain.828/post-14224>

Hi there!

I have been on paleo-like way of eating since my waking-up from the conventional wisdom faith one and a half years ago. Paleo-like because I just love hard cheeses, those forbidden to me for years due to a "high cholesterol", and I am at the moment also having some potatoes and rice, PHD-like. I am not trying to cure any ailment at all on myself and I will try (slowly) to go for optimal. My father is another case with its COPD, that I am trying to keep in check with vitamins D and C, and NAC in two weeks. I do not foresee convincing him to try CT in the immediate future.

I suppose I am 1/10th cold adapted, since it has been more than 20 years showering always in cold water. I am 43. I have been trying to expose myself to cold air in the last month (driving with AC on and walking at night outside in shorts), and I had to stop twice due to two colds that I have treated [titrating with ascorbic acid](http://http://www.doctoryourself.com/titration.html). So it seems that I will have to be more strict with the diet (have I mentioned cheese? ) on my third attempt, this time with cold baths, probably next autumn.

Actually, I am an ascorbic-nut for the same reason that I am focusing on being a cold-nut. They just make sense:

-   Ascorbic acid: animals without the [GULO mutation](http://http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/L-gulonolactone_oxidase) [increases their ascorbic acid production upon stress](http://http://perfecthealthdiet.com/?p=636).
    CT: Successful polar explorers ate only meat with lots of fat and they did not become hypothyroid (or with euthyroid sick syndrome), AFAIK.

So the next logical step is self-experimentation.
